Project {
  database_type: 'Databricks Unity Catalog'
  Note: 'Lakehouse data model for Manufacturing - Version 1'
}

Table production.work_center {
  work_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the work center record in the lakehouse silver layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and references.']
  asset_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.class. Business justification: Work centers are classified by asset class (e.g., CNC, press, conveyor) in manufacturing ERP systems. Production planners use this daily to filter capacity by equipment class and apply class-level mai']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.location. Business justification: Work centers are physically situated at specific plant or facility locations. This link is used by logistics planners for material routing, transport planning to the correct shop floor area, and capac']
  activity_type_code varchar [note: 'SAP Controlling activity type code (e.g., MACH, LABOR) used to allocate costs from this work center to production orders. Enables granular cost-of-goods-sold (COGS) analysis.']
  capacity_available_hours_per_day decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total available production hours per day for this work center, calculated from active shifts and break schedules. Used as the baseline for capacity requirements planning (CRP) in MRP II.']
  capacity_category varchar [note: 'Defines the primary capacity resource type tracked for this work center (machine hours, labor hours, energy units, tooling). Used in MRP II capacity requirements planning (CRP).']
  category_code varchar [note: 'SAP work center category code (e.g., 0001 for machine, 0002 for labor) that controls which fields and formulas are active for capacity and costing. Drives MRP II planning behavior.']
  code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric business key identifying the work center within the plant, as defined in SAP PP (field ARBPL). Used in routing operations, production orders, and MRP II capacity planning.']
  control_key varchar [note: 'SAP PP control key assigned to operations at this work center, determining whether operations are confirmed, printed, scheduled externally, or subject to milestone confirmation. Governs MES integration behavior.']
  costing_formula varchar [note: 'Identifier of the formula used to calculate production costs (machine costs, labor costs, overhead) for operations performed at this work center. Links to SAP CO activity types and cost rates.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where this work center is physically located. Supports multinational regulatory compliance, labor law adherence, and regional capacity reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the work center master data record was first created in the source system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and master data governance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the local currency used in cost rate calculations and activity pricing for this work center. Supports multi-currency costing in multinational operations.']
  cycle_time_seconds decimal(10,4) [note: 'Standard time in seconds to complete one unit of production at this work center under normal operating conditions. Compared against takt time to identify bottlenecks and balance production lines.']
  efficiency_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ratio of standard time to actual time expressed as a percentage, representing how efficiently the work center converts available time into productive output. Used in capacity planning and OEE calculations.']
  floor_area_sqm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical floor space occupied by the work center in square meters. Used in facility layout planning, 5S audits, and capacity expansion assessments.']
  hierarchy varchar [note: 'Hierarchical grouping path of the work center within the plant structure (e.g., \'Plant A > Production Area 2 > Assembly Line 1\'). Used for aggregated capacity reporting and organizational drill-down in analytics.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the work center master data record. Used for incremental data loading, change tracking, and master data governance in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  move_time_hours decimal(10,4) [note: 'Standard time in hours required to physically transport WIP from this work center to the next operation in the routing. Contributes to total manufacturing lead time in MRP II scheduling.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the work center (e.g., \'CNC Machining Cell 3\', \'Final Assembly Line A\'). Used in shop floor scheduling, routing, and reporting.']
  number_of_labor_resources varchar [note: 'Number of workers or operators assigned to this work center per shift. Used in labor capacity planning, workforce scheduling via Kronos, and standard cost calculations.']
  number_of_machines varchar [note: 'Count of individual machines or equipment units grouped within this work center. Used to calculate aggregate capacity and determine parallel processing capability.']
  number_of_shifts varchar [note: 'Number of production shifts per day scheduled at this work center. Directly determines available capacity hours and is used in shift-based scheduling and OEE calculations.']
  oee_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) percentage set for this work center, representing the desired level of availability, performance, and quality combined. Used as the KPI benchmark in TPM and continuous improvement programs.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where this work center is physically located. Supports multi-plant and multinational capacity planning.']
  production_strategy varchar [note: 'Manufacturing execution strategy applicable to this work center: Make-to-Order (MTO), Make-to-Stock (MTS), Engineer-to-Order (ETO), or Assemble-to-Order (ATO). Drives order type and scheduling behavior.']
  queue_time_hours decimal(10,4) [note: 'Standard waiting time in hours that a production order or WIP lot is expected to wait in queue before processing begins at this work center. Used in lead time scheduling and order float calculations.']
  responsible_supervisor varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the shop floor supervisor or foreman accountable for operations at this work center. Used for escalation routing, shift handover, and accountability reporting.']
  routing_usage varchar [note: 'Defines the permitted usage of this work center in routing operations: production routing, inspection routing, rework routing, maintenance routing, or all types. Controls which routing types can reference this work center.']
  setup_time_minutes decimal(10,4) [note: 'Standard time in minutes required to prepare (set up) the work center before production begins for a new operation or product. Used in routing time calculations and SMED improvement programs.']
  shift_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard duration in hours of a single production shift at this work center. Combined with number_of_shifts to derive total daily available capacity.']
  standard_value_key varchar [note: 'SAP PP standard value key defining which standard values (setup, machine, labor, teardown) are relevant for operations at this work center. Drives costing formula selection.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the work center. Active work centers are available for scheduling; under_maintenance are temporarily unavailable; decommissioned are retired from production.']
  takt_time_seconds decimal(10,4) [note: 'The rate at which products must be completed at this work center to meet customer demand, expressed in seconds per unit. Calculated as available production time divided by customer demand rate. Core metric in Lean Manufacturing and line balancing.']
  teardown_time_minutes decimal(10,4) [note: 'Standard time in minutes required to clean up and restore the work center after production of an operation is complete. Included in total operation lead time calculations.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the work center by its primary operational mode: machine (CNC, robot), labor (manual assembly), process (chemical/batch), assembly (multi-component joining), inspection (QA station), or storage (buffer/WIP area).']
  utilization_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of available capacity time that is actually scheduled or consumed by production orders. A key input to capacity leveling and bottleneck analysis in MRP II.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this work center record is valid and available for production scheduling and routing assignments. Supports time-dependent master data management in SAP PP.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this work center record is valid. After this date, the work center is no longer available for new routing or scheduling assignments. Supports planned decommissioning and capacity transitions.']

  Note: 'Master data for manufacturing work centers (SAP PP Work Center / Opcenter Resource). Represents a physical or logical production unit on the shop floor — a machine, assembly cell, production line, or labor group. Stores work center code, description, plant, cost center linkage, capacity category, available capacity (shifts, hours), efficiency rate, utilization rate, setup time, teardown time, queue time, move time, work center type (machine, labor, process), and responsible supervisor. SSOT for work center capacity and routing assignments used in MRP II planning and shop floor scheduling.'
}

Table production.routing {
  routing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a manufacturing routing record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for all routing-related joins and references.']
  bop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.bop. Business justification: Manufacturing routings implement engineering Bill of Process specifications. Production engineering translates design intent into executable shop floor operations with this link.']
  base_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Reference quantity for which all standard times (setup, machine, labor) in the routing operations are defined. Typically 1 or a standard batch size. Used to scale standard values proportionally for different production order quantities.']
  base_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure corresponding to the base quantity (e.g., EA=Each, KG=Kilogram, L=Liter, M=Meter). Aligns with the SAP material master base unit and ISO unit standards.']
  bop_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this routing to the corresponding Bill of Process (BoP) in Siemens Teamcenter PLM. The BoP defines the engineering-side process structure, while the routing is the ERP execution counterpart.']
  costing_relevance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this routing is used as the basis for standard cost calculation in SAP CO Product Costing. When true, the routing\'s standard times and work center activity rates are used to compute the manufacturing cost component of the product standard cost.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where manufacturing under this routing takes place. Used for regulatory compliance (CE Marking, RoHS, REACH), customs declarations, and multinational production reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the routing record was originally created in the source system (SAP PP). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and routing lifecycle tracking.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the routing, explaining the overall manufacturing process, intended product, and any special processing notes for production planners and shop floor engineers.']
  ecn_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) that authorized the creation or last modification of this routing. Provides traceability between process changes and engineering change management.']
  group varchar [note: 'SAP PP routing group key that, together with the routing group counter, uniquely identifies a task list. Groups related routing variants for the same material or product family.']
  group_counter varchar [note: 'SAP PP group counter that differentiates multiple routing variants (standard, rework, alternate) within the same routing group. Combined with routing_group to form the unique SAP task list key.']
  has_alternative_sequences boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the routing contains alternative operation sequences that can be selected based on work center availability, capacity constraints, or material characteristics. Supports flexible shop floor scheduling.']
  has_inspection_operations boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the routing includes quality inspection operations (linked to SAP QM inspection characteristics). Supports integration with the Quality Management System (QMS) and First Article Inspection (FAI) processes.']
  has_parallel_sequences boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the routing contains parallel operation sequences (concurrent process branches) in addition to the standard sequence. Parallel sequences enable simultaneous execution of independent operations, reducing total lead time.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code for the language in which the routing description and operation texts are maintained (e.g., EN, DE, FR, ZH). Supports multilingual routing documentation for global manufacturing operations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the routing record in the source system. Used for change detection, incremental data loading in the lakehouse Silver Layer, and audit compliance.']
  lot_size_from decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum production lot size for which this routing is valid. Used in SAP PP lot-size-dependent routing selection to ensure the correct process sequence is applied based on production quantity.']
  lot_size_to decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum production lot size for which this routing is valid. Together with lot_size_from, defines the quantity range for routing selection during production order creation and MRP II planning.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'SAP material master number of the finished or semi-finished product that this routing is designed to manufacture. Links the routing to the product engineering domain and Bill of Materials (BOM).']
  name varchar [note: 'Short descriptive name or title of the routing, providing a human-readable label for the manufacturing process sequence (e.g., \'Standard Assembly Routing – Motor Drive Unit\').']
  number varchar [note: 'Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the routing as defined in SAP PP (Routing Group / Task List Number). Used by production planners and shop floor personnel to reference the manufacturing process sequence.']
  operation_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual operations (process steps) defined in this routing. Provides a quick indicator of routing complexity and is used for routing comparison and process simplification analysis.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP organizational unit code identifying the manufacturing plant where this routing is valid and executed. Supports multi-plant, multinational operations and plant-level capacity planning.']
  plm_routing_code varchar [note: 'External identifier of the corresponding routing or process plan object in Siemens Teamcenter PLM. Enables bidirectional traceability between the ERP routing (SAP PP) and the PLM process definition for Engineering Change Management.']
  production_strategy varchar [note: 'Manufacturing production strategy associated with this routing: Make-to-Order (MTO), Make-to-Stock (MTS), Engineer-to-Order (ETO), or Assemble-to-Order (ATO). Determines how the routing is triggered and consumed in production order processing.']
  released_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the routing status was changed to \'released\', indicating formal approval for production use. Key lifecycle event for quality management traceability and Engineering Change Order (ECO) implementation tracking.']
  revision_level varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric revision level or version identifier of the routing (e.g., A, B, 01, 02). Incremented with each approved Engineering Change Order (ECO). Supports routing version control and audit traceability.']
  scheduling_relevance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this routing is used for production order scheduling and capacity requirements planning (CRP) in MRP II. When true, the routing drives work center load calculations and finite/infinite scheduling.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this routing record was sourced (e.g., SAP_S4HANA for PP module, TEAMCENTER for PLM BoP, OPCENTER for MES routing). Supports data lineage and multi-system reconciliation in the lakehouse.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the routing record: \'draft\' during initial creation, \'released\' when approved for production use, \'frozen\' when locked against changes, \'obsolete\' when superseded, \'under_review\' during Engineering Change Order (ECO) processing.']
  takt_time_sec decimal(18,3) [note: 'Takt time in seconds for this routing, representing the rate at which products must be completed to meet customer demand. Calculated as available production time divided by customer demand rate. Key Lean Manufacturing metric for line balancing and flow design.']
  task_list_type varchar [note: 'SAP PP task list type code indicating the category of the routing: N=Standard Routing, R=Reference Operation Set, M=Rate Routing, S=Rough-Cut Planning Profile, E=Equipment Task List, Q=Inspection Plan. Drives system behavior in MRP II and shop floor scheduling.']
  total_labor_time_min decimal(18,3) [note: 'Aggregate standard labor time in minutes across all routing operations for the base quantity. Used for workforce capacity planning, standard cost calculation (labor cost component), and productivity analysis.']
  total_lead_time_days decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total manufacturing lead time in days for the complete routing, representing the elapsed calendar time from start of first operation to completion of last operation. Used in MRP II scheduling, ATP (Available to Promise), and delivery date calculation.']
  total_machine_time_min decimal(18,3) [note: 'Aggregate standard machine processing time in minutes across all routing operations for the base quantity. Core input for MRP II capacity requirements planning (CRP), takt time analysis, and production scheduling.']
  total_setup_time_min decimal(18,3) [note: 'Aggregate standard setup time in minutes across all operations in the routing for the base quantity. Used for capacity planning, costing, and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) analysis. Supports SMED (Single-Minute Exchange of Dies) improvement tracking.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the routing variant indicating its intended use: \'standard\' for normal production, \'rework\' for non-conforming material reprocessing, \'alternate\' for secondary process paths, \'inspection\' for quality inspection routings, \'reference\' for reference operation sets.']
  usage varchar [note: 'SAP PP usage key defining the application context of the routing (e.g., 1=Production, 2=Engineering, 3=Universal, 5=Sales and Distribution). Controls which business processes can reference this routing.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date from which this routing version is effective and valid for use in production order creation and MRP II planning. Supports date-effective routing management and Engineering Change Order (ECO) implementation scheduling.']
  valid_to date [note: 'Date until which this routing version remains valid for production use. After this date, the routing is superseded by a newer version or marked obsolete. Used for routing version lifecycle management.']

  Note: 'Master data for manufacturing routings (SAP PP Routing / BoP — Bill of Process). Defines the sequence of operations required to manufacture a product, including operation numbers, work center assignments, standard values (setup time, machine time, labor time), control keys, and inspection characteristics. Supports multiple routing variants (standard, rework, alternate). Links to the engineering BOM and drives MRP II capacity planning, shop floor scheduling, and costing. SSOT for the manufacturing process sequence and standard times.'
}

Table production.routing_operation {
  routing_operation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a single operation step within a manufacturing routing. Serves as the primary key for the routing_operation entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  bop_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.bop_operation. Business justification: Routing operations execute specific BOP operations defined by engineering. Work instructions and quality checkpoints reference this to ensure design compliance during manufacturing.']
  routing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.routing. Business justification: routing_operation is a child step within a routing. The FK routing_id creates the proper parent-child relationship between individual operation steps and their parent routing, enabling routing structu']
  task_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.task_list. Business justification: Routing operations for production equipment setup or changeover reference asset task lists that define the exact maintenance or inspection steps required. Process engineers link these to standardize o']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: routing_operation is assigned to a specific work_center for execution. The FK work_center_id normalizes the work_center_code reference and enables joins to work_center master data for routing analysis']
  activity_type_code varchar [note: 'SAP CO activity type code (e.g., MACH for machine hours, LABOR for labor hours) used to calculate the standard cost of this operation. Links operation time values to cost rates defined in the controlling module for product cost calculation.']
  base_quantity decimal(13,3) [note: 'Reference quantity to which the standard time values apply. For example, if standard machine time is 5 minutes per 100 pieces, the base quantity is 100. Enables accurate time scaling for variable production lot sizes.']
  change_number varchar [note: 'Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) number that authorized the creation or modification of this routing operation. Provides full traceability of routing changes back to the originating engineering change request.']
  control_key varchar [note: 'SAP routing control key that governs operation behavior — determines whether the operation is scheduled, costed, printed on shop floor papers, requires confirmation, or triggers quality inspection. Examples: PP01 (internal processing), PP03 (external processing), QM01 (inspection).']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP Controlling (CO) cost center code associated with this operation\'s work center. Enables operation-level cost rollup, activity-based costing, and COGS allocation to the correct organizational unit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this routing operation record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage tracking, and compliance with ISO 9001 record control requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the external processing price (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY). Supports multi-currency operations in multinational manufacturing environments.']
  external_processing_price decimal(15,4) [note: 'Standard price agreed with the subcontractor for performing this operation externally. Used for purchase order creation, standard cost calculation, and make-vs-buy analysis. Applicable only when is_subcontracting is true.']
  is_inspection_operation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this operation is a quality inspection step that triggers an inspection lot in the Quality Management (QM) module. When true, the operation creates an inspection point and requires quality results recording before the production order can be confirmed.']
  is_milestone_operation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this operation is a milestone (gate) in the production routing. Milestone operations must be confirmed before subsequent operations can begin, enabling stage-gate control of the production flow and partial goods receipt.']
  is_subcontracting boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this operation is performed by an external subcontractor rather than internally. When true, the operation triggers a subcontracting purchase order in SAP MM and requires goods issue/receipt processing for the outsourced step.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this routing operation record. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse pipeline.']
  maximum_lot_size decimal(13,3) [note: 'Maximum production quantity that can be processed in a single run of this operation. Prevents overloading of work centers and ensures quality control limits are not exceeded per batch.']
  minimum_lot_size decimal(13,3) [note: 'Minimum production quantity that can be processed in a single run of this operation. Constrains MRP II lot sizing and production order splitting decisions to ensure operational efficiency.']
  move_time decimal(10,4) [note: 'Standard time required to transport materials or WIP from this operation to the next work center. Contributes to total lead time calculation and is used in scheduling to account for inter-operation material movement.']
  number_of_labor_resources varchar [note: 'Standard number of workers (labor resources) required to perform this operation simultaneously. Used in workforce capacity planning, labor cost calculation, and shop floor staffing models.']
  number_of_machines varchar [note: 'Standard number of machines required to perform this operation simultaneously. Used in machine capacity planning and OEE-based scheduling to determine resource requirements per production order.']
  operation_long_text varchar [note: 'Detailed work instructions, standard operating procedure (SOP) narrative, or technical guidance for performing the operation. Includes tooling requirements, safety precautions, quality checkpoints, and step-by-step execution notes.']
  operation_number varchar [note: 'Sequential numeric identifier for the operation within the routing, typically in increments of 10 (e.g., 0010, 0020, 0030). Determines the execution sequence on the shop floor and drives MES scheduling order.']
  operation_short_text varchar [note: 'Brief descriptive label for the operation step (e.g., \'CNC Milling\', \'Spot Welding\', \'Final Assembly\', \'Visual Inspection\', \'Powder Coating\'). Displayed on shop floor traveler documents and work order printouts.']
  operation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the manufacturing operation by process type. Enables filtering and grouping of operations for capacity analysis, cost rollup by process category, and shop floor scheduling by operation family.']
  operation_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the operation quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, M for meter). Defines how the operation output is counted and reported on the shop floor and in MES confirmations.']
  overlap_type varchar [note: 'Defines whether and how this operation can overlap with the preceding operation in the routing sequence. \'no_overlap\' requires full completion before the next starts; \'time_overlap\' allows a fixed time offset; \'quantity_overlap\' allows partial quantity transfer. Enables lead time compression in scheduling.']
  overlap_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric value defining the extent of overlap with the preceding operation. Interpreted based on overlap_type: for time_overlap, the number of time units (per time_unit); for quantity_overlap, the minimum quantity to transfer before the next operation starts.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where this routing operation is executed. Supports multi-plant routing management and plant-specific capacity planning in multinational operations.']
  queue_time decimal(10,4) [note: 'Standard waiting time (queue) before this operation begins, representing the time a work order waits in the queue at the work center. Included in lead time calculation and critical path scheduling. Expressed in the unit defined by time_unit.']
  scrap_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected percentage of material or components that will be scrapped during this operation under normal production conditions. Used in MRP II to inflate planned order quantities, in standard cost calculation, and in yield analysis. Expressed as a percentage (0–100).']
  standard_labor_time decimal(10,4) [note: 'Standard labor (person) time required per base quantity unit for this operation. Used for labor cost rollup, workforce capacity planning, and standard cost calculation. Expressed in the unit defined by time_unit.']
  standard_machine_time decimal(10,4) [note: 'Standard machine processing time per base quantity unit for this operation. Drives machine capacity loading in MRP II, OEE calculations, and takt time analysis. Expressed in the unit defined by time_unit.']
  standard_setup_time decimal(10,4) [note: 'Standard time required to set up the work center before production begins for this operation (e.g., tooling changeover, fixture installation, machine calibration). Used in capacity planning, SMED analysis, and production scheduling. Expressed in the unit defined by time_unit.']
  standard_teardown_time decimal(10,4) [note: 'Standard time required to dismantle tooling, clean the work center, and restore it to a ready state after the operation completes. Included in total operation duration for scheduling and capacity loading.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the routing operation record. \'active\' operations are available for production order assignment; \'inactive\' are temporarily disabled; \'under_review\' are pending engineering change approval; \'obsolete\' are retired and no longer used.']
  time_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure applied to all standard time values (setup, machine, labor, teardown) for this operation. Ensures consistent interpretation of time fields across scheduling, costing, and capacity planning systems.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this routing operation version becomes effective and can be used in production order scheduling. Supports date-effective routing management and engineering change control (ECN/ECO) implementation.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'Date after which this routing operation version expires and is no longer valid for new production orders. Used in conjunction with valid_from_date to manage engineering change effectivity and routing version control.']
  wait_time decimal(10,4) [note: 'Standard time the material must wait after this operation completes before the next operation can begin (e.g., curing time, cooling time, drying time). Included in lead time scheduling and critical path analysis.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the work center (machine, production cell, or labor group) where this operation is performed. Used for capacity planning, scheduling, and cost center assignment in MRP II resource planning.']

  Note: 'Individual operation steps within a manufacturing routing. Each record represents a single operation in the production sequence — e.g., machining, welding, assembly, inspection, painting. Stores operation number, operation short text, work center, control key, standard setup time, standard machine time, standard labor time, operation unit, scrap percentage, inspection flag, and long-text instructions. Enables granular shop floor scheduling, capacity loading per operation, and operation-level cost rollup. Child entity of routing.'
}

Table production.schedule {
  schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each production schedule record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for the production_schedule data product.']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.batch. Business justification: production_schedule references a batch for batch-managed production planning. The FK batch_id normalizes the batch_number string reference and enables joins to batch master data for batch-level schedu']
  forecast_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_forecast. Business justification: Master production scheduling uses sales forecasts to plan capacity and material requirements for make-to-stock items. Production planners align manufacturing schedules with anticipated demand from sal']
  fulfillment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.fulfillment_plan. Business justification: Production schedules are built to satisfy fulfillment plans that aggregate customer order commitments. Master schedulers align production schedules directly to fulfillment plans to ensure on-time deli']
  planned_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.planned_order. Business justification: production_schedule is built from MRP-generated planned_orders. The FK planned_order_id normalizes the planned_order_number string reference and enables joins to planned_order master data for schedule']
  routing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.routing. Business justification: production_schedule references a routing for scheduling purposes. The FK routing_id normalizes the routing_number string reference and enables joins to routing master data for capacity planning and sc']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.shift. Business justification: Production schedules specify which shift executes the work. Shift supervisors use this for crew assignments and labor planning.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: production_schedule is planned for a specific work_center. The FK work_center_id normalizes the work_center_code reference and enables joins to work_center master data for capacity load analysis and s']
  atp_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production schedule entry contributes to Available to Promise (ATP) stock calculations in SAP S/4HANA SD. When true, the planned production quantity is included in ATP/CTP commitment logic for the order domain.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot number assigned to the production run under this schedule entry, used for batch traceability in regulated or quality-controlled manufacturing environments. Aligns with SAP batch management and QM module requirements.']
  capacity_load_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the work center\'s available capacity consumed by this production schedule entry. A value of 100 indicates full utilization; values above 100 indicate overload conditions requiring rescheduling or capacity leveling in SAP PP.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity confirmed as feasible by capacity planning and resource availability checks in SAP PP or Siemens Opcenter MES. May differ from planned quantity when capacity constraints exist. Used for Capable to Promise (CTP) commitments.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the manufacturing plant location where this production schedule is executed. Supports multinational reporting, regulatory compliance tracking (CE, RoHS, REACH), and regional capacity analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this production schedule record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA PP or Siemens Opcenter MES). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the plant\'s local currency, used when cost estimates or standard cost values are associated with this production schedule entry in SAP CO.']
  cycle_time_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned cycle time in seconds for producing one unit at the assigned work center under this schedule. Used to validate feasibility against takt time targets and to calculate total scheduled duration for capacity planning.']
  freeze_horizon_date date [note: 'Date up to which the production schedule is frozen and protected from automatic MRP II rescheduling. Orders within the freeze horizon require manual authorization to change, ensuring shop floor stability and preventing nervousness in the plan.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to this production schedule record in the source system. Used for change detection in the Databricks Silver Layer incremental load process and audit compliance.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Short description of the material or finished good to be produced, as maintained in the SAP material master. Provides human-readable context for schedule review and shop floor communication.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA material master number identifying the finished good or semi-finished material to be produced under this schedule. Used to link to BOM, routing, and MRP planning data.']
  mrp_controller varchar [note: 'SAP MRP controller code or name responsible for managing the material\'s planning parameters and reviewing the generated production schedule. Used for accountability and exception management in the planning process.']
  mrp_run_date date [note: 'Date on which the MRP II planning run was executed in SAP S/4HANA that generated or last updated this production schedule. Provides auditability of the planning cycle and enables comparison between planning runs.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive human-readable name for the production schedule, used for identification in planning dashboards and MES scheduling boards (e.g., \'Week 32 Motor Assembly Plan - Plant DE01\').']
  number varchar [note: 'Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the production schedule, used for cross-system referencing between SAP S/4HANA PP and Siemens Opcenter MES. Typically follows a plant-year-sequence format.']
  oee_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) percentage set for the work center during this schedule period. OEE combines availability, performance, and quality rates. Used as a KPI baseline for shop floor performance monitoring via Siemens MindSphere and Opcenter Analytics.']
  planned_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total production cost for this schedule entry in the plant\'s local currency, derived from SAP CO standard cost estimates. Includes material, labor, and overhead components. Used for budget planning and COGS analysis.']
  planned_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the MRP-generated planned order in SAP S/4HANA PP that this schedule entry is derived from. Enables traceability from the production schedule back to the originating MRP II planning run and demand signal.']
  planned_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of the material planned for production within this schedule entry\'s time bucket and work center. Expressed in the material\'s base unit of measure as defined in the SAP material master.']
  planning_horizon_end date [note: 'The last calendar date of the planning horizon covered by this production schedule. Defines the end boundary for MRP II capacity planning and scheduled production orders.']
  planning_horizon_start date [note: 'The first calendar date of the planning horizon covered by this production schedule. Defines the start boundary for MRP II capacity planning and scheduled production orders.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA plant identifier representing the manufacturing facility for which this production schedule is generated. Aligns with the organizational unit in SAP PP and Siemens Opcenter MES plant configuration.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking assigned to this production schedule entry for shop floor sequencing. Lower values indicate higher priority. Used by Siemens Opcenter MES to sequence competing orders at shared work centers.']
  production_strategy varchar [note: 'Manufacturing execution strategy governing how production orders are triggered. Make to Order (MTO) produces against customer orders; Make to Stock (MTS) produces to replenish inventory; Engineer to Order (ETO) requires custom engineering; Assemble to Order (ATO) assembles from standard components on demand.']
  released_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the production schedule status was changed to \'released\', authorizing shop floor execution. Captured for audit trail and compliance with ISO 9001 documented information requirements.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP PP routing (Bill of Process) that defines the sequence of operations, work centers, and standard times for producing the scheduled material. Ensures the schedule is aligned with the approved manufacturing process.']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date on which production for this schedule entry is expected to be completed at the assigned work center. Drives delivery date commitments and ATP/CTP calculations for the order domain.']
  scheduled_end_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which production is scheduled to complete, including shift and time-zone context. Used by Siemens Opcenter MES for detailed shop floor sequencing and completion tracking.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date on which production for this schedule entry is expected to begin at the assigned work center. Used for shop floor sequencing and ATP/CTP date commitments to the order domain.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which production is scheduled to begin, including shift and time-zone context. Used by Siemens Opcenter MES for detailed shop floor sequencing and takt time alignment.']
  scheduling_strategy varchar [note: 'Scheduling direction applied during MRP II planning. Forward scheduling calculates the earliest possible completion date from a start date; backward scheduling calculates the latest start date from a required delivery date; mixed applies both depending on order priority.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Shift configuration planned for this production schedule entry, defining the number of operating shifts per day. Drives available capacity calculation and Kronos Workforce Central labor scheduling alignment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this production schedule record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution when multiple systems contribute schedule data to the Silver Layer.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the production schedule. Draft indicates initial planning; released means approved for shop floor execution; frozen means locked against changes; in_execution means active on the shop floor; completed means all planned orders fulfilled; cancelled means schedule voided.']
  takt_time_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target takt time in seconds for this production schedule entry, representing the rate at which products must be completed to meet customer demand. Calculated as available production time divided by customer demand rate. Used for line balancing and OEE target setting.']
  time_bucket varchar [note: 'Granularity of the planning time bucket used in this schedule. Daily buckets are used for short-horizon detailed scheduling; weekly for medium-term MRP II planning; monthly for long-range capacity planning.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for the planned production quantity, aligned with the SAP material master UoM definition (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, PC for piece). Ensures consistent quantity interpretation across MES and ERP systems.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version counter for the production schedule. Each re-planning or revision cycle increments this value, enabling version history tracking and comparison of planning iterations under MRP II.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center (machine group, production line, or cell) to which this schedule entry is assigned. Corresponds to the SAP PP work center and Siemens Opcenter MES resource definition used for capacity load calculation.']

  Note: 'Planned production schedule generated by MRP II / capacity planning for a planning horizon. Captures planned production quantities per finished good or semi-finished material, per work center, per time bucket (daily/weekly). Stores schedule version, planning horizon start/end, planned order references, capacity load percentage, scheduling strategy (forward/backward), takt time targets, and schedule status (draft, released, frozen). Drives shop floor execution sequencing and feeds ATP/CTP commitments to the order domain. SSOT for the production plan.'
}

Table production.planned_order {
  planned_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a planned order record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for this entity.']
  demand_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.demand_forecast. Business justification: Planned production orders are often driven by demand forecasts in make-to-stock manufacturing. Production planning teams trace planned orders back to the originating forecast to validate production qu']
  forecast_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.forecast. Business justification: Production planning teams consume sales forecasts to generate planned orders. MRP/demand-driven planning directly links a planned order back to the sales forecast that triggered it — used daily in pro']
  fulfillment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.fulfillment_plan. Business justification: Planned production orders are created in direct response to a fulfillment plan. Production planning teams link planned orders to fulfillment plans daily to ensure manufacturing output aligns with comm']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: In batch-managed manufacturing, planned orders are pre-assigned a lot/batch number before production starts. This enables early reservation and traceability linkage between planning and inventory lot ']
  mrp_planned_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.mrp_planned_order. Business justification: MRP generates planned orders that convert to production orders or purchase requisitions. Production planning uses MRP output daily to determine what to manufacture versus procure externally.']
  order_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.order_type. Business justification: planned_order.order_type (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the order_type master. Adding order_type_id FK normalizes this relationship, enabling joins to retrieve order category, confirmation co']
  priority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.priority. Business justification: Planned orders inherit or align with customer order priority classifications. Production schedulers use order priority to sequence planned orders, ensuring high-priority customer orders drive manufact']
  product_cost_estimate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.product_cost_estimate. Business justification: Planned orders reference the product cost estimate to determine standard costs for materials and activities. Production planning and cost controlling use this link to compare planned vs. actual costs ']
  product_variant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.product_variant. Business justification: Planned orders are created for a specific product variant. Production planning must reference the exact engineering variant to pull the correct BOM and routing. Used daily in MRP/ERP order generation.']
  routing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.routing. Business justification: planned_order references a routing for MRP planning purposes. The FK routing_id normalizes the routing_number string reference and enables joins to routing master data for lead time calculation and ca']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Planned orders specify the target storage location where produced goods will be received. MRP/MPS planning uses this to allocate storage capacity and direct goods receipt postings to the correct locat']
  version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.production_version. Business justification: planned_order references a production_version that defines the valid BOM and routing combination for manufacturing. The FK production_version_id normalizes the production_version string code and enabl']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: planned_order is assigned to a specific work_center for execution. The FK work_center_id normalizes the work_center_code reference and enables joins to work_center master data for MRP capacity plannin']
  bom_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Bill of Materials assigned to this planned order, defining the component materials and quantities required for production. Sourced from the production version or material master.']
  conversion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the planned order was converted to a production order or purchase requisition. Supports lead time analysis and planning cycle performance measurement.']
  converted_order_number varchar [note: 'The production order number or purchase requisition number generated when this planned order was converted. Provides traceability from planned demand to shop floor execution.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant where this planned order is to be executed. Supports multi-country regulatory compliance, reporting, and regional production analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the planned order record was created in the source system (SAP PP). Supports audit trail, data lineage, and planning cycle analysis.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the planned order cost valuation (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Supports multi-currency cost planning in multinational manufacturing operations.']
  demand_source_number varchar [note: 'The identifier of the originating demand element (e.g., sales order number, forecast record ID, project WBS element) that triggered this planned order. Enables end-to-end demand-to-production traceability.']
  demand_source_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the originating demand element that triggered the creation of this planned order by MRP. Supports demand traceability and planning analysis across MTO, MTS, and ETO strategies.']
  exception_message_code varchar [note: 'SAP MRP exception message code indicating a planning anomaly requiring planner attention (e.g., 01=Reschedule in, 02=Reschedule out, 10=Cancel, 15=New planned order). Drives the MRP exception monitor workflow.']
  goods_receipt_processing_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'The number of working days required to process the goods receipt after production completion before the material is available in stock. Included in MRP scheduling calculations.']
  in_house_production_days decimal(7,2) [note: 'The planned in-house production lead time in calendar days for this planned order, as determined by the routing or material master. Used by MRP for backward and forward scheduling calculations.']
  is_converted boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this planned order has been fully converted to a production order or purchase requisition. Once converted, the planned order is no longer an open planning element.']
  is_firmed boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the planned order has been manually firmed (fixed) by a planner, protecting it from automatic rescheduling or deletion by subsequent MRP runs. A firmed planned order represents a committed production plan.']
  last_changed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the planned order record was last modified in the source system. Supports change tracking, delta processing in the lakehouse pipeline, and audit compliance.']
  lot_size_procedure varchar [note: 'MRP lot-sizing procedure code used to calculate the planned order quantity (e.g., EX=Exact lot size, FX=Fixed lot size, HB=Replenishment up to maximum stock level, MB=Monthly lot size). Determines how demand quantities are grouped into planned orders.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Short descriptive text for the material to be produced, sourced from the SAP material master. Supports readability in reports and dashboards without requiring a join to the material master.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'SAP material master identifier for the finished good or semi-finished product to be produced under this planned order. Links to the material master record in SAP MM.']
  mrp_controller varchar [note: 'Three-character code identifying the MRP controller (planner) responsible for managing this planned order. Used for workload distribution and exception message routing in SAP PP.']
  mrp_run_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the MRP or MPS run was executed that generated this planned order. Enables traceability of planned orders back to specific planning cycles.']
  mrp_run_reference varchar [note: 'Unique reference identifier for the MRP/MPS planning run that generated this planned order. Supports audit traceability and re-planning analysis.']
  number varchar [note: 'Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the planned order as assigned by the MRP/MPS run in SAP PP. Used by planners and shop floor personnel to reference a specific planned production requirement.']
  opening_date date [note: 'The date on which the planned order should be converted to a production order or purchase requisition to allow sufficient lead time for execution. Calculated by MRP based on in-house production time and goods receipt processing time.']
  planned_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total production cost for this planned order in the local plant currency, including material costs, labor costs, and overhead. Used for cost planning, variance analysis, and COGS forecasting.']
  planning_plant varchar [note: 'The SAP plant code of the planning plant responsible for MRP planning of this material, which may differ from the production plant in cross-plant planning scenarios. Supports multi-plant and central planning configurations.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Four-character SAP plant identifier representing the manufacturing facility where the planned order is to be executed. Supports multi-plant production planning in a multinational environment.']
  production_strategy varchar [note: 'Manufacturing strategy governing how the planned order was generated: Make to Order (MTO), Make to Stock (MTS), Engineer to Order (ETO), or Assemble to Order (ATO). Determines demand traceability and inventory posting behavior.']
  requirements_date date [note: 'The date by which the planned order quantity must be available to satisfy the originating demand requirement (e.g., sales order, dependent demand from a higher-level BOM). Aligns with the MRP requirements element date.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production routing (sequence of operations) assigned to this planned order. Defines the manufacturing process steps, work centers, and standard times for producing the material.']
  scheduled_finish_date date [note: 'The planned calendar date on which production of this order is scheduled to be completed, as calculated by MRP forward scheduling. Drives delivery date commitments and ATP checks.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'The planned calendar date on which production of this order is scheduled to begin, as calculated by MRP backward scheduling. Used for capacity planning and material availability checks.']
  scrap_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Assembly scrap percentage applied to the planned order quantity to account for expected production losses. The planned quantity is inflated by this percentage to ensure the net required quantity is met after scrap.']
  special_procurement_type varchar [note: 'SAP special procurement type key indicating non-standard procurement scenarios such as subcontracting, consignment, stock transfer, or phantom assembly. Influences how MRP generates and processes the planned order.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the planned order. \'created\' indicates MRP-generated and open; \'firmed\' indicates manually fixed and protected from MRP rescheduling; \'converted\' indicates fully converted to a production order or purchase requisition; \'partially_converted\' indicates a split conversion; \'deleted\' indicates cancelled.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'SAP storage location code where the finished goods produced under this planned order will be received into inventory upon completion. Supports warehouse and inventory management integration.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for the planned quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, PC for piece, L for liter). Aligned with the material master base UoM in SAP MM.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary work center or production resource assigned to execute this planned order. Used for capacity requirements planning (CRP) and shop floor scheduling.']

  Note: 'MRP/MPS-generated planned order representing a future production requirement before conversion to a production order (SAP PP Planned Order). Stores planned order number, material number, plant, planned quantity, scheduled start date, scheduled finish date, order type (planned order for in-house production vs. purchase requisition), MRP run reference, firming indicator, and conversion status. Acts as the bridge between demand planning and shop floor execution. Converted to production orders upon release.'
}

Table production.shop_order_operation {
  shop_order_operation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each shop order operation execution record in the silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for this entity.']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.batch. Business justification: shop_order_operation processes a specific batch on the shop floor. The FK batch_id normalizes the batch_number string reference and enables joins to batch master data for batch-level operation traceab']
  machine_work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific machine or equipment asset assigned to execute this operation. Links to the equipment master in Maximo EAM and Siemens MindSphere for OEE tracking, predictive maintenance, and asset utilization reporting.']
  production_order_cost_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.production_order_cost. Business justification: Each shop order operation incurs costs tracked in production_order_cost. Cost controllers link operational execution to financial cost objects daily to capture actual labor, machine, and overhead cost']
  routing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.routing. Business justification: shop_order_operation executes operations defined in a routing. The FK routing_id normalizes the routing_number string reference and enables joins to routing master data for operation sequence validati']
  routing_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.routing_operation. Business justification: shop_order_operation executes a specific routing_operation step on the shop floor. The FK routing_operation_id creates a direct relational link between execution records and their corresponding routin']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.shift. Business justification: shop_order_operation is executed during a specific shift. The FK shift_id normalizes the shift_code reference and enables joins to shift master data for shift-level productivity analysis, labor cost a']
  tooling_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.tooling_equipment. Business justification: Shop order operations specify which engineering-defined tooling or equipment must be used. Process engineers define tooling requirements; production dispatchers assign them to operations. Critical for']
  wip_stock_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.wip_stock. Business justification: Each shop order operation consumes or produces WIP inventory at intermediate routing steps. Production control links operations to WIP stock records to track in-process material quantities across the ']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: shop_order_operation executes at a specific work_center. The FK work_center_id normalizes the work_center_code reference and enables joins to work_center master data for capacity analysis, OEE attribu']
  work_order_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.work_order_operation. Business justification: Production operations on a shop order sometimes require a concurrent maintenance work order operation (e.g., tooling change, calibration mid-run). Planners link these to coordinate production and main']
  actual_finish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the operation execution was completed on the shop floor, as recorded by the MES or operator confirmation. Used for cycle time calculation, throughput analysis, and OEE performance measurement.']
  actual_labor_time_min decimal(10,3) [note: 'Actual direct labor time in minutes consumed by the operator(s) for this operation. Used for actual cost confirmation in SAP CO, labor efficiency variance analysis, and workforce productivity reporting.']
  actual_run_time_min decimal(10,3) [note: 'Actual machine or process run time in minutes for this operation, excluding setup and teardown. Core input for OEE performance rate calculation and cycle time variance analysis against standard run time.']
  actual_setup_time_min decimal(10,3) [note: 'Actual time in minutes spent on machine/tooling setup before production run began. Used for SMED (Single-Minute Exchange of Dies) analysis, changeover optimization, and OEE availability loss calculation.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the operation execution began on the shop floor, as recorded by the MES or operator confirmation. Used for cycle time calculation, schedule adherence, and OEE availability analysis.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Production batch or lot number associated with the material being processed in this operation. Critical for batch traceability, genealogy tracking, and regulatory compliance in industries requiring lot-level control (e.g., serialized components).']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'SAP PP operation confirmation document number (CO11N confirmation number) generated when the operation is confirmed in the ERP system. Provides the audit trail link between the MES execution record and the ERP financial posting.']
  control_key varchar [note: 'SAP PP routing control key that governs the scheduling, confirmation, and costing behavior of this operation (e.g., PP01 for internally processed, PP03 for milestone confirmation). Determines whether the operation is relevant for scheduling, capacity planning, and cost confirmation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shop order operation record was first created in the source system (MES or ERP). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  cycle_time_sec decimal(10,3) [note: 'Actual cycle time in seconds measured for this operation execution, calculated from actual start to actual finish divided by yield quantity. Compared against takt time and standard cycle time for OEE performance rate and lean analysis.']
  downtime_min decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total unplanned downtime in minutes recorded during this operation execution (e.g., machine breakdown, material shortage, quality stop). Core input for OEE availability rate calculation and downtime root cause analysis.']
  downtime_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded classification of the primary downtime cause during this operation (e.g., MECH_FAILURE, TOOLING, MATERIAL, QUALITY_HOLD, CHANGEOVER, PLANNED_MAINT). Used for Pareto analysis of downtime losses and TPM (Total Productive Maintenance) improvement programs.']
  hold_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason for placing this operation in \'held\' status (e.g., QUALITY_HOLD, MATERIAL_SHORTAGE, SAFETY_STOP, ENGINEERING_REVIEW). Populated when status is \'held\'. Supports root cause analysis and downtime categorization.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this shop order operation record in the source system. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail in the Databricks silver layer.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'SAP material number of the finished or semi-finished product being produced by this operation. Enables product-level OEE, yield, and scrap analysis across production orders.']
  nc_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a non-conformance (NC) was detected during this operation. When true, triggers NCR (Non-Conformance Report) creation in the QMS and may initiate a hold on the production order. Key quality gate indicator.']
  nc_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Non-Conformance Report (NCR) raised against this operation when nc_flag is true. Provides direct traceability to the quality management system record for CAPA and disposition tracking.']
  operation_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the manufacturing operation being performed (e.g., \'CNC Milling\', \'Weld Assembly\', \'Final Inspection\'). Sourced from the routing master in SAP PP or Opcenter MES operation definition.']
  operation_number varchar [note: 'Sequential operation number within the production routing (e.g., 0010, 0020, 0030). Corresponds to the routing operation sequence in SAP PP and Opcenter MES, defining the order of execution steps within a production order.']
  operation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the operation by its functional role in the manufacturing process. Distinguishes between value-adding processing steps, setup/teardown activities, quality inspection, rework, transport, and wait states for OEE and lean analysis.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where this operation is executed. Supports multi-plant, multinational production reporting and cost allocation by plant.']
  production_order_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible production order number (SAP PP order number or Opcenter MES work order number) to which this operation belongs. Provides the primary traceability link between the operation execution record and the parent production order.']
  production_strategy varchar [note: 'Manufacturing strategy under which this operation is executed: Make-to-Order (MTO), Make-to-Stock (MTS), Engineer-to-Order (ETO), or Assemble-to-Order (ATO). Drives scheduling priority, cost allocation, and customer order linkage rules.']
  quantity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for yield, scrap, and rework quantities (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, M for meter, L for liter). Sourced from the SAP MM material master base unit of measure.']
  rework_quantity decimal(15,4) [note: 'Quantity of units requiring rework (reprocessing to meet specification) identified during this operation. Distinct from scrap as units are recoverable. Used for rework cost tracking, quality trend analysis, and CAPA initiation.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing routing (Bill of Process / BoP) from which this operation was derived. Links the actual execution back to the planned routing master for variance analysis and process improvement.']
  scheduled_finish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned finish date and time for this operation as determined by the production scheduler. Used for on-time delivery tracking, schedule adherence KPIs, and downstream operation sequencing.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned start date and time for this operation as determined by the production scheduler (SAP PP/DS or Opcenter Scheduling). Used for schedule adherence measurement and capacity planning variance analysis.']
  scrap_quantity decimal(15,4) [note: 'Quantity of units scrapped (irreversibly rejected) during this operation. Feeds OEE quality rate calculation, scrap cost reporting in SAP CO, and quality management CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) processes.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the specific unit being processed in this operation, applicable for serialized production (e.g., ETO or high-value assemblies). Enables unit-level traceability from manufacturing through field service.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Production shift during which this operation was executed (e.g., DAY, AFTERNOON, NIGHT, WEEKEND). Used for shift-level OEE analysis, labor cost allocation by shift, and production performance benchmarking across shifts.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating operational system of record for this operation record (Siemens Opcenter MES, SAP PP, or manual entry). Supports data lineage, reconciliation between MES and ERP, and silver layer data quality management.']
  spc_violation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Statistical Process Control (SPC) control chart violation (e.g., out-of-control signal) was detected during this operation. Triggers quality alert and potential hold in the MES quality module.']
  standard_run_time_min decimal(10,3) [note: 'Planned or standard run time in minutes for this operation as defined in the routing master. Used as the baseline for cycle time variance, OEE performance rate, and manufacturing cost standard costing.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current execution status of the shop order operation on the shop floor. Drives WIP tracking, MES workflow routing, and production scheduling visibility. \'Held\' indicates a quality or safety stop; \'partially_confirmed\' indicates a mid-operation confirmation in SAP PP.']
  takt_time_sec decimal(10,3) [note: 'Planned takt time in seconds for this operation, representing the rate at which products must be completed to meet customer demand. Used for line balancing, capacity planning, and lean manufacturing analysis.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center (machine group, production cell, or cost center) where this operation is executed. Maps to the SAP PP Work Center master and Opcenter MES Resource. Used for capacity planning, scheduling, and cost allocation.']
  yield_quantity decimal(15,4) [note: 'Quantity of conforming (good) units produced by this operation that pass to the next step or are completed. Core metric for OEE quality rate, first-pass yield (FPY), and production order confirmation in SAP PP.']

  Note: 'Execution-level record for each operation performed against a production order on the shop floor (Opcenter MES Operation / SAP PP Confirmation at operation level). Tracks actual start/finish timestamps, operator ID, work center, machine ID, actual setup time, actual run time, actual labor time, yield quantity, scrap quantity, rework quantity, operation status (queued, active, completed, held), and NC (non-conformance) flag. Provides granular WIP tracking and feeds OEE calculations and actual cost confirmations.'
}

Table production.production_confirmation {
  production_confirmation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a production confirmation record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.batch. Business justification: production_confirmation references the batch produced during the confirmed operation. The FK batch_id normalizes the batch_number string reference and enables joins to batch master data for batch-leve']
  finished_goods_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Production confirmations specify where finished goods are received into inventory. Warehouse systems use this for automatic putaway task generation.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Production confirmations are assigned to fiscal periods for period-end closing, work-in-process valuation, and cost of goods manufactured reporting. Controllers use this for monthly financial close.']
  goods_issue_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.goods_issue. Business justification: Production confirmations trigger or reference goods issue documents when finished goods are transferred to fulfillment. Warehouse and production teams reconcile production output against goods issue r']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: When a production confirmation is posted, it triggers or references an inspection lot for goods-receipt or in-process quality check. This is a standard SAP PP-QM integration used in every manufacturin']
  measurement_reading_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.measurement_reading. Business justification: When confirming production output, operators record machine counter or meter readings (e.g., press strokes, spindle hours) that update asset measurement points. This link drives usage-based maintenanc']
  product_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.product_specification. Business justification: Production confirmations record that output was verified against a specific product specification revision. Quality inspectors use this link to confirm which spec version governed the production run —']
  routing_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.routing_operation. Business justification: production_confirmation is posted for a specific routing_operation. The FK routing_operation_id enables joins to routing_operation master data for operation-level confirmation analysis and standard ti']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.shift. Business justification: production_confirmation is posted for a specific shift. The FK shift_id normalizes the shift string reference and enables joins to shift master data for shift-level confirmation analysis and labor tim']
  wip_stock_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.wip_stock. Business justification: Production confirmations update WIP stock levels as operations complete. Each confirmation reduces or transfers WIP inventory, requiring a direct reference to the WIP stock record being consumed or tr']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: production_confirmation is posted at a specific work_center. The FK work_center_id enables joins to work_center master data for confirmation analysis and capacity utilization reporting. work_center_na']
  actual_cost_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual manufacturing cost settled to the production order upon confirmation, including material, labor, machine, and overhead components. Used for actual vs. standard cost variance analysis and COGS reporting.']
  actual_finish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the confirmed operation was completed on the shop floor. Combined with actual start timestamp to derive actual cycle time and compare against takt time.']
  actual_labor_time_min decimal(12,4) [note: 'Actual labor time in minutes consumed during the confirmed operation. Used for labor cost settlement, workforce productivity analysis, and comparison against standard labor time from the routing.']
  actual_machine_time_min decimal(12,4) [note: 'Actual machine runtime in minutes consumed during the confirmed operation. Used for OEE performance rate calculation, machine utilization reporting, and actual vs. standard cost variance analysis.']
  actual_setup_time_min decimal(12,4) [note: 'Actual changeover/setup time in minutes recorded for the confirmed operation. Used for SMED analysis, OEE availability calculation, and identification of changeover reduction opportunities.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the confirmed operation began on the shop floor. Used to calculate actual cycle time, machine utilization, and OEE availability rate.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch or lot number assigned to the confirmed production output. Critical for batch traceability, quality inspection lot linkage, and regulatory compliance (e.g., serialization, REACH/RoHS).']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of good units successfully produced and confirmed against the production order operation. Represents the yield posted to inventory. Used for OEE quality rate, throughput, and order completion percentage calculations.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the manufacturing plant where the confirmation was posted. Supports multi-country regulatory compliance reporting (e.g., CE Marking, RoHS, REACH, OSHA) and regional analytics.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the actual cost amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Supports multi-currency cost reporting for multinational manufacturing operations.']
  goods_movement_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP material document number generated by the inventory posting (goods receipt) triggered by this production confirmation. Provides the audit trail link between the confirmation event and the resulting stock and accounting entries.']
  is_auto_goods_receipt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the goods receipt (inventory posting from WIP to finished goods) was triggered automatically upon confirmation posting, as configured in the production order control key.']
  is_final_confirmation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this confirmation is the final posting for the production order operation, triggering order status change to \'Technically Completed\' (TECO) in SAP PP and closing WIP balances.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Short description of the material being confirmed, as maintained in the SAP material master. Supports self-contained reporting without requiring a material master lookup.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'SAP material master number of the finished or semi-finished product being confirmed. Links the confirmation to the product catalog, BOM, and costing records.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'SAP goods movement type code associated with the inventory posting triggered by this confirmation (e.g., 101 = GR for production order, 531 = GR for by-product). Determines the accounting entries and stock update logic.']
