  ncr_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the Non-Conformance Report raised against this confirmation when scrap or rework quantities are posted. Triggers CAPA workflow and feeds the QMS for defect tracking and regulatory reporting.']
  number varchar [note: 'Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the production confirmation, as assigned by SAP PP or Siemens Opcenter MES. Used for cross-system traceability and operator reference on the shop floor.']
  operation_number varchar [note: 'Routing operation sequence number (e.g., 0010, 0020) within the production order for which this confirmation is posted. Identifies the specific manufacturing step confirmed.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant identifier representing the manufacturing facility where the production confirmation was posted. Enables multi-plant production reporting and cross-site OEE benchmarking.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Accounting-relevant date on which the goods movement and cost settlement are posted to the general ledger. May differ from the confirmation timestamp when backdating is applied for period-end closing.']
  production_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the production order against which this confirmation is posted. Links shop floor execution output back to the originating manufacturing order in SAP PP.']
  production_strategy varchar [note: 'Manufacturing strategy under which the confirmed production order was executed: Make-to-Order (MTO), Make-to-Stock (MTS), Engineer-to-Order (ETO), or Assemble-to-Order (ATO). Drives demand linkage and inventory valuation logic.']
  quality_inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'SAP QM inspection lot number automatically created upon posting of the production confirmation. Links the confirmed output to the quality inspection process for usage decision and AQL-based sampling.']
  rework_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of units identified as requiring rework during the confirmed operation. Drives rework order creation and feeds cost-of-poor-quality (COPQ) reporting and CAPA processes.']
  scrap_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of units scrapped during the confirmed operation. Triggers scrap cost postings and feeds scrap rate KPI reporting. Used in Six Sigma and Lean Manufacturing waste analysis.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Manufacturing shift during which the production confirmation was recorded (e.g., Day, Afternoon, Night, Shift-A). Used for shift-level OEE analysis, throughput reporting, and labor cost allocation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system from which the production confirmation record was extracted (e.g., SAP_PP, OPCENTER_MES, MANUAL). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements in the Silver Layer.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the production confirmation record. \'Posted\' indicates successful goods movement; \'Reversed\' indicates a corrective reversal has been applied; \'Blocked\' indicates a hold pending quality or variance review.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'SAP storage location to which confirmed finished goods or semi-finished goods are transferred upon posting. Drives inventory placement in WMS and supports ATP/CTP calculations.']
  timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the production confirmation was posted in the MES or ERP system. Serves as the authoritative completion event timestamp for WIP-to-finished-goods inventory postings and actual cost settlement.']
  type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this confirmation represents a partial quantity posting, a final completion posting, a reversal of a prior confirmation, or a milestone-based confirmation. Drives order status transitions in SAP PP.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for confirmed, scrap, and rework quantities (e.g., EA, KG, L, M). Aligns with SAP material master base UoM to ensure consistent inventory and cost postings.']
  variance_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason explaining deviations from planned quantities, times, or costs (e.g., machine breakdown, material shortage, quality rejection). Feeds variance analysis in SAP CO and supports CAPA and root cause analysis.']
  variance_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context for the variance reason code. Captures operator or supervisor notes on deviations for root cause analysis and continuous improvement initiatives.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center (machine, production line, or cell) where the confirmed operation was executed. Used for capacity utilization, OEE calculation, and cost center allocation.']

  Note: 'Goods confirmation record posted against a production order upon completion of production activities (SAP PP Goods Receipt / Order Confirmation). Captures confirmed quantity (yield), scrap quantity, rework quantity, confirmation timestamp, confirming operator, work center, movement type, storage location, batch number, and variance reason codes. Triggers inventory postings (WIP to finished goods), actual cost settlement, and order status updates. SSOT for production output quantities and completion events.'
}

Table production.material_staging {
  material_staging_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each material staging and issuance record on the shop floor. Serves as the primary key for the material_staging data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.batch. Business justification: material_staging references the batch being issued to the shop floor. The FK batch_id normalizes the batch_number string reference and enables joins to batch master data for batch-level material flow ']
  delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.delivery. Business justification: Material staging is directly triggered by an inbound or outbound delivery document. Production planners and warehouse staff link staged materials to the delivery to confirm what has arrived and is rea']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Material staging in production is directly triggered by goods receipts from procurement. Warehouse and production teams use this link to confirm that received materials are staged to the correct work ']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line_item. Business justification: Materials are staged for specific order line items in configure-to-order environments. Warehouse and production teams use this to ensure correct components are available for customer-specific configur']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_location. Business justification: Material staging occurs at specific logistics locations (warehouses, staging areas, docks) before production. Production planners coordinate with logistics to ensure materials are positioned correctly']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Production material staging references the originating purchase order to trace which procurement document authorized the material supply. Used by production planners to resolve material shortages and ']
  schedule_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.schedule_line. Business justification: Material staging is driven by customer order schedule lines that specify required quantities and dates. Production logistics teams stage materials against specific schedule lines to meet confirmed del']
  source_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Material staging pulls from specific storage locations. Pick lists direct warehouse staff to exact bin locations for production material retrieval.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: material_staging.work_center_code (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the work_center master. Material staging records are associated with the work center to which materials are being staged (supp']
  actual_staging_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the material was physically moved to the staging area or production supply area. Captured by MES or WMS system. Used for staging lead time analysis and JIT performance measurement.']
  backflush_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is subject to backflushing (automatic goods issue upon production order confirmation) rather than manual goods issue. Backflushed components do not require explicit staging transactions.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot number (SAP CHARG) of the material being staged or issued. Critical for batch-managed materials, traceability, quality management, and regulatory compliance (e.g., RoHS, REACH).']
  bom_component_position varchar [note: 'Position or item number of the component within the Bill of Materials (BOM) for the production order. Enables traceability from the staged material back to the BOM structure in PLM/ERP.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the manufacturing plant where the staging event occurred (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN). Supports multinational regulatory compliance, REACH/RoHS jurisdiction tracking, and regional reporting.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the material cost values (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CNY). Supports multi-currency operations across global manufacturing plants.']
  goods_issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the formal goods issue (SAP movement type 261) was posted in SAP S/4HANA, confirming material consumption against the production order. Triggers WIP cost update and inventory reduction.']
  hazardous_material_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the staged material is classified as hazardous under applicable regulations (e.g., REACH, RoHS, OSHA HazCom). Triggers additional handling, documentation, and safety protocols on the shop floor.']
  issued_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity of material formally issued to the production order via SAP Goods Issue (movement type 261). Represents confirmed material consumption posted to the production order and used for WIP costing.']
  issuing_warehouse varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse or warehouse complex (SAP WM Warehouse Number / Infor WMS Warehouse) from which the material is physically picked and issued to the shop floor. Supports WMS integration.']
  kanban_card_number varchar [note: 'Kanban card or container identifier associated with this material staging record when the replenishment_strategy is \'Kanban\'. Links the staging event to the Kanban control cycle for pull-based replenishment tracking.']
  material_cost_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard or moving average cost per unit of the material at the time of goods issue, as valuated in SAP S/4HANA CO. Used for WIP costing, COGS calculation, and production order cost analysis.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'SAP inventory movement type code (BWART) associated with the staging or issuance transaction. Common values: 261 (Goods Issue to Production Order), 262 (Reversal of GI), 311 (Transfer to Production Storage Location), 312 (Reversal of Transfer).']
  planned_staging_date date [note: 'Planned date on which the material should be staged or issued to the shop floor, as determined by MRP II scheduling and production order requirements. Used for material availability planning.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code (WERKS) identifying the manufacturing facility where the material staging and issuance takes place. Supports multi-plant, multinational operations.']
  production_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the production order (SAP PP Production Order / Process Order) for which materials are being staged or issued. Links material consumption to the manufacturing execution context.']
  production_strategy varchar [note: 'Manufacturing production strategy under which the production order was created: MTO (Make to Order), MTS (Make to Stock), ETO (Engineer to Order), or ATO (Assemble to Order). Determines material planning and staging behavior.']
  replenishment_strategy varchar [note: 'Material replenishment strategy governing how this component is supplied to the shop floor. Supports JIT (Just-In-Time), Kanban pull-based replenishment, MRP-driven push replenishment, manual staging, or consignment stock.']
  required_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Planned quantity of the material required for the production order as specified in the BOM or reservation. Represents the demand quantity before actual issuance. Used for shortage analysis and MRP II planning.']
  reservation_line varchar [note: 'Line item within the material reservation corresponding to the specific component being staged. Used to match the staging record to the exact reservation item in SAP.']
  reservation_number varchar [note: 'SAP reservation number (RSNUM) created by MRP or production order release, representing the planned material requirement that this staging record fulfills. Supports JIT and MRP II material planning workflows.']
  reversal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when a goods issue reversal (SAP movement type 262) was posted, cancelling a previously issued quantity. Applicable only when staging_status is \'reversed\'. Supports audit trail and WIP cost correction.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the specific component unit being issued, applicable for serialized materials. Supports unit-level traceability and asset lifecycle management.']
  short_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of material that could not be staged or issued due to insufficient stock (shortage). Calculated as required_quantity minus issued_quantity when status is \'short\'. Drives material shortage alerts and expediting actions.']
  shortage_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a material shortage exists for this staging record (i.e., required_quantity cannot be fully fulfilled from available stock). Drives shortage alerts, expediting, and JIT replenishment triggers.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this material staging record originated. Identifies whether the record was created in SAP S/4HANA PP/MM, Siemens Opcenter MES, Infor WMS, or entered manually. Supports data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  staged_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of material physically moved to the staging area or production supply area (PSA) but not yet formally issued via goods issue posting. Represents the intermediate staging step in JIT and Kanban workflows.']
  staging_document_line varchar [note: 'Line item number within the staging document, identifying the specific component or material being staged or issued within a multi-line goods issue document.']
  staging_document_number varchar [note: 'Business document number assigned by SAP S/4HANA (PP/MM) to the goods issue or material staging transaction. Corresponds to the SAP Material Document Number (MBLNR) generated upon goods issue posting.']
  staging_document_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year associated with the staging document number in SAP S/4HANA. Required to uniquely identify a material document in conjunction with the document number.']
  staging_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the material staging record. \'planned\' = reservation created; \'staged\' = material moved to staging area; \'issued\' = goods issue posted; \'short\' = insufficient stock; \'partially_issued\' = partial goods issue; \'reversed\' = goods issue reversed; \'cancelled\' = staging cancelled.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'SAP storage location (LGORT) from which the material is staged or issued. Identifies the physical warehouse or shop floor storage area within the plant from which inventory is consumed.']
  supply_area varchar [note: 'SAP Production Supply Area (PSA) or line-side staging location where the material is delivered for shop floor consumption. Represents the physical destination on the shop floor for staged materials.']
  total_issued_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total cost of the issued material quantity (issued_quantity × material_cost_per_unit) in the transaction currency. Feeds WIP costing, production order actual cost, and COGS reporting.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure (SAP MEINS) for the material quantities (required, staged, issued, short). Examples: EA (each), KG (kilogram), L (liter), M (meter), M2 (square meter). Ensures consistent quantity reporting.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'Costing method used to value the issued material: standard cost (fixed standard price), moving average price (MAP), or actual cost. Determines how WIP and COGS are calculated for the production order.']

  Note: 'Records the staging and issuance of raw materials and components from inventory to the shop floor for a production order (SAP PP Goods Issue / Material Staging). Stores production order reference, component material number, required quantity, issued quantity, staging date/time, storage location, batch number, issuing warehouse, and staging status (planned, staged, issued, short). Tracks material availability on the shop floor and feeds WIP costing. Supports JIT and Kanban material replenishment workflows.'
}

Table production.order_status {
  order_status_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each production order status transition event record in the Silver layer lakehouse.']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.batch. Business justification: order_status snapshots reference the batch associated with the production order at the time of status transition. The FK batch_id normalizes the batch_number string reference and enables joins to batc']
  order_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.order_type. Business justification: order_status.production_order_type (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the order_type master. Adding order_type_id FK normalizes this, allowing status lifecycle records to be filtered and analyzed']
  routing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.routing. Business justification: order_status.routing_number (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the routing master. Production order status lifecycle records are associated with a specific routing that defines the operations bei']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: order_status.work_center_code (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the work_center master. Status transitions on production orders are often associated with a specific work center (e.g., a HOLD sta']
  actual_finish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when production execution was completed for this order, as recorded in Opcenter MES or SAP PP technical completion. Used to calculate actual cycle time and on-time delivery performance.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when production execution began for this order, as recorded in Opcenter MES or SAP PP confirmation. Used to calculate actual lead time and schedule variance.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The production batch or lot number associated with the production order at the time of the status transition. Critical for batch traceability, recall management, and GMP compliance.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code representing the legal entity responsible for the production order. Supports multi-entity financial reporting, IFRS/GAAP compliance, and intercompany manufacturing scenarios.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The cumulative quantity of the production order that has been confirmed (goods receipt posted) at the time of the status transition. Enables partial completion tracking and yield analysis.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP cost center associated with the production order at the time of the status transition. Supports COGS allocation, production cost tracking, and financial controlling.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant or manufacturing site where the production order status transition occurred. Supports multinational regulatory compliance and regional performance reporting.']
  duration_minutes decimal(12,2) [note: 'The elapsed time in minutes that the production order spent in the from_status before transitioning to the to_status. Enables dwell time analysis, bottleneck identification, and SLA breach detection.']
  from_status varchar [note: 'The production order status immediately before the transition event. Represents the prior lifecycle state as recorded in SAP PP or Opcenter MES (e.g., CREATED, RELEASED, IN_PROCESS).']
  is_sla_breached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the production order exceeded its defined SLA target duration at the time of this status transition. Drives escalation workflows and production performance KPI reporting.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Short description of the material or finished product being manufactured, as maintained in the SAP material master. Provides human-readable context for reporting without requiring a join.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'SAP material number (SKU) of the finished good or semi-finished product being manufactured by this production order. Links the status history to the product being produced.']
  mes_operation_number varchar [note: 'The specific MES operation or activity number within the production order routing that was active or completed at the time of the status transition. Provides granular shop floor execution context.']
  ncr_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the Non-Conformance Report (NCR) that triggered a QM hold or rework status transition. Enables traceability between production order status events and quality non-conformances.']
  order_category varchar [note: 'SAP order category distinguishing production orders (discrete manufacturing), process orders (batch/process manufacturing), maintenance orders, rework orders, and inspection orders.']
  order_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total planned production quantity for the order at the time of the status transition. Captures the order size context for throughput and capacity analysis.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where the production order is being executed. Supports multi-plant and multinational reporting.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'The business priority assigned to the production order, influencing scheduling and resource allocation decisions. EXPEDITE indicates customer-driven urgency or supply chain escalation.']
  production_order_number varchar [note: 'The business key of the production order (SAP PP order number or Opcenter work order number) whose status is being tracked. Serves as the primary cross-system reference for the order lifecycle.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP profit center assigned to the production order, enabling cost and revenue attribution by business unit or product line for management accounting and EBITDA reporting.']
  qm_inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'The SAP QM inspection lot number associated with this status transition when the trigger is a quality hold, quality release, or inspection result. Links production order status to quality inspection outcomes.']
  quantity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the order quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, PC for piece). Ensures correct interpretation of quantity values across materials.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this status transition record was created in the Silver layer lakehouse. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and incremental load processing.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this status transition record was last updated in the Silver layer lakehouse. Supports change tracking, data quality monitoring, and SCD Type 1 correction scenarios.']
  scheduled_finish_date date [note: 'The planned completion date of the production order as scheduled in SAP PP or Opcenter MES at the time of the status transition. Baseline for on-time delivery and SLA compliance measurement.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'The planned start date of the production order as scheduled in SAP PP or Opcenter MES at the time of the status transition. Used for schedule adherence and lead time SLA tracking.']
  scrap_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of production output declared as scrap at the time of the status transition. Used for scrap rate calculation and quality performance reporting.']
  sla_target_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The contracted or internally defined SLA target duration in hours for the production order to complete from release to technical completion. Used to assess whether the order met its lead time commitment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating operational system that generated or recorded this status transition event (e.g., SAP_PP for ERP-driven transitions, OPCENTER_MES for shop floor-driven transitions). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system_event_code varchar [note: 'The unique event or change document identifier from the originating source system (SAP change document number or Opcenter event ID) for this status transition. Enables traceability back to the system of record.']
  status_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code explaining why the status transition occurred (e.g., MATERIAL_SHORTAGE, CAPACITY_CONSTRAINT, QM_INSPECTION_FAIL, CUSTOMER_CHANGE, PLANNED_MAINTENANCE). Supports root cause analysis and production performance reporting.']
  status_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context for the status transition reason, supplementing the structured reason code. Allows planners and supervisors to document specific circumstances.']
  to_status varchar [note: 'The production order status immediately after the transition event. Represents the new lifecycle state the order has entered (e.g., RELEASED, TECHNICALLY_COMPLETED, CLOSED).']
  transition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time (with timezone offset) at which the status transition occurred. Critical for SLA tracking, lead time calculation, and audit trail compliance.']
  trigger_event_type varchar [note: 'The business event or system action that caused the status transition (e.g., MRP run, manual release by planner, system confirmation from MES, QM hold from quality inspection). Enables root cause analysis of order lifecycle events.']
  triggered_by_user varchar [note: 'The SAP or Opcenter user ID of the person or system account that initiated the status transition. Used for accountability, audit trail, and change management compliance.']

  Note: 'Lifecycle status history of production orders, capturing every status transition event from creation through closure (SAP PP Order Status / Opcenter Work Order Status History). Stores production order reference, from-status, to-status, transition timestamp, triggered-by user, triggering event (MRP run, manual release, system confirmation, QM hold), and status reason code. Enables full audit trail of order lifecycle, supports SLA tracking for order lead times, and feeds production performance reporting.'
}

Table production.capacity_requirement {
  capacity_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each capacity requirement record generated by MRP II capacity planning in SAP PP (CM01). Serves as the primary key for the capacity_requirement data product in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  forecast_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.forecast. Business justification: Capacity requirements planning (CRP) derives load from sales forecasts. Planners trace capacity needs back to the originating forecast to validate whether the plant can meet projected sales demand.']
  fulfillment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.fulfillment_plan. Business justification: Capacity requirements are calculated from demand represented in fulfillment plans. Capacity planners trace capacity loads back to fulfillment plans to identify bottlenecks caused by specific customer ']
  maintenance_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.maintenance_plan. Business justification: Capacity planners must account for planned maintenance windows when calculating available machine capacity. Linking capacity requirements to maintenance plans ensures production scheduling respects pr']
  mrp_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.mrp_run. Business justification: Capacity requirements are calculated as a direct output of an MRP run. Production planners must trace which MRP run generated each capacity requirement to validate planning results and resolve overloa']
  production_order_cost_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.production_order_cost. Business justification: Capacity requirements carry planned activity costs (machine hours × cost rate) that feed into the production order cost object. Cost planners use this link to build the planned cost structure for manu']
  routing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.routing. Business justification: capacity_requirement is generated by MRP II for specific routings. The FK routing_id normalizes the routing_number string reference and enables joins to routing master data for capacity planning analy']
  routing_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.routing_operation. Business justification: capacity_requirement is computed per routing_operation for detailed capacity planning. The FK routing_operation_id normalizes the routing_operation_number string reference and enables joins to routing']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.shift. Business justification: capacity_requirement is planned per shift for detailed capacity analysis. The FK shift_id normalizes the shift_code reference and enables joins to shift master data for shift-level capacity load analy']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to production.work_center']
  available_capacity_hours decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total capacity (in hours) available at the work center during the planning period, derived from the work center\'s capacity definition and shift model in SAP PP. Used to compute load percentage and identify overloads.']
  capacity_category_code varchar [note: 'SAP capacity category code distinguishing the type of capacity being planned (e.g., machine capacity, labor capacity, tool capacity). Corresponds to SAP PP capacity category (e.g., 001 = Machine, 002 = Labor).']
  capacity_category_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the capacity category (Machine, Labor, Tool, Other). Used for filtering and grouping in capacity leveling and bottleneck analysis.']
  capacity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure in which capacity requirements and availability are expressed (e.g., H = Hours, MIN = Minutes). Ensures consistent interpretation of capacity figures across work centers.']
  capacity_version varchar [note: 'SAP planning version identifier (e.g., 000 for active version, simulation versions) under which this capacity requirement was calculated. Enables what-if scenario management and simulation-based capacity planning.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP Controlling (CO) cost center code associated with the work center. Enables financial allocation of capacity costs and integration with OPEX reporting.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant where the work center is located. Supports multi-country capacity reporting and compliance with regional labor regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the capacity requirement record was first created in the source system (SAP PP) or ingested into the Silver layer. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and incremental load processing.']
  efficiency_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Efficiency factor (as a percentage) applied to the work center when calculating available capacity, reflecting the expected productive utilization rate (e.g., 85% efficiency means 85% of nominal capacity is available). Sourced from SAP PP work center definition.']
  is_overloaded boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the work center is overloaded during the planning period (required capacity exceeds available capacity). Triggers capacity leveling actions and what-if scheduling scenarios.']
  last_changed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the capacity requirement record in the source system or Silver layer. Used for change detection, incremental ETL, and audit trail compliance.']
  leveling_action varchar [note: 'Action taken during capacity leveling to resolve an overload condition at the work center (e.g., Rescheduled, Split, Outsourced, Overtime, Deferred). Supports audit trail of capacity leveling decisions.']
  load_percentage decimal(8,4) [note: 'Ratio of required capacity to available capacity expressed as a percentage (required_capacity_hours / available_capacity_hours × 100). Key indicator for bottleneck identification and capacity leveling decisions.']
  mrp_run_date date [note: 'Date on which the MRP II planning run was executed that generated this capacity requirement record. Enables time-series comparison of capacity plans across successive MRP runs.']
  mrp_run_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the MRP II planning run that generated this capacity requirement. Supports audit traceability and comparison between planning runs for what-if scenario analysis.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for planner annotations, leveling justifications, or exception comments related to the capacity requirement record. Supports operational communication and audit documentation.']
  number_of_resources varchar [note: 'Number of individual machines, operators, or tools at the work center contributing to the available capacity for this planning period. Used to calculate per-resource load and support headcount/machine planning.']
  order_count varchar [note: 'Number of production orders and planned orders contributing to the capacity requirement for this work center and planning period. Indicates the complexity of the load and supports scheduling prioritization.']
  overload_hours decimal(18,4) [note: 'Number of hours by which required capacity exceeds available capacity during the planning period. Quantifies the severity of the overload for prioritization and leveling decisions.']
  planned_order_hours decimal(18,4) [note: 'Capacity hours contributed by MRP-generated planned orders (not yet converted to production orders) within the planning period. Distinguishes firm demand from planned demand for capacity analysis.']
  planning_horizon_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the planning time bucket used for capacity aggregation (Daily, Weekly, Monthly). Determines the resolution of capacity load visibility for scheduling and leveling.']
  planning_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the planning period for which the capacity requirement is calculated. Together with planning_period_start_date, defines the time bucket for capacity load aggregation.']
  planning_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the planning period (day, week, or shift bucket) for which the capacity requirement is calculated. Defines the time horizon of the capacity load record.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility for which the capacity requirement is calculated. Enables multi-plant capacity visibility across the enterprise.']
  processing_hours decimal(18,4) [note: 'Portion of the required capacity hours attributable to actual production/processing activities (machine run time or labor operation time) at the work center during the planning period.']
  production_order_hours decimal(18,4) [note: 'Capacity hours contributed by firm production orders (released or confirmed) within the planning period. Represents committed capacity load as opposed to planned/tentative demand.']
  remaining_capacity_hours decimal(18,4) [note: 'Residual available capacity hours after subtracting required capacity from available capacity (available_capacity_hours − required_capacity_hours). Negative values indicate overload conditions.']
  required_capacity_hours decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total capacity (in hours) required at the work center during the planning period, as calculated by MRP II from open production orders and planned orders. Core metric for capacity load analysis.']
  requirement_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the capacity requirement record as assigned by SAP PP capacity planning (CM01). Used for cross-system traceability and operational reference.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production routing (Bill of Process) from which the work center operation and capacity requirement was derived. Links capacity requirements to the engineering routing definition.']
  routing_operation_number varchar [note: 'Specific operation number within the routing that generates the capacity requirement at this work center. Enables operation-level capacity traceability within a multi-step routing.']
  setup_hours decimal(18,4) [note: 'Portion of the required capacity hours attributable to setup/changeover activities at the work center during the planning period. Supports SMED analysis and setup time reduction initiatives.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production shift (e.g., Day, Afternoon, Night) within the planning period. Enables shift-level capacity load analysis and scheduling.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current status of the capacity requirement record indicating its planning state: Active (current plan), Leveled (capacity leveling applied), Overloaded (exceeds available capacity), Cancelled (order cancelled), or Simulated (what-if scenario).']
  teardown_hours decimal(18,4) [note: 'Portion of the required capacity hours attributable to teardown/cleanup activities at the work center during the planning period. Included in total capacity requirement calculations.']
  utilization_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Utilization factor (as a percentage) applied to the work center capacity, representing the proportion of available time the resource is scheduled for productive work. Sourced from SAP PP work center definition.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center (machine group, production cell, or labor pool) for which the capacity requirement is calculated. Corresponds to the SAP PP work center object (CR01/CR02).']

  Note: 'Capacity requirement records generated by MRP II capacity planning for each work center and time period (SAP PP Capacity Requirements / CM01). Stores work center, planning period (date/shift), required capacity (hours), available capacity (hours), load percentage, order references contributing to the load, capacity category (machine, labor), and overload indicator. Enables capacity leveling, bottleneck identification, and what-if scheduling scenarios. SSOT for work center capacity load visibility.'
}

Table production.version {
  version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the production version record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Serves as the primary key for this entity.']
  bop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.bop. Business justification: Production versions link to Bill of Process defining manufacturing operations sequence. Production engineering uses this to establish work center routings and cycle times.']
  ecn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: A production version (BOM/routing revision) is created or superseded by an Engineering Change Notice. Production control teams use this link to know which ECN triggered a version change and when to cu']
  engineering_bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.bom. Business justification: Production versions define valid BOM alternatives for manufacturing. Production planning uses this to select cost-optimal or capacity-optimal manufacturing configurations.']
  routing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.routing. Business justification: A production version (SAP PP Production Version) defines the valid combination of a BOM and a routing for manufacturing a material. version currently references routing via denormalized string attribu']
  apqp_status varchar [note: 'The APQP approval status for this production version, indicating whether the manufacturing process has been formally validated through the Advanced Product Quality Planning process. Required for automotive and regulated manufacturing.']
  bom_alternative varchar [note: 'The BOM alternative number (STLAL) assigned to this production version. Multiple BOM alternatives can exist for the same material, each representing a different component configuration or manufacturing method.']
  bom_explosion_type varchar [note: 'Defines how the BOM is exploded during MRP II planning and production order creation for this version: single-level (one level only), multi-level (full BOM tree), or phantom (virtual assembly, components passed through).']
  bom_usage varchar [note: 'SAP BOM usage key (STLAN) indicating the application area for which the BOM is defined (e.g., 1=Production, 5=Sales, 6=Costing). Determines which BOM is selected during production order creation.']
  change_number varchar [note: 'The Engineering Change Number (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) reference that authorized the creation or last modification of this production version. Provides traceability to the change management process in Siemens Teamcenter PLM.']
  code varchar [note: 'The alphanumeric production version identifier (VERID) as defined in SAP PP. Uniquely identifies a specific BOM/routing combination for a material-plant combination. Enables multiple manufacturing methods for the same product.']
  costing_lot_size decimal(18,3) [note: 'The reference lot size used for product cost calculation (standard cost estimate) for this production version. Determines the per-unit cost allocation of fixed setup and overhead costs.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant where this production version is applicable. Supports multi-country regulatory compliance, reporting, and regional manufacturing strategy analysis.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The SAP user ID of the person who created this production version record. Supports accountability, audit trail, and change management traceability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this production version record was first created in the source SAP system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and change history analysis.']
  default_version boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the default (preferred) production version for the material-plant combination. When multiple versions are valid, MRP II and production planners use the default version unless overridden.']
  deletion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production version has been flagged for deletion in the source SAP system (MKAL.LOEKZ). A flagged version is excluded from MRP II planning and production order creation but retained for historical traceability.']
  description varchar [note: 'Short descriptive text explaining the purpose or distinguishing characteristics of this production version (e.g., \'Standard Line A Process\', \'High-Volume Variant\', \'Pilot Run Method\').']
  in_house_production_time_days decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total in-house production lead time in working days for this production version. Used by MRP II to calculate planned order dates and available-to-promise (ATP) commitments.']
  issue_storage_location varchar [note: 'The SAP storage location (LGORT) from which components are issued to the production order for this version. Drives goods movement and WIP inventory tracking on the shop floor.']
  last_changed_by_user varchar [note: 'The SAP user ID of the person who last modified this production version record. Supports accountability, audit trail, and change management traceability.']
  last_changed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this production version record was last modified in the source SAP system. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data loading in the lakehouse.']
  lot_size_max decimal(18,3) [note: 'The maximum production lot size (MABST) for which this production version is valid. MRP II will not propose this version for planned orders exceeding this quantity. Enables capacity-constrained version selection.']
  lot_size_min decimal(18,3) [note: 'The minimum production lot size (MTVFP/MABST) for which this production version is valid. MRP II will not propose this version for planned orders below this quantity. Supports lot-size-dependent manufacturing method selection.']
  lot_size_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure (MEINS) applicable to the minimum and maximum lot size values for this production version (e.g., EA, KG, L, M). Aligns with the base unit of measure of the material.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Short description of the material (MAKTX) for which this production version is defined. Provides human-readable context for reporting and analytics without requiring a join to the material master.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'The SAP material number (MATNR) identifying the finished or semi-finished product for which this production version is defined. Aligns with the material master in SAP MM.']
  mrp_relevance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production version is considered by MRP II during planning runs. When false, the version is excluded from automatic planned order generation even if it is within its validity period.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code (WERKS) identifying the manufacturing facility where this production version is valid. A production version is always plant-specific.']
  ppap_level varchar [note: 'The PPAP submission level required or achieved for this production version. Defines the extent of documentation and sample submission required to validate the manufacturing process for customer approval.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking used to determine the preferred production version when multiple versions are valid for the same lot size range and validity period. Lower number indicates higher priority.']
  production_line varchar [note: 'The specific production line or work center group (ARBPL) designated for this production version. Used in repetitive manufacturing and rate-based scheduling to assign the version to a physical line.']
  production_strategy varchar [note: 'The manufacturing strategy applicable to this production version: Make-to-Order (MTO), Make-to-Stock (MTS), Engineer-to-Order (ETO), or Assemble-to-Order (ATO). Drives MRP II planning logic and order type selection.']
  receiving_storage_location varchar [note: 'The SAP storage location (LGORT) to which the finished goods produced under this version are received upon goods receipt. Supports inventory management and warehouse operations.']
  repetitive_manufacturing_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production version is enabled for repetitive manufacturing (SAP MKAL.SERKZ). When true, rate-based planning and backflushing are applicable. Relevant for high-volume, continuous production scenarios.']
  routing_type varchar [note: 'The type of task list (PLNTY) associated with this production version. \'routing\' is standard discrete manufacturing; \'rate_routing\' is used for repetitive manufacturing; \'master_recipe\' is used for process manufacturing.']
  scheduling_type varchar [note: 'The scheduling direction used when calculating production dates for orders using this version: \'forward\' (from start date), \'backward\' (from required finish date), or \'current_date\'. Drives lead time calculation in MRP II.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the production version. \'active\' means it is available for MRP II planning and shop floor execution. \'inactive\' means it is defined but not currently used. \'locked\' means it is frozen for change control. \'obsolete\' means it has been superseded.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this production version is valid and can be used for MRP II planning and production order creation (SAP MKAL.ADATU). Defines the start of the validity period.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'The date until which this production version is valid (SAP MKAL.BDATU). After this date, the version is no longer proposed by MRP II for new production orders. Supports time-phased manufacturing method transitions.']

  Note: 'Production version master data defining the valid combination of BOM and routing for manufacturing a specific material at a specific plant (SAP PP Production Version). Stores material number, plant, production version code, description, valid-from/valid-to dates, BOM alternative, routing group/counter, lot size range (minimum/maximum), production line, and status (active, inactive). Enables multiple manufacturing methods for the same product and drives MRP II planning with the correct BOM/routing combination.'
}

Table production.batch {
  batch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the batch master record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for all batch-level traceability and genealogy operations.']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: In process manufacturing, production batches of raw materials are created at goods receipt. Traceability regulations require linking the production batch directly to the goods receipt for full upstrea']
  lifecycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_lifecycle. Business justification: Batches track which product lifecycle phase was active during manufacturing. This supports obsolescence management, warranty tracking, and determining which production standards/specifications were ap']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: A production batch directly corresponds to an inventory lot/batch record created upon goods receipt or production output. Pharmaceutical and discrete manufacturing require this link for end-to-end bat']
  material_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.material_specification. Business justification: Each production batch must be traceable to the engineering material specification it was produced against. Used in batch records, certificates of conformance, and regulatory traceability audits in ind']
  order_confirmation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_confirmation. Business justification: Production batches are created and traceable to specific confirmed customer orders, especially in make-to-order manufacturing. Quality and traceability teams link batches to order confirmations for en']
  certificate_of_analysis_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Certificate of Analysis (CoA) document associated with this batch. The CoA documents the actual test results and quality characteristics confirming the batch meets specification. Linked to SAP DMS or external document management.']
  changed_by_user varchar [note: 'SAP user ID (AENAM) of the person who last modified the batch master record. Supports change management audit trails and accountability in quality-regulated environments.']
  classification_class_number varchar [note: 'SAP classification class number (KLASSE) assigned to this batch for batch classification. Defines the set of characteristics (e.g., purity, viscosity, tensile strength) used to describe and search for batches.']
  classification_values varchar [note: 'Serialized JSON string containing all batch classification characteristic values from SAP Classification System (AUSP) that do not have dedicated columns (e.g., color, hardness, moisture content, particle size). Enables flexible storage of material-specific quality parameters without schema changes.']
  country_of_origin_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the batch material was manufactured or substantially transformed. Required for customs declarations, trade compliance, and preferential origin determination.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'SAP user ID (ERNAM) of the person who created the batch master record. Required for audit trail and accountability in regulated manufacturing environments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch master record was first created in the source system (SAP MCHA-ERSDA + MCHA-CRTM). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and batch lifecycle tracking.']
  goods_receipt_date date [note: 'Date on which the batch was received into inventory via goods receipt posting (SAP MSEG-BUDAT). For externally procured batches, this is the inbound logistics date used for aging, FIFO, and supplier lead time analysis.']
  goods_receipt_number varchar [note: 'SAP material document number (MBLNR) for the goods receipt posting that created or received this batch. Provides the inbound logistics and inventory posting reference for traceability.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, storage, labeling, and transport documentation (e.g., SDS, UN number). Drives HSE compliance workflows and warehouse segregation rules.']
  inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'SAP QM inspection lot number (PRUEFLOS) linked to this batch for quality inspection. Connects the batch to the inspection results, usage decision, and defect records in the Quality Management module.']
  is_restricted_substance_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this batch has been verified as compliant with restricted substance regulations including EU RoHS (Restriction of Hazardous Substances) and REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals). Required for CE marking and export compliance.']
  last_changed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent change to the batch master record in the source system (SAP MCHA-LAEDA + MCHA-LAEZT). Supports change detection, delta loading, and audit compliance.']
  manufacturing_date date [note: 'The date on which the batch was manufactured or produced (SAP MCHA-HSDAT). Used for shelf life calculation, FIFO/FEFO inventory management, and regulatory compliance documentation.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Short text description of the material associated with this batch, sourced from the SAP material master (MAKT-MAKTX). Supports human-readable identification in reports and quality documents.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'SAP material number (MATNR) of the material to which this batch belongs. Identifies the specific product or raw material managed in lot/batch control.']
  ncr_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the Non-Conformance Report (NCR) raised against this batch if quality defects or deviations were identified. Links the batch to the CAPA process and corrective action tracking in the QMS.']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic quality inspection of this batch. Supports recurring inspection planning for long-shelf-life or regulated materials and triggers QM inspection lot creation.']
  origin_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the batch was created through internal manufacturing (production order), external procurement (purchase order/GRN), subcontracting, or customer returns. Drives traceability and genealogy logic.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code (WERKS) where the batch was produced or received. Determines the organizational unit responsible for batch management, quality inspection, and inventory valuation.']
  production_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP production order number (AUFNR) that created this batch. Links the batch to the manufacturing execution context including routing, work center, BOM, and planned/actual quantities. Applicable for internally produced batches.']
  purity_percent decimal(7,4) [note: 'Purity level of the batch expressed as a percentage, stored as a batch classification characteristic value. Critical quality parameter for chemical, pharmaceutical, and specialty material batches used in acceptance/rejection decisions.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of material in this batch at the time of creation or last stock posting (SAP MCHB-CLABS + CEINM + CSPEM + CEINM). Represents the lot size for production planning, quality sampling, and inventory management.']
  reach_svhc_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this batch contains any Substance of Very High Concern (SVHC) as defined under EU REACH regulation. Triggers mandatory disclosure obligations to customers and downstream users when SVHC concentration exceeds 0.1% w/w.']
  remaining_shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Number of days remaining before the batch expires, calculated as the difference between expiry date and the current date. Used for FEFO prioritization, near-expiry alerts, and inventory disposition decisions.']
  retest_date date [note: 'Date by which the batch must be retested to confirm it still meets quality specifications (SAP MCHA-RRDAT). Used for periodic quality re-evaluation of long shelf-life materials and regulatory compliance.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Total shelf life of the batch expressed in days, as defined in the material master shelf life data (SAP MRP4 view - MHDRZ). Used to calculate expiry date and manage FEFO-based inventory consumption.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current stock/quality status of the batch as managed in SAP MM/QM (MCHB-EINME / Batch Classification). Determines whether the batch is available for use: unrestricted (available), quality_inspection (under QM hold), blocked (not available), restricted (conditionally available), or obsolete (expired/decommissioned).']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'SAP storage location code (LGORT) where the batch is physically stored within the plant. Used for inventory management, warehouse operations, and stock transfer processes.']
  supplier_batch_number varchar [note: 'The batch or lot number assigned by the external supplier for procured materials (SAP LICHA). Required for supplier traceability, incoming quality inspection, and regulatory compliance (e.g., REACH, RoHS).']
  supplier_number varchar [note: 'SAP vendor/supplier number (LIFNR) for externally procured batches. Enables supplier quality tracking, incoming inspection correlation, and supplier performance analytics.']
  tensile_strength_mpa decimal(10,4) [note: 'Tensile strength of the batch material in megapascals (MPa), stored as a batch classification characteristic. Critical mechanical property for metal, composite, and structural material batches used in quality release decisions.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for the batch quantity (SAP MEINH/MEINS), such as KG, L, EA, M, M2, M3. Aligns with SAP material master base UoM for consistent inventory and quality reporting.']
  usage_decision_code varchar [note: 'SAP QM usage decision code (ENTSCHEIDUNG) for the inspection lot associated with this batch. Determines the quality disposition: accept (unrestricted use), reject (block/scrap), or conditional release. Drives automatic batch status change.']
  viscosity_mpa_s decimal(12,4) [note: 'Viscosity measurement of the batch in millipascal-seconds (mPa·s), stored as a batch classification characteristic. Key quality parameter for fluid, adhesive, coating, and polymer material batches.']

  Note: 'Batch master record for lot-controlled materials produced or consumed in manufacturing (SAP MM/PP Batch Master). Stores batch number, material number, plant, storage location, batch status (unrestricted, quality inspection, blocked, restricted), manufacturing date, expiry date, shelf life, batch classification values (e.g., purity, viscosity, tensile strength), batch origin (internal production vs. external procurement), and certificate of analysis reference. SSOT for batch traceability and genealogy across the production and quality domains.'
}

Table production.batch_genealogy {
  batch_genealogy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each batch genealogy relationship record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for the batch_genealogy data product.']
  parent_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.batch. Business justification: batch_genealogy tracks the parent batch consumed during manufacturing. The FK parent_batch_id normalizes the parent_batch_number string reference and enables joins to batch master data for upstream tr']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line_item. Business justification: Batch genealogy must trace input materials back to the specific PO line item that sourced them. Regulatory compliance and recall management in industrial manufacturing require this upstream traceabili']
  routing_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.routing_operation. Business justification: batch_genealogy records the operation step at which batch transformation occurred. The FK routing_operation_id enables joins to routing_operation master data for process-level genealogy traceability a']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: batch_genealogy.work_center_code (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the work_center master. Batch genealogy records capture the work center at which the parent-child batch transformation occurred']
  child_batch_expiry_date date [note: 'Shelf life expiration date of the child (produced) batch as recorded in SAP batch master. Used for finished product shelf life management, customer delivery compliance, and regulatory traceability.']
  child_batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the child batch — the output material, assembly, or finished product batch that was produced or resulted from the manufacturing operation. In SAP PP, this corresponds to the \'To Batch\' in the batch where-used list.']
  child_batch_status varchar [note: 'Quality/inventory status of the child (produced) batch at the time of production confirmation, as recorded in SAP QM. Indicates whether the produced batch passed quality inspection or requires further disposition.']
  child_material_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the child batch material, denormalized for traceability reports, recall notifications, and regulatory submissions.']
  child_material_number varchar [note: 'SAP material number (or equivalent ERP identifier) of the child batch material. Enables cross-referencing to the material master for finished product specifications and compliance data.']
  consumed_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity of the parent batch that was consumed during the manufacturing operation. Used for material balance calculations, yield analysis, and traceability completeness verification.']
  consumed_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the consumed quantity of the parent batch (e.g., KG, L, EA). Aligned with SAP base unit of measure for the parent material.']
  country_of_manufacture_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the batch transformation occurred. Required for customs declarations, CE marking, RoHS/REACH compliance, and country-of-origin traceability in global supply chains.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the batch genealogy record was first created in the source system of record (SAP PP or Siemens Opcenter MES). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory record retention compliance.']
  genealogy_record_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the batch genealogy relationship record, used in quality investigations, recall management, and regulatory submissions. Typically sourced from SAP PP Batch Where-Used or Siemens Opcenter MES.']
  goods_movement_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP material document number (from MIGO/MB1A) recording the goods issue of the parent batch or goods receipt of the child batch. Provides the financial and inventory audit trail for the batch relationship.']
  inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'SAP QM inspection lot number associated with the child batch produced in this genealogy record. Links the batch genealogy to quality inspection results, usage decisions, and non-conformance reports (NCR).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the batch genealogy record in the source system. Supports change detection, incremental data loading in the Silver layer, and audit trail completeness.']
  merge_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number identifying the order of parent batches when multiple parent batches are merged into a single child batch (relationship_type = \'merge\'). Enables reconstruction of the full merge genealogy and proportional quantity attribution.']
  ncr_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the Non-Conformance Report (NCR) raised against the parent or child batch in this genealogy record. Enables direct linkage between batch traceability and quality defect management for CAPA investigations.']
  operation_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual end date and time of the manufacturing operation during which the batch relationship was established. Together with operation_start_timestamp, defines the production time window for traceability and cycle time analysis.']
  operation_number varchar [note: 'Routing operation number (e.g., 0010, 0020) within the production order at which the batch relationship was established — i.e., where the parent batch was consumed and the child batch was produced or transformed.']
  operation_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual start date and time of the manufacturing operation during which the batch relationship was established. Used for time-based traceability, shift analysis, and process timeline reconstruction during quality investigations.']
  parent_batch_expiry_date date [note: 'Shelf life expiration date of the parent batch as recorded in SAP batch master. Critical for FEFO (First Expired First Out) compliance, regulatory submissions, and determining if expired materials were used in production.']
  parent_batch_status varchar [note: 'Quality/inventory status of the parent batch at the time of consumption, as recorded in SAP QM batch status management. Indicates whether the consumed batch was in unrestricted use, under quality inspection, or blocked — critical for quality investigations.']
  parent_material_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the parent batch material, denormalized for reporting and traceability documents without requiring a join to the material master.']
  parent_material_number varchar [note: 'SAP material number (or equivalent ERP identifier) of the parent batch material. Enables cross-referencing to the material master for specification, classification, and compliance data.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where the batch genealogy relationship was established. Supports multi-plant traceability and regional regulatory compliance reporting.']
  produced_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity of the child batch that was produced or resulted from the manufacturing operation. Used for yield calculations, material balance, and production confirmation reporting.']
  produced_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the produced quantity of the child batch (e.g., KG, EA, L). Aligned with SAP base unit of measure for the child material.']
  production_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP PP production order or MES work order under which the parent-child batch relationship was created. Links genealogy records to the manufacturing execution context.']
  recall_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this batch genealogy record has been flagged as part of an active product recall or field action. When true, the record is included in recall scope reports and regulatory notifications. Critical for recall management and regulatory compliance.']
  recall_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the product recall or field action campaign to which this batch genealogy record belongs. Populated when recall_flag is true. Enables aggregation of all affected batches under a single recall event.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the batch genealogy record. \'active\' indicates a valid, confirmed relationship; \'reversed\' indicates the goods movement was reversed in SAP; \'cancelled\' indicates the production order was cancelled; \'superseded\' indicates the record was replaced by a corrected entry.']
  regulatory_compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory or quality standard under which this batch genealogy record must be maintained and reported. Drives retention period, traceability depth, and reporting format requirements. Multiple standards may apply; the most stringent is recorded.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the parent-child batch relationship. \'component_to_assembly\' is the standard BOM consumption; \'split\' occurs when one batch is divided into multiple child batches; \'merge\' when multiple parent batches are combined; \'rework\' when a non-conforming batch is reprocessed; \'co_product\' and \'by_product\' for simultaneous production outputs; \'reprocess\' for quality-driven reprocessing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this batch genealogy record was sourced (e.g., SAP PP Batch Where-Used, Siemens Opcenter MES). Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer reconciliation.']
  split_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number identifying the order of child batches when a parent batch is split into multiple child batches (relationship_type = \'split\'). Enables reconstruction of the full split genealogy tree and quantity reconciliation.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'SAP storage location from which the parent batch was issued or to which the child batch was received. Supports inventory traceability and warehouse management integration.']
  traceability_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates the direction of traceability this record supports. \'forward\' traces from raw material/component to finished product (where-used); \'backward\' traces from finished product to raw material/component (where-from); \'bidirectional\' supports both directions. Critical for product recall scope determination.']
  traceability_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which the batch genealogy relationship was recorded in the system of record (SAP PP or Siemens Opcenter MES). This is the authoritative timestamp for regulatory traceability and recall scope determination.']
  yield_rate_percent decimal(7,4) [note: 'Ratio of produced quantity to consumed quantity expressed as a percentage, representing the material conversion efficiency for this specific batch relationship. Supports yield analysis and process capability monitoring (SPC).']

  Note: 'Tracks parent-child relationships between batches consumed and produced during manufacturing operations, enabling full forward and backward batch traceability (SAP PP Batch Where-Used / Batch Genealogy). Stores parent batch reference, child batch reference, production order reference, operation reference, consumed quantity, produced quantity, relationship type (component-to-assembly, rework, split, merge), and traceability timestamp. Critical for product recall management, quality investigations, and regulatory compliance (ISO 9001, FDA, automotive IATF 16949).'
}

Table production.downtime_event {
  downtime_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying each downtime event record in the silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and OEE/TPM calculations.']
  asset_notification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_notification. Business justification: When a machine goes down on the shop floor, an asset notification is raised immediately. Production supervisors link downtime events to notifications to trigger maintenance response and track resoluti']
  cost_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_allocation. Business justification: Unplanned downtime incurs idle capacity costs that must be allocated to cost centers or orders. Finance uses this link to distribute machine downtime costs across cost objects during period-end cost a']
  failure_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.failure_record. Business justification: Production downtime caused by equipment failures must reference the formal failure record for root cause analysis, warranty claims, and reliability engineering. Links operational impact to technical f']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.location. Business justification: Downtime events are tied to a physical location on the shop floor or facility. Maintenance and operations managers use this to correlate downtime with logistics disruptions (e.g., blocked aisles, dela']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Unplanned downtime events often require emergency spare part procurement. Maintenance and production teams link the downtime event to the resulting purchase order to track response time, cost of downt']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.shift. Business justification: downtime_event occurs during a specific shift. The FK shift_id normalizes the shift_code reference and enables joins to shift master data for shift-level downtime analysis and MTTR/MTBF calculations.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: downtime_event occurs at a specific work_center. The FK work_center_id enables joins to work_center master data for downtime analysis, MTTR/MTBF calculations, and OEE availability loss attribution per']
  capa_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the formal CAPA record raised as a result of this downtime event. Links to the QMS CAPA module for systemic root cause elimination and recurrence prevention tracking.']
  category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the downtime event type. Drives OEE loss categorization (availability loss vs. planned stops), TPM pillar analysis, and MTBF/MTTR segmentation. Aligns with Opcenter MES downtime category taxonomy.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Description of the immediate corrective action taken to restore production (e.g., replaced bearing, reset PLC, cleared material jam). Distinct from CAPA — this is the immediate fix, not the systemic preventive action.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant or facility where the downtime event occurred. Supports multi-country OEE benchmarking, regulatory reporting, and regional maintenance performance analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the downtime event record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail, data freshness monitoring, and SLA compliance on downtime reporting timeliness.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the downtime event was detected and initiated. Distinguishes between human-reported events and automated detection via SCADA, PLC fault codes, or MindSphere predictive alerts. Supports IIoT maturity assessment.']
  duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total elapsed duration of the downtime event in minutes, calculated from start and end timestamps. Stored explicitly to support direct aggregation in OEE availability calculations and TPM dashboards without requiring timestamp arithmetic at query time.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the downtime event was resolved and production resumed. Used with start_timestamp to compute duration and MTTR. Null if the event is still open.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the downtime event, typically generated by Siemens Opcenter MES or SAP PM. Used for cross-system referencing, shop floor communication, and audit trails.']
  failure_mode varchar [note: 'Specific failure mode description identifying how the equipment or process failed, aligned with FMEA/PFMEA failure mode taxonomy. Supports root cause analysis and reliability engineering programs.']
  fault_code varchar [note: 'Fault or alarm code generated by the PLC, SCADA, or DCS system at the time of the downtime event. Provides precise machine-level diagnostic information for maintenance engineers and reliability analysis.']
  is_planned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the downtime event was planned (e.g., scheduled maintenance, changeover, trial run) or unplanned (e.g., breakdown, quality hold). Critical for correctly computing OEE availability — only unplanned downtime reduces OEE availability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the downtime event record. Supports incremental data loading in the Databricks lakehouse pipeline and change data capture (CDC) processing.']
  lost_production_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of production units lost or not produced as a direct result of the downtime event. Feeds OEE performance and availability loss calculations, and supports financial impact assessment of downtime.']
  lost_production_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the lost production quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, M for meters). Ensures dimensional consistency when aggregating lost production across different product types.']
  machine_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the specific machine or equipment asset within the work center that experienced the downtime. Corresponds to the equipment number in SAP PM and Maximo EAM.']
  machine_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the machine or equipment asset that experienced the downtime. Supports readability in OEE dashboards and TPM reports.']
  maintenance_work_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP PM or Maximo EAM maintenance work order created to address the downtime event. Links downtime records to maintenance execution, spare parts consumption, and labor cost tracking.']
  mes_event_code varchar [note: 'Native event identifier from Siemens Opcenter MES downtime module. Enables traceability back to the source system record for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  mindsphere_asset_code varchar [note: 'Asset identifier from Siemens MindSphere IIoT platform used to correlate the downtime event with real-time telemetry, predictive alerts, and condition monitoring data from the affected machine or work center.']
  oee_impact_category varchar [note: 'Classification of how this downtime event impacts the OEE calculation — specifically which OEE loss category it belongs to (availability loss for unplanned stops, planned stop for scheduled downtime). Enables automated OEE waterfall chart generation.']
