  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where the downtime event occurred. Enables multi-plant OEE benchmarking and global downtime analysis across the multinational enterprise.']
  production_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP production order number that was active or impacted at the time of the downtime event. Links the downtime to the specific manufacturing order for cost impact and schedule deviation analysis.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code from the MES or ERP downtime reason catalog that identifies the specific cause of the downtime event (e.g., mechanical failure, electrical fault, tooling wear). Enables Pareto analysis and FMEA correlation.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the downtime reason code. Provides context for operators and maintenance engineers when reviewing downtime records without requiring a lookup to the reason code catalog.']
  reported_by varchar [note: 'Employee ID or username of the operator or technician who reported or initiated the downtime event record in the MES or maintenance system. Supports accountability tracking and operator performance analysis.']
  resolved_by varchar [note: 'Employee ID or username of the maintenance technician or engineer who resolved the downtime event. Used for MTTR analysis by technician, skills gap identification, and TPM workforce planning.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Department accountable for resolving or owning the downtime event (e.g., Maintenance, Production, Quality, Logistics). Used for departmental KPI reporting and escalation routing.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Identified root cause of the downtime event as determined through investigation (e.g., 5-Why analysis, fishbone diagram). Populated after CAPA investigation is completed. Supports TPM and continuous improvement programs.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Production shift identifier during which the downtime event occurred (e.g., A-shift, B-shift, night shift). Enables shift-level downtime analysis and supports labor accountability in TPM programs.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which the downtime event was originated or ingested (e.g., Siemens Opcenter MES, MindSphere IIoT, SAP PM, Maximo EAM). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the lakehouse.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the downtime event began, as recorded by Opcenter MES or MindSphere IIoT. Critical for MTBF calculations, OEE availability component, and shift-level downtime reporting.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the downtime event record. Drives workflow routing in MES and maintenance systems — open events trigger alerts, resolved events enable MTTR calculation, closed events are locked for reporting.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the work center or production cell where the downtime event occurred. Aligns with SAP PP/PM work center master data and Opcenter MES resource definitions.']

  Note: 'Detailed record of unplanned and planned downtime events on the shop floor (Opcenter MES Downtime / MindSphere Asset Downtime). Stores work center/machine, downtime start/end timestamp, total duration (minutes), downtime category (breakdown, planned maintenance, changeover, material shortage, quality hold, operator absence), downtime reason code, responsible department, production order impacted, lost production quantity, and corrective action taken. Feeds TPM programs, MTBF/MTTR calculations, and OEE availability component. Distinct from OEE events in that it captures the full downtime record with root cause.'
}

Table production.shift {
  shift_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each production shift definition record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and OEE calculations.']
  break_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total scheduled break time in minutes for the shift (sum of all meal breaks, rest breaks, and paid/unpaid breaks). Subtracted from gross duration to derive net available production time.']
  break_schedule_details varchar [note: 'Structured text description of the break schedule within the shift, specifying break start times and durations (e.g., \'10:00-10:15 rest; 12:00-12:30 meal; 15:00-15:15 rest\'). Stored as a formatted string for display in MES and scheduling tools.']
  code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric business code uniquely identifying the shift definition within a plant (e.g., SH-DAY, SH-NIGHT, SH-SWING). Used as the natural key in MES and ERP integrations.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant location where this shift is defined (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN). Enables country-specific labor law compliance, regulatory reporting, and multi-regional OEE benchmarking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shift definition record was first created in the source system or Silver Layer, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  crosses_midnight boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shift spans across midnight (i.e., end_time is earlier than start_time on the clock). Critical for correct date assignment of production orders and OEE period calculations for night shifts.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to financial attributes of this shift record (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Supports multi-currency reporting in multinational manufacturing operations.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the shift, including its purpose, applicable production lines, or special operational notes (e.g., reduced-capacity weekend shift, holiday skeleton crew).']
  duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total gross duration of the shift in decimal hours (e.g., 8.00, 10.00, 12.00). Represents the full clock time from start to end before deducting breaks. Used as the denominator in OEE availability calculations.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The calendar date from which this shift definition becomes valid and applicable for production scheduling. Supports time-variant shift configurations for seasonal or contractual changes.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this shift definition expires and is no longer valid for production scheduling. A null value indicates the shift is open-ended with no planned expiry.']
  end_time varchar [note: 'Scheduled end time of the shift in HH:mm:ss 24-hour format (e.g., 14:00:00 for Day Shift). Combined with start_time to determine the gross shift window for capacity and OEE calculations.']
  friday_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shift definition is scheduled to operate on Fridays. Used in weekly production scheduling and MRP II capacity planning.']
  labor_agreement_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the collective bargaining agreement or labor contract governing this shift\'s working conditions, break entitlements, and overtime rules. Ensures compliance with labor regulations and union agreements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this shift definition record in the source system or Silver Layer. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance.']
  monday_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shift definition is scheduled to operate on Mondays. Used in weekly production scheduling and MRP II capacity planning.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the production shift (e.g., Day Shift, Night Shift, Swing Shift, Morning Shift, Afternoon Shift). Used in scheduling boards, OEE dashboards, and production reports.']
  net_available_time_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned available production time in decimal hours after deducting all scheduled breaks and planned downtime from the gross shift duration. This is the planned operating time used as the basis for OEE availability rate calculations per ISO 22400-2.']
  number_of_breaks varchar [note: 'Count of distinct scheduled break periods within the shift (e.g., 1 meal break + 2 rest breaks = 3). Used in MES break scheduling and shop floor Andon system configuration.']
  oee_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) percentage for this shift, representing the planned benchmark for availability, performance, and quality combined. Used to evaluate actual shift OEE against the planned target in production analytics.']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether production hours worked in this shift beyond the standard duration qualify for overtime compensation under the applicable labor agreement. Used in labor cost calculations and payroll integration.']
  pattern_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the shift rotation pattern or schedule group to which this shift belongs (e.g., 3-SHIFT-ROTATING, 2-SHIFT-FIXED, CONTINENTAL). Links individual shift definitions to their broader scheduling pattern for MRP II capacity planning.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility to which this shift definition belongs. Enables multi-plant OEE segmentation and shift-based capacity planning.']
  premium_pay_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage premium applied to the base labor rate for hours worked in this shift (e.g., 25% night shift premium, 50% weekend premium). Used in production cost calculations and COGS reporting.']
  production_strategy varchar [note: 'Manufacturing production strategy applicable to this shift (Make to Order, Make to Stock, Engineer to Order, Assemble to Order, or mixed). Determines how production orders are assigned and prioritized within the shift window.']
  saturday_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shift definition is scheduled to operate on Saturdays. Relevant for extended production weeks, overtime scheduling, and weekend capacity planning.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the shift within a 24-hour production day cycle (e.g., 1 = first shift, 2 = second shift, 3 = third shift). Used to order shifts in scheduling boards and rotation pattern definitions.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this shift definition was sourced (e.g., SAP_S4HANA, OPCENTER_MES). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  start_time varchar [note: 'Scheduled start time of the shift in HH:mm:ss 24-hour format (e.g., 06:00:00 for Day Shift). Used to calculate available production time and assign production orders to the correct shift window.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shift definition. Only active shifts are available for production order assignment and OEE calculations. Archived shifts are retained for historical reporting.']
  sunday_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shift definition is scheduled to operate on Sundays. Relevant for 24/7 continuous production environments and holiday/weekend capacity planning.']
  takt_time_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned takt time in seconds for this shift, calculated as net available production time divided by customer demand rate. Represents the required production rhythm to meet demand within the shift window. Used in Lean Manufacturing and shop floor pacing.']
  thursday_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shift definition is scheduled to operate on Thursdays. Used in weekly production scheduling and MRP II capacity planning.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the plant location where this shift operates (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/Berlin, Asia/Shanghai). Critical for correct timestamp interpretation in global MES integrations and cross-plant OEE reporting.']
  tuesday_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shift definition is scheduled to operate on Tuesdays. Used in weekly production scheduling and MRP II capacity planning.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shift by its operational time window or purpose (e.g., day, night, swing, weekend, holiday, overtime). Used to segment OEE metrics and apply differential labor cost rates.']
  wednesday_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shift definition is scheduled to operate on Wednesdays. Used in weekly production scheduling and MRP II capacity planning.']

  Note: 'Reference master for production shift definitions used in shop floor scheduling and OEE calculations. Stores shift code, shift name (e.g., Day Shift, Night Shift, Swing Shift), plant, shift start time, shift end time, shift duration (hours), break schedule, days of week applicability, and effective date range. Used to calculate available production time, assign production orders to shifts, and segment OEE metrics by shift. Distinct from workforce shift scheduling (owned by workforce domain) — this is the production-context shift reference.'
}

Table production.scrap_record {
  scrap_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each scrap record event in the production domain. Serves as the primary key for the scrap_record data product in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.batch. Business justification: scrap_record captures scrap generated from a specific batch. The FK batch_id normalizes the batch_number string reference and enables joins to batch master data for batch-level scrap analysis and yiel']
  defect_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.defect_code. Business justification: Production scrap must be categorized using standardized defect codes for Pareto analysis, trend reporting, and quality improvement initiatives. Used by production supervisors during scrap reporting.']
  failure_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.failure_record. Business justification: Scrap caused by equipment malfunction must be traceable to the specific failure record for warranty claims, quality investigations, and cost allocation. Quality engineers use this link daily in non-co']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Scrap records must reference the specific inventory lot/batch being scrapped for full material traceability. Regulatory compliance and quality investigations require linking scrap events to the origin']
  pfmea_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.pfmea. Business justification: Scrap events are classified against PFMEA failure modes so quality engineers can track actual vs. predicted failure rates. This closed-loop feedback between production scrap and process FMEA is standa']
  production_order_cost_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.production_order_cost. Business justification: Scrap events generate financial write-offs posted against the production order cost object. Cost accountants use this link to capture scrap variances and report waste costs against the manufacturing o']
  returns_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.returns_order. Business justification: Scrap records generated during rework of customer returns are linked to the originating returns order. Quality and production teams use this link to track disposition of returned goods and calculate r']
  routing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.routing. Business justification: scrap_record references the routing under which scrap was generated. The FK routing_id enables joins to routing master data for process-level scrap analysis and routing optimization.']
  routing_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.routing_operation. Business justification: scrap_record captures scrap generated at a specific routing_operation step. The FK routing_operation_id enables joins to routing_operation master data for operation-level scrap analysis and process im']
  scrap_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Scrapped materials move to designated scrap storage locations. Material handlers need exact locations for segregation and disposal processing.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.shift. Business justification: scrap_record captures scrap generated during a specific shift. The FK shift_id normalizes the shift_code reference and enables joins to shift master data for shift-level scrap analysis and quality tre']
  shipment_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment_item. Business justification: Scrap can originate from defective incoming shipment items. Quality and production teams trace scrap back to the specific shipment item to support supplier claims, incoming quality control, and root c']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Scrap caused by defective incoming materials is linked to the responsible supplier. Quality and procurement teams use this to raise supplier claims, update supplier performance scores, and drive corre']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: scrap_record captures scrap generated at a specific work_center. The FK work_center_id enables joins to work_center master data for scrap analysis, quality hotspot identification, and cost attribution']
  capa_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record initiated as a result of this scrap event. Enables traceability from scrap occurrence to quality improvement action.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the production confirmation document in SAP PP under which the scrap quantity was confirmed. Provides direct linkage to the goods movement and actual cost posting.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP controlling cost center to which the scrap cost is charged. Enables cost center-level scrap cost accountability and management reporting.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the scrap cost amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Supports multi-currency financial consolidation for multinational operations.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant where the scrap event occurred. Supports multi-country regulatory compliance reporting (EPA, EU environmental directives) and regional scrap benchmarking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the scrap record was created in the source system or ingested into the Silver layer. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SLA compliance for data freshness.']
  defect_code varchar [note: 'Specific defect classification code from the quality defect catalog (SAP QM or Opcenter Quality) identifying the type of defect that caused the scrap. Supports SPC analysis and defect Pareto reporting.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the scrapped material is classified as hazardous under applicable regulations (REACH, RoHS, EPA). Triggers mandatory hazardous waste disposal procedures and regulatory reporting obligations.']
  is_rework boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the scrapped item has been designated for rework rather than disposal. When true, a rework order reference should be present. Distinguishes reworkable non-conformances from permanent scrap for cost and yield analysis.']
  last_changed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the scrap record in the source system. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail maintenance.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the scrapped material as maintained in the material master. Supports reporting and communication without requiring a material master lookup.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'SAP material number of the scrapped component or finished/semi-finished product. Used for material-level scrap rate analysis, COGS variance, and MRP II feedback.']
  operation_number varchar [note: 'Routing operation number at which the scrap was identified and reported. Enables pinpointing the specific manufacturing step responsible for the scrap event.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where the scrap event occurred. Supports multi-plant scrap benchmarking and regional compliance reporting.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Accounting posting date for the scrap event in SAP, which may differ from the reporting timestamp. Determines the financial period in which the scrap cost is recognized for COGS variance and period-end closing.']
  production_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the production order under which the scrap event occurred. Links the scrap event to the originating manufacturing order for cost variance and OEE analysis.']
  production_strategy varchar [note: 'Manufacturing strategy under which the scrapped order was produced: Make to Order (MTO), Make to Stock (MTS), Engineer to Order (ETO), or Assemble to Order (ATO). Contextualizes scrap impact on customer commitments and inventory.']
  reporting_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the scrap event was reported in the MES or ERP system by the operator or automated process. Used for time-series scrap trend analysis and shift-level reporting.']
  rework_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the rework production order created to remediate the scrapped item. Populated only when is_rework is true. Enables end-to-end rework cost tracking and yield recovery analysis.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the production routing used for the order in which the scrap occurred. Enables routing-level scrap analysis to identify process steps with systemic quality issues.']
  scrap_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the scrap event origin: process scrap (inherent process loss), machine scrap (equipment malfunction), material defect (incoming material issue), operator error, design defect, tooling defect, or incoming material. Drives root cause categorization for Six Sigma and CAPA programs.']
  scrap_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the scrapped material calculated using the standard or moving average cost from the material master. Key input for COGS variance analysis, scrap cost reporting, and financial impact assessment of quality failures.']
  scrap_document_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing document number assigned to the scrap event by the source system (SAP PP or Opcenter MES). Used for cross-system traceability and audit purposes.']
  scrap_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of material declared as scrap in the base unit of measure. Core metric for scrap rate KPI calculation, COGS variance analysis, and Six Sigma improvement programs.']
  scrap_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code from the MES or ERP scrap reason catalog describing the specific cause of scrap (e.g., dimensional out-of-tolerance, surface defect, wrong material). Used for Pareto analysis and CAPA prioritization.']
  scrap_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context for the scrap reason beyond the standardized code. Captures operator observations and nuanced failure details for quality investigations.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Production shift identifier during which the scrap event occurred (e.g., Day, Afternoon, Night). Enables shift-level scrap rate benchmarking and operator performance analysis.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating operational system that generated the scrap record: SAP S/4HANA PP, Siemens Opcenter MES, or manual entry. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the scrap quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, M for meter). Aligns with SAP base unit of measure for the material.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the work center (machine, cell, or production line) where the scrap was generated. Used for work center-level scrap rate KPI reporting and capacity impact analysis.']

  Note: 'Records of scrap and rework generated during production operations (SAP PP Scrap / Opcenter MES Scrap Reporting). Stores production order reference, operation reference, work center, scrap quantity, scrap reason code, scrap category (process scrap, machine scrap, material defect, operator error), material number, scrap cost, rework flag, rework order reference, and reporting timestamp. Feeds scrap rate KPIs, COGS variance analysis, and quality improvement programs (Six Sigma, CAPA). Distinct from quality NCR records — this captures the production-side scrap event.'
}

Table production.order_type {
  order_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each production order type record in the lakehouse silver layer. Serves as the primary key for this reference data entity.']
  availability_check_rule varchar [note: 'SAP checking rule code that governs how component and capacity availability is verified when creating or releasing production orders of this type. Determines which stock categories and supply elements are considered in the ATP (Available to Promise) check.']
  batch_classification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether batch classification (assignment of batch characteristics and values) is mandatory for production orders of this type. Relevant for regulated industries and traceability requirements.']
  bom_usage varchar [note: 'SAP BOM usage key (e.g., 1 = Production, 5 = Sales, 6 = Costing) that determines which BOM alternative is selected during production order creation for this order type. Ensures the correct BOM is used for component determination.']
  capacity_relevance_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether production orders of this type generate capacity requirements on work centers. When true, orders of this type are included in capacity planning and leveling processes.']
  code varchar [note: 'SAP PP order type key (e.g., PP01, PP02, PI01, RW01, PR01) that uniquely identifies the order type in the source ERP system. Used during production order creation to default processing parameters.']
  confirmation_control_key varchar [note: 'SAP confirmation control key that defines the confirmation behavior for operations in production orders of this type, including whether milestone confirmations, partial confirmations, or automatic final confirmations are permitted.']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'SAP controlling area code associated with this order type, defining the organizational unit within which cost accounting and internal reporting are performed for production orders of this type.']
  costing_sheet varchar [note: 'SAP overhead costing sheet assigned to this order type, defining the overhead calculation rules (overhead rates, base values, credit objects) applied to production orders for overhead absorption and cost allocation.']
  costing_variant varchar [note: 'SAP costing variant code (e.g., PPP1, PPP2) assigned to this order type. Determines the valuation approach, cost component structure, and overhead calculation method used for production order cost estimation and actual cost collection.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production order type record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Used for audit trail and data lineage purposes.']
  description varchar [note: 'Full business description of the production order type, explaining its intended use, applicable scenarios, and any special processing rules associated with this type.']
  goods_issue_indicator varchar [note: 'Defines the component goods issue (GI) method for production orders of this type. Manual requires explicit GI posting; backflush automatically posts GI at confirmation; automatic triggers GI at order release.']
  goods_receipt_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an automatic goods receipt (GR) posting is triggered upon final confirmation of a production order of this type. When true, the finished goods inventory is updated automatically without a separate GR transaction.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'SAP QM inspection type code (e.g., 03 for goods receipt from production, 89 for in-process inspection) automatically assigned to inspection lots created for production orders of this type. Drives quality inspection planning and execution.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code for the language in which the order type name and description are maintained. Supports multilingual configuration for multinational manufacturing operations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this production order type record in the lakehouse silver layer. Supports change tracking, incremental data loads, and audit compliance.']
  mrp_relevance_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether production orders of this type are considered in Material Requirements Planning (MRP) and MRP II runs. When true, open orders of this type reduce net requirements during planning.']
  name varchar [note: 'Short descriptive name of the production order type (e.g., \'Standard Production Order\', \'Rework Order\', \'Prototype Order\', \'Co-Product Order\'). Used in UI dropdowns and reporting.']
  order_category varchar [note: 'SAP internal order category code that classifies the fundamental nature of the order type. Category 10 = PP Production Order, 20 = Process Order (PI), 30 = Inspection Order, 40 = Maintenance Order. Drives system behavior at the lowest level.']
  order_release_control varchar [note: 'Controls when a production order of this type transitions to Released status. Manual requires explicit user action; automatic_on_creation releases immediately upon order creation; automatic_on_save releases when the order is saved.']
  order_usage varchar [note: 'Business usage classification of the order type indicating the manufacturing scenario it supports: standard production, rework of non-conforming material, prototype/development builds, co-product manufacturing, disassembly, subcontracting, or repair orders.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code to which this production order type is applicable. Enables plant-specific configuration of order types, supporting multinational operations where different plants may use different order type configurations.']
  print_control varchar [note: 'SAP print control key that determines which shop floor documents (traveler, pick list, routing sheet, label) are automatically printed when a production order of this type is created or released.']
  production_strategy varchar [note: 'Manufacturing production strategy associated with this order type. MTO (Make to Order) produces against a specific sales order; MTS (Make to Stock) produces for inventory; ETO (Engineer to Order) involves custom engineering; ATO (Assemble to Order) assembles from standard components on demand.']
  reference_order_type varchar [note: 'SAP order type code used as the reference template when creating orders of this type. Enables inheritance of default parameters from a base order type, supporting standardized configuration management.']
  results_analysis_key varchar [note: 'SAP results analysis key assigned to the order type, controlling how work-in-progress (WIP) and reserves for unrealized costs are calculated during period-end closing. Relevant for ETO and long-running production orders.']
  routing_usage varchar [note: 'SAP routing usage key (e.g., 1 = Production, 3 = Universal) that determines which task list types (routings, reference operation sets) are valid for production orders of this type. Controls routing selection during order creation.']
  scheduling_type varchar [note: 'Determines the scheduling direction applied when creating production orders of this type. Forward scheduling calculates finish date from start; backward scheduling calculates start date from required finish; current_date uses today as the reference point.']
  serial_number_profile varchar [note: 'SAP serial number profile assigned to this order type, controlling whether and when serial numbers must be assigned to finished goods produced under orders of this type. Supports unit-level traceability for high-value or regulated products.']
  settlement_profile varchar [note: 'SAP settlement profile code that defines the rules for settling production order variances and costs to cost objects (e.g., material stock, cost center, profitability segment). Controls period-end cost settlement behavior.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this production order type configuration was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the production order type configuration record. Active types are available for production order creation; inactive types are temporarily disabled; obsolete types are retired and no longer used.']
  status_profile varchar [note: 'SAP user status profile assigned to this order type, defining the allowed user-defined statuses and permissible status transitions for production orders of this type (e.g., HOLD, QUAL, APPR). Enables differentiated status management beyond standard system statuses.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this production order type configuration is effective and available for use in production order creation. Supports time-dependent configuration management.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this production order type configuration remains effective. After this date, the order type is no longer available for new production order creation. Supports planned configuration transitions.']
  variance_key varchar [note: 'SAP variance key assigned to this order type, controlling which variance categories (input price, quantity, resource-usage, output price, mixed-price, remaining) are calculated during period-end variance analysis for production orders.']
  wip_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Defines the method used to calculate Work In Progress (WIP) value for production orders of this type during period-end closing. Target cost uses standard cost; actual cost uses confirmed actual costs; none excludes WIP calculation.']

  Note: 'Reference data defining the types of production orders used in manufacturing operations (SAP PP Order Type). Stores order type code, order type description, production strategy (MTO, MTS, ETO, ATO), settlement rule, costing variant, scheduling type (forward, backward, current date), availability check rule, goods receipt indicator, and applicable plant. Drives default behavior for production order creation and processing. Enables differentiated handling of standard production, rework orders, prototype orders, and co-product orders.'
}

Table customer.credit_profile {
  credit_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the customer credit profile record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for all credit risk and order-to-cash processes.']
  controlling_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.controlling_area. Business justification: Credit profiles are managed within controlling areas for credit risk monitoring and exposure analysis. Credit management teams track customer credit limits by controlling area for financial control.']
  credit_account_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the local account currency in which the credit limit and exposure are denominated for this customer (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  credit_block_status boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer account is currently subject to a credit block (True) or not (False). When True, new sales orders for this customer are blocked in SAP S/4HANA SD pending credit review and release by an authorized credit analyst.']
  credit_check_method varchar [note: 'SAP credit check method code controlling how credit checks are performed for this customer during order-to-cash processing (e.g., A=Static Credit Check, B=Dynamic Credit Check, C=Maximum Document Value, D=No Check). Determines the credit verification logic applied at sales order creation.']
  credit_control_area varchar [note: 'SAP organizational unit (credit control area code) responsible for managing and monitoring the customer\'s credit. Defines the currency, fiscal year variant, and credit management rules applicable to this customer\'s credit profile.']
  credit_exposure_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current total credit exposure for the customer in account currency, comprising open sales orders, open deliveries, open billing documents, and outstanding accounts receivable. Compared against credit limit to determine credit availability.']
  credit_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the customer account has been placed on credit hold. Used by credit analysts and order management teams to prioritize resolution actions and communicate hold rationale to sales teams.']
  credit_hold_reason_text varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing additional context or detail for the credit hold reason beyond the structured reason code. Entered by the credit analyst to document specific circumstances such as dispute details or negotiation status.']
  credit_insurance_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum insured amount covered by the trade credit insurance policy for this customer, expressed in account currency. Represents the insurer\'s approved exposure ceiling and is a key input to the internal credit limit approval process.']
  credit_insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the trade credit insurance policy covering this customer account, if applicable. Credit insurance mitigates the financial risk of customer default and may allow higher credit limits to be extended to insured customers.']
  credit_limit_account_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum credit amount extended to the customer expressed in the account\'s local currency. Defines the upper boundary of outstanding receivables permitted before credit block is triggered in SAP S/4HANA SD order processing.']
  credit_limit_approved_date date [note: 'Date on which the current credit limit was formally approved by the authorized credit analyst or credit committee. Provides an audit trail for credit limit changes and supports SOX compliance for financial controls.']
  credit_limit_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the currently approved credit limit expires and requires renewal or re-approval. After this date, the credit limit may be automatically reduced or the account placed on hold pending review.']
  credit_limit_group_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum credit amount extended to the customer expressed in the corporate group currency (e.g., USD for a US-headquartered multinational). Enables consolidated credit exposure reporting across all subsidiaries and legal entities.']
  credit_risk_class varchar [note: 'SAP credit risk class code assigned to the customer, representing the internal risk tier (e.g., 001=Very Low Risk, 002=Low Risk, 003=Medium Risk, 004=High Risk, 005=Very High Risk). Drives credit check rules and automatic credit limit proposals.']
  credit_risk_class_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the credit risk class assigned to the customer (e.g., \'Very Low Risk\', \'Medium Risk\', \'High Risk\'). Supports reporting and business user interpretation without requiring code lookups.']
  credit_segment varchar [note: 'SAP credit segment identifier grouping customers for credit management purposes, enabling separate credit limit management per business unit, sales organization, or geographic region within a single customer account.']
  credit_utilization_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the credit limit currently utilized, calculated as (credit_exposure_amount / credit_limit_account_currency) * 100. Used for credit risk monitoring dashboards and automated alert thresholds.']
  days_sales_outstanding decimal(7,2) [note: 'Average number of days the customer takes to pay invoices, calculated from invoice date to payment receipt date over a rolling period. A key KPI for accounts receivable management and credit risk assessment in the order-to-cash process.']
  dunning_bradstreet_rating varchar [note: 'External credit rating assigned by Dun & Bradstreet (D&B) for the customer organization, such as the D&B PAYDEX score or Financial Stress Score. Used as an external benchmark to validate and calibrate internal credit risk assessments.']
  dunning_bradstreet_score varchar [note: 'Numeric D&B PAYDEX score (1–100) reflecting the customer\'s payment performance history based on trade references reported to Dun & Bradstreet. Higher scores indicate better payment behavior. Used alongside internal scoring for credit limit decisions.']
  dunning_level varchar [note: 'Current dunning level (0–4) assigned to the customer in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR, reflecting the escalation stage of the collections process. Level 0 = no dunning; Level 4 = final notice / legal action. Drives automated dunning letter generation and collections escalation.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the current credit profile terms (credit limit, risk class, payment terms) became effective. Supports bi-temporal data modeling and historical credit profile analysis in the Silver Layer.']
  group_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the corporate group reporting currency used to express the group-currency credit limit and exposure (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  internal_credit_score varchar [note: 'Proprietary internal credit score assigned to the customer based on payment history, financial statements, order behavior, and risk class. Used by credit analysts to make credit limit recommendations and risk classification decisions.']
  last_dunning_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent dunning notice was issued to the customer. Used to enforce dunning interval rules and prevent premature re-dunning in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR collections management.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent formal credit review was completed for this customer. Used to track review cadence compliance and identify overdue reviews that may require updated credit limit or risk class reassessment.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal credit review of this customer account. Drives credit analyst workqueue prioritization and ensures timely reassessment of credit limits and risk classifications per internal credit policy.']
  oldest_open_item_days varchar [note: 'Number of days since the oldest unpaid open invoice item was due for this customer. Used in credit risk assessment and dunning level determination — a high value indicates chronic payment delays requiring escalation.']
  overdue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total value of outstanding invoices that have exceeded their payment due date, expressed in account currency. A primary trigger for credit block activation and dunning process initiation in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR.']
  payment_behavior_code varchar [note: 'Internal classification of the customer\'s historical payment behavior based on analysis of invoice payment patterns, average days past due, and frequency of late payments. Informs credit risk class assignment and credit limit decisions.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'SAP payment terms key (e.g., NT30, NT60, 2/10NET30) assigned to the customer, defining the agreed payment due dates, early payment discount percentages, and baseline date calculation. Directly impacts days sales outstanding (DSO) and credit exposure calculations.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credit profile record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA). Provides the creation audit trail for compliance, data lineage, and historical analysis in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the credit profile record in the source system. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the customer\'s credit profile is formally reviewed and updated. Typically determined by the customer\'s risk class — high-risk customers are reviewed more frequently than low-risk customers.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this credit profile record was sourced (e.g., SAP_S4HANA for FI/SD-originated records, SALESFORCE_CRM for CRM-originated records). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  special_credit_terms varchar [note: 'Description of any special or negotiated credit terms applicable to this customer that deviate from standard payment terms, such as extended payment windows, consignment arrangements, or letter of credit requirements. Documented for order management and finance reference.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the customer credit profile record. ACTIVE indicates a fully operational credit profile; UNDER_REVIEW indicates a formal credit review is in progress; SUSPENDED indicates temporary suspension pending resolution; CLOSED indicates the profile has been deactivated.']

  Note: 'Financial credit standing and risk assessment record for each customer account, managed in SAP S/4HANA FI/SD. Captures credit limit (in account currency and group currency), credit exposure, credit risk class, payment terms code, credit block status, credit check method, Dun & Bradstreet rating, internal credit score, last review date, next review date, credit analyst, and credit hold reason. Essential for order-to-cash risk management.'
}

Table customer.partner_function {
  partner_function_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each partner function assignment record in the silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for this entity.']
  address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: partner_function carries address_number as a STRING (SAP address number) representing the physical address associated with the partner function (e.g., ship-to address, bill-to address). The address pr']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Partner functions can be assigned to specific contact persons (e.g., a contact is designated as the \'sold-to\' or \'ship-to\' contact for a partner function). The contact_person_number STRING field is a ']
  sales_area_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.sales_area_assignment. Business justification: partner_function stores sales_organization, distribution_channel, and division as STRING attributes — these three fields together constitute a SAP sales area, which is precisely what sales_area_assign']
  assignment_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the level at which this partner function is assigned — at the customer master level (default for all transactions), sales area level, document header level, or document item level. Determines the scope of applicability.']
  authorization_group varchar [note: 'SAP authorization group code controlling which users or roles are permitted to view or modify this partner function assignment. Supports role-based access control and data security governance.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason or justification for the most recent change to this partner function assignment (e.g., \'Contract renewal\', \'Organizational restructuring\', \'Customer request\', \'Merger/acquisition\'). Supports audit trail and change management.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the partner fulfilling this function. Used for tax determination, export control compliance, and regulatory reporting.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or username of the person who created this partner function assignment in the source system. Supports audit trail and accountability tracking per quality management requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this partner function assignment record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and lifecycle tracking.']
  determination_procedure varchar [note: 'The SAP partner determination procedure code that governs how this partner function is resolved in sales documents. Defines the sequence and rules for automatic partner determination.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'The SAP sales document type (e.g., OR=Standard Order, QT=Quotation, CR=Credit Memo Request) for which this partner function assignment applies. Null indicates applicability across all document types.']
  erp_partner_number varchar [note: 'The source ERP system\'s native partner/customer number for this partner function assignment. Used for traceability back to the SAP S/4HANA source record and cross-system reconciliation.']
  function_category varchar [note: 'Standardized business category classifying the partner function into a canonical role type for cross-system analytics and reporting, independent of source system codes.']
  function_code varchar [note: 'SAP SD partner function code identifying the role of the partner in a commercial transaction (e.g., SP=Sold-To Party, SH=Ship-To Party, BP=Bill-To Party, PY=Payer, EU=End User, AG=Ordering Party). Directly maps to SAP VBPA-PARVW field.']
  function_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the partner function role (e.g., \'Sold-To Party\', \'Ship-To Party\', \'Bill-To Party\', \'Payer\', \'End User\', \'Forwarding Agent\'). Used for display in reports and order documents.']
  intercompany_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this partner function assignment represents an intercompany relationship (i.e., the partner belongs to the same corporate group). Used for intercompany billing and transfer pricing processes.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this partner function assignment is the default for the given function code within the sales area. When true, this partner is automatically proposed in new sales documents without manual selection.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this partner function is mandatory for order processing. When true, the system requires this function to be populated before a sales document can be saved or processed.']
  item_category varchar [note: 'The SAP sales document item category for which this partner function is applicable at the item level. Used when partner functions are assigned at document item granularity rather than header level.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code representing the preferred communication language for this partner function. Used to determine the language for order confirmations, invoices, and shipping documents sent to this partner.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or username of the person who last modified this partner function assignment in the source system. Supports audit trail and change accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this partner function assignment record in the source system. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments providing additional context about this partner function assignment, such as special handling instructions, contractual nuances, or operational remarks.']
  partner_name varchar [note: 'The name of the business partner (company or individual) fulfilling this partner function. Used for display on order confirmations, shipping documents, and invoices.']
  partner_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the type of business partner fulfilling this function — whether it is a customer account, an individual contact person, a vendor, an employee, or an organizational unit. Maps to SAP partner type (KU, AP, LI, PE).']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence number used when multiple partners fulfill the same function code for a given account and sales area. Determines the priority order for partner selection in document processing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this partner function assignment was sourced (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Salesforce CRM). Supports data lineage and cross-system reconciliation in the lakehouse.']
  source_system_key varchar [note: 'The natural/composite key from the source system uniquely identifying this partner function record (e.g., SAP KNVP composite key: KUNNR+VKORG+VTWEG+SPART+PARVW). Enables idempotent upserts and lineage tracking.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the partner function assignment. Drives whether the assignment is available for use in new sales documents and order routing.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'The tax jurisdiction code associated with this partner function, used for tax determination in sales transactions. Particularly relevant for Ship-To and Bill-To partner functions where tax liability is determined.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this partner function assignment becomes effective. Used to manage time-bound partner relationships and contractual validity periods.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'The date on which this partner function assignment expires. After this date, the partner function is no longer valid for new transactions. Used for contract expiry and partner relationship lifecycle management.']

  Note: 'Defines the functional roles that customer accounts or contacts play in commercial transactions, such as Sold-To Party, Ship-To Party, Bill-To Party, Payer, and End-User. Captures partner function code, partner function description, account reference, contact reference, default flag, and validity period. Directly maps to SAP S/4HANA SD partner determination schema and is critical for order routing and invoice generation.'
}

Table customer.account_classification {
  account_classification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each account classification record in the silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for this entity.']
  sales_area_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.sales_area_assignment. Business justification: account_classification stores sales_district and sales_office as STRING attributes representing the SAP sales area context for the classification. sales_area_assignment is the authoritative record for']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: account_classification contains a denormalized customer_segment STRING field that references the customer_segment reference table. Normalizing this to a FK eliminates the string duplication and ensure']
  abc_class varchar [note: 'The ABC classification assigned to the customer account based on revenue contribution and strategic importance. \'A\' represents the highest-value accounts (typically top 20% generating ~80% of revenue), \'B\' represents mid-tier accounts, and \'C\' represents lower-value accounts. Used in sales prioritization and resource allocation.']
  approved_by_user varchar [note: 'The username or user ID of the individual who approved the classification assignment. Required for export control classifications (ECCN) and high-risk credit classifications where dual-control approval is mandated.']
  assigned_by_user varchar [note: 'The username or user ID of the individual who assigned the classification to the customer account. Supports audit trail requirements and accountability for classification decisions.']
  assigned_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the classification was formally assigned to the customer account. Used for audit trails, compliance reporting, and tracking classification history.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'A free-text or coded reason explaining why the classification was changed or updated. Supports audit trail requirements and provides business context for classification history. Examples: \'Annual ABC review\', \'Export license obtained\', \'Credit risk reassessment\'.']
  classification_code varchar [note: 'The standardized short code representing the classification value within the classification scheme. Used for system-to-system integration, SAP S/4HANA customer classification fields, and export control screening lookups.']
  classification_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the classification value, providing business context for the assigned classification. For example, \'High-value strategic account\' for ABC-A, or \'Dual-use item requiring export license\' for an ECCN code.']
  classification_language varchar [note: 'The ISO 639-1 two-letter language code in which the classification description and value are maintained. Supports multi-language classification management for multinational operations.']
  classification_priority varchar [note: 'A numeric priority rank assigned to the classification record when multiple classifications of the same type exist for a customer account. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used to resolve conflicts when multiple classifications apply simultaneously.']
  classification_scheme varchar [note: 'The formal scheme, standard, or framework under which the classification value is defined. Examples include \'SAP ABC Analysis\', \'Nielsen Global Segment Framework\', \'US EAR Export Control\', \'EU Dual-Use Regulation\', or a company-defined internal scheme.']
  classification_type varchar [note: 'The category or scheme of classification applied to the customer account. Examples include ABC classification (revenue-based segmentation), Nielsen classification (market research segmentation), ECCN (Export Control Classification Number), EAR99 (Export Administration Regulations catch-all), industry sector, credit risk tier, and strategic account classification.']
  classification_value varchar [note: 'The specific classification value or code assigned to the customer account under the given classification type. For example, \'A\', \'B\', or \'C\' for ABC classification; an ECCN code such as \'3A001\' for export control; or a Nielsen segment code.']
  country_of_application varchar [note: 'The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country or jurisdiction in which this classification is applicable. Supports country-specific classification rules, export control by destination, and regional compliance requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the account classification record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting.']
  credit_risk_class varchar [note: 'The credit risk classification assigned to the customer account based on financial assessment, payment history, and creditworthiness. Drives credit limit setting, payment terms, and order release decisions in SAP S/4HANA FI/SD.']
  customer_account_group varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA customer account group code that determines the number range, field selection, and partner functions applicable to the customer master record. Examples include KUNA (domestic customers), KUNE (export customers), CPDL (one-time customers).']
  denied_party_screen_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which the customer account was screened against denied party and sanctions lists. Used to ensure screening is current and to schedule re-screening at required intervals.']
  denied_party_screen_result varchar [note: 'The outcome of the most recent denied party and sanctions screening for the customer account. \'clear\' indicates no match found; \'match_found\' triggers a compliance hold; \'potential_match\' requires manual review.']
  denied_party_screened boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer account has been screened against denied party, debarred, and sanctioned entity lists (e.g., US BIS Denied Persons List, OFAC SDN List, EU Consolidated Sanctions List). True = screening has been performed.']
  ear99_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer account has been classified as EAR99 under the US Export Administration Regulations, meaning the associated goods or transactions do not require a specific export license for most destinations. True = EAR99 classification applies.']
  eccn_code varchar [note: 'The Export Control Classification Number (ECCN) assigned to the customer account for export control screening purposes. Identifies whether the customer or their end-use requires an export license under the US Export Administration Regulations (EAR). Applicable for industrial manufacturing customers dealing in dual-use goods.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which the classification becomes operationally effective for pricing, reporting, and compliance purposes. May differ from the assigned date if the classification is pre-dated or post-dated.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the classification expires and is no longer valid. Applicable for time-bound classifications such as export control licenses, credit risk ratings, and contractual tier assignments.']
  export_license_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an export license is required for transactions with this customer account based on their export control classification, destination country, and end-use. Drives export compliance screening workflows.']
  industry_sector_code varchar [note: 'The industry sector classification code assigned to the customer account, aligned with standard industry classification systems such as NAICS, SIC, or NACE. Used for market segmentation, reporting, and targeted sales strategies.']
  industry_sector_name varchar [note: 'The human-readable name of the industry sector assigned to the customer account. Complements the industry sector code for reporting and analytics purposes.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this classification record is the primary or default classification for the given classification type on the customer account. True = this is the primary classification used in pricing and reporting when multiple classifications of the same type exist.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the account classification record was most recently updated. Supports change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and audit trail requirements.']
  pricing_group varchar [note: 'The pricing group code assigned to the customer account in SAP S/4HANA SD, used to determine applicable pricing conditions, discount schedules, and surcharges. Links the customer to a specific pricing strategy.']
  reach_rohs_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer account has confirmed compliance with EU REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals) and RoHS (Restriction of Hazardous Substances) regulations. Relevant for industrial manufacturing customers in the EU market.']
  revenue_tier varchar [note: 'The revenue-based tier assigned to the customer account, used for pricing, discount eligibility, and service level differentiation. Platinum and Gold tiers typically receive preferential pricing and dedicated account management.']
  review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next periodic review of the classification. Ensures classifications remain current and accurate, particularly for credit risk, export control, and strategic account tiers.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating operational system from which the classification record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between SAP S/4HANA SD, Salesforce CRM, and other systems of record.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the classification record in the originating source system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA customer classification key, Salesforce record ID). Enables traceability and reconciliation back to the system of record.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the account classification record. Indicates whether the classification is currently active and in use, pending review, expired, or superseded by a newer classification.']
  tax_classification varchar [note: 'The tax classification assigned to the customer account, determining the applicable tax treatment for sales transactions. Drives VAT/GST determination in SAP S/4HANA SD and ensures compliance with local tax regulations.']
  tax_exemption_number varchar [note: 'The official tax exemption certificate number issued by the relevant tax authority, confirming the customer account\'s exemption from applicable taxes. Required for compliance and audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Stores multi-dimensional classification attributes for customer accounts used in pricing, reporting, and compliance. Captures classification type (e.g., ABC classification, Nielsen classification, export control classification, industry sector), classification value, classification scheme, assigned date, and assigning user. Supports SAP S/4HANA customer classification and export control screening (ECCN, EAR99).'
}

Table customer.sales_area_assignment {
  sales_area_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each sales area assignment record linking a customer account to a specific SAP S/4HANA sales area combination (Sales Organization, Distribution Channel, Division).']
  pricing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.pricing_agreement. Business justification: sales_area_assignment stores pricing_procedure and price_list_type as STRING attributes that reference the pricing configuration applicable to the sales area. pricing_agreement is the authoritative re']
  sales_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sales_org. Business justification: A customer\'s sales area assignment maps directly to a sales organization that owns the customer relationship and product catalog access. This is a foundational ERP relationship in manufacturing — sale']
  account_assignment_group varchar [note: 'SAP Account Assignment Group for the customer, used in revenue account determination to map sales transactions to the correct general ledger (GL) revenue accounts during billing (e.g., 01=Domestic Revenue, 02=Export Revenue, 03=Service Revenue).']
  authorization_group varchar [note: 'SAP Authorization Group code controlling which user roles and organizational units are permitted to view or modify this customer\'s sales area data. Enforces data access governance and segregation of duties.']
  billing_block_code varchar [note: 'SAP Billing Block code indicating whether billing is currently blocked for this customer in this sales area and the reason (e.g., 01=Credit Block, 02=Dispute, 03=Audit Hold). A blank value indicates no billing block is active.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this sales area assignment record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA). Used for audit trail, data lineage tracking, and compliance with data governance policies.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code defining the default transaction currency for orders placed by this customer in this sales area (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY). Used in pricing determination and financial document posting.']
  customer_classification varchar [note: 'SAP Customer Classification code used for statistical analysis and ABC segmentation of customers by revenue contribution within this sales area (e.g., A=High Value, B=Medium Value, C=Low Value). Supports strategic account management.']
  customer_group_code varchar [note: 'SAP Customer Group code classifying the customer for pricing, reporting, and statistical purposes within this sales area (e.g., 01=OEM, 02=System Integrator, 03=Distributor, 04=End User, 05=Government). Used in pricing condition determination.']
  customer_group_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the customer group classification (e.g., \'OEM Partner\', \'System Integrator\', \'Distributor\', \'End User\') for business reporting and segmentation analysis.']
  delivery_block_code varchar [note: 'SAP Delivery Block code indicating whether outbound deliveries are currently blocked for this customer in this sales area and the reason (e.g., 01=Credit Limit Exceeded, 02=Export License Pending). A blank value indicates no delivery block.']
  delivery_priority varchar [note: 'SAP Delivery Priority code assigned to this customer in this sales area, controlling the sequence in which deliveries are processed during outbound logistics (e.g., 01=Urgent, 02=Normal, 03=Low). Impacts warehouse scheduling and shipping lead times.']
  distribution_channel_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the distribution channel (e.g., \'Direct Sales\', \'Distributor\', \'OEM Partner\', \'E-Commerce\') for reporting and business user clarity.']
  division_code varchar [note: 'SAP Division code representing the product line or business segment assigned to this customer in this sales area (e.g., 01=Automation Systems, 02=Electrification, 03=Smart Infrastructure). Determines which product catalog and pricing conditions apply.']
  division_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the division (product line or business segment) for reporting and analytics (e.g., \'Automation Systems\', \'Electrification Solutions\', \'Smart Infrastructure\').']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the default delivery terms and risk transfer point for orders placed by this customer in this sales area (e.g., EXW, FCA, DAP, DDP). Governs freight cost allocation and export documentation.']
  incoterms_location varchar [note: 'Named place or port associated with the Incoterms code specifying the exact point of delivery or risk transfer (e.g., \'Chicago Port\', \'Customer Warehouse Frankfurt\'). Required for complete Incoterms specification per ICC 2020 rules.']
  invoice_dates_code varchar [note: 'SAP Invoice Dates (billing schedule) code defining the default billing frequency or calendar for this customer in this sales area (e.g., monthly billing, weekly billing, milestone billing). Controls when billing documents are created.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this sales area assignment record was most recently updated in the source system. Supports change tracking, delta processing in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline, and audit compliance.']
  max_partial_deliveries varchar [note: 'Maximum number of partial deliveries allowed per sales order for this customer in this sales area. Limits the number of shipment splits permitted, balancing customer flexibility with logistics cost control.']
  order_block_code varchar [note: 'SAP Order Block code indicating whether new sales orders are currently blocked for this customer in this sales area and the reason (e.g., 01=Credit Hold, 02=Sanctions Review, 03=Contract Expired). A blank value indicates no order block is active.']
  order_combination_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether multiple open sales orders for this customer in this sales area can be combined into a single delivery document. When True, SAP SD will attempt to consolidate orders into one shipment to reduce freight costs and improve logistics efficiency.']
  order_probability_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Default probability percentage (0-100%) that a sales quotation or inquiry from this customer in this sales area will convert to a confirmed sales order. Used in sales pipeline forecasting and opportunity management.']
  partial_delivery_flag varchar [note: 'SAP Partial Delivery indicator controlling whether partial deliveries are permitted for this customer in this sales area (e.g., A=Partial delivery allowed, B=Only complete delivery, C=Partial delivery per item). Governs order fulfillment behavior.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'SAP Payment Terms code defining the default payment conditions for this customer in this sales area (e.g., NT30=Net 30 Days, 2/10NT30=2% discount if paid within 10 days net 30). Copied to sales orders and billing documents.']
  rebate_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this customer is eligible to participate in rebate agreements within this sales area. When True, SAP SD will process rebate accruals and settlements for qualifying transactions per the applicable rebate agreement.']
  sales_district_code varchar [note: 'SAP Sales District code grouping customers into geographic or market-based territories within the sales organization for territory management, sales rep assignment, and regional performance reporting.']
  sales_group_code varchar [note: 'SAP Sales Group code identifying the team or group of sales representatives within the sales office responsible for this customer account. Enables granular sales performance tracking at team level.']
  sales_office_code varchar [note: 'SAP Sales Office code identifying the regional sales office responsible for managing this customer account within the sales organization. Used for organizational reporting and sales performance analysis.']
  shipping_condition varchar [note: 'SAP Shipping Condition code defining the default mode and urgency of shipment for this customer in this sales area (e.g., 01=Standard Ground, 02=Express Air, 03=Sea Freight, 04=Customer Pickup). Used in route and shipping point determination.']
  source_record_key varchar [note: 'Natural composite key from the source SAP S/4HANA system uniquely identifying this record in the originating system (typically concatenation of Customer Number + Sales Org + Distribution Channel + Division, e.g., \'C0001234-US01-10-01\'). Enables traceability back to the source system.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational source system from which this sales area assignment record originated (e.g., \'SAP_S4H_PROD\', \'SAP_S4H_EU\'). Supports multi-system data lineage tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer for multinational deployments.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this customer sales area assignment record. \'active\' indicates the assignment is valid and orders can be processed; \'inactive\' indicates the assignment has been deactivated; \'blocked\' indicates a temporary hold; \'pending_review\' indicates the assignment is under compliance or credit review.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this sales area assignment becomes effective and the customer is authorized to place orders within this sales area. Used for time-bounded customer-sales area relationships and contract period management.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this sales area assignment expires and the customer is no longer authorized to place orders within this sales area. A null or far-future date (e.g., 9999-12-31) indicates an open-ended assignment with no planned expiry.']

  Note: 'Maps customer accounts to specific SAP S/4HANA sales areas (Sales Organization, Distribution Channel, Division) defining which products and pricing conditions apply to each customer in each sales area. Captures sales organization code, distribution channel, division, customer group, price list type, incoterms, delivery priority, and order combination flag. Foundational for order management and pricing determination.'
}

Table customer.account_contact_role {
  account_contact_role_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each account-contact role association record in the silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for this entity.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: account_contact_role is an association entity linking contacts to accounts. The contact_id FK is essential to establish which contact holds the role. No existing FK exists. No safe denormalized column']
  partner_function_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.partner_function. Business justification: account_contact_role carries erp_partner_function_code as a STRING representing the SAP partner function associated with the contact\'s role in commercial transactions. partner_function is the authorit']
  sales_area_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.sales_area_assignment. Business justification: account_contact_role carries assigned_sales_territory as a STRING representing the sales territory associated with the contact\'s role. sales_area_assignment is the authoritative record for SAP sales a']
  authority_limit_currency varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the purchase order authority limit is denominated. Supports multi-currency operations across global accounts.']
  ccpa_opt_out boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact has exercised their CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act) right to opt out of the sale or sharing of their personal data in the context of this role. Applicable to contacts in California.']
  communication_language varchar [note: 'The preferred language for communications directed to this contact in this role, expressed as an IETF BCP 47 language tag (e.g., en-US, de-DE, fr-FR). Supports multilingual B2B engagement across global accounts.']
  communication_preference varchar [note: 'The preferred channel through which this contact wishes to receive communications in the context of this role. Governs outbound communication routing for commercial, technical, and service interactions.']
  contract_authority_level varchar [note: 'Defines the level of contractual signing authority held by this contact in the context of this role. Critical for order management, procurement, and legal compliance workflows requiring authorized signatories.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this account-contact role record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail entry point for the record lifecycle.']
  crm_role_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier for this account-contact role record in the Salesforce CRM system. Enables cross-system traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the CRM source of record.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The business-effective date from which this account-contact role record is considered valid for operational and reporting purposes. Supports bi-temporal data modeling and historical reporting in the silver layer.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date on which the contact\'s role within the account was terminated or expired. A null value indicates the role is currently active. Used for historical role tracking and succession planning.']
  escalation_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority rank (1 = highest) assigned to this contact role for escalation routing in service, quality, or commercial dispute scenarios. Ensures critical contacts are engaged first during incident management.']
  gdpr_consent_date date [note: 'The date on which GDPR consent was obtained or last updated for this contact role. Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance in EU/EEA jurisdictions.']
  gdpr_consent_status varchar [note: 'Records the GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) consent status for processing this contact\'s personal data in the context of this role. Mandatory for contacts in EU/EEA jurisdictions to ensure regulatory compliance.']
