  influence_level varchar [note: 'Characterizes the degree of influence this contact exercises over purchasing, technical, or operational decisions within the account. Used in sales strategy, opportunity management, and stakeholder mapping.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is the primary point of contact for the specified role type within the account. Only one contact per role type per account should be flagged as primary at any given time.']
  last_interaction_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent recorded business interaction (call, meeting, email, site visit) with this contact in the context of this role. Used for relationship health monitoring and account engagement analytics.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent modification to this account-contact role record. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental loads, and audit trail maintenance in the lakehouse.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next formal review or reassessment of this contact\'s role assignment within the account. Supports periodic relationship governance and account planning cycles.']
  notification_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact has opted in to receive automated notifications (e.g., order status updates, shipment alerts, quality notifications) in the context of this role. Supports GDPR and CCPA compliant communication.']
  purchase_order_authority_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum monetary value (in the account\'s base currency) up to which this contact is authorized to approve or issue Purchase Orders (POs) in this role. Used in procurement workflow routing and approval chain configuration.']
  relationship_strength varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of the strength of the business relationship between the contact and the account in this role. Informs account management strategy and customer retention efforts.']
  role_category varchar [note: 'Broad grouping of the contact role used for segmentation and targeted communication strategies. Enables filtering of contacts by functional area across the account portfolio.']
  role_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context about the contact\'s specific responsibilities or scope within this role at the account. Captures nuances not covered by the standardized role type enumeration.']
  role_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the specific business function or responsibility the contact holds within the account. Drives communication routing, escalation paths, and relationship mapping across complex B2B accounts in CRM and ERP systems.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'The SLA (Service Level Agreement) tier applicable to this contact role, governing response time commitments and escalation priorities for service and support interactions. Aligns with the account\'s contracted service level.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this account-contact role record originated. Supports data lineage, audit, and master data management reconciliation.']
  source_system_key varchar [note: 'The natural or surrogate key of this record in the originating source system. Used for delta load processing, deduplication, and cross-system reconciliation in the lakehouse ingestion pipeline.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which the contact assumed the specified role within the account. Used for tenure tracking, historical analysis, and SLA (Service Level Agreement) assignment.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the account-contact role association. Determines whether the role is operationally active for communication routing and relationship management.']
  verification_date date [note: 'The date on which the contact\'s role assignment was last verified or confirmed. Used for data quality audits and to identify stale or unverified role assignments requiring refresh.']
  verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact\'s role assignment has been formally verified and confirmed by the account management team or the contact themselves. Supports data quality governance and master data management.']

  Note: 'Association entity linking contacts to accounts with specific business roles, capturing the nature of the relationship between a person and an organization. Captures role type (primary commercial contact, technical decision maker, accounts payable contact, plant manager, executive sponsor), start date, end date, active flag, and communication preference. Enables targeted communication routing and relationship mapping across complex B2B accounts.'
}

Table customer.pricing_coverage_assignment {
  pricing_coverage_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the pricing_coverage_assignment association']
  account_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific account hierarchy node (subsidiary, division, or affiliate) to which the pricing agreement is being assigned.']
  pricing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the pricing agreement being assigned to the account hierarchy node.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The current approval workflow status of this specific pricing coverage assignment. Tracks whether the assignment of this agreement to this hierarchy node has been reviewed and approved by the required authority level. Distinct from the pricing agreement\'s own approval_status.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this pricing agreement assignment is valid for the specific account hierarchy node. May differ from the pricing agreement\'s own validity start date when the agreement is rolled out to subsidiaries on a staggered schedule.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this pricing agreement assignment ceases to be valid for the specific account hierarchy node. Drives SAP S/4HANA condition record deactivation for this node.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pricing agreement grants exclusive pricing rights to this specific account hierarchy node, preventing the same agreement terms from being assigned to competing hierarchy nodes in the same region or market.']
  scope_type varchar [note: 'Defines the product scope for which this pricing agreement applies to this specific hierarchy node. A global agreement may apply in full to the parent but only to a specific product line for a subsidiary.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Contract between account_hierarchy and pricing_agreement. It captures the formal assignment of a negotiated pricing agreement to a specific account hierarchy node (subsidiary, division, or affiliate), defining the scope, validity period, approval status, and exclusivity of that coverage. Each record links one account_hierarchy node to one pricing_agreement with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific node-agreement assignment. Feeds SAP S/4HANA condition record scoping and global account management workflows.. Existence Justification: In industrial manufacturing, global pricing agreements are negotiated at the corporate parent level but must be explicitly applied to specific entities within the account hierarchy (subsidiaries, divisions, affiliates). A single pricing agreement covers multiple hierarchy nodes (e.g., all EMEA subsidiaries of a global OEM), and a given hierarchy node may be covered by multiple pricing agreements for different product lines or agreement types. This is an operationally managed relationship — commercial teams actively create, maintain, and expire these assignments as part of account management and SAP S/4HANA condition record management.'
}

Table engineering.bom_line {
  bom_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual BOM line item record in the silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for the bom_line data product.']
  cad_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.cad_model. Business justification: Each BOM line item (component) corresponds to a CAD model in the PLM/PDM system. Linking bom_line to cad_model provides direct traceability from the product structure to the 3D geometry definition, en']
  ecn_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) that authorized the creation or modification of this BOM line item. Supports full change traceability and audit compliance.']
  engineering_bom_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent BOM header to which this line item belongs. Enables multi-level BOM explosion and parent-child component hierarchy traversal for MRP and production planning.']
  material_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.material_specification. Business justification: BOM line items for raw material or semi-finished components must reference the applicable material specification defining chemical composition, mechanical properties, and compliance requirements. This']
  uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: Every BOM line specifies a component quantity in a unit of measure (each, kg, meter). Referencing the shared UOM table ensures consistency across engineering, procurement, and production quantity calc']
  alternative_item_group varchar [note: 'Groups multiple BOM line items that are interchangeable alternatives for the same functional requirement. During MRP and production order creation, the system selects the preferred or available alternative based on priority and stock availability. Supports substitute component management and supply chain flexibility.']
  alternative_item_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority rank within an alternative item group, where lower numbers indicate higher preference (1 = first choice). Used by MRP and production planning to determine which substitute component to consume first when multiple alternatives are defined for the same functional position.']
  alternative_item_usage_probability decimal(5,2) [note: 'The probability percentage (0–100) that this alternative component will be selected over other alternatives in the same group during production planning. Used by MRP to distribute planned requirements across alternative components proportionally, supporting multi-source procurement strategies.']
  bom_item_number varchar [note: 'Sequential item number assigned to this BOM line within the parent BOM header, typically in increments of 10 (e.g., 0010, 0020). Used for ordering and referencing line items within the BOM structure in SAP PP.']
  bom_level varchar [note: 'The hierarchical depth level of this component within the multi-level BOM structure, where level 0 is the finished good, level 1 is the first-tier sub-assembly, and so on. Used for BOM explosion reporting, MRP low-level code assignment, and production scheduling sequencing.']
  bom_usage varchar [note: 'Defines the business purpose for which this BOM is used, corresponding to SAP BOM usage keys (e.g., 1=Production, 2=Engineering/Design, 3=Universal, 4=Plant Maintenance, 5=Sales, 6=Costing). Determines which BOM is selected during MRP, costing, sales order processing, and maintenance planning.']
  component_number varchar [note: 'The manufacturer or internal part number of the child component as defined in the material master or PLM system. Used for procurement, inventory lookup, and cross-referencing with supplier catalogs and engineering drawings.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this BOM line record was originally created in the source system. Used for audit trail, change history analysis, and data lineage tracking in compliance with ISO 9001 document control requirements.']
  effectivity_end_date date [note: 'The date after which this BOM line item is no longer valid for production planning and MRP explosion. Used to phase out obsolete components following an Engineering Change Notice (ECN) while maintaining historical BOM traceability for previously manufactured products.']
  effectivity_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this BOM line item becomes valid and active for use in production planning and MRP explosion. Supports date-effective BOM management for engineering change implementation, ensuring new component versions are introduced at the correct production date.']
  find_number varchar [note: 'Balloon or find number referencing the component\'s callout position on the engineering drawing or assembly diagram. Used by manufacturing engineers and quality inspectors to locate the component on the drawing during First Article Inspection (FAI) and assembly.']
  fixed_quantity_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the component quantity on this BOM line is fixed regardless of the production order quantity (true) or variable and proportional to the order quantity (false). Fixed quantities are used for setup materials, tooling, or components consumed once per batch regardless of batch size.']
  is_bulk_material boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this BOM line item is a bulk material (e.g., adhesives, lubricants, solder paste, wire) that is consumed in variable quantities and typically not individually issued to production orders. Bulk materials are often backflushed or managed via replenishment rather than discrete picking.']
  is_co_product boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this BOM line item is a co-product or by-product produced alongside the primary output of the manufacturing process. Co-products have positive quantity and receive cost allocation; by-products may have negative cost impact. Relevant for process manufacturing and chemical/electrification solution production.']
  is_phantom_assembly boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this BOM line represents a phantom assembly — a logical grouping of components that is not physically stocked or produced as a discrete item but is exploded through during MRP to directly consume its child components. Common in modular product structures and configurable BOMs.']
  issue_location varchar [note: 'The warehouse storage location from which this component is issued to the production order or work order. Drives automatic goods issue (backflush) and pick list generation in SAP WM/WMS and Infor WMS for shop floor material replenishment.']
  item_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the BOM line item type as defined in SAP PP. Stock items (L) are warehouse-managed materials; non-stock items (N) are procured directly to the order; document items (D) reference engineering documents; phantom assemblies (P) are logical groupings not physically stocked; variable-size items (R) have variable dimensions.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent modification to this BOM line record in the source system. Supports incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail requirements for engineering change management and quality management system (QMS) compliance.']
  long_text varchar [note: 'Extended descriptive text or engineering notes associated with this BOM line item. May include assembly instructions, special handling requirements, quality inspection notes, or engineering change justifications visible to production planners and shop floor operators.']
  operation_activity_number varchar [note: 'The routing operation or activity number to which this BOM component is assigned for consumption. Links the component to the specific manufacturing step in the Bill of Process (BoP) where it is consumed, enabling accurate work-in-progress (WIP) tracking and operation-level material staging.']
  operation_lead_time_offset decimal(9,2) [note: 'The number of days (positive or negative) by which the component requirement date is offset from the production order start date. Used in multi-level scheduling to stage component availability at the correct operation step, supporting JIT (Just-In-Time) material staging on the shop floor.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or production site for which this BOM line is valid. BOMs can be plant-specific to accommodate local sourcing, manufacturing processes, or regional regulatory requirements across the multinational enterprise.']
  procurement_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the component on this BOM line is manufactured in-house (make), externally procured (buy), or can be sourced either way. Drives MRP planning logic to generate either planned production orders or purchase requisitions for the component.']
  quantity decimal(18,6) [note: 'The quantity of the child component required to produce one unit of the parent assembly as specified on this BOM line. Used directly by MRP (Material Requirements Planning) to calculate gross requirements and by production planning for work order component staging.']
  reference_designator varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric label identifying the specific physical location(s) of a component on a printed circuit board (PCB) or assembly drawing (e.g., R1, C12, U5). Critical for electronics manufacturing, PCB assembly, and automated optical inspection (AOI) processes.']
  scrap_factor_percent decimal(7,4) [note: 'The anticipated scrap or yield loss percentage for this component during the manufacturing process, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.00 = 5%). MRP inflates the gross requirement by this factor to account for expected material loss, ensuring sufficient components are procured or issued to production orders.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this BOM line record was sourced (e.g., SAP PP for production BOMs, Siemens Teamcenter for engineering/design BOMs). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between PLM and ERP BOM structures in the silver layer.']
  source_system_key varchar [note: 'The natural or composite key of this BOM line record as it exists in the originating source system (e.g., SAP BOM number + item number + plant, or Teamcenter BOM line UID). Enables traceability back to the system of record and supports delta load reconciliation in the lakehouse ingestion pipeline.']
  special_procurement_type varchar [note: 'Specifies a special procurement strategy for this component, such as subcontracting (component sent to external vendor for processing), consignment stock, inter-plant stock transfer, or direct production. Overrides the standard procurement type for specific supply chain scenarios.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this BOM line item. Active lines are included in MRP explosion and production planning. Pending approval lines are awaiting ECN authorization. Obsolete lines are retained for historical traceability but excluded from planning runs.']
  supply_area varchar [note: 'The production supply area or supermarket location on the shop floor where this component is staged for consumption at the work center. Supports Kanban and JIT (Just-In-Time) material flow management in Siemens Opcenter MES and SAP PP.']

  Note: 'Individual line-item within a Bill of Materials defining each child component, quantity, unit of measure, reference designator, find number, item category (stock, non-stock, document), scrap factor, and effectivity date range. Supports multi-level BOM explosion for MRP and production planning. Sourced from Siemens Teamcenter BOM line items and SAP PP BOM item records.'
}

Table engineering.bop_operation {
  bop_operation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying an individual operation step within a Bill of Process. Serves as the primary key for this entity in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  bop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.bop. Business justification: bop_operation is a child record of bop — each operation step belongs to a Bill of Process header. Without this FK, bop_operation is siloed from its parent. This is the fundamental parent-child relatio']
  pfmea_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.pfmea. Business justification: bop_operation.pfmea_reference_number is a STRING reference to the PFMEA that analyzes failure modes for this operation. Replacing with FK pfmea_id → pfmea.pfmea_id enforces the PFMEA-to-operation trac']
  tooling_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.tooling_equipment. Business justification: BOP operations specify required tooling and equipment. Production scheduling and maintenance systems use this to ensure correct tools are available, calibrated, and assigned to workstations before ope']
  base_quantity decimal(13,3) [note: 'Reference quantity to which the standard time values (machine time, labor time) apply. For example, a machine time of 5 minutes per base quantity of 100 pieces. Corresponds to SAP PP BMSCH field.']
  base_quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the base quantity (e.g., EA for each, PC for pieces, KG for kilograms). Aligns with SAP unit of measure configuration and ISO 80000 measurement standards.']
  control_key varchar [note: 'SAP PP control key (e.g., PP01, PP03, QM01) that governs the scheduling, costing, confirmation, and quality inspection behavior of the operation. Determines whether the operation is relevant for capacity planning, external processing, or quality inspection.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP cost center code associated with the work center performing this operation. Used for cost allocation, COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) calculation, and financial reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this BoP operation record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and change history tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the external processing price (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Supports multi-currency operations in multinational manufacturing environments.']
  ecn_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) that introduced or last modified this operation. Provides full traceability of process changes to engineering change management records.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this operation version becomes valid and can be used in production planning and execution. Supports date-effective routing management and Engineering Change Notice (ECN) implementation.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date after which this operation version is no longer valid. Used to manage operation version supersession during Engineering Change Order (ECO) implementation and routing revision cycles.']
  external_processing_price decimal(15,4) [note: 'Standard price per unit for externally processed operations performed by a subcontractor. Used in product costing and purchase order creation for subcontracting. Corresponds to SAP PP PEINH/PREIS fields.']
  hazardous_material_relevant boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this operation involves handling of hazardous materials, chemicals, or substances subject to REACH, RoHS, or OSHA regulations. Triggers mandatory safety documentation and PPE requirements on the shop floor.']
  inspection_characteristic_group varchar [note: 'Code or identifier of the inspection characteristic group (master inspection characteristic catalog) assigned to this operation for in-process quality control. Defines the measurement parameters and tolerances to be checked.']
  inspection_relevant boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this operation requires a quality inspection step (in-process inspection). When true, inspection characteristics and AQL (Acceptable Quality Level) parameters must be defined for this operation.']
  interoperation_time_min decimal(10,3) [note: 'Standard wait and move time in minutes between this operation and the next operation in the routing. Includes queue time, move time, and wait time. Used in lead time calculation and scheduling. Corresponds to SAP PP interoperation times.']
  labor_time_min decimal(10,3) [note: 'Standard labor time in minutes per base quantity unit required for this operation. Used for workforce planning, labor cost calculation, and takt time analysis. Corresponds to SAP PP ARBZE field.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this BoP operation record in the source system. Supports incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance.']
  long_text varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the operation including step-by-step instructions, safety notes, and quality requirements. Supports shop floor instruction generation and Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) documentation.']
  machine_time_min decimal(10,3) [note: 'Standard machine processing time in minutes per base quantity unit for this operation. Used for capacity planning, OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculation, and production scheduling. Corresponds to SAP PP BEAZE field.']
  maximum_lot_size decimal(13,3) [note: 'Maximum production quantity that can be processed in a single run of this operation. Constrains batch sizes for capacity and quality management purposes.']
  minimum_lot_size decimal(13,3) [note: 'Minimum production quantity that can be processed in a single run of this operation. Used in production planning and scheduling to enforce batch size constraints.']
  nc_program_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the CNC (Computer Numerical Control) or NC program used to execute this operation on automated machinery. Links to the CAM (Computer-Aided Manufacturing) system for program retrieval.']
  number_of_resources varchar [note: 'Number of parallel machines or labor resources required to execute this operation simultaneously. Used for capacity planning and scheduling. Corresponds to SAP PP ANZZL field.']
  operation_description varchar [note: 'Short descriptive text for the operation step, summarizing the manufacturing activity performed (e.g., \'Weld Frame Assembly\', \'CNC Milling – Housing\', \'Final Inspection\'). Corresponds to SAP PP LTXA1 field.']
  operation_number varchar [note: 'Sequential numeric identifier for the operation step within the routing or Bill of Process, typically in increments of 10 (e.g., 0010, 0020, 0030). Corresponds to the SAP PP routing operation number (VORNR) and Teamcenter process step sequence number.']
  operation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the operation indicating whether it is performed in-house, by an external subcontractor, or as a quality inspection step. Drives procurement and costing logic.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where this operation is performed. Supports multi-plant BoP management for multinational operations.']
  scheduling_type varchar [note: 'Determines the scheduling direction for this operation in production planning — forward scheduling (from start date), backward scheduling (from required finish date), or current date. Corresponds to SAP PP scheduling parameters.']
  setup_time_min decimal(10,3) [note: 'Standard setup time in minutes required to prepare the work center for this operation, including tooling changeover and machine configuration. Used in capacity planning, scheduling, and SMED (Single-Minute Exchange of Dies) analysis. Corresponds to SAP PP RUSTE field.']
  sop_reference_number varchar [note: 'Document number or identifier of the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) governing the execution of this operation. Links to the document management system for shop floor instruction retrieval.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this BoP operation was sourced (e.g., SAP_PP for SAP S/4HANA PP routing, TEAMCENTER for Siemens Teamcenter manufacturing process steps). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  standard_cost_activity_type varchar [note: 'SAP CO activity type code used to assign standard costs to this operation (e.g., machine hours, labor hours). Links the operation to cost center accounting and product costing. Corresponds to SAP CO LSTAR field.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the BoP operation record. \'Active\' operations are available for production order assignment; \'obsolete\' operations are retained for historical traceability but cannot be used in new routings.']
  subcontracting_vendor_code varchar [note: 'Vendor or supplier code for externally processed operations where the operation is performed by a subcontractor. Applicable when operation_type is \'external\' or \'subcontracting\'. Corresponds to SAP PP external processing vendor.']
  teardown_time_min decimal(10,3) [note: 'Standard teardown or post-processing time in minutes required after the operation is completed, including cleaning, tool removal, and work center reset. Corresponds to SAP PP ABRUE field.']
  tooling_list_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the tooling list or production resource/tool (PRT) assignment associated with this operation. Specifies jigs, fixtures, cutting tools, and gauges required. Corresponds to SAP PP PRT assignment.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this operation step, incremented when the operation is revised through the engineering change process. Enables version-controlled routing management and historical comparison.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the work center (machine, production line, or labor group) where this operation is performed. Corresponds to SAP PP ARBPL field and links to the work center master data.']
  work_center_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the work center where the operation is executed (e.g., \'CNC Cell 3\', \'Welding Station A\', \'Final Assembly Line 2\'). Supports shop floor reporting and scheduling.']

  Note: 'Individual operation step within a Bill of Process defining the work center, operation description, standard values (setup time, machine time, labor time), control key, inspection characteristics, tooling requirements, and SOP reference. Supports PFMEA linkage and shop floor instruction generation. Sourced from SAP PP routing operations and Teamcenter manufacturing process steps.'
}

Table engineering.cad_model {
  cad_model_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a CAD/CAM design model record within the PLM/PDM system. Serves as the primary key for the cad_model data product in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  ecn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: cad_model.ecn_number is a STRING reference to the ECN that authorized the CAD model revision. Replacing with FK ecn_id → ecn.ecn_id enforces change management traceability for design geometry. ecn_num']
  plm_lifecycle_state_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.plm_lifecycle_state. Business justification: cad_model.lifecycle_state is a STRING that should reference the authoritative PLM lifecycle state reference table. Replacing with FK plm_lifecycle_state_id → plm_lifecycle_state.plm_lifecycle_state_id']
  bounding_box_x_mm decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum extent of the CAD model geometry along the X-axis in millimeters, representing the width of the component\'s bounding envelope. Used for packaging analysis, fixture design, and logistics planning (e.g., pallet/container sizing).']
  bounding_box_y_mm decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum extent of the CAD model geometry along the Y-axis in millimeters, representing the depth of the component\'s bounding envelope. Used for packaging analysis, fixture design, and logistics planning.']
  bounding_box_z_mm decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum extent of the CAD model geometry along the Z-axis in millimeters, representing the height of the component\'s bounding envelope. Used for packaging analysis, fixture design, and logistics planning.']
  cad_tool varchar [note: 'Name of the CAD/CAM software application used to create and maintain the model (e.g., Siemens NX, CATIA V5/V6, SolidWorks). Determines file format compatibility, interoperability requirements, and licensing needs for downstream engineering and manufacturing processes.']
  cad_tool_version varchar [note: 'Version or release number of the CAD/CAM software used to create or last modify the model (e.g., \'NX 2206\', \'CATIA V5 R31\'). Critical for file compatibility management, migration planning, and ensuring models can be opened by the correct software version in the engineering environment.']
  checked_out_by varchar [note: 'Username or employee ID of the engineer who currently has the CAD model checked out for editing in the PLM/PDM system. A checked-out model is locked for exclusive editing. Null if the model is not currently checked out. Corresponds to Teamcenter \'Checked Out By\'.']
  checkout_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the CAD model was checked out from the PLM/PDM vault for editing. Used to track concurrent engineering activities, identify long-running checkouts that may block other engineers, and support audit trails.']
  classification_code varchar [note: 'Standardized classification code assigned to the CAD model based on the company\'s engineering parts classification system (e.g., eCl@ss, UNSPSC, or internal taxonomy). Enables design reuse, variant management, and supplier sourcing by grouping similar components.']
  component_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the engineering component or assembly represented by the CAD model. Determines BOM structure, procurement strategy (make vs. buy), and applicable design standards. \'assembly\' models link to child part models via the BOM hierarchy in PLM.']
  coordinate_system varchar [note: 'Type of coordinate system used to define the geometry of the CAD model (e.g., Cartesian, Cylindrical). Ensures correct spatial orientation when integrating models into assemblies, simulation environments, or digital twin platforms.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating the country where the component represented by this CAD model is designed or manufactured (e.g., DEU, USA, CHN). Used for export control compliance (ITAR/EAR), customs declarations, and regional regulatory reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the CAD model record was first created in the PLM/PDM system. Provides the starting point for the model\'s audit trail and lifecycle history. Corresponds to Teamcenter \'Creation Date\' metadata.']
  design_owner varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the responsible design engineer who owns and maintains the CAD model. Accountable for model accuracy, revision control, and release approvals. Corresponds to the Teamcenter \'Owning User\' field.']
  dfa_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Design for Assembly (DFA) analysis for this CAD model. Evaluates the ease of assembly, part count reduction opportunities, and assembly process compatibility. Relevant for assembly-type models and sub-assemblies.']
  dfm_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Design for Manufacturability (DFM) analysis for this CAD model. Indicates whether the design has been reviewed for manufacturing feasibility, cost optimization, and process compatibility. A \'passed\' status is typically required before release to production.']
  digital_twin_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this CAD model to its corresponding digital twin instance on the Siemens MindSphere IIoT platform. Enables real-time correlation between the design geometry and operational asset behavior for predictive maintenance and performance optimization.']
  export_control_class varchar [note: 'Export control classification code assigned to the CAD model, indicating whether the design data is subject to export regulations (e.g., ITAR USML category, EAR ECCN code, or \'EAR99\' for unrestricted). Critical for controlling access to design data shared with international suppliers or subsidiaries.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'Native or neutral file format of the CAD model dataset (e.g., STEP, IGES, JT, NX native). Determines interoperability with downstream systems including CAM, FEA simulation, and supplier collaboration. STEP (ISO 10303) is the preferred neutral exchange format for cross-system sharing.']
  file_name varchar [note: 'Full file name of the CAD model dataset as stored in the PLM/PDM vault, including file extension (e.g., \'motor_housing_v2.prt\', \'drive_shaft_asm.CATProduct\'). Used for file retrieval, version tracking, and integration with CAM and simulation tools.']
  file_size_mb decimal(12,3) [note: 'Size of the CAD model file in megabytes as stored in the PLM vault. Used for storage capacity planning, data migration scoping, and identifying oversized models that may impact system performance or collaboration efficiency.']
  freeze_date date [note: 'Date on which the CAD model was frozen, preventing further modifications. Typically aligned with production launch milestones (e.g., PPAP approval, SOP - Start of Production). A frozen model can only be changed via a formal ECO process.']
  is_master_model boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this CAD model is designated as the master geometry model for the component or assembly. The master model is the authoritative source for all downstream derivations (drawings, simulations, CAM programs). Only one master model should exist per component revision.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the CAD model record in the PLM/PDM system. Used for change tracking, synchronization with downstream systems (SAP, MES), and audit compliance. Corresponds to Teamcenter \'Last Modified Date\'.']
  mass_kg decimal(12,4) [note: 'Calculated mass of the component or assembly as derived from the CAD model geometry and assigned material properties, expressed in kilograms. Used for structural analysis, logistics weight calculations, BOM costing, and compliance with weight-sensitive design requirements.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the CAD/CAM model as defined by the design engineer. Typically reflects the component or assembly name (e.g., \'Motor Housing Assembly\', \'Drive Shaft Bracket\'). Corresponds to Teamcenter Item Name.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned alphanumeric identifier for the CAD/CAM model as registered in the PLM/PDM system. Used for cross-referencing with BOM, ECN/ECO, and engineering documentation. Corresponds to the Teamcenter Item ID.']
  model_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the CAD model by its geometric or representational type. Determines the applicable CAD tools, downstream processes (e.g., CAM toolpath generation, FEA simulation), and BOM linkage. \'3d_solid\' is the primary type for CNC machining and DFM analysis.']
  obsolescence_date date [note: 'Date on which the CAD model revision was marked as obsolete, indicating it has been superseded by a newer revision or the component has been discontinued. Used for lifecycle management, archive planning, and BOM cleanup.']
  owning_group varchar [note: 'Name of the engineering team, department, or organizational group responsible for the CAD model (e.g., \'Mechanical Design\', \'Electrical Engineering\', \'Automation Systems\'). Used for workload distribution, access control, and organizational reporting in PLM.']
  primary_material varchar [note: 'Name or code of the primary engineering material assigned to the CAD model (e.g., \'Aluminum 6061-T6\', \'Stainless Steel 316L\', \'ABS Plastic\'). Drives material requirements planning (MRP), cost estimation, and compliance checks for REACH/RoHS regulations.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'Name of the product line or product family to which the designed component or assembly belongs (e.g., \'Automation Systems\', \'Electrification Solutions\', \'Smart Infrastructure\'). Supports portfolio-level reporting and PLM product structure navigation.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the engineering project or program under which the CAD model was created. Links the model to project planning, resource allocation, and CAPEX tracking in SAP PS (Project System). Supports APQP phase tracking.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date on which the CAD model revision was formally released (approved and promoted to \'released\' lifecycle state) in the PLM system. Marks the point at which the design is authorized for use in manufacturing, procurement, and BOM structures.']
  revision varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric revision identifier indicating the version of the CAD model (e.g., A, B, C, 01, 02). Incremented upon each approved engineering change. Corresponds to Teamcenter Item Revision ID and is critical for configuration management and BOM accuracy.']
  surface_finish varchar [note: 'Required surface finish or roughness specification for the component as defined in the CAD model (e.g., \'Ra 1.6 µm\', \'Anodized\', \'Powder Coated\'). Drives manufacturing process selection, quality inspection criteria, and supplier specifications.']
  tolerance_class varchar [note: 'General tolerance class applied to the CAD model for dimensions not individually toleranced (e.g., Fine, Medium, Coarse per ISO 2768). Determines manufacturing precision requirements, process selection, and inspection rigor. Critical for DFM analysis.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measurement used in the CAD model geometry (e.g., MM for millimeters, IN for inches). Critical for dimensional accuracy, interoperability with CAM systems, and compliance with regional engineering standards. Must be consistent across all models in an assembly.']

  Note: 'Master record for CAD/CAM design models and 3D geometry files managed within the PLM/PDM system. Captures model number, CAD tool (e.g., NX, CATIA, SolidWorks), file format, revision, lifecycle state (in-work, released, frozen), design owner, associated component or assembly, model type (3D solid, 2D drawing, simulation), and digital twin linkage. Sourced from Siemens Teamcenter Document Management.'
}

Table engineering.eco {
  eco_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the Engineering Change Order record in the lakehouse silver layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.']
  ecn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: eco.ecn_number is a STRING reference to the parent ECN that this ECO implements. The ECO is the formal authorization child of the ECN (ECN proposes, ECO authorizes implementation). Replacing with FK e']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual total cost incurred to implement the engineering change, captured upon completion. Compared against estimated_cost for variance analysis and continuous improvement of change cost estimation accuracy.']
  affected_bom_revisions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of the Bill of Materials (BOM) revision identifiers that are created or updated as a result of this ECO. Provides traceability between the change order and specific BOM versions in SAP PP and Teamcenter.']
  affected_drawing_revisions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of the engineering drawing revision identifiers (CAD/CAM drawings) that are updated as part of this ECO. Ensures drawing version control traceability in Teamcenter PDM.']
  affected_part_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of material/part numbers (SAP material numbers or Teamcenter item IDs) directly affected by this engineering change. Used for impact analysis and to identify all downstream BOM assemblies requiring update.']
  affected_routing_revisions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of the production routing (Bill of Process/BoP) revision identifiers updated by this ECO. Routing changes affect work center assignments, operation sequences, and standard times in SAP PP.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which the ECO received final authorization to proceed with implementation. Marks the transition from approval workflow to release for manufacturing execution.']
  change_class varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the engineering change. Class 1 (Major) changes affect form, fit, or function and require full PPAP re-submission; Class 2 (Minor) changes require limited validation; Class 3 (Administrative) changes are documentation-only.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of engineering change being implemented. Determines which downstream systems (SAP PP BOM, Teamcenter drawings, routing) must be updated and which approval workflows are triggered.']
  completed_date date [note: 'The actual date on which all implementation tasks for the ECO were confirmed complete, including BOM updates, drawing revisions, routing changes, and shop floor verification. Used for cycle time analysis.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary country where this engineering change is being implemented. Supports multi-country regulatory compliance tracking and regional reporting for multinational operations.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the estimated and actual cost fields (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency reporting for multinational operations.']
  customer_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal written customer approval must be obtained before the engineering change can be implemented. Applicable for changes to customer-specific designs or regulated products under customer quality agreements.']
  customer_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer must be formally notified of this engineering change prior to or upon implementation. Required for changes affecting form, fit, or function on customer-specific products or under contractual SLA obligations.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the technical change being implemented, including what is changing, why it is changing, and the expected outcome. Supports traceability and regulatory audit requirements.']
  disposition_of_stock varchar [note: 'Defines how existing finished goods and raw material inventory affected by the change are to be handled. Determines whether pre-change stock can be consumed, must be reworked, or must be scrapped.']
  disposition_of_wip varchar [note: 'Defines how existing Work in Progress (WIP) inventory and in-process assemblies are to be handled at the time the engineering change becomes effective. Drives shop floor instructions and material disposition in SAP MM/PP.']
  effectivity_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the engineering change becomes effective in production, applicable when effectivity_type is \'date_based\'. Used to schedule BOM and routing updates in SAP PP and Teamcenter.']
  effectivity_lot_number_from varchar [note: 'The starting production lot number from which the engineering change becomes effective, applicable when effectivity_type is \'lot_based\'. All lots at or above this number incorporate the change.']
  effectivity_serial_number_from varchar [note: 'The starting serial number from which the engineering change becomes effective, applicable when effectivity_type is \'serial_number_based\'. All units with serial numbers at or above this value incorporate the change.']
  effectivity_type varchar [note: 'Defines the method by which the engineering change becomes effective in production. Date-based effectivity triggers on a calendar date; serial-number-based triggers at a specific unit; lot-based triggers at a specific production lot. Drives SAP PP BOM and routing effectivity configuration.']
  estimated_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost to implement the engineering change, including engineering labor, tooling, material scrap/rework, and validation costs. Used for change impact assessment, CAPEX/OPEX planning, and ROI analysis.']
  implementation_deadline date [note: 'The mandatory completion date by which all BOM revisions, drawing revisions, routing revisions, and shop floor updates must be fully implemented. Used for scheduling, escalation management, and compliance tracking.']
  number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, business-facing identifier for the Engineering Change Order as assigned by the PLM/PDM system (Siemens Teamcenter). Used for cross-system referencing in SAP PP, shop floor communications, and audit trails.']
  originating_department varchar [note: 'The organizational department that initiated the engineering change request (e.g., Product Engineering, Quality, Manufacturing, Customer Service). Used for change volume analytics and departmental accountability reporting.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or facilities where the engineering change must be implemented. Supports multi-plant rollout coordination and plant-specific BOM/routing updates.']
  ppap_level varchar [note: 'The PPAP submission level required for this engineering change, ranging from Level 1 (Part Submission Warrant only) to Level 5 (full documentation retained at customer location). Determined by customer requirements and change classification.']
  ppap_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) submission is required for this engineering change, typically mandated for Class 1 (Major) changes affecting form, fit, or function for automotive or regulated customers.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the ECO, reflecting urgency of implementation. Critical priority ECOs (e.g., safety or regulatory-driven) are fast-tracked through approval and implementation workflows.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code indicating the primary business driver for the engineering change. Used for root cause analysis, change trend reporting, and CAPA linkage.']
  regulatory_driven boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this engineering change is mandated by a regulatory requirement (e.g., RoHS/REACH compliance, CE Marking, UL certification, OSHA safety requirement). Regulatory-driven ECOs receive elevated priority and mandatory completion tracking.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory standard, directive, or requirement number that mandates this engineering change (e.g., \'RoHS 2011/65/EU\', \'REACH SVHC List Update\', \'CE Marking EN 60204-1\'). Populated only when regulatory_driven is true.']
  released_date date [note: 'The date on which the ECO was formally released to manufacturing and the updated BOM/routing/drawings were published in SAP PP and Teamcenter for shop floor execution.']
  requested_date date [note: 'The date on which the ECO was formally requested or initiated. Used for lead time analysis and change management performance reporting.']
  responsible_engineer varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the lead engineer accountable for coordinating and completing the ECO implementation. This is the primary point of contact for all technical questions related to the change.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this ECO record was sourced (e.g., Siemens Teamcenter PLM, SAP S/4HANA). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this ECO record in the originating operational system (e.g., Teamcenter Change Order UID or SAP Change Number). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse and the system of record.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ECO, tracking its progression from initial drafting through approval, release to manufacturing, implementation, and closure. Drives workflow routing in Teamcenter and SAP PP.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short, descriptive title summarizing the nature and scope of the engineering change. Used in dashboards, approval workflows, and stakeholder communications.']

  Note: 'Engineering Change Order record representing the formal authorization and implementation instruction derived from an approved ECN. Captures ECO number, linked ECN, implementation scope (affected BOM revisions, drawing revisions, routing revisions), effectivity type (date-based, serial number-based, lot-based), implementation deadline, responsible engineer, and completion status. Drives BOM and routing updates in SAP PP and Teamcenter.'
}

Table engineering.change_affected_item {
  change_affected_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying each association record between an engineering change (ECN/ECO) and a specific affected item (component, BOM, drawing, routing, or document) within the Siemens Teamcenter PLM change management module.']
  bom_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.bom_line. Business justification: When an ECN/ECO affects a specific BOM component (the most common change scenario in manufacturing), the change_affected_item record should reference the specific bom_line being changed. This provides']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_contract. Business justification: Engineering change affected items for purchased parts must reference the active procurement contract. Change control boards use this link to assess commercial impact of design changes and trigger cont']
  control_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.control_plan. Business justification: Engineering changes that affect manufacturing processes require control plan updates — the affected item record links to the impacted control plan. This is a mandatory step in engineering change impac']
  drawing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.drawing. Business justification: When an ECN/ECO affects a specific engineering drawing, the change_affected_item record should reference the specific drawing being changed. This provides direct traceability from the change managemen']
  ecn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: change_affected_item.ecn_number is a STRING reference to the ECN this affected item is associated with. change_affected_item is the association record linking ECNs/ECOs to affected items. Replacing wi']
  eco_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.eco. Business justification: change_affected_item.eco_number is a STRING reference to the ECO this affected item is associated with. Replacing with FK eco_id → eco.eco_id enforces referential integrity for the ECO-to-affected-ite']
  inspection_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_plan. Business justification: Engineering changes affect inspection plans when component specifications change. Change management tracks which inspection plans require updates to reflect new tolerances or characteristics.']
  affected_plant_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of SAP plant codes for all manufacturing facilities where this affected item is produced or used, and where the engineering change must be implemented. Supports global change rollout coordination across multinational manufacturing sites.']
  bom_position varchar [note: 'The BOM line item position or component item number within the parent assembly\'s Bill of Materials where this affected item resides. Enables precise identification of the structural location of the change within the product hierarchy.']
  capa_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the associated Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record in the Quality Management System (QMS) that triggered or is linked to this engineering change for the affected item. Enables traceability between quality events and engineering change actions.']
  change_action varchar [note: 'The specific action to be performed on the affected item as a result of the engineering change. \'Revise\' updates an existing item to a new revision; \'Create\' introduces a new item; \'Obsolete\' retires an item from active use; \'Supersede\' replaces one item with another; \'Incorporate\' merges changes into an existing revision; \'Cancel\' voids a previously planned change.']
  change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of what specifically changed for this affected item, including the nature of the modification, the technical rationale, and any relevant engineering justification. Provides the human-readable explanation of the change for engineers, quality auditors, and regulatory reviewers.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code categorizing the primary business reason driving the engineering change for this affected item. Supports root cause analysis, change trend reporting, and CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) linkage in the QMS.']
  cost_impact_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the financial impact of the engineering change on this affected item. Indicates whether the change is expected to reduce, maintain, or increase the cost of the item, supporting financial impact assessment and CAPEX/OPEX planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this change affected item association record was created in the PLM/PDM system. Provides the audit trail entry point for the change record lifecycle.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the estimated cost impact value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CNY). Supports multi-currency financial reporting in global manufacturing operations.']
  disposition_action varchar [note: 'The required disposition for existing inventory or work-in-progress (WIP) stock of the affected item at the time the change is implemented. Determines whether existing stock should be used as-is, reworked to the new revision, scrapped, returned to supplier, retrofitted in the field, or held pending further review.']
  effectivity_date date [note: 'The calendar date from which the engineering change becomes effective for this affected item when date-based effectivity is used. Drives production scheduling, procurement cutover, and inventory disposition decisions in SAP S/4HANA MRP.']
  effectivity_serial_number varchar [note: 'The manufacturing serial number from which the engineering change becomes effective when serial-number-based effectivity is used. Ensures that units produced at or after this serial number incorporate the change, while earlier units retain the previous configuration.']
  effectivity_type varchar [note: 'Defines the method by which the engineering change becomes effective for this item. Date effectivity applies the change from a specific calendar date; serial number effectivity applies from a specific unit serial number; lot effectivity applies from a specific production lot; unit effectivity applies to specific unit numbers; immediate applies without a scheduled effectivity point.']
  estimated_cost_impact decimal(18,4) [note: 'The estimated financial impact (positive or negative) of implementing the engineering change on this affected item, expressed in the transaction currency. Negative values indicate cost savings; positive values indicate cost increases. Used for change approval financial justification.']
  form_fit_function_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the engineering change affects the form, fit, or function of the affected item. A true value triggers mandatory customer notification, PPAP re-submission, and regulatory re-evaluation workflows in accordance with customer-specific requirements and quality standards.']
  impact_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of the severity and scope of the engineering change impact on this affected item. Critical changes affect form, fit, or function and may require customer notification or regulatory re-approval; major changes affect performance or interchangeability; minor changes are non-functional improvements; cosmetic changes affect appearance only.']
  implementation_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the engineering change was physically implemented for this affected item in the production environment. Distinguishes the planned effectivity date from the actual execution date for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  implementation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the engineering change implementation for this specific affected item. Tracks the lifecycle from initial identification through verification, enabling program managers and engineers to monitor change execution progress across all affected items.']
  item_name varchar [note: 'The descriptive name or title of the affected item (e.g., part description, drawing title, document name) as recorded in the PLM/PDM system. Provides human-readable identification alongside the item number.']
  item_number varchar [note: 'The unique part number, document number, or identifier of the specific item affected by the engineering change, as maintained in the PLM/PDM or ERP system (SAP S/4HANA MM or Teamcenter). This is the primary business key for the affected object.']
  item_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the item affected by the engineering change. Indicates whether the affected object is a component, assembly, Bill of Materials (BOM), engineering drawing, manufacturing routing, controlled document, tooling, specification, software artifact, or firmware image.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this change affected item record was most recently updated in the PLM/PDM system. Supports change audit trails, data lineage tracking, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  ncr_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Non-Conformance Report (NCR) associated with this affected item change record. Links the engineering change to the specific quality non-conformance event that necessitated the design or process modification.']
  new_revision varchar [note: 'The revision identifier assigned to the affected item after the engineering change is implemented. Represents the updated configuration baseline and is used for release control and downstream BOM/routing updates.']
  old_revision varchar [note: 'The revision identifier (e.g., A, B, 01, REV-C) of the affected item prior to the engineering change being applied. Establishes the baseline from which the change is made and supports full revision history traceability.']
  owning_plant_code varchar [note: 'The SAP plant code or facility identifier of the manufacturing plant responsible for implementing the change on this affected item. Supports multi-plant change management and ensures the correct facility executes the change in global manufacturing operations.']
  parent_item_number varchar [note: 'The part number or identifier of the parent assembly or document that directly contains or references the affected item. Supports where-used analysis and impact assessment across the product structure when evaluating the scope of an engineering change.']
  planned_implementation_date date [note: 'The originally scheduled date for implementing the engineering change on this affected item. Used for program planning, resource scheduling, and on-time delivery tracking. Compared against the actual implementation date to measure change execution performance.']
  regulatory_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the engineering change to this affected item requires formal approval from an external regulatory body (e.g., CE Marking re-assessment, UL re-certification, RoHS/REACH compliance re-evaluation) before the change can be released to production.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the regulatory approval process for the engineering change on this affected item. Tracks whether external certification or compliance re-evaluation has been initiated, submitted to the authority, approved, or rejected.']
  responsible_engineer varchar [note: 'The name or employee identifier of the design or process engineer accountable for implementing and verifying the engineering change on this specific affected item. Establishes clear ownership for change execution and audit trail purposes.']
  safety_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the affected item is classified as safety-critical, requiring mandatory safety review, DFMEA/PFMEA update, and regulatory authority notification before the change can be implemented. Applies to items subject to CE Marking, UL certification, ISO 45001, or OSHA requirements.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence number defining the order in which affected items within the same ECN/ECO should be processed or implemented. Ensures correct sequencing of interdependent changes across multiple affected items within a single engineering change.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this change affected item record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and multi-system reconciliation in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.']
  verification_date date [note: 'The date on which verification of the engineering change was completed for this affected item. Used to confirm that the change has been validated before the item is released for full production use.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'The engineering verification method required to confirm that the change has been correctly implemented and meets design intent for this affected item. First Article Inspection (FAI) is commonly required for safety-critical or form-fit-function changes.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the verification activity for this affected item following change implementation. Indicates whether the required verification (test, inspection, FAI) has been completed successfully, is in progress, has failed, or has been formally waived.']

  Note: 'Association record linking an ECN or ECO to each specific item (component, BOM, drawing, routing, document) affected by the engineering change. Captures the affected item type, item identifier, old revision, new revision, change action (revise, obsolete, create), and implementation status. Enables full traceability of what changed, when, and why across the product structure.'
}

Table engineering.dfmea {
  dfmea_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the DFMEA record within the enterprise data platform. Serves as the primary key for all downstream references and linkages.']
  cad_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.cad_model. Business justification: Design FMEA analyzes failure modes of specific components and assemblies whose geometry is defined in CAD models. Linking dfmea to cad_model provides direct traceability from the design risk analysis ']
  ecn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: dfmea.ecn_reference is a STRING reference to the ECN that triggered or is associated with this DFMEA update. Replacing with FK ecn_id → ecn.ecn_id enforces change management traceability for design ri']
  engineering_bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_bom. Business justification: DFMEA is conducted at the assembly/sub-assembly level defined in the engineering BOM. Linking dfmea to engineering_bom identifies which product assembly the design risk analysis covers, enabling BOM-l']
  fmea_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.fmea. Business justification: Design FMEA is a specialized type of FMEA managed in quality systems. Engineering references the quality FMEA framework for risk assessment methodology and tracking.']
  product_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.product_specification. Business justification: DFMEA failure modes are directly tied to product specifications — a failure mode represents the inability to meet a specific functional or performance specification. Linking dfmea to product_specifica']
  action_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the recommended action was completed and verified. Used to measure APQP schedule adherence and close open DFMEA action items.']
  action_priority varchar [note: 'Action Priority classification (H=High, M=Medium, L=Low) per the AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook, used alongside RPN to determine urgency of recommended actions. High priority requires immediate action regardless of RPN value.']
  action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of the actual corrective or preventive action implemented to address the failure mode. Recorded after completion to close the DFMEA action loop and support CAPA documentation.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the engineering manager or quality authority who formally approved the DFMEA document for release.']
  apqp_phase varchar [note: 'The APQP phase during which this DFMEA was created or last updated. Phase 1=Plan and Define, Phase 2=Product Design and Development, Phase 3=Process Design and Development, Phase 4=Product and Process Validation, Phase 5=Feedback Assessment and Corrective Action.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the engineering site or manufacturing location where the design is being developed. Supports multinational regulatory compliance tracking (e.g., CE Marking for EU, UL for USA).']
  customer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external customer or internal program for which this DFMEA is being prepared. Relevant for PPAP submissions and customer-specific requirements (CSR).']
  design_control_detection varchar [note: 'Current design controls in place to detect the failure mode or cause before the product reaches the customer (e.g., \'prototype testing\', \'FEA simulation\', \'design review checklist\', \'First Article Inspection (FAI)\'). Addresses detection-type controls per AIAG-VDA Step 5.']
  design_control_prevention varchar [note: 'Current design controls in place to prevent the failure cause from occurring (e.g., \'design standard XYZ applied\', \'material specification review\', \'DFM analysis completed\'). Addresses prevention-type controls per AIAG-VDA Step 5.']
  detection_rating varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (1–10) assessing the ability of current design controls to detect the failure cause or failure mode before product release. A rating of 10 indicates no detection capability; 1 indicates near-certain detection.']
  failure_cause varchar [note: 'The specific design deficiency or root cause mechanism that leads to the failure mode (e.g., \'insufficient wall thickness\', \'incorrect material specification\', \'inadequate clearance between mating parts\'). Identified through DFMEA root cause analysis.']
  failure_effect varchar [note: 'The consequence of the failure mode on the next higher-level assembly, end customer, or end user. Describes what the customer experiences if the failure mode occurs (e.g., \'loss of motor control\', \'vehicle does not start\', \'operator injury\').']
  failure_mode varchar [note: 'The specific manner in which the design element could potentially fail to meet its intended function or design intent (e.g., \'fracture under load\', \'electrical short circuit\', \'corrosion of contact surface\'). Core DFMEA field per AIAG-VDA Step 3.']
  last_revised_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent revision to the DFMEA document. Tracks the currency of the risk assessment and supports document control requirements.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'The model year or product generation to which this DFMEA applies. Used for product lifecycle tracking and APQP milestone alignment.']
  number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned alphanumeric identifier for the DFMEA document, used for cross-referencing in APQP deliverables, PPAP packages, and Teamcenter PLM. Unique within the organization.']
  occurrence_rating varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (1–10) estimating the likelihood that the failure cause will occur and result in the failure mode. A rating of 10 indicates near-certain occurrence; 1 indicates extremely unlikely. Based on historical data, design similarity, and engineering judgment.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or engineering site responsible for the product design covered by this DFMEA. Supports multi-site and multinational operations.']
  ppap_submission_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this DFMEA is required as part of a PPAP submission package to the customer. When true, the DFMEA must meet customer-specific requirements (CSR) for PPAP level compliance.']
  preparation_date date [note: 'Date on which the DFMEA was initially prepared and documented. Used for APQP timeline tracking and document control.']
  prepared_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the engineer or analyst who originally prepared and authored the DFMEA document.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Specific corrective or preventive action recommended to reduce severity, occurrence, or detection ratings for high-priority failure modes. May include design changes, additional testing, material substitution, or tolerance revisions.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this DFMEA addresses failure modes related to regulatory compliance requirements such as RoHS, REACH, CE Marking, UL certification, or OSHA/EPA mandates. Triggers mandatory compliance review workflow.']
  responsible_engineer varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the design engineer responsible for implementing the recommended action and updating the DFMEA. Assigned per AIAG-VDA Step 6 accountability requirements.']
  revised_detection_rating varchar [note: 'Updated detection rating after implementation of recommended actions. Reflects improved detection capability post-mitigation.']
  revised_occurrence_rating varchar [note: 'Updated occurrence rating after implementation of recommended actions. Reflects the reduced likelihood of the failure cause occurring post-mitigation.']
  revised_rpn varchar [note: 'Recalculated Risk Priority Number (Revised S × Revised O × Revised D) after corrective actions have been implemented. Used to confirm risk reduction and validate DFMEA closure.']
  revised_severity_rating varchar [note: 'Updated severity rating after implementation of recommended actions. Reflects the residual risk level post-mitigation. Compared against original severity to demonstrate risk reduction effectiveness.']
  revision varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric revision identifier indicating the current version of the DFMEA document (e.g., A, B, C, 01, 02). Incremented upon each approved Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or design update.']
  rpn varchar [note: 'Risk Priority Number calculated as the product of Severity × Occurrence × Detection ratings (S × O × D). Ranges from 1 to 1000. Used to prioritize failure modes requiring corrective action. Note: AIAG-VDA also uses Action Priority (AP) as a complementary risk metric.']
  safety_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any failure mode in this DFMEA has a severity rating of 9 or 10, classifying it as safety-critical or regulatory-critical. Safety-critical DFMEAs require mandatory escalation and additional design validation per ISO 26262, IEC 61508, or applicable product safety standards.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the specific component, assembly, or system covered by this DFMEA, including functional boundaries and interfaces considered in the analysis.']
  scope_level varchar [note: 'Defines the hierarchical level of the design analysis scope: component (individual part), subsystem (grouped components), assembly (full assembly), or system (complete product system). Determines the depth and breadth of the FMEA analysis.']
  severity_rating varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (1–10) indicating the seriousness of the failure effect on the customer or end user. A rating of 10 indicates a safety-critical failure with no warning; 1 indicates no discernible effect. Defined per AIAG-VDA severity criteria.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the DFMEA record: draft (initial creation), in_review (under engineering review), approved (formally approved and baselined), closed (all actions completed), obsolete (superseded by a newer revision).']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the recommended action must be completed and verified. Used for APQP milestone tracking and PPAP readiness assessment.']
  teamcenter_document_code varchar [note: 'Unique document identifier assigned by Siemens Teamcenter PLM for the DFMEA document object. Enables direct traceability to the authoritative source document in the PLM/PDM system.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the DFMEA document, typically referencing the product, component, or assembly being analyzed (e.g., \'Motor Drive Assembly DFMEA – Electrification Module\').']

  Note: 'Design Failure Mode and Effects Analysis record capturing the structured risk assessment of potential design failures for a component or assembly. Records DFMEA number, revision, scope (component or system level), each failure mode, potential effect, severity rating, potential cause, occurrence rating, current design controls, detection rating, RPN (Risk Priority Number), recommended actions, and responsible engineer. Supports APQP and PPAP deliverables. Managed in Teamcenter and linked to quality domain.'
}

Table engineering.pfmea {
  pfmea_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying each PFMEA record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.']
  bop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.bop. Business justification: Process FMEA is conducted for a specific Bill of Process (manufacturing routing). Linking pfmea to bop at the header level provides traceability from the process risk analysis to the complete process ']
  ecn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: pfmea.ecn_reference is a STRING reference to the ECN associated with this PFMEA update. Replacing with FK ecn_id → ecn.ecn_id enforces change management traceability for process risk analysis. ecn_ref']
  fmea_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.fmea. Business justification: Process FMEA is a specialized FMEA type managed in quality systems. Manufacturing engineering uses quality FMEA structure for process risk analysis and mitigation tracking.']
  action_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the recommended action was completed and evidence of implementation was recorded. Used to close out action items in CAPA workflows and update the PFMEA with revised ratings.']
  action_priority varchar [note: 'AIAG-VDA 2019 Action Priority classification (High/Medium/Low) derived from the combination of Severity, Occurrence, and Detection ratings using the AP lookup table. Supersedes sole reliance on RPN for prioritization in the updated PFMEA methodology.']
  action_responsibility varchar [note: 'Name or role of the person or team responsible for implementing the recommended action. Used for accountability tracking in CAPA workflows and PFMEA review meetings.']
  action_target_date date [note: 'Target date by which the recommended action must be completed and verified. Monitored in SAP QM CAPA tracking and PFMEA review cycles to ensure timely risk mitigation.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the PFMEA document was formally approved and released for production use. Establishes the effective date for quality control plan alignment and PPAP submission records.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the quality or engineering authority who formally approved the PFMEA document for release. Required for PPAP Level 3/4 submission and customer-facing quality documentation.']
