  capa_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the linked Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record in SAP QM or the Quality Management System (QMS). Establishes traceability between the PFMEA risk item and the formal corrective action workflow.']
  control_plan_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the associated SPC Control Plan that incorporates the detection controls and inspection frequencies derived from this PFMEA. Links PFMEA outputs to Siemens Opcenter quality inspection plans.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the PFMEA record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage tracking, and PFMEA lifecycle reporting in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  current_detection_control varchar [note: 'Description of existing process controls designed to detect the failure mode or failure cause before the product reaches the next operation or customer (e.g., \'In-process CMM inspection\', \'Vision system check\', \'SPC control chart monitoring\').']
  current_prevention_control varchar [note: 'Description of existing process controls designed to prevent the failure cause or failure mode from occurring (e.g., \'Poka-Yoke fixture\', \'Operator SOP training\', \'Incoming material inspection\'). Supports Poka-Yoke and error-proofing documentation.']
  detection_rating varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (1–10) assessing the ability of current detection controls to identify the failure cause or failure mode before the product leaves the process. A rating of 1 indicates near-certain detection; 10 indicates no detection capability.']
  failure_cause varchar [note: 'Root cause or mechanism by which the failure mode could occur in the process. Describes the specific process deficiency, variation source, or error condition (e.g., \'Worn electrode tip\', \'Operator setup error\', \'Material hardness variation\').']
  failure_effect varchar [note: 'Description of the consequence or impact of the failure mode on the next operation, end customer, or product quality. Used to justify the severity rating and drive prioritization of corrective actions.']
  failure_mode varchar [note: 'Description of the specific way in which the process could potentially fail to meet its intended function or process requirement. Core element of the PFMEA risk assessment (e.g., \'Insufficient weld penetration\', \'Incorrect torque applied\').']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the PFMEA record. Supports change detection in ETL pipelines, audit compliance, and version control within the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  lead varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the lead process engineer responsible for authoring, maintaining, and updating the PFMEA document. Primary point of contact for PFMEA-related quality inquiries.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year or program year associated with the product or platform for which this PFMEA was developed. Used for APQP program tracking and PPAP submission documentation.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of the PFMEA to ensure it remains current with process changes, quality data trends, and engineering change orders. Supports proactive risk management per IATF 16949 requirements.']
  number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned document control number for the PFMEA, used for cross-referencing in quality management systems, Siemens Teamcenter PLM, and SAP QM. Follows the enterprise document numbering convention.']
  occurrence_rating varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (1–10) estimating the likelihood or frequency of the failure cause occurring during production. Based on historical process data, Statistical Process Control (SPC) data, or engineering judgment per AIAG-VDA criteria.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the manufacturing plant or facility where the process being analyzed is executed. Supports multi-site PFMEA management and plant-level quality reporting across global operations.']
  process_function varchar [note: 'The intended function or purpose of the process step being analyzed, describing what the operation is designed to achieve (e.g., \'Join two steel components with specified weld penetration depth\').']
  process_step varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the specific process step or sub-operation within the BoP operation being analyzed in this PFMEA record. Provides granular context for the failure mode analysis.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Specific corrective or preventive action recommended to reduce the severity, occurrence, or detection rating of the identified failure mode. Drives CAPA initiation in SAP QM and Siemens Opcenter quality workflows.']
  revised_detection_rating varchar [note: 'Updated detection rating (1–10) after implementation of the recommended action. Reflects improved detection capability resulting from new inspection methods, SPC controls, or automated detection systems.']
  revised_occurrence_rating varchar [note: 'Updated occurrence rating (1–10) after implementation of the recommended action. Reflects the reduced likelihood of the failure cause occurring following process improvements or error-proofing measures.']
  revised_rpn varchar [note: 'Recalculated Risk Priority Number (Revised S × Revised O × Revised D) after completion of recommended actions. Demonstrates risk reduction effectiveness and supports PFMEA closure criteria.']
  revised_severity_rating varchar [note: 'Updated severity rating (1–10) after implementation of the recommended action. Reflects the residual risk level post-mitigation and is used to recalculate the revised RPN for the PFMEA record.']
  revision varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric revision identifier indicating the current version of the PFMEA document. Incremented upon each approved Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or process change that impacts the risk assessment.']
  rpn varchar [note: 'Risk Priority Number calculated as the product of Severity × Occurrence × Detection ratings (range 1–1000). Used to prioritize failure modes requiring corrective action. High RPN values trigger mandatory CAPA initiation per quality management policy.']
  severity_rating varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (1–10) assessing the seriousness of the failure effect on the customer or next process. A rating of 10 indicates a safety or regulatory compliance impact. Defined per AIAG-VDA severity evaluation criteria.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the PFMEA document. Controls workflow routing in Siemens Teamcenter PLM and determines whether the PFMEA is active for production use in Siemens Opcenter MES quality inspection plans.']
  team_members varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of names or employee IDs of cross-functional team members who participated in the PFMEA development session. Documents team composition for APQP and PPAP submission requirements.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the PFMEA document, typically referencing the process, product family, or manufacturing operation being analyzed (e.g., \'Welding Process PFMEA – Automation Frame Assembly\').']
  work_center varchar [note: 'SAP work center or Opcenter resource identifier where the manufacturing operation is performed. Provides traceability from the PFMEA risk item to the physical production resource on the shop floor.']

  Note: 'Process Failure Mode and Effects Analysis record capturing the structured risk assessment of potential process failures for each manufacturing operation in the Bill of Process. Records PFMEA number, revision, linked BOP operation, failure mode, potential effect on product quality, severity, potential cause, occurrence, current process controls, detection rating, RPN, recommended actions, and CAPA linkage. Feeds SPC control plans and Opcenter quality inspection plans.'
}

Table engineering.plm_lifecycle_state {
  plm_lifecycle_state_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each authorized lifecycle state record within the Teamcenter PLM governance framework.']
  lifecycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.lifecycle. Business justification: PLM lifecycle states in engineering must map to the commercial product lifecycle (introduction, growth, maturity, end-of-life). This synchronization drives release gates, obsolescence planning, and af']
  allowed_transitions varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of target state codes to which a PLM object may transition from this state (e.g., \'IN_REVIEW|RELEASED|OBSOLETE\'). Defines the authorized state machine edges for workflow enforcement.']
  applicable_object_types varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of PLM-managed object types to which this lifecycle state applies (e.g., \'Component|BOM|Drawing|CAD_Model|Document\'). Governs which object classes can be assigned this state.']
  apqp_phase_alignment varchar [note: 'Maps this lifecycle state to the corresponding APQP phase, enabling traceability between PLM object maturity and the formal product quality planning process used for new product introduction.']
  color_code_hex varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal color code used to visually represent this lifecycle state in PLM dashboards, engineering portals, and reporting tools. Supports intuitive visual differentiation of product data maturity stages.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lifecycle state configuration record was first created in the data product. Provides audit trail for governance and compliance purposes.']
  downstream_system_trigger varchar [note: 'Identifies downstream operational systems that are triggered upon a PLM object entering this lifecycle state (e.g., \'SAP_MM_Material_Master|SAP_PP_BOM|Opcenter_MES\'). Supports IT/OT convergence and PLM-ERP integration governance.']
  ecn_required_for_entry boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a valid Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) must be referenced before a PLM object can be transitioned into this lifecycle state. Enforces formal change control governance.']
  ecn_required_for_exit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a valid Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) must be referenced to transition a PLM object out of this lifecycle state. Typically applies to released or frozen states.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this lifecycle state configuration is valid and available for use in PLM workflows. Supports versioned governance of lifecycle state definitions over time.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date after which this lifecycle state configuration is no longer valid for new PLM object assignments. Null indicates the state is currently active with no planned expiry.']
  export_control_check_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an export control classification review (e.g., EAR, ITAR, dual-use goods assessment) must be completed before a PLM object can be transitioned into this lifecycle state.']
  icon_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path for the icon image associated with this lifecycle state in the PLM user interface. Used for visual representation in Teamcenter and integrated engineering portals.']
  is_frozen_state boolean [note: 'Indicates whether PLM objects in this state are frozen (read-only) and cannot be directly modified. Changes must be initiated via an Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) process.']
  is_initial_state boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lifecycle state is the default starting state assigned to newly created PLM objects. Only one state per applicable object type should be flagged as the initial state.']
  is_released_state boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lifecycle state constitutes a formal release milestone, meaning the PLM object is authorized for use in production, procurement, or downstream processes such as BOM activation or MES execution.']
  is_terminal_state boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lifecycle state is a terminal (end) state from which no further forward transitions are permitted (e.g., Obsolete, Archived). Terminal states represent the end of the active product data lifecycle.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code for the locale in which the state_name and state_description are expressed (e.g., \'en\', \'de\', \'zh-CN\'). Supports multinational PLM deployments with multi-language state label requirements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this lifecycle state configuration record. Used for change tracking, data lineage, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  maturity_level varchar [note: 'Standardized maturity level classification associated with this lifecycle state, indicating the degree of completeness and validation of the product data. Used for portfolio-level maturity reporting and APQP phase alignment.']
  max_dwell_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of calendar days a PLM object is permitted to remain in this lifecycle state before an escalation or alert is triggered. Supports governance of stale or stuck product data records.']
  minimum_approver_count varchar [note: 'Minimum number of distinct approvers required to authorize a transition into this lifecycle state. Supports multi-signatory approval requirements for safety-critical or regulatory-relevant product data.']
  notification_recipient_roles varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of organizational roles that receive automated notifications upon a PLM object entering this lifecycle state (e.g., \'Procurement|Manufacturing_Engineering|Quality_Assurance\').']
  notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automated notifications must be sent to stakeholders (e.g., engineering team, quality, procurement) when a PLM object transitions into this lifecycle state.']
  plm_workflow_template_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Teamcenter PLM workflow template associated with transitions into this lifecycle state. Identifies the formal approval and review workflow process definition used to govern state entry.']
  ppap_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) submission is required as a prerequisite for transitioning a PLM object into this lifecycle state. Relevant for states representing production readiness.']
  quality_gate_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal quality gate review (e.g., APQP phase gate, design review, DFMEA completion check) must be passed before a PLM object can enter this lifecycle state.']
  regulatory_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether regulatory compliance review (e.g., CE Marking, RoHS/REACH, UL certification, export control classification) must be completed before a PLM object can enter this lifecycle state.']
  required_approval_roles varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of organizational roles or job functions that must provide approval before a PLM object can enter this lifecycle state (e.g., \'Design_Engineer|Quality_Manager|Chief_Engineer\'). Enforces the approval governance matrix.']
  sequence_order varchar [note: 'Numeric ordering value that defines the canonical progression sequence of lifecycle states from concept through obsolescence. Used for sorting and maturity progression reporting.']
  sla_transition_days varchar [note: 'Target number of business days within which a PLM object should complete the review and approval process to transition out of this lifecycle state. Used for engineering workflow performance monitoring against SLA targets.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this lifecycle state configuration was sourced (e.g., \'Teamcenter\'). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  state_category varchar [note: 'High-level category grouping lifecycle states for reporting and analytics. Enables aggregation of states into broader maturity phases (e.g., all review-type states grouped under \'review\').']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the lifecycle state within the PLM system (e.g., CONCEPT, IN_REVIEW, RELEASED, OBSOLETE). Used as the business key for state lookups and workflow routing.']
  state_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the lifecycle state, including its business meaning, entry criteria, and the maturity level it represents within the product data governance framework.']
  state_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the lifecycle state as displayed in the Teamcenter PLM user interface and engineering reports (e.g., \'Concept\', \'Under Review\', \'Released for Production\', \'Obsolete\').']
  status varchar [note: 'Operational status of this lifecycle state configuration record. \'Active\' states are available for assignment to PLM objects; \'Inactive\' states are suspended; \'Deprecated\' states are retained for historical reference only.']
  teamcenter_policy_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Teamcenter PLM lifecycle policy (policy object) under which this state is defined. A lifecycle policy groups related states and transition rules for a specific object type family.']
  teamcenter_state_uid varchar [note: 'Native Siemens Teamcenter PLM system unique identifier (UID) for this lifecycle state object. Used for cross-system traceability and integration with Teamcenter APIs and downstream PLM-ERP interfaces.']

  Note: 'Reference record defining the authorized lifecycle states and state transition rules for PLM-managed objects (components, BOMs, drawings, CAD models) within the Teamcenter PLM workflow. Captures state code, state name, allowed transitions, required approvals, and applicable object types. Provides the governance framework for product data maturity progression from concept through obsolescence.'
}

Table engineering.substitute_component {
  substitute_component_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for the substitute component association record in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  bom_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.bom_line. Business justification: A substitute component is defined as an approved alternate for a specific primary component in the BOM. Linking substitute_component to bom_line identifies the exact BOM line item (primary component p']
  ecn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: substitute_component.ecn_number is a STRING reference to the ECN that authorized this substitution. Replacing with FK ecn_id → ecn.ecn_id enforces change management traceability for approved substitut']
  engineering_bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.bom. Business justification: substitute_component.applicable_bom_number is a STRING reference to the BOM where this substitution is applicable. Replacing with FK bom_id → bom.bom_id establishes the authoritative substitution-to-B']
  material_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.material_specification. Business justification: Substitute components must meet the same or equivalent material specifications as the primary component they replace. Linking substitute_component to material_specification provides traceability from ']
  substitution_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.substitution. Business justification: Engineering-approved substitute components must align with commercial substitution rules managed in the product domain. Procurement and production use this link to validate that engineering substitute']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Substitute components are typically sourced from alternative suppliers. Engineering\'s substitute component record must reference the procurement supplier master so buyers can immediately source the su']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the authorized engineer or manager who approved this substitute component association for use in production.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the substitute component association was formally approved, providing an audit trail for quality management and regulatory compliance.']
  bom_usage varchar [note: 'Specifies the BOM usage context in which this substitution is applicable, aligning with SAP BOM usage categories (e.g., production BOM, engineering BOM, costing BOM).']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the standard cost delta value, supporting multi-currency operations across global manufacturing sites.']
  cost_impact_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates the directional cost impact of using the substitute component compared to the primary component, supporting cost management and COGS analysis without storing calculated values.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this substitute component association record was first created in the system, providing a full audit trail for data governance and compliance purposes.']
  export_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the substitute component is subject to export control regulations (e.g., EAR, ITAR), which may restrict its use in certain geographies or customer applications and require additional compliance review.']
  form_fit_function_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the substitute component is a true form, fit, and function equivalent to the primary component, meaning it can be used without any design or process modification. Critical for engineering change and quality management decisions.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this substitute component association record was most recently updated, supporting change tracking, delta processing, and audit compliance.']
  lead_time_delta_days varchar [note: 'The difference in procurement or manufacturing lead time in days between the substitute component and the primary component (substitute lead time minus primary lead time). Negative values indicate faster availability. Used in MRP scheduling and supply chain resilience planning.']
  mrp_relevance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this substitute component record is active and visible to the MRP engine for automatic exception handling and alternate material selection during planning runs.']
  originating_department varchar [note: 'The business department that originated the substitute component request, used for workflow routing, accountability tracking, and cross-functional reporting.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or site where this substitute component association is valid. Substitutions may be plant-specific due to local supplier availability or process constraints.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric rank indicating the preference order when multiple substitutes exist for the same primary component. Lower values indicate higher preference (1 = most preferred). Used by MRP to sequence substitute selection.']
  quantity_conversion_factor decimal(18,6) [note: 'Multiplier applied to the original BOM quantity when using the substitute component. A value of 1.0 means the substitute is used in the same quantity; values other than 1.0 indicate a quantity adjustment is required (e.g., 2.0 means twice as many substitutes are needed).']
  record_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the substitute component association record, used for cross-system referencing and audit trails.']
  regulatory_compliance_impact varchar [note: 'Indicates whether using this substitute component triggers a regulatory compliance review, such as RoHS, REACH, CE Marking, or UL certification re-assessment. Ensures regulatory obligations are not inadvertently violated during substitution.']
  requested_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the person who initiated the request to establish this substitute component association, typically a supply chain planner, procurement specialist, or design engineer.']
  requires_engineering_approval boolean [note: 'Indicates whether use of this substitute component requires explicit engineering sign-off prior to production use, beyond the standard approval captured in the status field.']
  requires_quality_revalidation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether use of this substitute component triggers a quality revalidation activity such as First Article Inspection (FAI), updated control plan, or re-qualification testing before production release.']
  restriction_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes describing any restrictions, limitations, or special handling instructions associated with using this substitute component, including regulatory, safety, or performance constraints.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this substitute component association record was sourced, supporting data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  source_system_key varchar [note: 'The natural or surrogate key of this record in the originating source system (e.g., SAP substitute material record key), enabling reverse lookup and delta processing in the Silver Layer ingestion pipeline.']
  standard_cost_delta decimal(18,4) [note: 'The difference in standard cost per unit between the substitute component and the primary component (substitute cost minus primary cost). Negative values indicate cost savings. Used for financial impact assessment and BOM costing.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle and approval status of the substitute component record, governing whether it may be used in production planning and Material Requirements Planning (MRP) runs.']
  substitution_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code explaining why this substitute component was identified and approved. Supports root cause analysis, supply chain resilience reporting, and procurement strategy decisions.']
  substitution_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the substitution relationship: \'full\' indicates the substitute is interchangeable in all contexts; \'conditional\' requires specific conditions to be met; \'temporary\' is time-bounded; \'emergency\' is approved only for urgent supply disruptions.']
  teamcenter_item_code varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for this substitute component association record in Siemens Teamcenter PLM, enabling cross-system traceability between the Databricks Silver Layer and the PLM system of record.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure applicable to the substitute component quantity, which may differ from the primary component\'s unit of measure when a conversion is required.']
  use_condition varchar [note: 'Free-text or structured description of the specific conditions under which this substitute may be used, such as \'only when primary component stock is zero\', \'applicable for customer orders above 100 units\', or \'valid only for non-safety-critical assemblies\'.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this substitute component association becomes effective and may be used in production planning and BOM resolution.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'The date after which this substitute component association expires and is no longer valid for use. Null indicates no expiry. Used to manage temporary substitutions and time-bounded approvals.']

  Note: 'Association record defining approved substitute or alternate components that can replace a primary component in a BOM when the primary is unavailable or obsolete. Captures the primary component reference, substitute component reference, substitution type (full, conditional, temporary), applicable BOM context, approval status, and any use restrictions or quantity adjustments. Supports MRP exception handling and supply chain resilience.'
}

Table engineering.component_tooling_assignment {
  component_tooling_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the component_tooling_assignment association']
  bom_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the BOM line item (component) that requires this tooling assignment.']
  tooling_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the tooling or equipment master record assigned to this component.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this component-tooling assignment is valid for production use. Aligns with engineering change effectivity and PPAP approval windows. Belongs to the assignment, not to the BOM line or tool master independently.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The date after which this component-tooling assignment is no longer valid. Used to manage tool retirement, design changes, and supersession. Belongs to the assignment relationship.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where this specific component-tooling assignment applies. The same component may require different tools at different plants. Belongs to the assignment because it scopes the pairing to a specific production site.']
  setup_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric order in which this tool must be set up relative to other tools required for the same BOM component. Belongs to the assignment because sequence is specific to the component-tool pairing, not to the tool or component alone.']
  usage_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the functional role of the tool in manufacturing this component (e.g., FIXTURE for holding, CUTTING_TOOL for machining, GAUGE for inspection). Belongs to the assignment because the same tool may serve different roles for different components.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Assignment relationship between a BOM line item (component) and a tooling/equipment master record. It captures which specific tools are required to manufacture a given component, including the role of each tool, its setup sequence, effectivity window, and the plant where the assignment applies. Each record links one bom_line to one tooling_equipment with attributes that exist only in the context of this component-tool pairing. Supports PPAP qualification, tooling planning, and MRP tooling requirement explosion.. Existence Justification: In industrial manufacturing, a BOM line item (component) genuinely requires multiple specialized tools simultaneously — e.g., a machined part needs a fixture, a cutting tool, and a gauge — while each tool is shared across many different BOM components across different assemblies and plants. This is an operationally managed relationship: engineering teams actively create, update, and retire component-tooling assignments as part of PPAP qualification, tooling planning, and production readiness. The relationship carries its own data (usage type, setup sequence, effectivity dates, plant scope) that belongs to neither the BOM line nor the tool master alone.'
}

Table order.quotation {
  quotation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a commercial quotation record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.']
  blanket_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.blanket_order. Business justification: Quotations may be issued under a blanket order framework. The string contract_number is replaced by FK blanket_order_id.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.channel. Business justification: Commercial quotations are issued through specific sales channels. The channel reference table captures the full channel definition including SLA hours, credit check requirements, commission rates, and']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Quotations are addressed to specific contacts at customer accounts. Sales teams use this to direct quotes to the right decision-maker and track engagement.']
  currency_exchange_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.currency_exchange_rate. Business justification: Quotations for international manufacturing customers are issued in customer currency. The exchange rate at quotation time must be locked and referenced for accurate price-to-order conversion and finan']
  discount_structure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.discount_structure. Business justification: Quotations apply a negotiated discount structure (volume, channel, customer tier). Sales and finance use this link to validate that discounts on quotes comply with approved structures and margin thres']
  pricing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.pricing_agreement. Business justification: Quotations reference existing pricing agreements to provide consistent pricing. Sales teams use this to generate accurate quotes based on negotiated customer terms.']
  rfq_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.rfq_request. Business justification: A quotation is issued in response to an RFQ/RFP. The string rfq_reference_number is replaced by FK rfq_request_id, establishing the quote-to-RFQ traceability.']
  sales_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sales_org. Business justification: Quotations are issued by a specific sales organization which governs product availability, pricing, and terms. Sales teams use this to ensure the correct product catalog and pricing rules are applied ']
  sales_price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.price_list. Business justification: Quotations reference price lists to determine base pricing before discounts. Sales teams select appropriate price list based on customer type, region, and contract terms.']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.service_contract. Business justification: Quotations for industrial equipment services or parts are frequently issued under an existing service contract that governs pricing, SLAs, and scope. Sales teams reference the service contract on the ']
  tax_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.tax_code. Business justification: Quotations must include applicable tax codes for accurate total pricing presented to customers. Finance and sales operations use this link to validate tax compliance before quotation approval.']
  accepted_rejected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the customer formally accepted or rejected the quotation. Used to calculate quotation cycle time and measure sales velocity.']
  competitor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the competing supplier identified during the quotation process. Populated when the win/loss reason involves a competitor selection. Used for competitive intelligence and market share analysis.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'Delivery date confirmed by manufacturing/supply chain based on ATP or CTP availability check. Represents the committed delivery commitment communicated to the customer in the quotation.']
  converted_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP sales order number created when this quotation was accepted and converted. Populated only when status is \'converted\'. Enables end-to-end order-to-cash traceability.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary values on this quotation are expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency reporting and FX conversion.']
  customer_account_number varchar [note: 'SAP customer account number (sold-to party) associated with this quotation. Links the quotation to the customer master record for credit, pricing, and order management purposes.']
  customer_name varchar [note: 'Legal or trading name of the customer or prospect to whom the quotation is addressed. Captured at quotation time to preserve the commercial record even if the customer master is subsequently updated.']
  delivery_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days from order placement to expected delivery as quoted to the customer. Derived from production scheduling and logistics planning for the specific fulfillment mode.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall header-level discount percentage applied to the quotation net value. Captures the commercial discount negotiated with the customer for margin analysis and pricing governance.']
  division varchar [note: 'SAP division code representing the product line or business unit responsible for the quoted products. Used for sales area determination and reporting segmentation.']
  gross_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross value of the quotation before application of discounts, representing the sum of all line item list prices. Used for discount analysis and margin reporting.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the transfer of risk, cost responsibility, and delivery obligations between seller and buyer for the quoted shipment.']
  incoterms_location varchar [note: 'Named place or port associated with the Incoterms code, specifying the exact point of risk and cost transfer (e.g., \'FOB Shanghai Port\'). Required for logistics and insurance documentation.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the quotation was formally issued and transmitted to the customer or prospect.']
  net_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total net commercial value of the quotation after all discounts and surcharges but before taxes. Represents the revenue-recognizable amount if the quotation is accepted and converted to a sales order.']
  number varchar [note: 'Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the quotation as assigned by SAP S/4HANA SD. Used in all customer-facing documents, correspondence, and cross-system references.']
  opportunity_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this quotation to the originating sales opportunity in Salesforce CRM. Supports opportunity-to-order conversion tracking and win/loss analysis.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Agreed payment terms code specifying the due date calculation and any early payment discount conditions (e.g., Net 30, 2/10 Net 30). Sourced from SAP payment terms configuration.']
  probability_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Sales representative\'s estimated probability (0–100%) that this quotation will be accepted and converted to a sales order. Used for pipeline forecasting and revenue planning.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Customer-requested delivery date as stated in the RFQ/RFP. Used for Available to Promise (ATP) and Capable to Promise (CTP) checks and delivery lead time commitment.']
  sales_office varchar [note: 'SAP sales office code identifying the geographic or organizational unit from which the quotation is issued. Supports regional sales performance reporting.']
  sales_representative varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the sales representative responsible for preparing and managing this quotation. Used for commission calculation, workload reporting, and customer relationship management.']
  shipping_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the customer\'s ship-to destination. Used for export control screening, customs documentation, and logistics routing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this quotation record was sourced. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements in the Silver layer.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the quotation. Drives workflow routing, reporting, and order-to-cash pipeline visibility. \'converted\' indicates the quotation has been turned into a sales order.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the quotation was formally submitted to the customer. Used for response time SLA measurement and sales process analytics.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount (VAT, GST, sales tax) applicable to the quotation as calculated by the tax determination procedure. Required for statutory invoicing and tax reporting.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the quotation by commercial structure. Standard quotations cover single transactions; framework quotations establish pricing for repeat orders; blanket quotations cover volume commitments; project quotations support ETO/large capital projects; spot quotations address one-time urgent requirements.']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date through which the quoted prices, terms, and conditions remain valid for customer acceptance. After this date the quotation status transitions to \'expired\'.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date from which the quoted prices, terms, and conditions are commercially binding and valid for customer acceptance.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version counter incremented each time the quotation is revised and reissued to the customer. Version 1 represents the initial issue; subsequent revisions increment the counter.']
  win_loss_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing additional context for the win or loss outcome beyond the structured reason code. Captured by the sales representative for qualitative pipeline analysis.']
  win_loss_reason varchar [note: 'Categorized reason code explaining why the quotation was accepted (won) or rejected (lost). Critical input for sales analytics, pricing strategy, and competitive intelligence reporting.']

  Note: 'Commercial quotation issued to a customer or prospect in response to an RFQ/RFP. Captures quotation number, validity start and end dates, customer reference, quoted products and configurations, pricing conditions, discount structures, delivery lead times, payment terms, incoterms, quotation status (draft, submitted, accepted, rejected, expired), win/loss reason, linked opportunity reference, and sales representative. Supports MTO, ETO, and ATO quoting workflows. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD quotation module.'
}

Table order.quotation_line_item {
  quotation_line_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each individual line item within a commercial quotation. Serves as the primary key for the quotation_line_item entity.']
  discount_structure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.discount_structure. Business justification: Individual quotation line items in complex industrial deals may carry line-level discount structures (e.g., product-family discounts). Sales controllers validate per-line discounts against approved st']
  product_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_configuration. Business justification: Quotation line items are built around specific product configurations. Sales engineers reference product configurations when preparing quotes to ensure accurate pricing and feasibility for configured ']
  quotation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.quotation. Business justification: quotation_line_item is a child of quotation. No existing quotation_id or quotation_number string key is visible in quotation_line_item attributes, so a new FK quotation_id is added to establish the pa']
  uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: Quotation line items require a unit of measure for accurate quantity and pricing. Sales teams use this to align quoted quantities with product catalog UOM definitions before sending to customers.']
  atp_ctp_method varchar [note: 'Method used to confirm the delivery date for this line item: Available-to-Promise (ATP) based on existing stock, Capable-to-Promise (CTP) based on production capacity, MTO lead time calculation, or manually set by sales.']
  configuration_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable summary of the product configuration options selected for this line item, including key technical specifications, options, and features. Supports customer-facing quotation documentation.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'Internally confirmed delivery date for this line item based on Available-to-Promise (ATP) or Capable-to-Promise (CTP) checks. Represents the committed delivery commitment to the customer.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the quoted product is manufactured or assembled. Required for customs declarations, import/export compliance, and trade preference programs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this quotation line item record was created in the system. Used for audit trail, SLA tracking, and quotation turnaround time analysis.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the line item pricing is expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency quotation management for global operations.']
  customs_tariff_number varchar [note: 'Harmonized System (HS) tariff classification number for the quoted product, used for customs declarations, import duty calculation, and trade compliance. Required for cross-border transactions.']
  delivery_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Committed or estimated number of calendar days from order confirmation to delivery for this specific line item. Reflects production lead time, material availability, and logistics scheduling.']
  discount_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the list price to arrive at the quoted net price for this line item. Reflects customer-specific pricing agreements, volume discounts, or promotional pricing.']
  engineering_change_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) applicable to the quoted material at the time of quotation. Ensures the correct revision level is quoted and traceable.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quoted product is classified as a hazardous material requiring special handling, packaging, labeling, or transport documentation under applicable regulations.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between seller and buyer for this line item. Governs logistics and insurance responsibilities.']
  item_category varchar [note: 'SAP SD item category code that controls the processing behavior of this line item, including billing relevance, delivery relevance, pricing, and scheduling. Examples: TAN (standard), TAK (consignment), TAP (third-party).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this quotation line item record was last updated. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and data freshness monitoring in the Silver layer.']
  line_net_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total net value of this line item, calculated as quoted net price multiplied by quoted quantity. Represents the commercial value of this specific line in the quotation.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential position number of this line item within the parent quotation. Used to order and reference individual items within a multi-line quotation document.']
  list_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard published catalog or list price per unit of measure for the quoted material, before any discounts or surcharges are applied. Represents the baseline pricing reference.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Short descriptive text for the material or product being quoted on this line item, as maintained in the SAP material master. Provides human-readable identification of the quoted item.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'SAP material master identifier (SKU) for the product, component, or system being quoted on this line. Links to the material master record in SAP S/4HANA MM module.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or distribution center responsible for fulfilling this line item. Determines production scheduling, inventory sourcing, and shipping origin.']
  pricing_date date [note: 'Reference date used to determine applicable pricing conditions, exchange rates, and discount agreements for this line item. Pricing conditions valid on this date are applied.']
  product_group varchar [note: 'Classification grouping for the quoted material, used for pricing, reporting, and sales analysis. Corresponds to the SAP material group or product hierarchy node.']
  quoted_net_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net unit price offered to the customer on this line item after all applicable discounts, surcharges, and pricing conditions have been applied. This is the contractually binding price per unit.']
  quoted_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of the material or product being quoted on this line item, expressed in the specified unit of measure. Basis for pricing and delivery planning.']
  reason_for_rejection varchar [note: 'Reason code or description explaining why this specific line item was rejected or removed from the quotation. Used for win/loss analysis and sales process improvement.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Customer-requested delivery date for this line item as specified in the RFQ or quotation request. Used as the baseline for ATP/CTP commitment checks.']
  rohs_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quoted product complies with the EU Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) Directive, restricting the use of specific hazardous materials in electrical and electronic equipment.']
  sales_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure used for sales and pricing purposes on this line item, which may differ from the base unit of measure in the material master (e.g., selling in sets while stocking in pieces).']
  sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit code representing the specific product variant being quoted, including configuration and packaging distinctions. Used for inventory and fulfillment planning.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of this quotation line item within the order-to-cash workflow. Tracks the lifecycle from initial creation through approval, conversion to sales order, or rejection.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'SAP storage location within the plant from which the quoted material will be sourced or shipped. Used for inventory reservation and warehouse management.']
  sub_line_number varchar [note: 'Secondary sequence number used to identify sub-items or component breakdowns within a parent line item, supporting hierarchical line item structures common in Engineer-to-Order (ETO) and complex system quotations.']
  surcharge_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage surcharge applied to the base price for this line item, covering special handling, expedite fees, hazardous material handling, or custom engineering premiums.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Calculated tax amount applicable to this line item based on the tax code and line net value. Supports tax reporting and compliance with local fiscal regulations.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code applicable to this line item, determining the applicable VAT, GST, or sales tax rate based on the product type and destination country. Used for tax calculation and compliance reporting.']
  technical_spec_summary varchar [note: 'Condensed summary of the key technical specifications for the product or system being quoted on this line item, including performance parameters, ratings, and compliance certifications relevant to the customer\'s application.']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'Expiration date after which the pricing and terms on this quotation line item are no longer valid. Customer must accept or request renewal before this date.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the pricing and terms on this quotation line item are valid. Defines the start of the quotation validity window for this specific line.']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a commercial quotation detailing a specific product, system, or component being quoted. Captures line number, material/SKU, quoted quantity, unit of measure, list price, quoted net price, discount percentage, delivery lead time, configuration options, technical specifications summary, and line item status. Enables granular pricing and configuration tracking at the item level for complex industrial manufacturing quotations.'
}

Table order.rfq_request {
  rfq_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying each inbound RFQ or RFP record in the order domain. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.campaign. Business justification: RFQs in industrial manufacturing are frequently triggered by marketing campaigns (trade shows, product launches). Sales ops tracks which campaign sourced the RFQ to measure campaign ROI and pipeline c']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.channel. Business justification: Inbound RFQ/RFP requests arrive through specific sales channels (direct, distributor, portal, EDI). The source_channel STRING attribute is a denormalized channel identifier that is superseded by the F']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: RFQ requests come from specific contacts at customer organizations. Sales teams use this to respond directly to the requesting person and maintain communication history.']
  priority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_priority. Business justification: RFQ requests carry a priority level for response urgency. The string priority is replaced by FK order_priority_id.']
  product_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.product_specification. Business justification: RFQ requests in industrial manufacturing are issued against a defined product specification. Procurement and engineering jointly use this link to ensure suppliers quote against the correct technical r']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_territory. Business justification: RFQs originate from a specific sales territory in manufacturing. Territory managers review and route RFQs; linking ensures correct team ownership, quota attribution, and regional reporting.']
  application_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the end-use application domain for which the requested products or systems are intended. Drives product line routing, engineering specialization assignment, and market segment reporting.']
  assigned_sales_engineer varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the sales engineer or application engineer responsible for managing the RFQ/RFP response. Drives accountability, workload tracking, and commission attribution.']
  assigned_sales_office varchar [note: 'The sales office or regional sales unit responsible for managing the customer relationship and RFQ response. Used for territory management, revenue attribution, and regional performance reporting.']
  awarded_date date [note: 'The date on which the customer formally awarded the contract or purchase order to the company following the RFQ/RFP evaluation. Marks the transition from pre-sales to order management.']
  budget_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the budget indication amount provided by the customer. Supports multi-currency commercial operations across global markets.']
  budget_indication_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The customer\'s indicated or estimated budget for the project or procurement as disclosed in the RFQ/RFP. Used for commercial feasibility assessment, pricing strategy, and win probability scoring.']
  competitor_names varchar [note: 'Names of known competing suppliers or vendors also invited to respond to the RFQ/RFP. Used for competitive intelligence, bid strategy, and win/loss analysis.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Customer-specified regulatory, safety, or certification requirements that the supplied products or systems must meet, such as CE Marking, UL certification, RoHS/REACH compliance, or IEC standards. Drives product compliance validation.']
  confidentiality_agreement_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer requires a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) or confidentiality agreement to be in place before technical details of the RFQ/RFP can be shared internally or with subcontractors.']
  country_of_delivery varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the destination country where the ordered goods or systems are to be delivered. Drives export compliance checks, trade regulation screening, and logistics planning.']
  customer_reference_number varchar [note: 'The customer\'s own internal document or project reference number as provided on the RFQ/RFP submission. Used for cross-referencing with the customer\'s procurement system and for communication alignment.']
  decline_reason varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code explaining why the RFQ/RFP was declined, either by the company (no-bid decision) or by the customer (lost to competitor). Used for win/loss analysis and continuous improvement.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the customer is engaging for this RFQ, such as direct sales, distributor, OEM partner, or system integrator. Affects pricing tiers, discount structures, and margin reporting.']
  estimated_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Internal estimate of the total contract value if the RFQ/RFP is won, used for pipeline reporting, revenue forecasting, and resource allocation decisions. Distinct from the customer\'s budget indication.']
  evaluation_criteria varchar [note: 'Description of the criteria the customer will use to evaluate and select a supplier response, such as price, technical compliance, delivery lead time, quality certifications, or after-sales support. Informs bid strategy and proposal emphasis.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) applicable to the requested delivery, defining the transfer of risk and responsibility between seller and buyer. Used for logistics planning, insurance, and contract terms.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent update to the RFQ record, capturing any changes to status, assignments, or commercial data. Used for data freshness tracking, audit compliance, and change detection in the Silver layer.']
  nda_signed_date date [note: 'The date on which the Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) or confidentiality agreement was formally signed between the company and the customer for this RFQ/RFP. Required before sharing proprietary technical information.']
  payment_terms_requested varchar [note: 'The payment terms requested or indicated by the customer in the RFQ/RFP, such as net 30, net 60, or milestone-based payments. Used for credit assessment and commercial proposal structuring.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'The name of the customer\'s project or initiative for which the RFQ/RFP is being issued. Provides business context for the request and is used for opportunity tracking and pipeline reporting.']
  quantity_required decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total quantity of units, systems, or assemblies requested by the customer in the RFQ/RFP. Used for capacity planning, ATP/CTP checks, and commercial pricing calculations.']
  quantity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure associated with the requested quantity (e.g., EA for each, SET for a system set, KG for weight-based items). Ensures accurate commercial and production planning interpretation.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which the RFQ/RFP was received and registered in the system. Used for SLA compliance measurement, response time analytics, and audit trail.']
  required_delivery_date date [note: 'The date by which the customer requires delivery of the ordered goods or systems. Used for Available-to-Promise (ATP) and Capable-to-Promise (CTP) checks and production scheduling feasibility assessment.']
  responded_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which the formal quotation or proposal response was submitted to the customer. Used to measure response cycle time and SLA adherence.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which the customer requires a formal quotation or proposal response. Drives prioritization of sales and engineering resources and triggers escalation workflows when approaching.']
  rfp_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the inbound request is a formal Request for Proposal (RFP) requiring a detailed technical and commercial proposal, as opposed to a standard Request for Quotation (RFQ) focused on pricing. True = RFP; False = RFQ.']
  rfq_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing alphanumeric identifier assigned to the inbound RFQ or RFP document, used for cross-system reference and customer communication. Corresponds to the customer-provided document number or internally assigned tracking number.']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'The SAP sales organization code representing the legal entity or business unit responsible for the sale. Determines pricing procedures, output determination, and revenue booking entity.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inbound RFQ/RFP record, tracking progression from initial receipt through evaluation, response, and final disposition. Drives workflow routing and pipeline visibility.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the customer or prospect formally submitted the RFQ or RFP to the company. Used to calculate response lead times and track compliance with response SLAs.']
  technical_requirements_summary varchar [note: 'Free-text summary of the customer\'s technical specifications, performance requirements, and engineering constraints as stated in the RFQ/RFP document. Provides context for application engineering and product configuration.']
  warranty_requirement varchar [note: 'Customer-specified warranty duration, coverage scope, or warranty terms required for the products or systems being quoted. Informs commercial pricing, risk assessment, and after-sales service planning.']
  win_probability_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Sales team\'s estimated probability (0-100%) of winning the RFQ/RFP and converting it to a sales order. Used for weighted pipeline reporting and revenue forecasting.']

  Note: 'Inbound Request for Quotation (RFQ) or Request for Proposal (RFP) received from a customer or prospect. Captures RFQ/RFP number, customer identity, submission date, response due date, project name, application type (factory automation, building electrification, transportation infrastructure), required delivery date, technical requirements summary, quantity requirements, budget indication, evaluation criteria, RFQ status (received, under review, responded, awarded, declined), and assigned sales/application engineer. SSOT for inbound customer RFQ/RFP records.'
}

Table order.schedule_line {
  schedule_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each delivery schedule line record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Serves as the primary key for the order_schedule_line entity.']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line_item. Business justification: Schedule lines are associated with a specific order line item. The string order_line_number is replaced by FK order_line_item_id.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plant. Business justification: Schedule lines define delivery dates and quantities from a specific plant. Production planning and logistics use this to align delivery schedules with plant-level inventory and production capacity for']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Schedule lines represent confirmed delivery quantities and dates tied to available stock. Order scheduling teams reference stock positions to confirm partial delivery quantities across multiple schedu']
  atp_ctp_check_result varchar [note: 'Result of the ATP (Available to Promise) or CTP (Capable to Promise) availability check performed for this schedule line. Indicates whether the full quantity was confirmed, partially confirmed, or not confirmed by the availability check engine.']
  category varchar [note: 'SAP schedule line category code (ETTYP) that controls the behavior of the schedule line, including relevance for MRP, delivery, goods movement, and billing. Examples: CP (standard with MRP), CN (no MRP), BN (no delivery).']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'The ATP/CTP-confirmed date on which the quantity on this schedule line is committed for delivery to the customer. Represents the binding delivery promise date used in fulfillment scheduling and customer communication.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity confirmed by ATP (Available to Promise) or CTP (Capable to Promise) check for delivery on the confirmed delivery date. Represents the binding quantity commitment for this schedule line.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the material was manufactured or substantially transformed. Required for customs declarations, preferential trade agreements, and import/export compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this schedule line record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and order processing timeline analysis.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the net value of this schedule line is expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency reporting in multinational operations.']
  customs_tariff_number varchar [note: 'The Harmonized System (HS) tariff classification number for the material on this schedule line. Required for customs clearance, duty calculation, and trade compliance reporting in cross-border shipments.']
  delivered_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual quantity that has been goods-issued and delivered against this schedule line. Used to track fulfillment progress and calculate open/remaining delivery quantity.']
  delivery_block_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating whether this schedule line is blocked for delivery processing. A non-empty value prevents the creation of an outbound delivery document. Common blocks include credit holds, export control holds, and customer-requested holds.']
  delivery_block_reason varchar [note: 'Descriptive reason explaining why the delivery block was applied to this schedule line (e.g., \'Credit limit exceeded\', \'Export license pending\', \'Customer requested hold\'). Supports operational resolution workflows.']
  delivery_relevance_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this schedule line is relevant for outbound delivery document creation. When false, the schedule line is informational only and will not trigger a delivery in the WMS/SD process.']
  export_control_status varchar [note: 'Status of export control compliance review for this schedule line. Indicates whether the shipment has been cleared for export, is pending license review, or is blocked due to export control regulations (e.g., EAR, ITAR, EU Dual-Use).']
  goods_issue_date date [note: 'The planned or actual date on which goods are issued from the warehouse/plant for this schedule line delivery. Triggers inventory reduction and initiates the billing process in the order-to-cash workflow.']
  goods_issue_status varchar [note: 'Current status of goods issue processing for this schedule line. Tracks whether goods have been fully issued, partially issued, or not yet issued from the warehouse, enabling fulfillment progress monitoring.']
  hazardous_goods_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material on this schedule line is classified as hazardous goods requiring special handling, packaging, and documentation during transportation. Triggers dangerous goods compliance checks.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code applicable to this schedule line delivery, defining the transfer of risk and responsibility between seller and buyer. May differ from the order-level Incoterms for split deliveries.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent modification to this schedule line record in the source system. Supports change detection, delta processing in the lakehouse pipeline, and audit compliance.']
  loading_date date [note: 'The planned date on which goods for this schedule line are to be loaded onto the transport vehicle at the shipping point. Used for warehouse and dock scheduling coordination.']
  material_availability_date date [note: 'The date by which the material must be available in the warehouse for picking and packing to meet the loading date. Derived from backward scheduling in SAP SD and used to trigger MRP requirements.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'The SAP material number (Stock Keeping Unit) identifying the product or component to be delivered on this schedule line. Aligns with the material master in SAP MM.']
  mrp_relevance_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this schedule line is relevant for MRP (Material Requirements Planning) processing. When true, the confirmed quantity and date generate a demand requirement in the MRP run to trigger production or procurement.']
  net_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net monetary value of this schedule line, calculated as confirmed quantity multiplied by the net unit price. Used for revenue recognition, billing, and order-to-cash financial reporting.']
  number varchar [note: 'Sequential number identifying the delivery schedule line within a sales order line item, as assigned by SAP S/4HANA SD (VBEP-ETENR). Typically a 4-digit counter (e.g., 0001, 0002) representing each distinct delivery split.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The originally requested quantity for this schedule line as entered on the sales order, before ATP/CTP confirmation. Enables comparison between customer request and confirmed commitment.']
  over_delivery_tolerance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum percentage by which the delivered quantity may exceed the confirmed quantity without requiring a new order or customer approval. Used in goods receipt and delivery processing to handle minor quantity variances.']
  partial_delivery_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a partial delivery is permitted for this schedule line if the full confirmed quantity cannot be shipped on the confirmed delivery date. Drives ATP/CTP split logic and customer service decisions.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The manufacturing or distribution plant code responsible for fulfilling this schedule line. Determines the supplying location for MRP planning, inventory allocation, and goods issue processing.']
  quantity_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure (UoM) applicable to the confirmed, ordered, and delivered quantities on this schedule line (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, M for meter). Aligns with SAP base unit of measure.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'The delivery date originally requested by the customer for this schedule line, prior to ATP/CTP confirmation. Used to measure delivery promise accuracy and customer service level performance.']
  route_code varchar [note: 'The transportation route code assigned to this schedule line, defining the path from shipping point to destination. Determines transit time, carrier selection, and transportation costs in the TMS.']
  shipping_point varchar [note: 'The SAP shipping point (VSTEL) from which the goods for this schedule line will be dispatched. Represents a physical location (warehouse, plant gate, dock) and determines the outbound delivery processing unit.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'The specific storage location within the plant from which goods will be picked for this schedule line delivery. Used by the Warehouse Management System (WMS) for inventory allocation and pick order generation.']
  transportation_planning_date date [note: 'The date by which transportation must be planned and arranged for this schedule line shipment. Used by the Transportation Management System (TMS) to initiate carrier booking and route planning.']
  under_delivery_tolerance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum percentage by which the delivered quantity may fall short of the confirmed quantity and still be considered a complete delivery. Prevents unnecessary open item management for minor shortfalls.']

  Note: 'Delivery schedule lines associated with a sales order line item, representing ATP/CTP-confirmed delivery commitments broken into specific dates and quantities. Captures schedule line number, confirmed delivery date, confirmed quantity, delivery block status, goods issue date, transportation planning date, loading date, route, shipping point, and MRP-driven availability confirmation details. Critical for fulfillment scheduling and delivery promise management in multi-plant manufacturing environments. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD schedule lines.'
}

Table order.status_history {
  status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each order status history record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for the order_status_history data product.']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.line_item. Business justification: The status_history product is described as the chronological audit trail of all status transitions for a sales order throughout its lifecycle. The primary entity being tracked is the sales order line ']
  reversal_reference_status_history_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the order_status_history_id of the original status transition that this record reverses. Enables bidirectional traceability between a reversal event and the original transition it corrects. NULL when is_reversal is FALSE.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status transition required a formal approval step (e.g., credit manager approval for credit release, export compliance approval). Supports workflow governance and segregation of duties compliance.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the approval for this status transition was granted. Used to measure approval cycle time and identify approval bottlenecks in the order-to-cash process.']
  approved_by_user varchar [note: 'System user ID of the approver who authorized this status transition when approval was required. NULL when no approval was required. Supports segregation of duties and audit trail for compliance.']
  atp_ctp_check_result varchar [note: 'Result of the Available to Promise (ATP) or Capable to Promise (CTP) availability check performed during this status transition. Captures whether inventory or production capacity was confirmed at the time of the transition.']
  block_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the type of block applied or released during this status transition. Enables analysis of block frequency by type, resolution time, and impact on order-to-cash cycle time.']
  blocking_reason_code varchar [note: 'SAP SD delivery or billing block reason code applied when the status transition results in an order being blocked (e.g., credit block, export control hold, quality hold). NULL when no block is applied. Aligns with SAP SD blocking reason configuration.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the business reason for the order status transition (e.g., CREDIT_HOLD, CUSTOMER_REQUEST, DELIVERY_CONFIRMED, PAYMENT_RECEIVED, SYSTEM_AUTO). Enables root cause analysis and process improvement analytics.']
  change_reason_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the reason for the status transition. Provides additional context beyond the reason code, including free-text notes entered by the user or generated by the system.']
  changed_by_user varchar [note: 'System user ID or service account identifier of the actor who triggered the status transition. May represent a human user (SAP logon ID), an automated process (batch job), or an integration service account. Supports accountability and audit trail requirements.']
  changed_by_user_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the user or process that triggered the status transition. Complements the user ID for human-readable audit reporting and compliance documentation.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments entered by the user or system at the time of the status transition. Provides additional context, exception details, or customer communication notes not captured by structured reason codes.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the sales organization or ship-to location associated with this order status transition. Supports multi-country regulatory compliance, GDPR jurisdiction determination, and regional analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this order status history record was created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Represents the data ingestion time, which may differ from the status_change_timestamp (the business event time). Used for data lineage and pipeline monitoring.']
  credit_check_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the credit check performed during this status transition, if applicable. Relevant for transitions involving credit status changes (e.g., order release from credit block). Aligns with SAP SD credit management integration.']
  duration_from_previous_minutes varchar [note: 'Time elapsed in minutes between the previous status transition and this status transition. Enables cycle time analysis for each status stage, bottleneck identification, and order-to-cash process optimization.']
  export_control_check_result varchar [note: 'Result of the export control compliance screening performed during this status transition. Relevant for orders involving controlled goods, dual-use items, or shipments to restricted countries. Supports regulatory compliance with EAR, ITAR, and EU dual-use regulations.']
  is_reversal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status transition represents a reversal or rollback of a previous status change (e.g., un-cancelling an order, reversing a delivery confirmation). TRUE when the transition moves the order backward in its lifecycle. Supports exception and error analysis.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this order status history record in the Silver Layer. Supports incremental data processing, change data capture, and audit trail completeness verification.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'The order status value assigned as a result of this transition. Captures the \'to\' state in the status transition pair. Represents the current status of the order at the point of this event.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a customer notification (e.g., order confirmation, shipment notification, cancellation notice) was sent as a result of this status transition. Supports customer communication compliance and SLA tracking.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sales order type at the time of this status transition. Provides context for interpreting the status transition within the appropriate order processing workflow.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code associated with the order at the time of this status transition. Enables plant-level analysis of order status progression, fulfillment performance, and SLA compliance across manufacturing sites.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'The order status value immediately before this transition occurred. Captures the \'from\' state in the status transition pair. NULL for the initial status record when the order is first created.']
