  actual_goods_issue_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the goods issue was posted at the source location, confirming physical departure of stock. Triggers inventory reduction at source and initiates in-transit stock accounting.']
  actual_goods_receipt_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the goods receipt was posted at the destination location, confirming physical arrival and acceptance of transferred stock. Triggers inventory increase at destination and closes in-transit stock.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code representing the legal entity initiating the stock transfer. Critical for intercompany transfer identification, financial accounting segregation, and regulatory reporting across multinational operations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the stock transfer order was created in the source system (SAP MM or Infor WMS). Provides the starting point for transfer lifecycle duration analysis and audit trail compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the transfer value and valuation price are expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global manufacturing plants and distribution centers.']
  customs_declaration_number varchar [note: 'Official customs declaration or export/import document reference number for cross-border stock transfers. Required for regulatory compliance with customs authorities and trade compliance reporting in multinational operations.']
  delivery_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP outbound delivery document number (VL10) or Infor WMS shipment document number associated with the stock transfer. Links the inventory transfer to the logistics delivery process for goods issue posting and shipment tracking.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the destination plant or warehouse location. Used for cross-border transfer identification, import duty determination, customs compliance, and regulatory reporting.']
  destination_plant_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the receiving manufacturing plant or facility to which the stock is being transferred. Corresponds to the SAP receiving plant code. Used for inter-plant inventory reconciliation and cost center allocation.']
  destination_warehouse_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the receiving warehouse within the destination plant. Corresponds to the SAP warehouse number or Infor WMS warehouse code at the destination. Used for inbound planning and putaway operations.']
  goods_issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the goods issue was posted at the source location. Provides sub-day precision for operational tracking, shift-level reporting, and audit trail requirements beyond the goods issue date.']
  goods_receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the goods receipt was posted at the destination location. Enables transit time calculation at sub-day precision and supports SLA compliance monitoring for inter-plant replenishment.']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the stock transfer crosses international borders, requiring customs documentation, export/import declarations, and compliance with trade regulations (e.g., CE Marking, REACH, RoHS, export control).']
  is_intercompany boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the stock transfer crosses legal entity boundaries (intercompany transfer). When true, the transfer requires intercompany billing, transfer pricing compliance, and consolidated financial elimination entries.']
  issued_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity of the SKU physically issued or picked from the source location and dispatched for transfer. May differ from the planned transfer quantity due to partial shipments or stock availability constraints.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the stock transfer record in the source system. Used for change detection in data pipeline processing and audit trail maintenance.']
  movement_type_code varchar [note: 'SAP MM movement type code that classifies the inventory transaction (e.g., 641 for goods issue for STO, 101 for goods receipt against STO). Determines the accounting entries and inventory update logic in SAP S/4HANA.']
  planned_receipt_date date [note: 'The scheduled calendar date on which the transferred stock is expected to arrive and be received at the destination location. Used for inbound planning, safety stock calculations, and Available to Promise (ATP) at the destination.']
  planned_transfer_date date [note: 'The scheduled calendar date on which the stock transfer is planned to be executed (goods issue from source). Used for production planning, MRP scheduling, and Available to Promise (ATP) calculations.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the stock transfer order, used by warehouse and logistics teams to sequence picking, packing, and dispatch activities. Urgent transfers may be triggered by production line stoppages or critical customer orders.']
  received_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity of the SKU confirmed as received at the destination location. Used to calculate transfer variance and update destination inventory positions. Triggers goods receipt posting in SAP MM.']
  receiving_company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code of the receiving legal entity in an intercompany stock transfer. When this differs from the issuing company code, the transfer triggers intercompany billing and cross-company financial postings.']
  source_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the source plant or warehouse location. Used for cross-border transfer identification, customs compliance, trade statistics, and regulatory reporting.']
  source_plant_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the originating manufacturing plant or facility from which the stock is being transferred. Corresponds to the SAP plant code (WERKS). Essential for inter-plant inventory movement tracking and cost allocation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which the stock transfer record originated (SAP S/4HANA MM, Infor WMS, or manual entry). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between systems in the Silver layer.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the stock transfer order. Tracks progression from creation through in-transit movement to receipt confirmation or cancellation. Used for operational monitoring and inventory position accuracy.']
  stock_type varchar [note: 'Inventory stock category of the material being transferred, indicating its usability status: unrestricted (available for use), quality inspection (under QM hold), blocked (not available), restricted, in-transit, or consignment. Drives inventory availability calculations and MRP planning.']
  transfer_order_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing document number assigned to the stock transfer order by the source system (SAP MM STO number or Infor WMS transfer order number). Used for cross-system traceability and communication with logistics and warehouse teams.']
  transfer_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Ordered or planned quantity of the SKU to be transferred, expressed in the base unit of measure. This is the quantity requested on the transfer order document, which may differ from the actual received quantity.']
  transfer_reason_code varchar [note: 'Business reason code explaining why the stock transfer was initiated. Used for demand classification, cost allocation, and operational analytics to understand the drivers of inter-plant and inter-warehouse stock movements.']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the stock transfer indicating the scope and nature of the movement: plant-to-plant (inter-plant), warehouse-to-warehouse, storage location transfer within a plant, or a formal Stock Transport Order (STO) involving procurement and logistics. Drives process routing and document flow.']
  transfer_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total monetary value of the stock transfer, calculated as transfer quantity multiplied by the valuation price. Used for financial reporting, intercompany billing, and inventory cost accounting. Stored as a raw business figure; aggregations are performed in the analytics layer.']
  transport_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number linking the stock transfer to the associated transport or shipment document (e.g., SAP shipment number, carrier waybill, or TMS reference). Enables traceability between inventory movement and physical logistics execution.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure in which the transfer quantity is expressed (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for litre, PAL for pallet). Aligned with SAP base unit of measure and ISO 80000 measurement standards.']
  valuation_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Unit price used to value the transferred stock at the time of transfer, based on the material\'s standard price or moving average price (MAP) in SAP. Used for inventory valuation, intercompany pricing, and financial accounting of stock movements.']

  Note: 'Records inter-plant, inter-warehouse, and inter-location stock transfer orders and their execution. Captures transfer order number, transfer type (plant-to-plant, warehouse-to-warehouse, storage location transfer, STO), source plant/warehouse/location, destination plant/warehouse/location, SKU, quantity, transfer status, planned date, actual transfer date, and transport reference. Aligned with SAP MM STO and Infor WMS transfer order.'
}

Table inventory.cycle_count {
  cycle_count_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each cycle count record within the inventory management system. Serves as the primary key for all cycle count transactions.']
  physical_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.physical_inventory. Business justification: A cycle count program can be conducted as part of a broader physical inventory event (e.g., a wall-to-wall count that uses cycle counting methodology for specific zones). Adding physical_inventory_id ']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: cycle_count.storage_location is a denormalized location code. Cycle counts are scoped to specific storage zones/locations. Normalizing to storage_location_id FK links the count to the granular locatio']
  abc_class varchar [note: 'ABC inventory classification of the materials included in this cycle count. Class A items are high-value/high-velocity requiring most frequent counting; Class B is medium; Class C is low; Class D is inactive/obsolete.']
  actual_count_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the cycle count was physically performed. Used to measure schedule adherence and compliance with counting frequency requirements.']
  assigned_counter_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the warehouse employee assigned to perform the physical count. Used for operational reporting and accountability tracking.']
  count_cycle_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the cycle count, used for cross-system reference between SAP MM and Infor WMS. Follows the format CC-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  count_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the physical counting activity was completed. Combined with count_start_timestamp to calculate count duration for labor and efficiency reporting.']
  count_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which this cycle count is scheduled to recur. Drives the cycle count program calendar and aligns with ABC classification-based counting requirements in SAP MM.']
  count_method varchar [note: 'Physical counting method used by warehouse operatives during the cycle count. Includes manual paper-based, RF scanner, barcode scanning, RFID, or voice-directed counting. Impacts accuracy and efficiency metrics.']
  count_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the physical counting activity commenced. Used for duration tracking, labor reporting, and operational analytics.']
  count_type varchar [note: 'Type of cycle count being performed. Standard counts use system book quantities as reference; blind counts do not show expected quantities to counters; recounts are triggered by variance; audit counts are compliance-driven.']
  count_zone varchar [note: 'Logical or physical zone within the warehouse designated for this cycle count. Zones are defined in Infor WMS and used to segment counting activities for operational efficiency.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the facility where the cycle count is performed. Supports multi-country regulatory compliance reporting and regional inventory analytics.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the monetary values reported in this cycle count record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations for multinational facilities.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal year and period (YYYY-MM) in which the cycle count is scheduled or completed. Used for financial period-end inventory reporting and alignment with SAP FI/CO period-close activities.']
  infor_wms_count_code varchar [note: 'Native cycle count identifier from the Infor WMS system. Used for cross-system reconciliation between Infor WMS and SAP S/4HANA MM inventory records.']
  inventory_adjustment_posted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inventory adjustment resulting from the cycle count variance has been formally posted in SAP MM, updating the book inventory to reflect the physical count results.']
  inventory_category varchar [note: 'Category of inventory being counted. Distinguishes between Raw Material, Work In Progress (WIP), Finished Goods, Maintenance Repair and Operations (MRO) stock, packaging, and semi-finished goods.']
  items_exceeding_threshold varchar [note: 'Count of SKU line items where the variance between book quantity and counted quantity exceeded the defined variance threshold, requiring recount or investigation.']
  items_within_threshold varchar [note: 'Count of SKU line items where the variance between book quantity and counted quantity fell within the defined variance threshold. Used to calculate count accuracy rate.']
  material_group varchar [note: 'SAP material group code used to filter the scope of materials included in the cycle count when sku_scope is set to material_group. Enables targeted counting by commodity category.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, observations, or exceptions recorded during the cycle count. May include reasons for variances, environmental conditions, or special circumstances affecting the count.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or distribution center where the cycle count is being performed. Aligns with SAP organizational structure.']
  reconciliation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cycle count variances were reviewed, approved, and inventory adjustments were posted in SAP MM. Marks the formal closure of the count cycle.']
  recount_count varchar [note: 'Number of recount iterations performed for this cycle count record. Tracks how many times counting was repeated due to variance exceedances. A value of 0 indicates no recount was needed.']
  recount_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a recount has been triggered for one or more SKUs in this cycle count due to variances exceeding the defined threshold. Drives workflow escalation in Infor WMS.']
  sap_physical_inventory_doc varchar [note: 'SAP MM physical inventory document number generated when the cycle count is initiated in SAP S/4HANA. Provides the cross-reference between the WMS cycle count and the SAP inventory posting document.']
  scheduled_count_date date [note: 'The planned calendar date on which the cycle count is scheduled to be performed. Used for workforce scheduling and operational planning.']
  sku_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the scope of Stock Keeping Units (SKUs) included in this cycle count. Determines whether all SKUs, a specific ABC class, a material group, or individual SKUs are counted.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the cycle count. Tracks the lifecycle from planning through execution to reconciliation. Aligned with SAP MM physical inventory document status and Infor WMS count status.']
  total_bins_counted varchar [note: 'Total number of storage bins or locations physically counted during this cycle count. Used to measure count coverage and completeness against the planned scope.']
  total_sku_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) included in the scope of this cycle count. Used for workload estimation and count completeness verification.']
  total_variance_qty decimal(15,3) [note: 'Aggregate net quantity variance across all SKUs counted in this cycle count, calculated as the sum of (counted quantity minus book quantity). Positive values indicate overages; negative values indicate shortages.']
  total_variance_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of inventory variance identified during the cycle count, expressed in the local currency. Used for financial impact assessment and inventory adjustment postings in SAP FI/CO.']
  variance_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable percentage variance between the system book quantity and the physically counted quantity before a recount or investigation is triggered. Expressed as a percentage of book quantity.']
  variance_threshold_qty decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum allowable absolute quantity variance between system book quantity and physically counted quantity before a recount or investigation is triggered. Complements the percentage threshold for high-value items.']

  Note: 'Manages cycle counting programs for continuous physical inventory verification without full warehouse shutdowns. Captures count cycle ID, warehouse, count zone, SKU scope, count frequency (daily, weekly, monthly), ABC class trigger, scheduled count date, actual count date, count status (planned, in-progress, completed, reconciled), assigned counter, and variance threshold. Aligned with SAP MM cycle counting configuration and Infor WMS cycle count module.'
}

Table inventory.cycle_count_result {
  cycle_count_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each cycle count result record at the SKU and storage location level. Serves as the primary key for this entity in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  cycle_count_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cycle_count. Business justification: cycle_count_result is a child record of cycle_count. Each result captures the counted quantity for a specific SKU/bin within a count event. The cycle_count_id FK establishes the parent-child relations']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Cycle count variances (differences between counted and book quantity) trigger automatic journal entries to adjust inventory value. Finance auditors trace every cycle count difference to its GL posting']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: cycle_count_result.batch_number is a denormalized batch reference. Normalizing to lot_batch_id FK enables batch-level traceability in cycle count results.']
  physical_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.physical_inventory. Business justification: cycle_count_result.physical_inventory_document_number references a physical inventory document. Normalizing to physical_inventory_id FK links count results to the physical inventory event that generat']
  quality_notification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_notification. Business justification: When a cycle count reveals a significant discrepancy or damaged goods, a quality notification is raised to investigate root cause. The cycle count result references the notification to track the quali']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: cycle_count_result.storage_location_code is a denormalized location code. Normalizing to storage_location_id FK links each count result to the granular bin/rack location.']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'ABC inventory classification of the SKU based on consumption value or movement frequency. A-items are high-value/high-frequency; C-items are low-value/low-frequency. Drives cycle count frequency and priority.']
  adjustment_posted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inventory adjustment has been posted to the SAP material ledger following approval of the cycle count variance. When true, the book stock has been updated to reflect the counted quantity.']
  adjustment_posting_date date [note: 'Accounting date on which the inventory adjustment was posted to the general ledger and material ledger. Determines the financial period in which the inventory variance is recognized.']
  adjustment_posting_reference varchar [note: 'SAP material document number generated when the inventory difference was posted to adjust the book stock to the counted quantity. Links the cycle count result to the financial and inventory adjustment transaction.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the cycle count result. Drives the approval and stock adjustment posting workflow: pending awaits review, approved allows posting, recount_required triggers a new count round.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cycle count result was approved by the authorized supervisor. Supports audit trail and SLA compliance for inventory adjustment turnaround time.']
  book_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'System-recorded (book) quantity of the SKU at the storage location at the time the physical inventory document was created. Represents the expected on-hand quantity before the physical count.']
  count_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the physical cycle count was performed for this SKU and storage location. Used for inventory accuracy trending and compliance reporting.']
  count_method varchar [note: 'Method used to perform the physical count (e.g., manual paper-based, RF scanner, barcode scan, RFID). Supports analysis of count accuracy by method and drives process improvement initiatives.']
  count_note varchar [note: 'Free-text remarks entered by the counter or supervisor to explain unusual findings, discrepancies, or special circumstances observed during the cycle count (e.g., damaged goods, misplaced stock).']
  count_round_number varchar [note: 'Sequential round number of the count for this SKU and location (1 = initial count, 2 = first recount, 3 = second recount, etc.). Tracks the count iteration history for audit and accuracy analysis.']
  count_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the individual count line item within the physical inventory document. Tracks progression from not yet counted through to final posting.']
  count_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the physical count entry was recorded in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and time-of-day analysis for count accuracy.']
  counted_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual physical quantity of the SKU recorded by the counter during the cycle count. This is the primary result of the count event and is compared against the book quantity to determine variance.']
  counter_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the warehouse employee who performed the physical count. Captured for audit trail and accountability purposes in cycle count documentation.']
  exceeds_tolerance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the variance percentage exceeds the configured tolerance threshold for this SKU. When true, triggers a recount workflow or supervisor approval before stock adjustment posting.']
  inventory_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the inventory item by its role in the manufacturing process: raw material, work in progress (WIP), finished goods, or maintenance repair and operations (MRO) stock.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the local currency in which the variance value is expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Supports multi-currency reporting for multinational operations.']
  physical_inventory_document_item varchar [note: 'Line item number within the SAP physical inventory document identifying the specific SKU and storage location combination being counted.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or distribution center where the cycle count was performed. Supports multi-plant inventory accuracy reporting.']
  recount_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this count record is a recount (second or subsequent count) triggered by an out-of-tolerance variance from the initial count. Supports multi-round count workflows.']
  recount_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Physical quantity recorded during the recount round, if a recount was performed. Populated only when recount_flag is true. Used to reconcile the final accepted quantity.']
  stock_type varchar [note: 'SAP stock category indicating the usability status of the inventory being counted (e.g., unrestricted use, quality inspection, blocked). Determines how the stock can be consumed or transferred.']
  storage_bin varchar [note: 'Specific bin or slot identifier within the warehouse where the material was physically located and counted. Provides the most granular location reference for inventory accuracy.']
  tolerance_percentage decimal(10,4) [note: 'Configured acceptable variance percentage threshold for this SKU or ABC class. Variances within tolerance may be auto-approved; variances exceeding tolerance require supervisor review and recount.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure in which the material quantity is expressed for the cycle count (e.g., EA, KG, L, M, PC). Aligns with the SAP material master base UoM.']
  valuation_method varchar [note: 'Inventory valuation method applied to calculate the financial value of the variance (e.g., standard price, moving average price, FIFO). Determines how the adjustment impacts the material ledger and P&L.']
  valuation_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Unit price used to calculate the monetary value of the inventory variance, sourced from the SAP material master (standard price or moving average price). Reflects the inventory valuation method in use.']
  variance_percentage decimal(10,4) [note: 'Variance quantity expressed as a percentage of the book quantity. Used to assess relative inventory accuracy and determine whether the variance exceeds the tolerance threshold for adjustment posting.']
  variance_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Difference between the counted quantity and the book quantity (counted_quantity minus book_quantity). A positive value indicates a surplus; a negative value indicates a shortage.']
  variance_value_local_currency decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary value of the inventory variance in the plant local currency, calculated as variance quantity multiplied by the standard or moving average price. Used for financial impact assessment and P&L reporting.']
  zero_count_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the counter explicitly confirmed a zero quantity count (as opposed to the count not yet being entered). Distinguishes between a confirmed zero stock and a missing count entry in SAP.']

  Note: 'Captures the detailed results of each cycle count event at the SKU and storage location level. Records counted quantity, system book quantity, variance quantity, variance percentage, count date and time, counter identity, recount flag, recount quantity, approval status, and adjustment posting reference. Drives inventory accuracy KPIs and triggers stock adjustment postings when variances exceed tolerance. Aligned with SAP MM physical inventory document items.'
}

Table inventory.stock_adjustment {
  stock_adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each stock adjustment record in the inventory management system. Serves as the primary key for the stock_adjustment data product.']
  cycle_count_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cycle_count_result. Business justification: A stock adjustment is the direct downstream action triggered by a cycle count result that exceeds tolerance thresholds. The cycle_count_result is the parent event (the counted variance), and the stock']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Stock adjustments must be posted to the correct fiscal period for period-end inventory reconciliation and financial closing. Controllers enforce period assignment on every inventory posting in manufac']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Every inventory stock adjustment (write-off, correction, shrinkage) generates a corresponding journal entry for accounting. Finance teams reconcile inventory adjustments to GL postings daily in manufa']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: stock_adjustment.batch_number is a denormalized batch reference. Normalizing to lot_batch_id FK enables batch-level traceability for inventory adjustments.']
  physical_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.physical_inventory. Business justification: stock_adjustment.physical_inventory_document references the physical inventory document that triggered the adjustment. Normalizing to physical_inventory_id FK links adjustments to the physical invento']
  quality_notification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_notification. Business justification: Stock adjustments made to write off or reclassify defective material must reference the quality notification that authorized the adjustment. This provides the audit trail required by finance and quali']
  serialized_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit. Business justification: stock_adjustment.serial_number is a denormalized serial number reference. Normalizing to serialized_unit_id FK links serialized item adjustments to the serialized unit master.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: stock_adjustment.storage_location is a denormalized location code. Normalizing to storage_location_id FK links each adjustment to the granular bin/rack location.']
  usage_decision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.usage_decision. Business justification: Stock adjustments that move material from inspection stock to unrestricted, blocked, or scrap are directly authorized by a usage decision. The adjustment must reference the decision to maintain a comp']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'ABC inventory classification of the SKU at the time of adjustment, indicating its relative value and management priority (A=high value/high attention, B=medium, C=low value/low attention). Used for prioritizing cycle count frequency and adjustment review thresholds.']
  adjusted_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The net quantity change applied to the inventory position. Positive values indicate an inventory increase (surplus found); negative values indicate a decrease (shortage, write-off, or rejection). Expressed in the material\'s base unit of measure.']
  adjustment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing document number assigned to the stock adjustment, used for cross-referencing with SAP MM inventory difference postings and audit trails. Follows the enterprise document numbering convention.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inventory adjustment indicating the business reason category. Drives accounting treatment, approval workflow, and reporting segmentation. Aligned with SAP MM movement type categories.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the inventory adjustment was formally approved by the authorized approver. Used for SLA compliance tracking and audit documentation.']
  approval_threshold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary threshold value that triggered the approval requirement for this adjustment. Captures the applicable authorization limit at the time of submission, supporting audit and SOX compliance documentation.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the manager or authorized personnel who approved the inventory adjustment. Captured for audit trail and segregation of duties compliance. Not a direct personal identifier in the operational context.']
  book_quantity_after decimal(18,4) [note: 'The system-recorded (book) inventory quantity for the SKU at the storage location immediately after the adjustment was posted. Confirms the resulting inventory position and supports reconciliation.']
  book_quantity_before decimal(18,4) [note: 'The system-recorded (book) inventory quantity for the SKU at the storage location immediately before the adjustment was posted. Provides the baseline for variance calculation and audit trail.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code representing the legal entity responsible for the inventory adjustment. Used for legal entity-level financial reporting, intercompany reconciliation, and statutory compliance.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'The controlling cost center responsible for absorbing the financial impact of the inventory adjustment. Used for internal cost allocation and management reporting.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant or distribution center where the adjustment occurred. Supports multi-country regulatory compliance reporting and regional inventory analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the stock adjustment record was initially created in the system. Provides a precise audit trail timestamp for compliance and data lineage purposes.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the financial impact amount and unit cost are expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency reporting for multinational operations.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date the adjustment document was created or the physical event occurred (e.g., date of cycle count, date damage was discovered). May differ from the posting date when adjustments are processed with a delay.']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the inventory adjustment calculated as adjusted quantity multiplied by unit cost. Represents the debit or credit to the inventory account and corresponding expense or income account. Negative values indicate a write-down.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which the inventory adjustment posting belongs. Used for period-end financial reporting, inventory reconciliation, and year-end closing activities.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The General Ledger (GL) account code to which the financial impact of the inventory adjustment is posted. Determined by SAP automatic account determination based on movement type and valuation class.']
  inventory_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the material by its role in the production process: raw material, Work In Progress (WIP), finished goods, Maintenance Repair and Operations (MRO), or semi-finished goods. Supports domain-specific inventory reporting.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'SAP MM three-digit movement type code governing the inventory posting logic (e.g., 551 for scrapping, 701 for inventory difference posting). Determines the general ledger account determination and stock category impact.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or distribution center where the inventory adjustment is being made. Supports multi-plant inventory management across global operations.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the inventory adjustment was posted to the SAP inventory ledger and financial accounts. Marks the completion of the adjustment lifecycle and the point at which stock quantities were updated.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting posting date on which the inventory adjustment was recorded in the financial ledger. Determines the fiscal period to which the financial impact is attributed. May differ from the physical count date.']
  profit_center varchar [note: 'The SAP profit center associated with the inventory adjustment for segment reporting and profitability analysis. Enables P&L impact attribution at the business unit level.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code explaining the specific cause of the inventory adjustment (e.g., CYCLE_COUNT_VAR, DAMAGE_INBOUND, QA_REJECT, SYS_ERR). Used for root cause analysis, CAPA tracking, and trend reporting.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing additional context and explanation for the inventory adjustment beyond the standardized reason code. Captures specific circumstances, findings from cycle counts, or quality inspection outcomes.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'The document number of the source business document that triggered the adjustment (e.g., physical inventory document number, cycle count document number, NCR number, quality inspection lot number). Enables cross-system traceability.']
  reference_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of the source business document that triggered or authorized the inventory adjustment (e.g., cycle count document, physical inventory document, Non-Conformance Report (NCR), quality inspection lot). Provides traceability to the originating business event.']
  requires_approval boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adjustment requires formal management approval before being posted to the inventory ledger. Typically triggered by financial impact thresholds or specific adjustment types such as write-offs.']
  reversal_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP material document number of the reversal posting that cancelled this adjustment. Populated only when reversal_indicator is true. Enables complete audit trail of correction activities.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this stock adjustment has been reversed (cancelled after posting). A value of true means the adjustment has been negated by a subsequent reversal document, and the net inventory impact is zero.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which the stock adjustment originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA MM, Infor WMS, Siemens Opcenter MES). Supports data lineage tracking in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the stock adjustment record, tracking its lifecycle from initial creation through approval, financial posting, and potential reversal. Drives process control and reporting filters.']
  stock_type varchar [note: 'Category of stock being adjusted, indicating the inventory valuation and availability status. Determines whether the adjustment affects unrestricted-use stock, quality inspection stock, or blocked stock.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The standard or moving average cost per unit of the material at the time of the adjustment, used to calculate the financial impact. Sourced from the SAP material valuation record.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The base unit of measure in which the adjusted quantity is expressed (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, M for meter). Aligned with SAP base unit of measure configuration.']
  valuation_method varchar [note: 'The costing method applied to value the inventory adjustment (e.g., standard cost, moving average price, FIFO). Determines how the financial impact amount is calculated and which accounting entries are generated.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The SAP user ID or system user identifier of the person who created the stock adjustment record. Supports segregation of duties auditing and traceability requirements.']

  Note: 'Records approved inventory adjustments made to correct book stock quantities following cycle counts, physical inventories, quality holds, or write-offs. Captures adjustment number, SKU, storage location, adjustment type (count variance, damage write-off, quality rejection, system correction), adjusted quantity, reason code, financial impact, approver, approval date, and reference document. Aligned with SAP MM inventory difference posting.'
}

Table inventory.reorder_policy {
  reorder_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each reorder policy record in the inventory replenishment system. Serves as the primary key for the reorder_policy data product.']
  asset_bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_bom. Business justification: Reorder policies for spare parts are defined per asset BOM component. Maintenance planners set min/max stock levels based on which BOM positions are critical to keep assets running — a standard MRO in']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: reorder_policy.storage_location_code is a denormalized location code. Reorder policies are defined at the storage location level. Normalizing to storage_location_id FK links the policy to the granular']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'ABC inventory classification of the SKU based on consumption value or demand frequency. A = high-value/high-frequency, B = medium, C = low-value/low-frequency, D = dormant/obsolete. Drives differentiated replenishment strategies and cycle count frequencies.']
  average_daily_usage_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'The average quantity of the SKU consumed or issued per calendar day at this location, based on historical consumption data. Used as the baseline for reorder point and safety stock calculations.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values associated with this reorder policy (e.g., ordering cost, holding cost rate). Supports multi-currency operations across global plants.']
  demand_variability_std_dev decimal(18,4) [note: 'The standard deviation of demand during the replenishment lead time period, used in statistical safety stock calculations. Reflects the variability of consumption patterns for this SKU at this location.']
  economic_order_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'The Economic Order Quantity (EOQ) — the mathematically optimal order quantity that minimizes total inventory holding costs and ordering costs. Used as the target replenishment quantity in cost-optimized procurement strategies.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this reorder policy becomes active and drives replenishment decisions. Enables future-dated policy changes and seasonal parameter adjustments.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this reorder policy expires and is no longer applicable. Used for time-bound policies such as seasonal replenishment strategies or temporary safety stock adjustments.']
  fixed_lot_size_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'The fixed quantity used for replenishment orders when the lot sizing procedure is set to fixed lot size. Overrides EOQ calculations and is typically aligned with supplier standard pack sizes or production batch sizes.']
  goods_receipt_processing_days varchar [note: 'The number of working days required to process a Goods Receipt Note (GRN) after physical delivery, including inspection, quality checks, and putaway. Contributes to total replenishment lead time calculation.']
  holding_cost_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The annual inventory holding cost expressed as a percentage of the unit value (e.g., 20.00 = 20% per year). Includes storage, insurance, obsolescence, and capital cost of inventory. Used in EOQ and total cost of ownership calculations.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'The date on which the reorder policy parameters were last formally reviewed and validated by the inventory planning team. Supports governance and audit requirements for replenishment parameter maintenance.']
  lot_sizing_procedure varchar [note: 'Defines the lot sizing method used by MRP to calculate replenishment order quantities. Options include exact quantity, fixed lot size, Economic Order Quantity (EOQ), period lot sizing, lot-for-lot, and min/max replenishment.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the SKU by material category: raw material, Work In Progress (WIP), finished good, Maintenance Repair and Operations (MRO), semi-finished, trading good, or packaging. Determines applicable replenishment rules and valuation methods.']
  maximum_order_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'The maximum quantity that can be ordered in a single replenishment order. Used to prevent over-ordering, manage storage capacity constraints, and control cash flow exposure.']
  maximum_stock_level_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'The maximum allowable inventory quantity for the SKU at this location. Replenishment orders are sized to bring stock up to but not exceed this level. Used in min/max replenishment strategies in Infor WMS.']
  minimum_order_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'The Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) — the smallest quantity that can be ordered from a supplier or produced in a single replenishment run. Reflects supplier contract terms or production batch constraints.']
  mrp_type varchar [note: 'Defines the replenishment planning method for the SKU at this location. Determines how SAP MRP or Infor WMS triggers replenishment: reorder point (ROP), MRP-driven, consumption-based, Kanban, manual, or vendor-managed inventory (VMI).']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next formal review of this reorder policy. Drives workflow alerts and ensures parameters remain aligned with current demand patterns and supply conditions.']
  order_quantity_rounding decimal(18,4) [note: 'The rounding value applied to calculated replenishment quantities to align with supplier pack sizes, pallet quantities, or production batch increments. Ensures order quantities are practical multiples.']
  ordering_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The fixed cost incurred each time a replenishment order is placed, including administrative, processing, and supplier transaction costs. Used as an input to Economic Order Quantity (EOQ) calculations.']
  planned_delivery_time_days varchar [note: 'The planned number of working days required for a supplier to deliver the material after purchase order creation. Distinct from total replenishment lead time as it excludes internal processing time.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant or facility code identifying the manufacturing plant, distribution center, or warehouse location where this reorder policy is in effect. Enables location-specific replenishment rules.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Business-facing alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the reorder policy, used for cross-system reference and reporting. Typically sourced from SAP MRP planning data or Infor WMS min/max configuration.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for the reorder policy, providing a human-readable label for reporting, dashboards, and business communication.']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the reorder policy. Active policies drive automated replenishment triggers. Inactive or suspended policies are excluded from MRP runs. Obsolete policies are retained for audit history.']
  procurement_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU is replenished via external procurement (purchase from supplier), in-house production (manufacturing order), or both. Drives MRP planning logic in SAP S/4HANA.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this reorder policy record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and change history tracking.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this reorder policy record was most recently modified. Enables change detection, incremental data loads, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  reorder_point_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'The Reorder Point (ROP) quantity — the inventory level at which a replenishment order is automatically triggered. When on-hand stock falls to or below this level, a procurement or production order is initiated.']
  replenishment_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The total number of calendar days from placement of a replenishment order to receipt of goods at the storage location. Includes supplier lead time, transit time, and goods receipt processing time. Used to calculate the reorder point.']
  review_cycle_days varchar [note: 'The frequency in calendar days at which inventory levels are reviewed and replenishment decisions are made. Applicable to periodic review replenishment policies (P-system). A value of 0 indicates continuous review.']
  safety_stock_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum buffer stock quantity maintained to protect against demand variability and supply uncertainty. Represents the floor inventory level below which stock should not fall under normal operating conditions.']
  seasonal_policy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reorder policy incorporates seasonal demand adjustments. When true, reorder point and safety stock parameters may vary by season or planning period.']
  service_level_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target inventory service level expressed as a percentage (e.g., 95.00 = 95%). Represents the desired probability of fulfilling demand from available stock without a stockout. Used to statistically calculate safety stock requirements.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this reorder policy was sourced: SAP S/4HANA MRP planning data, Infor WMS min/max configuration, or manually entered. Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  special_procurement_type varchar [note: 'Specifies a special procurement strategy such as consignment stock, subcontracting, inter-plant stock transfer, direct production, phantom assembly, or vendor-managed inventory (VMI). Extends the standard procurement type.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The base unit of measure in which all quantity fields (reorder point, safety stock, MOQ, EOQ, etc.) are expressed for this SKU. Examples: EA (each), KG, L, M, PC, PAL.']
  xyz_classification varchar [note: 'XYZ demand variability classification of the SKU. X = stable/predictable demand, Y = variable demand, Z = highly irregular/sporadic demand. Used in conjunction with ABC classification to optimize safety stock and replenishment strategy.']

  Note: 'Defines reorder parameters and replenishment policies for each SKU at each plant or warehouse location. Captures reorder point (ROP), safety stock level, minimum order quantity (MOQ), maximum stock level, economic order quantity (EOQ), replenishment lead time, MRP type (reorder point, MRP, consumption-based), and review cycle. Drives automated replenishment triggers in SAP MRP and Infor WMS min/max replenishment.'
}

Table inventory.replenishment_order {
  replenishment_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for each replenishment order record in the Infor WMS replenishment module. Serves as the primary key for the replenishment_order data product.']
  ap_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: Replenishment orders (purchase orders fulfilled) are matched to AP invoices during three-way matching (PO-GR-Invoice). Accounts payable teams link every goods receipt to its vendor invoice for payment']
  engineering_bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_bom. Business justification: Production replenishment orders are exploded from a specific engineering BOM revision. MRP and production planning teams must reference the exact BOM used to generate the order to ensure correct compo']
  freight_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.freight_order. Business justification: A replenishment order triggers a freight order to physically move stock from supplier to warehouse. Procurement and logistics teams use this link daily to track inbound shipment status against open re']
  inbound_delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.inbound_delivery. Business justification: Replenishment orders trigger inbound deliveries from suppliers or other warehouses. Inventory planning links replenishment to expected deliveries for stock availability forecasting.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: replenishment_order.lot_number is a denormalized lot/batch reference. Normalizing to lot_batch_id FK enables batch-level traceability for replenishment movements.']
  mrp_planned_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.mrp_planned_order. Business justification: MRP planned orders drive replenishment orders in manufacturing. The inventory replenishment order must reference the MRP planned order that originated it, enabling planners to trace supply coverage ba']
  pricing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.pricing_agreement. Business justification: Replenishment orders for customer-owned or consignment inventory must reference the active pricing agreement to apply correct transfer prices. Procurement and finance teams use this link to ensure rep']
  purchase_requisition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_requisition. Business justification: When inventory reorder triggers fire, a replenishment order converts directly into a purchase requisition in procurement. Inventory planners use this link daily to trace replenishment demand through t']
  reorder_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.reorder_policy. Business justification: Replenishment orders are generated by reorder policies (triggered when stock falls below reorder point). Adding reorder_policy_id FK links each replenishment order to the governing policy, enabling po']
  sales_price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_price_list. Business justification: Replenishment orders in industrial manufacturing reference the active sales price list to align procurement cost benchmarks with current selling prices — procurement planners use this daily to validat']
  source_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: replenishment_order.source_location_code is a denormalized location code for the origin. Normalizing to source_storage_location_id FK with a descriptive prefix distinguishes it from the destination lo']
  stock_transfer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_transfer. Business justification: A replenishment order is frequently fulfilled by creating a stock transfer order (STO) to move inventory from a source location to the replenishment destination. Adding stock_transfer_id to replenishm']
  tax_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.tax_code. Business justification: Replenishment orders carry a tax code that determines VAT/GST treatment on the purchase. Tax codes on procurement documents drive AP invoice tax calculation and compliance reporting in manufacturing.']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'ABC inventory classification of the SKU being replenished, indicating its relative consumption value and replenishment priority. A-class items are high-value/high-velocity; C-class items are low-value/low-velocity. Used for prioritization and cycle count scheduling.']
  actual_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual quantity of the SKU physically moved and confirmed at the destination location upon completion of the replenishment task. Used for variance analysis against requested and confirmed quantities.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the replenishment order was cancelled. Used for exception analysis, process improvement, and identifying systemic issues in replenishment triggering logic.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the replenishment order was cancelled, if applicable. Populated only for orders with status \'cancelled\'. Used for exception reporting and root cause analysis.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the replenishment order was fully completed and stock confirmed at the destination location. Used for cycle time measurement, SLA compliance, and inventory availability updates.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity confirmed as available and allocated for replenishment at the source location. May differ from requested quantity if source stock is insufficient.']
  destination_location_code varchar [note: 'Warehouse location code identifying the target location where replenished stock must be delivered (e.g., forward pick face, kanban lane, production staging area). Corresponds to the Infor WMS location master.']
  destination_location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the destination warehouse location to which stock is being moved. Supports analytics on replenishment demand by location type and operational zone.']
  destination_on_hand_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The on-hand stock quantity at the destination location at the time the replenishment order was created. Provides context for the replenishment trigger and supports inventory position reporting.']
  is_partial_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a partial fulfillment of the replenishment order is acceptable when the full requested quantity is not available at the source location. When false, the order must be fulfilled in full or held.']
  is_urgent boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the replenishment order has been escalated as urgent, typically due to an imminent or active stockout at the destination pick location. Triggers expedited handling by warehouse supervisors.']
  kanban_card_number varchar [note: 'The kanban card or signal identifier that triggered this replenishment order, applicable when trigger_type is \'kanban\'. Enables traceability between physical kanban signals and digital replenishment records.']
  license_plate_number varchar [note: 'The License Plate Number (LPN) or handling unit identifier associated with the stock being replenished. Used in Infor WMS for unit-level tracking of pallets, totes, or containers during replenishment movements.']
  max_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The maximum stock level threshold at the destination location for min/max replenishment. The replenishment order quantity is typically calculated to bring stock up to this level.']
  min_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum stock level threshold at the destination location that triggered the replenishment order (applicable for min/max trigger type). When on-hand quantity falls below this level, a replenishment order is generated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for warehouse operators or supervisors to record additional instructions, exceptions, or observations related to the replenishment order execution.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the replenishment order, used for tracking and communication across warehouse operations teams. Formatted as REP-{YEAR}-{SEQUENCE}.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing or distribution plant associated with the replenishment order. Enables cross-plant inventory reporting and MRP alignment.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the replenishment order, used by warehouse management to sequence and expedite fulfillment. Critical priority indicates immediate action required to prevent stockout at pick location.']
  priority_score varchar [note: 'Numeric priority score (1–100) assigned by the WMS to enable fine-grained sequencing of replenishment tasks when multiple orders share the same priority tier. Higher scores indicate greater urgency.']
  replenishment_method varchar [note: 'The inventory replenishment strategy applied to this order. Fixed quantity replenishes a set amount each time; fixed cycle replenishes on a schedule; two-bin and kanban are signal-driven; wave-pick is batch-driven by outbound order waves.']
  requested_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of the SKU requested to be moved from the source location to the destination location as specified when the replenishment order was created.']
  requested_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the replenishment order was created or triggered in the WMS. Serves as the start of the replenishment lifecycle and is used for lead time and aging analysis.']
  required_by_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time by which the replenishment must be completed to prevent a stockout at the destination pick location. Used for SLA compliance monitoring and escalation triggers.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The safety stock level maintained at the destination location to buffer against demand variability and replenishment lead time uncertainty. Informs replenishment urgency and priority assignment.']
  source_location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the source warehouse location from which stock is being moved. Used for operational routing and analytics on replenishment flow patterns.']
  source_on_hand_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The on-hand stock quantity available at the source location at the time the replenishment order was created. Used to assess feasibility of fulfilling the requested quantity and for inventory position analysis.']
  started_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the warehouse operator began executing the replenishment task (first scan or task acceptance). Used to measure task execution time and operator productivity.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the replenishment order. Tracks progression from open (created, not yet started) through in-progress (movement underway) to completed (stock moved to destination) or cancelled/on-hold states.']
  storage_type_code varchar [note: 'Infor WMS storage type code defining the physical storage method (e.g., pallet rack, floor storage, carousel, automated storage and retrieval system) applicable to the replenishment movement.']
  task_number varchar [note: 'The Infor WMS warehouse task identifier assigned to the physical replenishment movement instruction. Used by warehouse operators on handheld devices or voice-directed systems to execute the replenishment.']
  trigger_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the mechanism that initiated the replenishment order. Min/max triggers fire when stock falls below minimum threshold; kanban triggers are signal-card driven; manual orders are created by warehouse staff; demand-driven triggers are based on forecasted pick demand.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure in which the replenishment quantities (requested, confirmed, actual) are expressed. Aligned with the SKU\'s base unit of measure in the material master.']
  wave_number varchar [note: 'The outbound picking wave number associated with this replenishment order when triggered by a wave-pick process. Links replenishment activity to the outbound order fulfillment wave for operational coordination.']

  Note: 'Tracks internal replenishment requests and warehouse replenishment orders generated to move stock from reserve storage to pick locations or from bulk storage to forward pick areas. Captures replenishment order number, trigger type (min/max, kanban, manual), source location, destination location, SKU, requested quantity, replenishment status (open, in-progress, completed), priority, and completion timestamp. Aligned with Infor WMS replenishment module.'
}

Table inventory.inventory_valuation {
  inventory_valuation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each inventory valuation record in the silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for the inventory_valuation data product.']
  asset_valuation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_valuation. Business justification: Inventory valuation for spare parts and MRO items is reconciled against asset valuation during financial close. Finance teams link these to ensure capitalized spare parts are correctly reflected in as']
  controlling_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.controlling_area. Business justification: Inventory valuation is scoped to a controlling area in manufacturing finance (e.g., plant-level vs. company-wide). Controlling area determines the costing variant and currency used for valuation runs.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Inventory valuations are calculated and frozen per fiscal period for financial reporting. Period-end close process requires accurate period assignment for audit trails.']
  price_list_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.price_list_item. Business justification: Inventory valuation references the authoritative price list item for standard cost or transfer price. Finance uses this to ensure valuation aligns with approved pricing, eliminating duplicated price d']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: inventory_valuation currently has only a cross-domain FK (fiscal_period_id → finance) and no in-domain FK, making it siloed within the inventory domain. Adding storage_location_id connects inventory_v']
  supplier_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_invoice. Business justification: Inventory valuation (especially for moving average or standard cost updates) is directly impacted by supplier invoice prices. Finance and inventory accounting teams link valuations to invoices to reco']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'The ABC inventory classification of the material based on consumption value or strategic importance. A = High value/high priority, B = Medium value/medium priority, C = Low value/low priority. Used for inventory control prioritization, cycle counting frequency, and safety stock policy.']
  base_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The base unit of measure in which the material stock quantity is managed and the valuation price is expressed (e.g., EA, KG, L, M, PC). Corresponds to SAP MARA.MEINS. Required for accurate stock value calculation.']
  class varchar [note: 'SAP valuation class (BKLAS) that determines the general ledger accounts updated during inventory movements. Groups materials with similar accounting treatment (e.g., raw materials, finished goods, WIP, MRO). Critical for COGS and balance sheet posting.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'The SAP company code representing the legal entity or organizational unit responsible for the inventory valuation. Used for financial consolidation, intercompany reconciliation, and statutory reporting across multinational operations.']
  cost_estimate_number varchar [note: 'The SAP product cost estimate number (from CO-PC) that was used to calculate or release the current standard price. Links the inventory valuation to the underlying cost estimate for traceability and variance analysis. Corresponds to SAP KEKO.KALNR.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the inventory valuation amounts (standard price, moving average price, total stock value) are expressed. Corresponds to the local currency of the company code or valuation area (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  date date [note: 'The business date as of which the inventory valuation snapshot is captured. For period-end closing, this is the last day of the fiscal period. For on-demand valuations, this is the date the valuation was requested or extracted.']
  exchange_rate decimal(14,6) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate applied to translate the local currency inventory value to the group currency as of the valuation date. Used for multi-currency financial consolidation and reporting.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year to which this valuation record belongs (01–12 for standard periods, up to 16 for special periods). Corresponds to SAP MBEW.LFMON. Used for period-end inventory closing and financial reporting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this inventory valuation record belongs. Used for period-end closing, financial reporting, and year-over-year inventory analysis. Aligns with the company\'s fiscal calendar.']
  future_planned_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The planned standard price for the next cost estimate or future period, staged for release. Corresponds to SAP MBEW.ZKPRS (future price field). Used in standard cost roll and cost estimate release planning.']
  future_price_valid_from_date date [note: 'The date from which the future planned price becomes effective. Used to schedule standard cost releases at the start of a new fiscal period or cost estimate period. Corresponds to SAP MBEW.ZKDAT.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The general ledger account code to which the inventory value is posted on the balance sheet. Determined by the valuation class and account determination configuration. Used for financial reconciliation between MM and FI modules.']
  group_currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the corporate group reporting currency used in consolidated financial statements (e.g., USD, EUR). Enables multi-currency inventory valuation reporting across global operations.']
  inventory_category varchar [note: 'The category of inventory being valued, classifying the material by its stage in the production lifecycle. Raw Material (RM), Work In Progress (WIP), Finished Goods (FG), Maintenance Repair and Operations (MRO), Semi-Finished, Trading Goods, or Spare Parts. Used for balance sheet sub-classification and COGS analysis.']
  last_price_change_date date [note: 'The date on which the standard price or moving average price was last changed for this material at the valuation area. Corresponds to SAP MBEW.LAEPR. Used for price variance analysis, audit trails, and identifying stale standard costs.']
  lowest_value_devaluation_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage devaluation applied to the inventory under the lowest value principle (LVP) for balance sheet reporting. Reflects write-downs for slow-moving, obsolete, or damaged stock. Corresponds to SAP MBEW.ABWKZ / devaluation indicator logic. Required for IFRS IAS 2 net realizable value (NRV) assessment.']
  method varchar [note: 'The accounting valuation method applied to determine the cost of inventory. Standard Cost uses a predetermined cost; Moving Average Price recalculates with each receipt; FIFO (First-In First-Out) and LIFO (Last-In First-Out) are layer-based methods. Determines COGS and balance sheet inventory value.']
  moving_average_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The current weighted moving average cost per unit of measure, recalculated after each goods receipt or invoice verification when price control is \'V\'. Corresponds to SAP MBEW.VERPR. Reflects the most current average procurement cost.']
  net_realizable_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The estimated selling price of the inventory in the ordinary course of business less the estimated costs of completion and the estimated costs necessary to make the sale. Required under IFRS IAS 2 to test whether inventory should be written down below cost. Used for period-end impairment assessment.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The SAP plant code representing the manufacturing facility or distribution center where the inventory is held and valued. Corresponds to MBEW.BWKEY (valuation area) when valuation is at plant level.']
  previous_standard_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The standard price that was in effect before the most recent price change. Corresponds to SAP MBEW.ZKPRS. Used for price variance analysis, standard cost roll comparison, and audit of cost changes during period-end closing.']
  price_control_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates the inventory valuation method applied to the material. \'S\' = Standard Price (fixed standard cost used for all movements); \'V\' = Moving Average Price (price recalculated with each goods receipt). Corresponds to SAP MBEW.VPRSV. Drives how inventory is valued on the balance sheet.']
  record_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this inventory valuation record was extracted from the source system and loaded into the silver layer lakehouse. Used for data freshness tracking, incremental load processing, and audit trail.']
  snapshot_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this valuation record was captured as part of a scheduled period-end closing, year-end closing, or an on-demand/ad-hoc valuation request. Used to distinguish official financial closing snapshots from operational queries.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this inventory valuation record was sourced. Primarily SAP S/4HANA MM (MBEW table), but may include Infor WMS for warehouse-level stock positions or manual adjustments. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  special_stock_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the inventory belongs to a special stock category. SAP special stock types include: E = Sales Order Stock, K = Consignment Stock at Customer, O = Parts Provided to Vendor, Q = Project Stock, V = Consignment Stock from Vendor, W = Vendor Managed Inventory. Empty for standard unrestricted stock.']
  total_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The total quantity of valuated stock on hand for the material at the plant/valuation area as of the valuation date. Corresponds to SAP MBEW.LBKUM. Used to calculate total stock value (total_stock_quantity × price).']
  total_stock_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total financial value of the valuated stock at the plant level, calculated as total_stock_quantity × (standard_price or moving_average_price) / price_unit. Corresponds to SAP MBEW.SALK3 (standard price) or MBEW.VERPR-based value. Used for balance sheet inventory reporting and period-end closing.']
  total_value_group_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total inventory value translated into the group (corporate) reporting currency for consolidated financial statements. Supports multinational consolidation and group-level balance sheet reporting. Derived from local currency value using the applicable exchange rate.']
  total_value_local_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total inventory value expressed in the local (company code) currency. Corresponds to SAP MBEW.SALK3 for standard price or the MAP-based value. Used for local statutory financial reporting and balance sheet preparation.']
  type varchar [note: 'SAP split valuation type (BWTAR) used when a single material is valuated differently based on origin, quality, or batch. For example, externally procured vs. internally produced stock may carry different values. Empty if split valuation is not active.']

  Note: 'Captures the financial valuation of inventory positions by SKU, plant, and valuation class at period-end and on-demand. Records valuation method (standard cost, moving average price, FIFO, LIFO), standard price, moving average price, total stock value, currency, valuation date, price control indicator, and last price change date. Supports COGS calculation, balance sheet inventory reporting, and period-end closing. Aligned with SAP MM material valuation (MBEW).'
}

Table inventory.wip_stock {
  wip_stock_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each WIP stock record in the silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for the wip_stock data product.']
  bop_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.bop_operation. Business justification: WIP stock is staged at a specific BOP operation step (e.g., \'after welding\', \'before painting\'). Production control uses this to locate WIP on the shop floor and calculate operation-level throughput a']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: WIP stock balances are calculated and frozen per fiscal period for period-end financial reporting. Manufacturing finance teams run WIP settlement by fiscal period to transfer costs to finished goods o']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: wip_stock.batch_number is a denormalized batch reference. WIP inventory is often batch-managed. Normalizing to lot_batch_id FK enables batch-level traceability for WIP.']
  production_order_cost_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.production_order_cost. Business justification: Work-in-process inventory must link to production order costs for accurate WIP valuation and variance calculation. Cost accounting uses this for manufacturing cost analysis.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: wip_stock.storage_location_code is a denormalized location code. WIP inventory is staged at specific storage locations on the shop floor. Normalizing to storage_location_id FK links WIP records to the']
  work_order_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.work_order_operation. Business justification: WIP stock is consumed and tracked at the work order operation level. Production controllers link WIP inventory to specific operations to track material consumption, backflushing, and in-process quanti']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'ABC inventory classification of the material being produced, indicating its relative value and management priority (A=high value, B=medium, C=low). Drives WIP monitoring frequency and management attention.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'The date on which the production order was actually fully confirmed and all WIP was converted to finished goods or settled. Populated upon order completion; null while order is open.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI company code representing the legal entity responsible for the production order and WIP valuation. Supports multi-entity financial consolidation and statutory reporting.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of the production order that has been confirmed as completed at the current or previous operations. Used to calculate remaining WIP and track production progress.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant where WIP stock is held. Supports multi-country regulatory compliance, customs reporting, and regional WIP analysis.']
  expected_completion_date date [note: 'The planned or revised expected date by which the production order is expected to be fully confirmed and WIP converted to finished goods. Critical for delivery commitment and ATP calculations.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (posting period) within the fiscal year for which WIP is calculated and reported. Used for period-end WIP balance sheet postings in SAP CO.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the WIP stock record is relevant for financial reporting and period-end WIP calculation. Aligns with SAP FI fiscal year variant.']
  goods_issue_date date [note: 'The date on which raw materials or components were issued from inventory to the production order, initiating the WIP position. Corresponds to SAP goods issue posting date.']
