  group_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the corporate group reporting currency (e.g., USD, EUR). Used for consolidated WIP valuation across all plants and regions.']
  last_confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time of the most recent operation confirmation posted against the production order in SAP PP or Siemens Opcenter MES. Tracks real-time production progress.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the plant\'s local currency used in WIP valuation (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Required for multi-currency financial reporting.']
  make_to_strategy varchar [note: 'Manufacturing strategy governing the production order: Make-To-Order (MTO), Make-To-Stock (MTS), Engineer-To-Order (ETO), or Assemble-To-Order (ATO). Determines how WIP is linked to customer demand.']
  mrp_controller varchar [note: 'The MRP controller code responsible for the material being produced. Used to assign WIP monitoring and exception management responsibilities to the correct planner.']
  operation_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the routing operation step where WIP resides (e.g., \'Welding\', \'Assembly\', \'Painting\'). Supports shop floor reporting and WIP analysis.']
  operation_number varchar [note: 'The routing operation step number (e.g., 0010, 0020) at which WIP is currently positioned within the production order. Identifies the specific manufacturing step where materials are in progress.']
  order_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total planned quantity of the production order. Used to calculate completion percentage and remaining WIP quantity against the original order target.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where the WIP stock resides. Enables multi-plant WIP reporting and inventory valuation by plant.']
  production_order_type varchar [note: 'SAP order type classification indicating the nature of the production order (e.g., PP01 standard production, PP02 repetitive, PI01 process industry). Drives cost collection and settlement rules.']
  production_scheduler varchar [note: 'The production scheduler or planner code responsible for managing the production order. Supports accountability tracking and workload distribution in production planning.']
  production_start_date date [note: 'The scheduled or actual start date of the production order. Used for WIP aging analysis, lead time calculation, and production scheduling performance reporting.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP CO profit center assigned to the production order. Enables WIP reporting and profitability analysis at the profit center level for management accounting.']
  scrap_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of material scrapped during the production process at the current operation. Impacts WIP valuation and is used for scrap rate analysis and quality reporting.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current status of the WIP stock record reflecting the production order\'s progress. Drives WIP reporting, financial period-end closing, and inventory position accuracy.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for the WIP quantity (e.g., EA, KG, L, M, PC). Aligns with the material master unit of measure in SAP.']
  valuation_method varchar [note: 'The inventory valuation method applied to calculate WIP value (e.g., standard cost, moving average, actual cost). Determines how WIP is valued on the balance sheet.']
  wip_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of material currently in progress at the specified operation step, expressed in the base unit of measure. Represents materials issued to production but not yet confirmed as finished goods.']
  wip_value_group_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'The financial value of WIP inventory translated to the corporate group reporting currency. Supports consolidated financial reporting across multinational operations.']
  wip_value_local_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'The financial value of WIP inventory in the plant\'s local currency, calculated using SAP CO WIP calculation (actual or standard cost). Used for balance sheet reporting and inventory valuation.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP work center identifier where the current operation is being performed. Represents the machine, production line, or labor group executing the operation.']

  Note: 'Tracks Work-In-Progress (WIP) inventory at the production order and operation level, capturing materials that have been issued to production but not yet confirmed as finished goods. Records production order reference, operation step, WIP quantity, WIP valuation, plant, cost center, WIP status (open, partially confirmed, fully confirmed), and expected completion date. Bridges the inventory and production domains for accurate WIP reporting. Aligned with SAP PP/CO WIP calculation.'
}

Table inventory.quarantine_hold {
  quarantine_hold_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each quarantine or quality hold record in the inventory management system.']
  calibration_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.calibration_record. Business justification: Inventory items failing calibration checks are placed on quarantine hold referencing the calibration record. Quality and metrology teams use this link to block non-conforming serialized tools or instr']
  ecn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: Quarantine holds are frequently triggered by or resolved through an Engineering Change Notice. Quality and engineering teams link the hold to the ECN that either caused the nonconformance or authorize']
  failure_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.failure_record. Business justification: When an asset failure is recorded, associated parts or components may be quarantined pending investigation. Quality teams link quarantine holds to the originating failure record to trace root cause an']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Quarantine holds are frequently placed on material at or immediately after goods receipt due to quality failures. Linking quarantine_hold to goods_receipt allows quality and procurement teams to block']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Quarantine holds that result in material write-offs or reclassification generate journal entries to adjust inventory value on the balance sheet. Quality-driven financial postings are standard in regul']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: quarantine_hold.batch_number is a denormalized batch reference. Normalizing to lot_batch_id FK enables batch-level traceability for quality holds.']
  regulatory_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_regulatory_certification. Business justification: Quarantine holds are frequently triggered by regulatory certification failures or expirations. Linking to the certification record allows quality and compliance teams to track which specific certifica']
  serialized_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit. Business justification: quarantine_hold.serial_number is a denormalized serial number reference. Normalizing to serialized_unit_id FK links serialized item holds to the serialized unit master for unit-level quality traceabil']
  sla_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.sla_agreement. Business justification: When inventory is quarantined that belongs to or is committed to a customer with an active SLA, the hold must reference that SLA to trigger escalation timelines and priority release decisions. Quality']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: quarantine_hold.storage_location is a denormalized location code. Normalizing to storage_location_id FK links each quarantine hold to the granular storage location.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Quarantine holds must reference the responsible supplier to trigger corrective action requests and update supplier quality scorecards. Procurement uses this link to manage supplier performance consequ']
  actual_release_date date [note: 'Actual calendar date on which the quarantine hold was resolved and the inventory was released, scrapped, returned, or otherwise dispositioned.']
  authorized_by_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee who authorized the disposition decision. Retained for audit trail and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  capa_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record initiated as a result of this quarantine hold. Links quality hold management to the broader quality improvement process.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code of the legal entity responsible for the quarantined inventory. Required for financial posting of scrap, rework costs, and vendor debit memos.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant or facility where the quarantine hold is active. Supports multi-country regulatory compliance and regional reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the quarantine hold record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the hold value and valuation price (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Supports multi-currency financial reporting for multinational operations.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final disposition decision made by the quality authority for the quarantined inventory. Drives downstream actions such as stock release, rework order creation, scrap posting, or vendor return.']
  disposition_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the rationale, conditions, or instructions associated with the disposition decision, including any deviations or special handling requirements.']
  expected_release_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the quarantine hold is expected to be resolved and the inventory released or dispositioned. Used for production planning, ATP calculations, and supply chain impact assessment.']
  hold_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the SKU/material placed under quarantine hold, expressed in the base unit of measure. Represents the total blocked stock quantity pending disposition.']
  hold_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the specific reason the inventory was placed on hold, providing context beyond the hold type classification for quality investigation and reporting.']
  hold_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized short code representing the hold reason, used for categorization, reporting, and integration with SAP QM defect codes and Infor WMS hold reason configuration.']
  hold_reference_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing alphanumeric reference number assigned to the quarantine hold, used for cross-system tracking and communication with quality, procurement, and operations teams.']
  hold_start_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the inventory was officially placed under quarantine hold. Used for aging analysis, SLA tracking, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  hold_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the quarantine hold was initiated in the system, including timezone offset. Used for exact duration calculations and audit trail.']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the quarantine hold indicating the primary reason category for blocking the stock. Drives the disposition workflow and responsible team assignment.']
  hold_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total financial value of the quarantined inventory (hold quantity × valuation price) in the local currency. Used for financial exposure reporting and management escalation thresholds.']
  initiating_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the source document (NCR number, GRN number, complaint ID, etc.) that triggered the quarantine hold, enabling cross-document traceability.']
  initiating_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of source document that triggered the quarantine hold (e.g., Non-Conformance Report, Goods Receipt Note, customer complaint). Establishes the business context and traceability chain.']
  inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'SAP QM inspection lot number linked to this quarantine hold, enabling direct traceability to the quality inspection results and usage decision in SAP.']
  inventory_category varchar [note: 'Category of the quarantined inventory indicating its stage in the production lifecycle (raw material, WIP, finished goods, MRO, etc.). Drives reporting segmentation and disposition workflows.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to the quarantine hold record. Supports change tracking, SLA monitoring, and data freshness validation.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing or distribution facility where the quarantined inventory is physically located.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulatory requirement, standard, or authority notice that triggered or governs the hold (e.g., REACH Article 33, RoHS Directive, OSHA citation). Applicable for regulatory holds.']
  release_authorization_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the formal authorization document approving the disposition decision and release of the quarantine hold. Required for regulated industries and audit compliance.']
  released_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the held inventory that was approved for release back to unrestricted stock following inspection or disposition. May be less than the original hold quantity if partial disposition occurred.']
  return_to_vendor_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the held inventory to be returned to the originating supplier. Initiates the vendor return process in SAP MM and may trigger supplier corrective action.']
  rework_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the held inventory directed to rework operations to bring it into conformance with specifications. Triggers rework production order creation in SAP PP/MES.']
  scrapped_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the held inventory that was dispositioned as scrap following inspection. Used for scrap rate reporting, COGS impact analysis, and waste tracking.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this quarantine hold record originated (e.g., SAP QM, Infor WMS, Siemens Opcenter MES). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the quarantine hold record, tracking progression from initial hold through inspection, disposition decision, and final resolution.']
  stock_type varchar [note: 'SAP stock type classification of the quarantined inventory (e.g., blocked stock, quality inspection stock). Determines how the stock is treated in MRP and availability checks.']
  supplier_number varchar [note: 'SAP vendor/supplier number associated with the quarantined material, applicable when the hold is related to incoming goods from a specific supplier. Enables supplier quality performance tracking.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for the hold quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for liter). Aligns with SAP base UoM for the material.']
  valuation_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Unit valuation price of the quarantined material at the time of hold creation, used to calculate the financial exposure of the blocked stock for inventory valuation and financial reporting.']

  Note: 'Manages inventory items placed under quality hold, quarantine, or blocked stock status pending inspection, disposition, or regulatory clearance. Captures hold reference number, SKU, batch, quantity, storage location, hold type (quality inspection, customer return, regulatory hold, damage hold), hold reason, initiating document (NCR, GRN, customer complaint), hold start date, expected release date, disposition decision (accept, rework, scrap, return to vendor), and release authorization. Aligned with SAP QM usage decision and Infor WMS hold management.'
}

Table inventory.physical_inventory {
  physical_inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each physical inventory count event record in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Physical inventory counts are conducted within a specific fiscal period and must be closed before period-end financial statements are finalized. Finance requires period assignment to validate inventor']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: A physical inventory count event is conducted at a specific warehouse or storage location. physical_inventory currently has plant_code (STRING) but no typed FK to storage_location, meaning the specifi']
  actual_count_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the physical inventory count was performed. May differ from planned_count_date due to operational delays or rescheduling.']
  adjustment_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP material document number generated when inventory variance adjustments are posted to the general ledger following the physical inventory count. Provides traceability from count to financial posting.']
  adjustment_posted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inventory variance adjustment document has been financially posted to the general ledger in SAP FI/MM following completion and approval of the physical inventory count.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the physical inventory variance adjustments require formal management approval before financial posting. Driven by variance value thresholds and internal controls policy.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status for the physical inventory variance adjustments: not_required (below threshold), pending (awaiting approver action), approved (authorized for posting), rejected (returned for investigation).']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the physical inventory variance adjustments were formally approved for financial posting. Provides audit trail for internal controls compliance.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the manager or inventory controller who approved the physical inventory variance adjustments for financial posting.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code representing the legal entity responsible for the inventory valuation and financial posting of variances identified during the physical inventory.']
  count_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when all physical inventory counting activities were completed. Used to calculate count duration and for audit trail purposes.']
  count_method varchar [note: 'The method used to perform the physical inventory count: manual (paper-based or visual count), scanner (barcode/QR scanner via WMS), rfid (Radio Frequency Identification automated reading), or automated (conveyor/sensor-based counting).']
  count_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the physical inventory counting activity commenced on the shop floor or warehouse. Used for duration tracking and audit trail.']
  count_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical inventory count scope: full_count (wall-to-wall count of all stock), partial_count (selected storage locations or material groups), or spot_check (targeted verification of specific SKUs or bins).']
  counted_sku_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct SKUs that have been physically counted as of the current status. Enables tracking of count completion progress against total_sku_count.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant or warehouse location where the physical inventory is conducted. Supports multi-country regulatory compliance and regional reporting.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the currency in which inventory variance values and financial postings are expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP MM physical inventory document number that uniquely identifies the physical inventory event in the source ERP system. Aligns with SAP transaction MI01/MI04.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the physical inventory count event is conducted and financially posted. Used for period-end inventory valuation and financial reporting.']
  goods_movements_blocked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all goods movements (goods receipts, goods issues, transfers) are blocked in the system for the scope of this physical inventory to prevent stock changes during counting.']
  infor_wms_count_code varchar [note: 'Reference identifier from the Infor WMS system for the corresponding physical inventory count task. Enables cross-system traceability between SAP MM physical inventory document and WMS count execution.']
  inventory_category varchar [note: 'The category of inventory stock included in the physical inventory scope: raw materials, work-in-progress (WIP), finished goods, maintenance-repair-operations (MRO), semi-finished goods, spare parts, or all categories.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing operational notes, exceptions, special circumstances, or instructions related to the physical inventory count event (e.g., system outage during count, partial area exclusions).']
  planned_count_date date [note: 'The scheduled calendar date on which the physical inventory count is planned to be executed. Used for resource planning and production freeze scheduling.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing plant or distribution center where the physical inventory count is being conducted.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting posting date on which inventory variance adjustments are financially posted to the general ledger. Determines the fiscal period impact of inventory differences.']
  production_freeze_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether production activities in the plant or warehouse must be frozen (halted) during the physical inventory count to ensure accurate stock snapshot. Critical for wall-to-wall full counts.']
  recount_sku_count varchar [note: 'Number of SKUs flagged for recount due to variances exceeding the tolerance threshold. Tracks the scope of the recount phase within the physical inventory event.']
  responsible_employee_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the inventory controller or warehouse manager responsible for the physical inventory count event. Retained for reporting and audit trail purposes.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the physical inventory count event: created (document created, counting not started), counting (active count in progress), recounting (recount triggered by variance), completed (all counts recorded), posted (variance adjustments financially posted), cancelled (event cancelled before completion).']
  total_sku_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) included in the scope of this physical inventory event. Represents the universe of materials to be counted.']
  total_variance_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total financial value of all inventory variances identified during the physical inventory count, expressed in the document currency. Represents the aggregate financial impact of stock differences requiring GL adjustment.']
  variance_sku_count varchar [note: 'Number of SKUs where the physically counted quantity differs from the book quantity beyond the defined tolerance threshold. Drives recount and adjustment decisions.']
  variance_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage tolerance threshold above which a counted quantity variance triggers a recount or mandatory adjustment. Defined per inventory policy and used to classify items as within or exceeding tolerance.']

  Note: 'Manages full physical inventory count events (annual or periodic wall-to-wall counts) at the warehouse or plant level. Captures physical inventory document number, warehouse/plant scope, count type (full count, partial count, spot check), planned count date, actual count date, count status (created, counting, completed, posted), total SKUs in scope, counted SKUs, variance count, total variance value, and approval status. Distinct from cycle_count which manages continuous rolling counts. Aligned with SAP MM physical inventory document.'
}

Table asset.class {
  class_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each asset classification record within the enterprise asset taxonomy.']
  classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.classification. Business justification: Asset classes are aligned to product classification schemes (e.g., equipment taxonomy). Asset management and product engineering teams share classification structures to ensure consistent categorizati']
  applicable_standard varchar [note: 'Primary industry or regulatory standard governing the design, operation, or maintenance of assets in this class (e.g., ISO 9001, IEC 61131, ANSI/NFPA 70, CE Marking, UL 508A).']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the asset class represents a fixed asset, movable asset, leased asset, or tooling, driving accounting treatment and depreciation rules under IFRS/GAAP.']
  capitalization_threshold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum acquisition cost (in base currency) above which an asset of this class must be capitalized as a fixed asset rather than expensed as OPEX, per company CAPEX policy and GAAP/IFRS guidelines.']
  capitalization_threshold_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the capitalization threshold amount, supporting multi-currency operations across global manufacturing sites.']
  category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of the asset class into a major category (e.g., production equipment, facility infrastructure, IT/OT systems) for portfolio-level reporting and CAPEX/OPEX planning.']
  ce_marking_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets of this class require CE Marking for operation within the European Union, ensuring conformity with applicable EU directives (Machinery Directive, Low Voltage Directive, EMC Directive).']
  code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the asset class, used for cross-system referencing between Maximo EAM and SAP S/4HANA PM module.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Default SAP cost center code for assets of this class, used for OPEX maintenance cost allocation and management accounting reporting in SAP S/4HANA CO module.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this asset class record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  criticality_rating varchar [note: 'Standardized criticality rating for assets of this class based on impact to production throughput, safety, and quality. Drives maintenance prioritization, spare parts stocking strategy, and OEE reporting.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting depreciation method applied to assets of this class, governing how asset value is expensed over its useful life in compliance with IFRS/GAAP financial reporting standards.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the asset class, including its typical function, application context, and distinguishing characteristics within the manufacturing environment.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this asset class definition becomes active and valid for use in asset creation and classification across EAM and ERP systems.']
  energy_class varchar [note: 'Energy efficiency classification for assets of this class, supporting ISO 50001 energy management compliance and OPEX energy cost reporting across manufacturing facilities.']
  environmental_risk_class varchar [note: 'Environmental risk classification for assets of this class, indicating potential environmental impact (e.g., refrigerant leaks, chemical containment, emissions) for compliance with ISO 14001 and EPA regulations.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which this asset class definition is no longer valid for new asset creation, supporting controlled deprecation of obsolete classifications in the enterprise taxonomy.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'SAP General Ledger account code associated with this asset class for CAPEX capitalization and OPEX expense posting, ensuring correct financial accounting treatment in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of this asset class within the classification hierarchy tree, where level 1 represents the root category and higher numbers represent more granular sub-classifications.']
  iiot_monitoring_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets of this class are configured for real-time condition monitoring via the Siemens MindSphere IIoT platform, enabling predictive maintenance and digital performance analytics.']
  inspection_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory or certification body responsible for mandating or conducting inspections for assets of this class (e.g., OSHA, TÜV, UL, CE Notified Body, local fire authority).']
  inspection_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Mandatory interval in calendar days between regulatory compliance inspections for assets of this class, as prescribed by applicable governing bodies (OSHA, CE, UL, local regulations).']
  inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets of this class are subject to mandatory regulatory inspections (e.g., pressure vessels, lifting equipment, electrical installations) as required by OSHA, CE Marking, or UL certification standards.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this asset class record, used for change tracking, data synchronization between Maximo EAM and SAP S/4HANA, and audit compliance.']
  maintenance_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Standard interval in calendar days between scheduled preventive maintenance activities for assets of this class, used to auto-generate work orders in Maximo EAM.']
  maintenance_strategy varchar [note: 'Default maintenance strategy class assigned to assets of this type, aligning with Total Productive Maintenance (TPM) and Reliability-Centered Maintenance (RCM) frameworks configured in Maximo EAM.']
  mean_time_between_failures_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Industry-standard or OEM-specified Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) in hours for assets of this class, used as a benchmark for reliability analysis, maintenance planning, and OEE calculations.']
  mean_time_to_repair_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Industry-standard or OEM-specified Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) in hours for assets of this class, used to estimate downtime impact, plan maintenance labor resources, and set SLA targets for work orders.']
  name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the asset class (e.g., CNC Machining Center, Industrial Robot, PLC Controller, HVAC System, Power Transformer).']
  reach_rohs_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets of this class contain substances subject to REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals) or RoHS (Restriction of Hazardous Substances) compliance requirements.']
  residual_value_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated residual (salvage) value of assets in this class expressed as a percentage of original acquisition cost, used in depreciation base calculations per IFRS IAS 16.']
  safety_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets of this class are designated as safety-critical, requiring enhanced maintenance protocols, mandatory inspection records, and compliance with ISO 45001 occupational health and safety standards.']
  sap_asset_class_code varchar [note: 'Corresponding asset class code in SAP S/4HANA FI-AA module, used for financial accounting, depreciation area assignment, and CAPEX tracking. Ensures EAM-ERP synchronization.']
  scada_integrated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets of this class are integrated with SCADA systems for supervisory control and real-time operational data acquisition, relevant for OT/IT convergence and cybersecurity compliance.']
  spare_parts_strategy varchar [note: 'Default spare parts inventory strategy for assets of this class, used to configure MRO (Maintenance, Repair and Operations) inventory levels in SAP MM and Infor WMS.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the asset class record, controlling whether new assets can be created under this classification in EAM and ERP systems.']
  subcategory varchar [note: 'Secondary classification level within the asset category, providing finer granularity (e.g., CNC Machining, Robotic Welding, SCADA Systems, HVAC Chillers) for maintenance strategy alignment.']
  ul_certification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets of this class require UL (Underwriters Laboratories) product safety certification for operation in North American markets.']
  useful_life_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard expected useful life in years for assets belonging to this class, used to calculate depreciation schedules and plan asset replacement cycles under IFRS IAS 16.']
  warranty_duration_months varchar [note: 'Standard manufacturer warranty duration in months applicable to assets of this class, used to configure warranty tracking and claim management in Maximo EAM and Salesforce Service Cloud.']

  Note: 'Reference classification taxonomy for manufacturing assets defining asset categories (e.g., CNC Machining Center, Industrial Robot, PLC Controller, HVAC System, Power Transformer), depreciation class, maintenance strategy class, and regulatory inspection requirements. Used for standardized asset categorization across EAM and ERP systems.'
}

Table asset.maintenance_plan {
  maintenance_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the maintenance plan record within the enterprise asset management system.']
  account_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account_hierarchy. Business justification: Maintenance plans are created for a customer\'s equipment portfolio under a service contract. Service planners assign maintenance plans to the customer account to schedule preventive maintenance visits']
  class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_class. Business justification: Maintenance plans may be templated by asset class for standardized preventive maintenance across similar equipment types. This enables asset class-level maintenance strategy management.']
  cost_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_allocation. Business justification: Preventive maintenance plans carry annual budgeted costs assigned to a cost center. Plant controllers use this link to compare planned vs. actual maintenance spend per cost center during budget review']
  entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.entitlement. Business justification: Maintenance plans are scoped by what the customer is entitled to receive under their service agreement. Asset managers validate entitlement before scheduling planned maintenance to ensure the work is ']
  parts_origin_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.location. Business justification: Maintenance plans specify which stocking location supplies the required spare parts. Planners reference this logistics location when generating planned orders to ensure parts are sourced from the corr']
  product_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_configuration. Business justification: Maintenance plans are designed for specific product configurations. A motor with a specific voltage/power configuration requires a different maintenance schedule than another variant. Maintenance engi']
  tooling_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.tooling_equipment. Business justification: Maintenance plans specify which engineering-defined tooling and equipment is required to perform maintenance activities. Maintenance planners reference engineering tooling records to ensure correct to']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the maintenance manager or engineering authority who approved the maintenance plan for active use. Required for regulatory compliance and quality management system documentation.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which the maintenance plan was formally approved by the responsible authority. Required for quality management system (QMS) compliance and regulatory audit documentation.']
  call_horizon_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'SAP PM call horizon parameter expressed as a percentage of the maintenance cycle. Defines how early within the scheduling horizon a maintenance call is created (e.g., 100% means calls are created at the full horizon distance).']
  completion_requirement varchar [note: 'Indicates whether completion of maintenance activities under this plan is mandatory (regulatory or safety-critical), recommended (best practice), or optional (condition-dependent). Drives escalation and overdue handling logic.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'SAP CO cost center code to which maintenance costs generated by this plan are allocated. Enables OPEX tracking, departmental cost reporting, and budget variance analysis.']
  counter_reading_at_last_service decimal(18,4) [note: 'Meter or counter value (e.g., operating hours, production cycles, kilometers) recorded at the time of the last completed maintenance activity. Used as the baseline for counter-based scheduling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when the maintenance plan record was first created in the source system (SAP PM or Maximo EAM). Used for data lineage, audit trails, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the estimated and actual cost values on this maintenance plan (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multinational cost consolidation and reporting.']
  cycle_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the maintenance cycle value. Supports both time-based units (days, weeks, months) and counter-based units (operating hours, production cycles, kilometers) per SAP PM counter configuration.']
  cycle_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric value defining the maintenance cycle interval. For time-based plans this is the duration (e.g., 90 days), for counter-based plans this is the usage count (e.g., 500 operating hours or 10,000 production cycles).']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the scope, objectives, and coverage of the maintenance plan including equipment types, maintenance activities, and regulatory drivers.']
  end_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the maintenance plan expires and ceases to generate new maintenance calls. Null indicates an open-ended plan with no defined expiry.']
  equipment_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the equipment type covered by this maintenance plan. Aligns with SAP PM equipment categories and supports filtering for CNC machines, PLC systems, SCADA infrastructure, and other asset classes.']
  estimated_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned cost in the plan currency for one execution cycle of the maintenance activities, including labor, materials, and external services. Used for OPEX budgeting and CAPEX vs OPEX classification.']
  estimated_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned duration in hours required to complete one execution of the maintenance activities defined in this plan. Used for resource capacity planning, downtime scheduling, and OEE impact assessment.']
  external_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Cross-system reference identifier for this maintenance plan in the alternate source system (e.g., Maximo PM Program ID when the master record is in SAP PM, or vice versa). Enables reconciliation between SAP PM and Maximo EAM.']
  last_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recently executed maintenance activity under this plan was completed and confirmed. Used as the baseline for calculating the next due date in time-based plans.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to the maintenance plan record in the source system. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail maintenance.']
  last_scheduled_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent maintenance call was scheduled under this plan. Used to calculate the next due date and track scheduling cadence compliance.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days before the scheduled maintenance due date that a work order should be generated, allowing time for spare parts procurement, resource scheduling, and production planning coordination.']
  maintenance_plant varchar [note: 'The plant responsible for executing the maintenance activities, which may differ from the plant where the asset is located in multi-site or centralized maintenance organizations.']
  maintenance_type varchar [note: 'Operational classification of the maintenance execution type, distinguishing planned scheduled activities from emergency or shutdown-based maintenance windows.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the maintenance plan, typically reflecting the asset type, maintenance strategy, and frequency (e.g., \'Annual Turbine Overhaul Plan\').']
  next_due_date date [note: 'Calculated date on which the next maintenance activity is due based on the cycle parameters, last completion date, and scheduling rules. Critical for maintenance scheduling dashboards and overdue tracking.']
  notification_type varchar [note: 'Type of maintenance notification generated when the plan triggers a maintenance call. SAP PM notification types (M1 activity report, M2 malfunction report, M3 maintenance request) or digital alert channels.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'SAP PM order type or Maximo work order type that will be generated when this maintenance plan triggers a maintenance call. Determines the workflow, settlement rules, and cost accounting treatment.']
  plan_category varchar [note: 'Business category of the maintenance plan aligned with Total Productive Maintenance (TPM) disciplines: preventive, predictive, lubrication, calibration, regulatory inspection, or major overhaul.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the maintenance plan, used for cross-system referencing between SAP PM and Maximo EAM.']
  planner_group varchar [note: 'SAP PM planner group code identifying the maintenance planning team responsible for managing and scheduling this maintenance plan. Used for workload distribution and planning accountability.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant or Maximo site code identifying the manufacturing facility or operational site where the assets covered by this maintenance plan are located.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the maintenance plan, used for resource allocation and scheduling conflict resolution when multiple maintenance activities compete for the same assets or technicians.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory standard, clause, or legal requirement that mandates this maintenance plan (e.g., \'OSHA 29 CFR 1910.217\', \'ISO 45001 Clause 8.1\', \'EPA 40 CFR Part 63\'). Required for compliance audit trails.']
  regulatory_requirement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this maintenance plan is mandated by a regulatory body (e.g., OSHA, EPA, CE Marking, ISO 45001, pressure vessel inspection regulations). Regulatory plans receive elevated priority and compliance tracking.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version or revision identifier for the maintenance plan, incremented when significant changes are made to cycle parameters, scope, or strategy. Supports change management and audit trail requirements.']
  safety_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the maintenance plan covers safety-critical equipment or activities where failure could result in personnel injury, environmental incident, or production safety hazard. Triggers mandatory completion tracking under ISO 45001.']
  scheduling_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Forward-looking planning window in days within which the maintenance plan will generate scheduled work orders. Defines how far ahead the system creates maintenance calls for resource and capacity planning.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this maintenance plan record originated: SAP S/4HANA PM module, Maximo EAM, or manually entered. Supports data lineage and reconciliation between systems.']
  start_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the maintenance plan becomes active and begins generating scheduled maintenance calls or work orders.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the maintenance plan indicating whether it is being drafted, actively scheduling work orders, suspended, or retired.']
  strategy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance strategy governing this plan. Time-based triggers on calendar intervals, counter-based on usage meters (hours, cycles), condition-based on sensor thresholds, and predictive leverages IIoT/MindSphere analytics.']
  tolerance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Allowable deviation percentage from the scheduled maintenance due date or counter threshold before the maintenance is considered overdue. Used to accommodate production scheduling flexibility without triggering compliance violations.']
  work_center varchar [note: 'SAP PM or Maximo work center code identifying the maintenance team, crew, or organizational unit responsible for executing the maintenance activities defined in this plan.']

  Note: 'Defines preventive and time-based maintenance strategies for equipment assets including TPM schedules, inspection intervals, lubrication cycles, calibration frequencies, and regulatory inspection plans. Captures maintenance strategy type (time-based, counter-based, condition-based), cycle parameters, lead times, and scheduling horizon. Sourced from SAP PM Maintenance Plans and Maximo EAM PM Programs.'
}

Table asset.maintenance_item {
  maintenance_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each individual maintenance task item within a maintenance plan. Serves as the primary key for the maintenance_item entity in the asset domain.']
  maintenance_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.maintenance_plan. Business justification: Maintenance items are individual tasks within a maintenance plan. Adding maintenance_plan_id FK establishes the parent-child relationship and allows removal of the denormalized plan_number string.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Maintenance items (spare parts, consumables) are procured via purchase orders. Maintenance planners reference the PO that sourced the part used in a maintenance activity — a daily operational link in ']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Each maintenance item (spare part or consumable) is sourced from a specific supplier. Procurement and maintenance teams track which supplier provides each item for reordering and warranty claims.']
  task_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.task_list. Business justification: maintenance_item currently stores task_list_number (STRING) and task_list_type (STRING) as denormalized references to the task_list product. task_list has a `number` and `type` attribute that are the ']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'Cost accounting activity type used to allocate internal labor costs for this maintenance task to the appropriate cost center. Links maintenance execution to financial controlling (CO) in SAP.']
  calibration_standard varchar [note: 'Reference to the applicable calibration standard or specification that governs this calibration task item (e.g., \'ISO 9001 Clause 7.1.5\', \'NIST Traceable\', \'IEC 61315\'). Applicable only when task_type is \'calibration\'. Supports quality management system compliance.']
  control_key varchar [note: 'SAP PM control key that determines how the maintenance operation is processed, including whether it is internally performed, externally serviced, or a milestone operation. Controls costing, confirmation, and scheduling behavior.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Financial cost center code to which the costs of this maintenance task are allocated. Enables maintenance cost tracking by organizational unit, department, or production area for management accounting purposes.']
  cost_classification varchar [note: 'Financial classification of the maintenance task cost as either Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) — improving or extending asset life — or Operational Expenditure (OPEX) — routine maintenance to sustain current performance. Critical for financial reporting and asset accounting.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the estimated and actual cost values associated with this maintenance item (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations in a multinational enterprise.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this maintenance item record was created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with ISO 9001 document control requirements.']
  estimated_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned total cost estimate for executing this maintenance task item, including labor, materials, and external services. Used for maintenance budget planning, CAPEX/OPEX classification, and cost variance analysis.']
  estimated_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned time in hours required to complete this maintenance task item. Used for work order scheduling, capacity planning, and labor cost estimation. Basis for Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) benchmarking.']
  expected_measurement_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Target or nominal measurement value expected at the measurement point during inspection or calibration. Used to assess whether the equipment is operating within acceptable parameters and to trigger corrective actions when deviations occur.']
  hazardous_material_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this maintenance task involves handling, disposal, or exposure to hazardous materials (chemicals, lubricants, refrigerants, etc.). Triggers REACH/RoHS compliance checks and hazardous waste disposal procedures.']
  interval_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the maintenance interval, defining whether the interval is time-based (days, weeks, months), usage-based (operating hours, cycles, kilometers), or event-based. Determines the scheduling trigger type.']
  interval_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric value of the maintenance interval at which this task should be performed (e.g., 90 for every 90 days, 500 for every 500 operating hours). Combined with interval_unit to define the full maintenance cycle.']
  item_number varchar [note: 'Sequential counter number identifying the position of this task item within its parent maintenance plan or task list. Used for ordering and referencing individual steps (e.g., 0010, 0020, 0030 in SAP PM convention).']
  item_text varchar [note: 'Short descriptive text summarizing the specific maintenance task to be performed, such as \'Inspect hydraulic seals\', \'Lubricate conveyor bearings\', or \'Calibrate pressure sensor\'. Used for display and search.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this maintenance item record was last updated. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and data freshness monitoring in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in advance that preparation activities (material procurement, permit acquisition, resource booking) must begin before the scheduled execution date of this maintenance task. Supports proactive maintenance scheduling.']
  lockout_tagout_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) energy isolation procedures must be applied before performing this maintenance task. Mandatory for tasks involving electrical, hydraulic, pneumatic, or thermal energy sources to prevent accidental energization.']
  long_text varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the maintenance task including step-by-step instructions, technical specifications, acceptance criteria, and any special notes for the technician. Supports Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) documentation.']
  maintenance_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of maintenance strategy this item belongs to, distinguishing between preventive (scheduled), predictive (condition-triggered), corrective (reactive), statutory (regulatory-mandated), and other maintenance types. Supports TPM and reliability-centered maintenance (RCM) analysis.']
  material_list_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this maintenance task item has an associated list of required spare parts, consumables, or materials (Bill of Materials for maintenance). When true, material components are defined in the task list component assignment.']
  measurement_point varchar [note: 'Identifier or description of the specific measurement point on the equipment where readings are to be taken during inspection or condition monitoring tasks (e.g., \'Bearing Temperature - Drive End\', \'Vibration - Motor NDE\'). Supports IIoT and condition-based maintenance.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the expected and actual measurement values recorded during this maintenance task (e.g., °C, bar, mm/s, kPa, RPM, mA). Ensures consistent data capture and comparison against specifications.']
  number_of_technicians varchar [note: 'Number of maintenance technicians or workers required to perform this task item simultaneously. Used for crew sizing, capacity planning, and safety compliance (e.g., confined space entry requiring minimum two persons).']
  permit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal Permit to Work (PTW) is required before executing this maintenance task. Applies to high-risk activities such as hot work, confined space entry, electrical isolation, or working at height. Mandatory for safety compliance.']
  permit_type varchar [note: 'Type of work permit required for this maintenance task when permit_required is true. Determines the safety authorization workflow, hazard controls, and regulatory compliance requirements that must be satisfied before work begins.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the manufacturing plant or facility where this maintenance item is applicable. Supports multi-plant operations and plant-level maintenance reporting in a multinational enterprise.']
  ppe_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of the Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) required for safe execution of this maintenance task (e.g., \'Safety glasses, chemical-resistant gloves, steel-toed boots, hearing protection\'). Supports safety compliance and technician briefing.']
  qualification_requirement varchar [note: 'Required skill, trade certification, or qualification level that the assigned technician must possess to perform this maintenance task (e.g., \'Certified Electrician\', \'Hydraulics Level 2\', \'Confined Space Entry Certified\'). Supports workforce compliance.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version or revision identifier of this maintenance item definition, tracking changes made to task instructions, intervals, or requirements over time. Supports document control and change management in compliance with ISO 9001.']
  sop_reference varchar [note: 'Document number or identifier of the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that governs the execution of this maintenance task. Ensures technicians follow approved procedures and supports ISO 9001 document control requirements.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the maintenance item within the maintenance plan. Controls whether the item is actively scheduled and executed. Inactive or obsolete items are retained for audit history.']
  task_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the specific maintenance activity to be performed. Drives material requirements, skill requirements, and safety protocols. Supports Total Productive Maintenance (TPM) task decomposition and reporting.']
  tolerance_lower_limit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable measurement value for this maintenance task. Readings below this threshold indicate a non-conformance condition requiring corrective action or Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) initiation.']
  tolerance_upper_limit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable measurement value for this maintenance task. Readings above this threshold indicate a non-conformance condition requiring corrective action or Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) initiation.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this maintenance item definition becomes effective and applicable for scheduling and execution. Supports time-bound validity of maintenance task definitions and change management.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this maintenance item definition remains effective. After this date, the item is no longer scheduled or executed. Supports lifecycle management and planned obsolescence of maintenance tasks.']
  work_center varchar [note: 'Identifier of the maintenance work center (crew, team, or workshop) responsible for executing this task item. Used for capacity planning, scheduling, and labor cost allocation in SAP PM and Maximo.']

  Note: 'Individual maintenance task items within a maintenance plan, defining the specific inspection, lubrication, replacement, or calibration activity to be performed on a piece of equipment at a defined interval. Captures task list reference, work center assignment, estimated duration, required materials, and safety instructions. Supports TPM task decomposition.'
}

Table asset.asset_notification {
  asset_notification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the maintenance notification record in the lakehouse silver layer. Serves as the primary key for this entity.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Asset notifications (breakdowns, alerts) are raised by or reported to a specific customer contact. Maintenance coordinators use this link to communicate fault status, schedule access, and obtain sign-']
  installed_base_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.installed_base. Business justification: Every asset notification references the specific installed asset at the customer site. Dispatchers and planners use this link to identify which customer-owned equipment is affected and route the notif']
  assembly varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific assembly, sub-component, or part of the equipment where the fault or damage was observed. Supports root cause analysis and spare parts planning.']
  breakdown_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the notification represents a complete equipment breakdown (unplanned stoppage) as opposed to a degraded performance or minor fault condition. Drives breakdown-specific KPI reporting and emergency response protocols.']
  category varchar [note: 'Operational category classifying the maintenance trigger strategy: PREVENTIVE (scheduled TPM), CORRECTIVE (reactive repair), PREDICTIVE (IIoT/MindSphere condition-based alert), CONDITION_BASED (sensor threshold breach), EMERGENCY (immediate safety/production risk). Supports TPM and maintenance strategy analytics.']
  cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code from the cause catalog identifying the root cause of the equipment failure or malfunction (e.g., operator error, material fatigue, lubrication failure). Supports CAPA processes and reliability engineering.']
  cause_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the identified root cause corresponding to the cause code. Provides narrative context for CAPA documentation and reliability improvement initiatives.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the notification was formally closed after business completion confirmation, distinguishing technical completion from administrative closure. Used for lifecycle duration analytics.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the notification was marked as technically completed in the source system, indicating all associated maintenance tasks have been executed and documented.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant or facility where the notified asset is located. Supports multinational regulatory compliance reporting and regional maintenance performance benchmarking.']
  damage_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code from the damage catalog identifying the type of physical damage or defect observed on the equipment (e.g., wear, corrosion, fracture, leakage). Supports failure pattern analysis and FMEA validation.']
  damage_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the observed damage or defect corresponding to the damage code. Provides human-readable context for maintenance planners and engineers performing root cause analysis.']
  description varchar [note: 'Short text description of the malfunction, damage, or maintenance activity as entered by the reporter. Provides the primary narrative of the issue for maintenance planners and technicians.']
  effect_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing the operational effect or consequence of the malfunction on production (e.g., production stoppage, reduced output, quality defect). Used in FMEA severity assessment and impact analysis.']
  environment_relevant boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the notified fault has potential environmental impact (e.g., chemical leak, emissions exceedance). Triggers environmental incident reporting per ISO 14001 and EPA regulatory requirements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the notification record in the source system. Used for incremental data extraction, change tracking, and audit trail maintenance in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  long_text varchar [note: 'Extended free-text narrative providing detailed description of the fault, observed symptoms, environmental conditions, and any immediate actions taken by the operator at the time of reporting.']
  main_work_center varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary maintenance work center (crew or team) responsible for executing the maintenance activities triggered by this notification. Used for capacity planning and workload balancing.']
  malfunction_end_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment malfunction or breakdown was resolved and the asset was returned to operational status. Used for downtime duration calculation and MTTR analysis.']
  malfunction_end_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the equipment malfunction was resolved and normal operations resumed. Combined with malfunction start timestamp to compute actual downtime for MTTR and OEE calculations.']
  malfunction_start_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment malfunction or breakdown actually began, as reported by the operator. May differ from the reported date if the fault was observed after the fact. Used for downtime duration calculation.']
  malfunction_start_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the equipment malfunction or breakdown commenced. Used in conjunction with malfunction end timestamp to calculate actual downtime duration for OEE and MTBF/MTTR reporting.']
  number varchar [note: 'Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the maintenance notification as assigned by the source system (SAP PM or Maximo EAM). Used for cross-system traceability and shop floor reference.']
  object_part_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific part or component of the equipment object where the damage or fault was detected. Enables component-level failure frequency analysis and targeted preventive maintenance planning.']
  planner_group varchar [note: 'Code identifying the maintenance planning group responsible for processing this notification and creating the associated work order. Determines routing of the notification to the correct maintenance team.']
  planning_plant varchar [note: 'Plant code of the maintenance planning plant responsible for processing and planning the work associated with this notification. May differ from the plant where the asset is located in centralized maintenance organizations.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code or Maximo site identifier representing the manufacturing facility where the notified asset is located. Enables plant-level maintenance performance benchmarking across the multinational enterprise.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the notification indicating urgency of response: 1 (Very High/Emergency), 2 (High), 3 (Medium), 4 (Low). Influences work order scheduling and resource allocation.']
  reported_by varchar [note: 'User ID or personnel number of the individual who created and submitted the maintenance notification, typically a machine operator, technician, or shift supervisor on the shop floor.']
  reported_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the maintenance notification was formally reported and entered into the system. Used for aging analysis and SLA compliance measurement.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the notification was created in the source system, including timezone offset. Critical for calculating response time SLAs and shift-based analysis.']
  required_end_date date [note: 'Date by which the maintenance work associated with this notification must be completed. Defines the deadline for work order execution and is used for SLA breach detection and escalation.']
  required_start_date date [note: 'Date by which maintenance work associated with this notification must commence, as specified by the requester or maintenance planner. Used for scheduling and SLA compliance tracking.']
  safety_relevant boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the notified malfunction or damage has safety implications for personnel, equipment, or the environment. Triggers mandatory escalation to HSE teams and may invoke OSHA/ISO 45001 incident reporting protocols.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this notification record was sourced (e.g., SAP PM, Maximo EAM). Supports data lineage tracking and multi-system reconciliation in the lakehouse.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Native primary key or record identifier of the notification in the originating source system (e.g., SAP QMNUM or Maximo SR number). Enables reverse lookup and reconciliation with the system of record.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the notification using SAP PM system status codes: OSNO (Outstanding, Not in Process), NOPR (Notification in Process), ORAS (Order Assigned), NOCO (Notification Completed), CLSD (Closed), CANC (Cancelled). Drives workflow routing and reporting.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance notification per SAP PM standard types: M1 (Malfunction Report), M2 (Activity Request), M3 (Inspection Request), M4 (General Maintenance Request). Drives downstream work order creation logic and prioritization.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the maintenance work order created from this notification. Captures the downstream linkage between the notification trigger and the execution order for traceability and audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Maintenance notification records capturing equipment malfunction reports, damage notifications, activity requests, and operator-initiated fault reports from the shop floor. Serves as the upstream trigger for work order creation. Captures notification type (M1-malfunction, M2-activity, M3-inspection), reported damage, cause codes, and breakdown indicators. Sourced from SAP PM Notifications (QMEL) and Maximo EAM Service Requests.'
}

Table asset.measurement_point {
  measurement_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for a measurement point or counter position defined on a piece of equipment or technical object. Serves as the primary key for the measurement point entity.']
  maintenance_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.maintenance_plan. Business justification: measurement_point has a maintenance_strategy_code STRING attribute indicating its association with a maintenance strategy. In EAM counter-based maintenance, measurement points (counters) are directly ']
  product_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.product_specification. Business justification: Measurement points on assets are defined against product specifications from engineering, which set the nominal values and tolerance limits. Condition monitoring teams use this link to evaluate whethe']
  alert_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automated alerts or notifications are configured for this measurement point when readings breach the defined upper or lower limit thresholds. Drives real-time alarm management in SCADA and MindSphere.']
  calibration_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between required calibration events for the sensor or instrument at this measurement point. Used to schedule preventive calibration work orders and ensure measurement traceability.']
  calibration_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sensor or instrument associated with this measurement point requires periodic calibration to maintain measurement accuracy. Drives calibration work order generation and compliance with metrology standards.']
  category varchar [note: 'Classifies the measurement point as either a \'counter\' (accumulating numeric value such as operating hours or cycle count used for counter-based maintenance triggering) or a \'characteristic\' (instantaneous measurement such as temperature, pressure, or vibration used for condition monitoring).']
  characteristic_code varchar [note: 'Reference code linking this measurement point to a classification characteristic in the EAM/PLM system (e.g., SAP classification characteristic or Teamcenter attribute). Enables structured querying and grouping of measurement points by engineering characteristic.']
  condition_monitoring_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this measurement point is actively used for condition-based maintenance (CBM) monitoring. When true, readings are evaluated against thresholds to trigger predictive maintenance work orders via MindSphere or SAP PM.']
  counter_overflow_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Maximum value a counter-type measurement point can reach before rolling over to zero. Critical for correctly computing cumulative counter readings across overflow events (e.g., an odometer rolling over at 999,999 km). Applicable only to category=\'counter\'.']
  counter_reading_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the counter increments upward (ascending, e.g., operating hours accumulating) or decrements downward (descending, e.g., remaining service life countdown). Applicable only to category=\'counter\'.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this measurement point record was first created in the source system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and lifecycle tracking for the measurement point.']
  decimal_places varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places to which measurement readings are recorded and displayed for this measurement point. Controls precision of data capture and reporting.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent calibration of the sensor or instrument at this measurement point was performed. Used to calculate next calibration due date and assess current measurement validity.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this measurement point record was most recently updated in the source system. Used for change detection, incremental data loading in the lakehouse Silver layer, and audit compliance.']
  last_reading_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or automated process that recorded the most recent measurement reading. Supports audit trail and data quality validation for manual readings.']
  last_reading_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the most recent measurement reading was recorded at this point. Used to assess data freshness, identify stale sensors, and support maintenance scheduling based on elapsed time since last reading.']
  last_reading_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Most recent measurement reading recorded at this measurement point. Provides the current state of the monitored parameter for real-time dashboards, maintenance scheduling, and condition assessment without querying the full reading history.']
  lower_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Minimum acceptable value for readings at this measurement point. Readings below this threshold trigger alerts, condition-based maintenance work orders, or SCADA alarms. Used in Statistical Process Control (SPC) and predictive maintenance analytics.']
  maintenance_strategy_code varchar [note: 'Code of the maintenance strategy (e.g., time-based, counter-based, condition-based) associated with this measurement point. Determines how maintenance plans and task lists are triggered based on readings from this point.']
  mindsphere_aspect_name varchar [note: 'Name of the MindSphere aspect (logical grouping of related time-series variables) to which this measurement point\'s sensor variable belongs. Aspects organize sensor data streams for analytics and Digital Twin modeling in MindSphere.']
  mindsphere_asset_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the digital asset in Siemens MindSphere IIoT Platform to which this measurement point is bound. Enables real-time sensor data ingestion, edge computing, and predictive analytics via MindSphere.']
  mindsphere_variable_name varchar [note: 'Specific time-series variable name within the MindSphere aspect that maps to this measurement point. Provides the exact binding between the physical sensor signal and the MindSphere data model for real-time streaming and predictive analytics.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the measurement point that identifies its physical or logical location and purpose on the equipment (e.g., \'Spindle Vibration Sensor\', \'Operating Hours Counter\', \'Coolant Temperature\').']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code of the manufacturing plant or facility where the equipment bearing this measurement point is located. Supports multi-site reporting and plant-level condition monitoring dashboards.']
  plc_address varchar [note: 'Memory address or register reference in the PLC that provides the raw signal for this measurement point. Used for IT/OT convergence data pipelines to pull readings directly from PLC registers into the lakehouse.']
  point_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the measurement point as defined in the source EAM/CMMS system (e.g., SAP PM measurement point number). Used for cross-system referencing and shop-floor identification.']
  point_type varchar [note: 'Physical or engineering type of the measurement being captured at this point. Drives sensor configuration, alarm thresholds, and analytics models in Siemens MindSphere and SAP PM.']
  reading_method varchar [note: 'Indicates how measurement readings are captured: \'automatic\' (via IIoT sensor/SCADA integration), \'manual\' (technician enters reading during inspection or maintenance), or \'semi_automatic\' (sensor-assisted but requires manual confirmation).']
  sampling_interval_seconds varchar [note: 'Frequency in seconds at which sensor readings are captured and transmitted for this measurement point. Drives data volume planning, edge computing configuration, and real-time alerting latency in MindSphere and SCADA systems.']
  scada_tag_name varchar [note: 'Tag name or address in the SCADA or DCS system that corresponds to this measurement point. Enables mapping between the EAM measurement point and the operational technology (OT) data source for automated reading ingestion.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this measurement point record originates (e.g., SAP PM, Maximo EAM, Siemens MindSphere). Supports data lineage tracking and multi-system reconciliation in the Silver layer.']
  source_system_key varchar [note: 'Natural key or primary identifier of this measurement point record in the originating source system (e.g., SAP PM measurement point number, Maximo meter ID). Enables reverse lookup and reconciliation with the system of record.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the measurement point. \'active\' indicates readings are being collected; \'inactive\' means temporarily disabled; \'decommissioned\' means permanently retired; \'under_calibration\' means the point is offline for calibration; \'suspended\' means paused due to equipment downtime.']
  target_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Optimal or nominal expected value for readings at this measurement point under normal operating conditions. Used as the baseline for deviation analysis, OEE calculations, and predictive maintenance models.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Engineering unit in which measurement readings are recorded at this point (e.g., \'h\' for hours, \'rpm\' for revolutions per minute, \'°C\' for Celsius, \'bar\' for pressure, \'mm/s\' for vibration velocity). Aligns with SAP PM unit of measure configuration.']
  upper_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Maximum acceptable value for readings at this measurement point. Readings exceeding this threshold trigger alerts, condition-based maintenance work orders, or SCADA alarms. Used in SPC and predictive maintenance analytics.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Code of the work center or production cell associated with the equipment on which this measurement point resides. Enables OEE and condition monitoring analysis at the work center level.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date from which this measurement point definition is effective and readings should be collected. Supports temporal validity management for measurement point configurations that change over the asset lifecycle.']
  valid_to date [note: 'Date until which this measurement point definition is effective. After this date, the measurement point is considered expired or superseded. Used for lifecycle management and historical data segmentation.']

  Note: 'Defines measurement points and counter positions on equipment for condition monitoring, counter-based maintenance triggering, and IIoT sensor data integration. Captures measurement point category (counter, characteristic), unit of measure, upper/lower limits, and MindSphere sensor binding. Enables condition-based maintenance and predictive analytics via Siemens MindSphere.'
}

Table asset.measurement_reading {
  measurement_reading_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each measurement reading record in the asset domain. Serves as the primary key for the measurement_reading data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
