  framework_agreement_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of any active master supply agreement, blanket purchase order, or long-term framework contract with the account that governs pricing, terms, and delivery conditions for the plan period.']
  industry_vertical varchar [note: 'Industry sector classification of the account, used to align product offerings (automation systems, electrification solutions, smart infrastructure) with sector-specific needs and regulatory requirements.']
  is_global_account boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is managed as a global strategic account with coordinated multi-country engagement, requiring cross-regional sales team collaboration and consolidated reporting.']
  key_solution_focus varchar [note: 'Primary product or solution category targeted for growth within this account during the plan period, aligned to the company\'s core offerings in industrial automation, electrification, and smart infrastructure.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal review of the account plan, typically conducted quarterly during joint business reviews or pipeline review meetings to assess progress against targets.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording the most recent modification to the account plan record, used for change tracking, incremental data processing, and audit compliance.']
  plan_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the account plan period officially ends, after which the plan is reviewed for renewal, revision, or archival.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the strategic account plan, typically including the account name and planning cycle year (e.g., \'Siemens AG – FY2025 Strategic Account Plan\').']
  plan_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the account plan period officially begins, marking the start of the planning cycle for revenue targets, engagement activities, and strategic objectives.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the account plan by planning horizon: annual (12-month cycle), multi-year (2–5 year strategic), quarterly (90-day tactical), or campaign (project-specific engagement).']
  plan_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal or calendar year for which this strategic account plan is applicable, used to align planning cycles across the sales organization.']
  primary_competitor varchar [note: 'Name of the main competing vendor or supplier posing the greatest threat to revenue retention or growth within this account, used for competitive intelligence and win/loss analysis.']
  prior_year_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual revenue recognized from this account in the prior fiscal year, used as a baseline for growth target-setting and year-over-year performance benchmarking.']
  region varchar [note: 'Internal sales region designation (e.g., North America, EMEA, APAC) to which the account is assigned for territory management and quota allocation purposes.']
  relationship_strength varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of the current business relationship depth with the account, reflecting executive access, trust level, and historical engagement quality.']
  revenue_target_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total annual revenue target set for the account in the plan currency, representing the committed sales goal for the planning period across all product lines and solutions.']
  revenue_target_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the revenue target amount is denominated, supporting multi-currency planning for multinational accounts.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled cadence at which the account plan is formally reviewed and updated by the account team and sales leadership to track progress and adjust strategies.']
  sales_motion varchar [note: 'Primary go-to-market approach for this account: direct (company sales team engages the account directly), channel (engagement through distributor or partner), or hybrid (combination of both).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the account plan, tracking progression from initial drafting through approval, active execution, and eventual expiry or cancellation.']
  strategic_objective varchar [note: 'Primary strategic goal for the account during the plan period, such as expanding automation system footprint, displacing a competitor, securing a multi-year framework agreement, or growing electrification solution share.']
  whitespace_opportunity_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total addressable revenue from untapped product lines, solutions, or business units within the account where the company currently has no footprint, representing expansion potential.']

  Note: 'Strategic account planning record for key industrial B2B accounts including OEM partners, system integrators, and enterprise end-users. Captures annual revenue targets, strategic objectives, whitespace analysis, competitive positioning, and planned engagement activities per account. Supports direct and channel sales planning cycles.'
}

Table sales.territory_assignment {
  territory_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each territory assignment record linking a sales representative or channel partner to a specific territory.']
  sales_area_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.sales_area_assignment. Business justification: Territory assignments in manufacturing must align with customer sales area assignments (sales org, distribution channel, division) to ensure the correct sales rep covers the correct customer partition']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'External identifier of the territory record in Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud, enabling cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the CRM system of record.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the territory assignment was formally approved by the designated sales management authority.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the sales leadership or management authority who approved this territory assignment, supporting governance and audit requirements.']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business reference number for the territory assignment, used in correspondence, reporting, and cross-system reconciliation.']
  assignment_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for creating or modifying this territory assignment, supporting audit trails, change management, and territory planning analytics.']
  assignment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the assignment role: primary (sole owner), secondary (supporting rep), overlay (specialist overlay such as product or vertical specialist), backup (coverage during absence), channel (indirect partner), or split (shared territory with quota split).']
  commission_split_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of commissions earned on deals closed within the territory that are credited to this assignment record. May differ from quota split percent in overlay or specialist scenarios.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code representing the primary country of the territory assignment, supporting multinational operations and regulatory compliance.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the territory assignment becomes active and the representative or partner assumes responsibility for the territory, quota, and associated accounts.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the territory assignment ends. Null indicates an open-ended assignment with no planned termination. Used for time-bounded assignments such as project coverage or interim assignments.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Named geographic region covered by the territory assignment (e.g., \'Northeast US\', \'DACH\', \'Southeast Asia\'), used for regional sales reporting and territory planning.']
  industry_vertical varchar [note: 'Target industry vertical or market segment covered by this territory assignment, enabling vertical-specific sales strategy, quota planning, and performance reporting.']
  is_overlay boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this assignment is an overlay role (True), such as a product specialist or solution architect who supports the primary rep without owning the territory, versus a direct territory owner (False).']
  is_split_territory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this territory is shared among multiple sales representatives or partners (True), requiring quota and commission splits, or is exclusively owned by a single assignee (False).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, special conditions, or business rationale related to the territory assignment that is not captured in structured fields.']
  product_line_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the product lines or solution portfolios (e.g., \'Automation Systems\', \'Electrification Solutions\', \'Smart Infrastructure\') that the assigned representative is authorized to sell within the territory.']
  quota_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual or period-based revenue quota assigned to this representative or partner for the territory, expressed in the assignment currency. Supports split-territory scenarios where quota is distributed across multiple reps.']
  quota_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the territory quota amount is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP), supporting multi-currency global operations.']
  quota_fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the quota amount applies, aligned with the company\'s fiscal calendar. Enables year-over-year quota comparison and planning.']
  quota_period_type varchar [note: 'Time granularity of the quota cycle for this assignment (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, or monthly), determining how quota attainment and commission calculations are periodized.']
  quota_split_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the total territory quota allocated to this specific assignment record. Used in split-territory scenarios common in large industrial project sales where multiple reps collaborate on a single account or geography. All splits for a territory must sum to 100%.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the territory assignment record was first created in the source system, used for audit trail, data lineage, and change history tracking.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the territory assignment record in the source system, supporting change detection, incremental loads, and audit compliance.']
  sales_district_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA sales district code representing the sub-regional grouping within the sales organization to which this territory belongs.']
  sales_motion varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the territory is covered through direct sales (company-employed rep), indirect/channel sales (partner), or a hybrid of both motions.']
  sales_office_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA sales office code identifying the physical or virtual office responsible for managing this territory assignment.']
  sales_organization_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA sales organization code to which this territory assignment belongs, defining the legal entity and organizational unit responsible for the territory\'s revenue.']
  sales_role varchar [note: 'Functional sales role of the assigned representative within the territory, used for commission plan mapping, reporting hierarchy, and quota allocation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this territory assignment record originated, supporting data lineage and cross-system reconciliation in the Databricks Lakehouse silver layer.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the territory assignment record, controlling whether the assignment is in effect for routing, quota, and commission calculations.']

  Note: 'Association entity linking sales representatives or channel partners to specific territories with defined roles, effective periods, and quota allocations. Supports split-territory scenarios common in large industrial project sales where multiple reps collaborate on a single account or geography.'
}

Table sales.quota {
  quota_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each sales quota record assigned to a sales representative, channel partner, or territory team.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Sales quotas are defined per fiscal period to align rep targets with financial planning cycles. Finance uses this link to validate quota attainment against recognized revenue within the same period.']
  sales_area_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.sales_area_assignment. Business justification: Sales quotas in manufacturing are assigned per sales area (sales org + distribution channel + division). Linking quota to the customer sales area assignment ensures quota roll-up aligns with how reven']
  sales_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sales_org. Business justification: Sales quotas in manufacturing are assigned and tracked per sales organization. Sales management sets and monitors quota attainment at the sales org level as a core performance management process.']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Sales quotas are assigned to territories as the primary organizational unit for revenue targets. The sales_quota entity has territory_code as a denormalized string. Adding territory_id normalizes this']
  accelerator_threshold_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'The quota attainment percentage at which accelerated commission rates (accelerators) are triggered (e.g., 1.00 = 100% attainment). Above this threshold, higher commission multipliers apply to incentivize overperformance.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the sales manager or executive who formally approved this quota assignment. Required for governance and audit trail of quota authorization.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the quota was formally approved by the authorized sales manager or executive. Used for governance, audit trail, and determining the effective start of quota enforcement.']
  attainment_basis varchar [note: 'Defines the measurement basis used to calculate quota attainment: booked_revenue (order entry), invoiced_revenue (billing), collected_revenue (cash receipt), shipped_units, ordered_units, or gross_margin. Critical for commission calculation accuracy.']
  category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the quota is assigned to an individual sales representative, a sales team, a geographic territory, a channel partner, or an overlay specialist (e.g., solution architect).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the primary country of the sales territory or channel partner to which this quota applies (e.g., USA, DEU, GBR). Supports multi-country reporting and compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the quota record was initially created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the quota target amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global sales territories.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'The sales distribution channel through which quota attainment is measured: direct (company sales force), distributor, OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer), EPC (Engineering Procurement Construction), system integrator, online, or retail.']
  end_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the quota period ends. Defines the boundary for attainment calculation and commission payout eligibility.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate applied to convert the local currency quota target to USD at the time of quota creation or last revision. Sourced from the corporate treasury rate table.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The specific fiscal period within the fiscal year (e.g., Q1, Q2, M01–M12, H1, FY). Aligns quota tracking with the company\'s financial reporting periods.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this quota belongs, aligned with the company\'s financial reporting calendar (e.g., 2025). Used for annual planning and year-over-year attainment analysis.']
  floor_threshold_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'The minimum quota attainment percentage required before any commission is earned (e.g., 0.50 = 50% attainment floor). Below this threshold, no commission is paid regardless of sales activity.']
  industry_vertical varchar [note: 'The target industry vertical or end-market segment for which this quota is defined (e.g., automotive, aerospace, oil and gas, utilities). Enables vertical-specific sales performance analysis.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the quota record was last updated or revised. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for the quota plan, such as \'Q1 2025 EMEA Automation Revenue Quota\' or \'FY2025 Channel Partner Unit Quota – Drives & Controls\'.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special conditions, or management commentary associated with this quota record (e.g., \'Quota adjusted for new product launch in Q2\', \'Excludes strategic accounts managed by overlay team\').']
  number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business reference number for the quota record, used for cross-system identification and communication with sales management.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'The time granularity of the quota measurement cycle: monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, or annual. Determines the cadence for attainment tracking and commission calculations.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'The industrial product family or solution category in scope for this quota (e.g., \'Automation Systems\', \'Electrification Solutions\', \'Smart Infrastructure\', \'Drives and Controls\', \'Motion Control\'). Defines the product scope boundary for attainment credit.']
  product_line_code varchar [note: 'The internal product line or SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) category code that scopes the quota to a specific product line within the product family. Used for granular attainment tracking by product portfolio.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Internal sales region code (e.g., AMER, EMEA, APAC) representing the geographic region grouping for this quota. Used for regional performance aggregation and executive reporting.']
  revision_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason for the most recent quota revision or adjustment (e.g., territory realignment, headcount change, market adjustment). Required for audit compliance and management reporting on quota changes.']
  sales_motion varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the quota applies to direct sales (company-employed sales reps), channel sales (partner-driven), or a hybrid model combining both motions.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this quota record originated (e.g., Salesforce CRM, SAP S/4HANA). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across systems.']
  start_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the quota period begins. Used to determine the active window for attainment measurement and commission eligibility.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the quota record: draft (being prepared), pending_approval (submitted for review), approved (authorized but not yet active), active (in-period measurement), closed (period ended), cancelled (withdrawn), or superseded (replaced by a revised quota).']
  target_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'The primary monetary or unit target value assigned for this quota period. For revenue quotas, this is the revenue target in the transaction currency. For unit quotas, this represents the target unit count. For margin quotas, this is the gross margin target.']
  target_amount_usd decimal(20,4) [note: 'The quota target amount converted to US Dollars using the applicable exchange rate at the time of quota creation. Enables global consolidated reporting and cross-territory comparisons.']
  target_margin_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'The target gross margin percentage required to achieve full quota attainment for margin-based quotas. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.35 = 35%). Supports profitability-driven sales incentive programs.']
  target_units decimal(18,4) [note: 'The target number of product units or solution contracts to be sold within the quota period. Applicable for unit-based quotas; may be populated alongside revenue targets for blended quota plans.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the quota measurement basis: revenue (monetary sales target), units (volume of products sold), margin (gross margin target), activity (call/visit targets), or pipeline (opportunity pipeline value).']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the quota record, incremented each time the quota is revised or adjusted. Enables tracking of quota changes over the period and supports audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Defines periodic revenue, margin, and unit sales quotas assigned to individual sales representatives, channel partners, or territory teams for industrial product lines and solution categories. Tracks quota type (revenue/units/margin), period (monthly/quarterly/annual), product family scope, and attainment basis.'
}

Table sales.discount_structure {
  discount_structure_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each approved discount structure record within the sales domain.']
  account_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account_classification. Business justification: Discount structures in manufacturing are tiered by account classification (e.g., strategic, preferred, standard). This link ensures discounts are applied consistently based on the customer\'s official ']
  controlling_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.controlling_area. Business justification: Discount structures in manufacturing must be approved within a controlling area to enforce margin floors by business unit. Finance uses this link to audit discount impact on contribution margins per c']
  sales_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sales_org. Business justification: Discount structures are defined per sales organization. In industrial manufacturing, each sales org has its own discount authority and approval thresholds. Sales ops teams configure discounts scoped t']
  sales_price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_price_list. Business justification: A discount structure is fundamentally applied against a specific price list — it defines the approved discount framework on top of a base price list for industrial automation products. Linking discoun']
  approval_authority_level varchar [note: 'The minimum organizational authority level required to approve application of this discount structure to a deal or contract. Enforces discount governance and delegation of authority policies.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the discount structure was formally approved by the designated approval authority. Required for audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or user ID of the individual who granted final approval for this discount structure. Supports audit trail and delegation of authority compliance.']
  code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric business code used to identify and reference the discount structure across systems such as SAP SD and Salesforce CRM.']
  condition_type varchar [note: 'SAP SD condition type code representing this discount in the pricing procedure (e.g., \'K004\' for customer discount, \'K005\' for customer/material discount, \'K007\' for customer discount group).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code specifying the country where this discount structure is applicable. Null if the structure applies globally or regionally.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp recording when this discount structure record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  crm_price_rule_code varchar [note: 'External reference identifier for the corresponding price rule or discount record in Salesforce CRM, enabling bi-directional synchronization between SAP SD and CRM systems.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to fixed-amount discount structures (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Null for percentage-based structures.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'The customer classification or industry segment to which this discount structure is applicable. Enables targeted commercial policies for key accounts, strategic customers, or specific verticals.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explaining the purpose, scope, and business rationale of the discount structure for sales and finance teams.']
  discount_basis varchar [note: 'Defines how the discount is calculated: as a flat percentage, fixed monetary amount, tiered percentage by volume bracket, tiered fixed amount, or sliding scale.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the discount structure by commercial purpose: trade (channel/distributor), volume (quantity-based), project (large capital project), promotional (time-limited campaign), loyalty, competitive, rebate, early payment, or bundle.']
  distribution_channel_code varchar [note: 'SAP SD distribution channel code associated with this discount structure, enabling precise condition record determination in the pricing procedure.']
  exclusion_group varchar [note: 'SAP SD condition exclusion group code that governs mutual exclusivity between discount structures. Discount structures in the same exclusion group cannot be applied simultaneously.']
  fixed_discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The fixed monetary discount amount applied per unit or per order when discount_basis is fixed_amount. Denominated in the currency specified by currency_code.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this discount structure record. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  max_discount_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The ceiling discount percentage authorized under this structure. No individual deal may exceed this threshold without escalated approval. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 15.00 = 15%).']
  min_discount_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The floor discount percentage applicable under this structure. Ensures a baseline discount is always applied when the structure is invoked.']
  min_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum quantity of units or SKUs required to qualify for this discount structure. Supports volume-based discount eligibility determination.']
  min_order_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum order or contract value (in the specified currency) required to qualify for this discount structure. Enforces commercial thresholds for discount eligibility.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive business name of the discount structure (e.g., \'Industrial Automation Volume Tier 2025\', \'EMEA Channel Partner Trade Discount\').']
  owner varchar [note: 'Business owner responsible for maintaining and governing this discount structure (typically a Sales Director or Pricing Manager). Point of contact for escalations.']
  pricing_procedure varchar [note: 'SAP SD pricing procedure code within which this discount condition type is configured (e.g., \'RVAA01\', \'ZIND01\'). Determines the sequence and calculation logic for discount application.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority rank used to resolve conflicts when multiple discount structures are applicable to the same transaction. Lower number indicates higher priority.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'The product family or portfolio group to which this discount structure applies (e.g., \'Industrial Automation\', \'Electrification Solutions\', \'Smart Infrastructure\', \'Drives and Motion\'). Aligns with PLM product hierarchy.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'Specific product line within the product family to which the discount applies (e.g., \'SIMATIC PLC\', \'SINAMICS Drives\', \'SENTRON Switchgear\'). More granular than product_family.']
  quantity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure applicable to the minimum order quantity threshold (e.g., EA = Each, PC = Piece, KG = Kilogram). Aligns with SAP MM unit of measure configuration.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region where this discount structure is valid (e.g., \'North America\', \'EMEA\', \'APAC\', \'Global\'). Supports multinational discount governance.']
  requires_justification boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a written business justification is mandatory when applying this discount structure to a sales opportunity or order. Enforces governance for high-value or sensitive discounts.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'The sales channel(s) to which this discount structure applies: direct sales force, distributor, system integrator, OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer), EPC (Engineering Procurement Construction) contractor, online, or reseller.']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'The SAP SD sales organization unit responsible for this discount structure (e.g., \'US10\', \'DE20\', \'APAC30\'). Defines the legal and commercial entity owning the discount policy.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this discount structure originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA SD, Salesforce CRM). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  stackable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this discount structure can be combined (stacked) with other active discount structures on the same order or line item. Critical for margin protection governance.']
  standard_discount_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The default or baseline discount percentage applied when no special conditions are met. Serves as the starting point for sales negotiations.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the discount structure, governing whether it can be applied in the sales process.']
  superseded_by_code varchar [note: 'Business code of the discount structure that replaces this one when status is \'superseded\'. Enables traceability of discount policy evolution over time.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the discount structure, incremented each time the structure is revised or superseded. Supports change history tracking and audit compliance.']
  valid_from date [note: 'The date from which this discount structure becomes effective and can be applied to sales transactions. Aligns with SAP SD condition record validity.']
  valid_to date [note: 'The date on which this discount structure expires and can no longer be applied to new sales transactions. Supports time-limited promotional and project-specific discounts.']

  Note: 'Defines approved discount frameworks applicable to industrial product lines, customer segments, and sales channels. Captures discount type (trade/volume/project/promotional), maximum discount percentage, approval authority level, applicable product families, and validity period. Governs discount authorization in the sales process.'
}

Table sales.forecast {
  forecast_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each sales forecast record in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  contract_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.contract_line. Business justification: Sales forecasts for service renewals are directly tied to specific contract lines. Revenue forecasting teams use this link to project recurring service revenue by contract line, enabling accurate annu']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Sales forecasts align to fiscal periods for financial planning and reporting. Finance consolidates sales forecasts by fiscal period for revenue projections and board reporting.']
  product_variant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.product_variant. Business justification: Sales forecasts in manufacturing are tied to specific product variants to drive production planning and capacity scheduling. S&OP teams link forecast volumes to engineering-defined variants so manufac']
  quota_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_quota. Business justification: Sales forecasts are measured against quotas — the quota_amount and variance_to_quota_amount fields on sales_forecast are derived from the associated sales_quota record. Adding sales_quota_id links the']
  sales_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sales_org. Business justification: Sales forecasts are always scoped to a sales organization in manufacturing. Demand planning and S&OP processes require forecasts to be attributed to the responsible sales org for production planning a']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Sales forecasts are submitted by territory for pipeline visibility and revenue planning. The sales_forecast entity has territory_code as a denormalized string. Adding territory_id normalizes this refe']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Sales forecasts in industrial manufacturing are rolled up by customer segment (e.g., process industry, discrete manufacturing). Segment-level forecasting drives production planning and capacity alloca']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which this forecast version was formally approved by the authorized reviewer (e.g., Sales Director, Finance Controller) in the S&OP process.']
  category varchar [note: 'Sales rep or manager classification of the forecast confidence tier, aligned with CRM forecast categories. \'Commit\' indicates high confidence; \'pipeline\' indicates early-stage opportunity inclusion.']
  confidence_level varchar [note: 'Qualitative confidence rating assigned by the submitter indicating the reliability of the forecast (high, medium, low). Complements the numeric probability and forecast category fields.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the forecasted revenue is expected to be recognized.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this forecast record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  crm_forecast_code varchar [note: 'Native forecast record identifier from Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud, used for cross-system traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse and the CRM system of record.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the transaction currency in which the forecast amounts are expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  cycle_type varchar [note: 'Defines the cadence of the forecast cycle: monthly, quarterly, annual, or rolling 12-month. Determines the aggregation period and S&OP review frequency.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'The sales channel through which the forecasted revenue is expected to be realized: direct sales, channel partner, distributor, OEM, EPC contractor, or online. Supports both direct and channel sales motions.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate applied to convert the transaction currency forecast amount to USD. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI currency tables at the time of forecast submission.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (quarter Q1–Q4 or month P01–P12) within the fiscal year to which this forecast applies. Supports both monthly and quarterly forecast cycles.']
  forecasted_margin_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected gross margin amount (revenue minus cost of goods sold) for the forecast period in transaction currency. Used for profitability planning and EBITDA forecasting.']
  forecasted_margin_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected gross margin as a percentage of forecasted revenue. Key profitability KPI used in S&OP reviews and financial planning.']
  forecasted_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected gross revenue amount for the forecast period in the transaction currency. Represents the top-line revenue expectation before discounts and adjustments.']
  forecasted_revenue_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected gross revenue amount converted to US Dollars using the applicable exchange rate, enabling global consolidation and cross-currency reporting.']
  forecasted_units decimal(18,3) [note: 'Projected quantity of units (SKUs, systems, or assemblies) expected to be sold during the forecast period. Supports volume-based S&OP and production planning (MRP).']
  industry_vertical varchar [note: 'The end-customer industry vertical targeted by this forecast. Enables vertical-specific pipeline analysis and go-to-market performance tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this forecast record, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for change tracking and incremental data loading.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive label for the forecast record, typically including product family, territory, and period (e.g., \'Q3 2025 Industrial Automation EMEA Forecast\').']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for the submitter or approver to capture qualitative context, assumptions, risks, or exceptions relevant to this forecast record (e.g., large deal dependency, supply constraint risk).']
  number varchar [note: 'Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the forecast record, used for cross-system reference and audit traceability. Typically sourced from Salesforce CRM or SAP S/4HANA SD module.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'The calendar end date of the forecast period being projected. Defines the boundary of the forecast window for revenue recognition and planning purposes.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'The calendar start date of the forecast period being projected. Used for time-series alignment in S&OP and financial planning.']
  probability_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric probability (0–100%) that the forecasted revenue will be realized within the forecast period. Used for weighted pipeline calculations and risk-adjusted forecasting.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'The high-level product family grouping to which this forecast applies (e.g., Industrial Automation, Electrification Solutions, Smart Infrastructure, Motion Control). Aligns with PLM product hierarchy in Siemens Teamcenter.']
  product_line_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the specific product line within the product family (e.g., PLC series, drive systems, switchgear). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA material master.']
  quota_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The assigned sales quota target for the corresponding territory, product family, and period against which this forecast is measured. Sourced from the sales quota management process.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code grouping territories for regional performance reporting and S&OP aggregation (e.g., AMER, EMEA, APAC).']
  sales_motion varchar [note: 'The go-to-market sales motion applicable to this forecast (direct, channel, hybrid, OEM, EPC, service). Supports segmented performance analysis across different commercial models.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this forecast record originated (e.g., Salesforce CRM, SAP S/4HANA, manual entry). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the forecast record, tracking its lifecycle from draft creation through approval or rejection within the S&OP process.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The date on which this forecast version was formally submitted for review and approval within the S&OP cycle.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classifies what dimension the forecast is measuring: revenue (monetary), units (volume), margin (profitability), bookings (orders received), or pipeline (opportunity-based).']
  variance_to_quota_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Absolute difference between the forecasted revenue amount and the quota amount (forecast minus quota). Negative values indicate a forecast below quota; positive values indicate over-forecast.']
  version_label varchar [note: 'Human-readable label describing the nature of this forecast version (e.g., initial submission, revised, final, reforecast after major order change).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the forecast submission within a given period and cycle. Enables tracking of forecast revisions across rolling cycles (e.g., v1, v2, v3).']

  Note: 'Periodic sales forecast record capturing projected revenue, units, and margin by product family, territory, and time period. Supports rolling forecast cycles (monthly/quarterly) for industrial automation and electrification product lines. Tracks forecast version, submission date, confidence level, and variance to quota. Feeds S&OP and financial planning.'
}

Table sales.activity {
  activity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each sales activity record in the lakehouse silver layer. Serves as the primary key for the sales_activity data product.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.campaign. Business justification: Sales activities such as demos, calls, and site visits are often executed as part of a campaign. Linking activities to campaigns lets marketing measure field engagement and sales effort per campaign i']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Sales activities (calls, meetings, emails) are logged against specific customer contacts. Activity history drives relationship management and follow-up scheduling.']
  installed_base_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.installed_base. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing, sales activities (upgrade pitches, service upsells) are frequently tied to a specific installed asset at the customer site. Field sales uses this to target retrofit and mo']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_territory. Business justification: Sales engagement activities (customer visits, product demonstrations, RFQ follow-ups) are conducted within defined sales territories. Linking activity to sales_territory via sales_territory_id enables']
  attendee_count varchar [note: 'Total number of participants (both internal and customer-side) who attended the sales activity. Indicates engagement breadth and multi-stakeholder involvement.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Sales channel through which the activity was conducted. Distinguishes between direct sales engagements and channel/partner-mediated interactions for channel performance analysis.']
  competitor_mentioned varchar [note: 'Name of any competitor(s) mentioned or evaluated by the customer during the activity. Supports competitive intelligence tracking and win/loss analysis for industrial automation market positioning.']
  contact_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the primary customer contact (e.g., Plant Manager, VP Engineering, Procurement Director). Supports stakeholder mapping and executive engagement tracking.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the location where the sales activity took place. Supports multinational reporting, regional performance analysis, and regulatory compliance tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sales activity record was first created in the source CRM system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and activity creation velocity analysis.']
  crm_activity_code varchar [note: 'Native activity identifier from Salesforce CRM (Task or Event object ID). Used for cross-system traceability and reconciliation with the source system of record.']
  date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the sales activity occurred or is scheduled to occur. Used for time-series analysis of sales engagement cadence and pipeline progression.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Planned or actual duration of the sales activity in minutes, as recorded in Salesforce. Used for sales rep productivity analysis and customer engagement depth measurement.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the sales activity concluded. Combined with start_timestamp to derive activity duration for productivity and engagement analytics.']
  industry_segment varchar [note: 'Industry vertical of the customer account targeted in this activity. Enables segment-specific sales performance analysis and go-to-market strategy alignment.']
  internal_attendees varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of internal employee names or IDs who participated in the activity (e.g., sales rep, pre-sales engineer, product specialist). Supports team selling analysis.']
  is_key_account boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the activity is associated with a strategically designated key account. Key accounts receive elevated engagement standards and executive sponsorship per the account plan framework.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'IETF BCP 47 language code representing the primary language in which the sales activity was conducted (e.g., en-US, de-DE, zh-CN). Supports multilingual sales team management in multinational operations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the sales activity record in the source CRM system. Supports incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance.']
  location_address varchar [note: 'Physical address or virtual meeting URL where the sales activity was conducted. Supports field sales logistics, territory management, and travel expense reporting.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Type of venue or medium where the sales activity took place. Distinguishes on-site customer visits, virtual meetings, factory tours, and trade show engagements for engagement channel analytics.']
  next_step varchar [note: 'Defined follow-up action or next step agreed upon as a result of this activity. Drives pipeline progression and ensures accountability for subsequent customer engagement.']
  next_step_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the defined next step must be completed. Used for follow-up tracking, overdue activity alerts, and sales cadence management.']
  number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business reference number for the sales activity, formatted as ACT-YYYY-NNNNNN. Used in communications, reporting, and cross-functional references.']
  opportunity_stage varchar [note: 'Stage of the associated sales opportunity at the time this activity was conducted. Enables analysis of activity patterns by pipeline stage and stage-to-stage conversion effectiveness.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Overall result or outcome of the sales activity as assessed by the sales representative. Indicates whether the engagement advanced the opportunity, was neutral, or resulted in a negative signal.']
  outcome_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the result of the sales activity, key discussion points, customer reactions, and any commitments made. Captured from Salesforce activity notes.']
  owner_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the sales representative or account manager who owns this activity. Denormalized for reporting convenience and dashboard display.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the activity by the sales representative or manager. Drives scheduling and resource allocation for high-value industrial accounts.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'Product family or portfolio group featured in the sales activity (e.g., PLC systems, drives, switchgear, building automation). Supports product-level engagement analytics and cross-sell identification.']
  requires_technical_support boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the activity required or will require involvement of pre-sales engineers, application specialists, or technical consultants. Supports resource planning for technical sales support.']
  rfq_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the RFQ associated with this activity, when the activity involves responding to or discussing a customer quotation request. Links activity to formal procurement events.']
  solution_type varchar [note: 'Category of industrial solution discussed or demonstrated during the activity. Aligns with the company\'s core product lines: automation systems, electrification solutions, smart infrastructure, and system integration contracts.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this sales activity record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking and multi-source reconciliation in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the sales activity started. Supports duration calculation, scheduling conflict detection, and time-zone-aware reporting for multinational sales teams.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sales activity. Tracks whether the activity has been planned, is underway, completed, or cancelled, enabling pipeline hygiene and follow-up management.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title or subject line of the sales activity, as recorded in Salesforce. Provides a quick summary of the engagement purpose (e.g., \'Q3 OEE Solution Demo – Plant Manager\').']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sales engagement activity. Distinguishes between field visits, technical demonstrations, RFQ/RFP responses, and other customer-facing interactions aligned with industrial automation and electrification sales motions.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of sales engagement activities including customer visits, product demonstrations, technical presentations, RFQ/RFP responses, and follow-up actions associated with an opportunity or account. Captures activity type, date, participants, outcome, and next steps. Sourced from Salesforce Activity Management.'
}

Table sales.competitor {
  competitor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the competitor master record in the industrial automation and electrification market.']
  battlecard_url varchar [note: 'URL link to the latest competitive battlecard document for this competitor, stored in the content management system. Provides sales teams with quick access to competitive positioning guidance.']
  certifications varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of known industry certifications held by the competitor (e.g., ISO 9001, ISO 14001, CE Marking, UL, IEC 62443). Used to assess compliance parity and differentiation in regulated markets.']
  competitive_threat_level varchar [note: 'Assessed level of competitive threat posed by this competitor to the company\'s business, based on market overlap, win/loss history, and strategic positioning. Used to prioritize competitive intelligence investment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the competitor master record was first created in the system, used for data lineage, audit trail, and record age analysis.']
  crm_competitor_code varchar [note: 'External identifier for this competitor record in the Salesforce CRM system, enabling cross-system traceability and deduplication.']
  employee_count_range varchar [note: 'Approximate employee headcount range of the competitor organization, used to gauge organizational scale and investment capacity.']
  estimated_annual_revenue_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated annual revenue of the competitor in US dollars, sourced from public filings, analyst reports, or market intelligence. Used for competitive sizing and market share analysis.']
  estimated_market_share_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated percentage of the relevant market held by the competitor, based on analyst reports or internal market intelligence. Used for competitive positioning and strategic planning.']
  geographic_presence varchar [note: 'Scope of the competitor\'s geographic market coverage. Global indicates worldwide operations; multi_regional spans several regions; regional is limited to one region; local is country or sub-country level.']
  go_to_market_model varchar [note: 'The competitor\'s primary sales and distribution model (direct sales force, channel partners, OEM, distributor network, or hybrid). Used to assess competitive reach and channel conflict risk.']
  headquarters_city varchar [note: 'City where the competitor\'s global headquarters is located, used for geographic competitive analysis and regional strategy planning.']
  headquarters_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country where the competitor\'s global headquarters is located.']
  industry_segment varchar [note: 'Primary industry segment in which the competitor operates and competes, aligned to the company\'s own market segmentation for competitive overlap analysis.']
  intelligence_as_of_date date [note: 'Date as of which the competitive intelligence in this record was last validated or refreshed. Used to flag stale records requiring review.']
  intelligence_source varchar [note: 'Primary source from which competitive intelligence for this record was gathered. Used to assess data reliability and freshness for competitive analysis.']
  known_strengths varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the competitor\'s recognized competitive strengths, such as technology leadership, brand recognition, pricing, distribution network, or service capabilities. Used in sales strategy and opportunity competitive analysis.']
  known_weaknesses varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the competitor\'s recognized weaknesses or vulnerabilities, such as limited product range, poor service, high pricing, or geographic gaps. Used to identify differentiation opportunities in sales engagements.']
  loss_rate_against_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of competitive opportunities where the company lost to this competitor, calculated from closed opportunity win/loss data. Identifies areas requiring competitive strategy improvement.']
  market_segments_served varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of market verticals or customer segments the competitor actively targets (e.g., automotive, food and beverage, oil and gas, utilities), supporting targeted competitive strategy.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the competitor master record, used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and audit compliance.']
  name varchar [note: 'Official registered legal or trade name of the competitor organization as recognized in the market.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, observations, or strategic commentary about the competitor that does not fit into structured fields. Used by sales and product teams for qualitative intelligence.']
  operating_countries varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes representing countries where the competitor actively operates or sells, used for territory-level competitive mapping.']
  owned_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the sales or competitive intelligence team member responsible for maintaining and updating this competitor record.']
  parent_company_name varchar [note: 'Name of the parent or holding company if the competitor is a subsidiary or division of a larger corporate group, supporting corporate hierarchy analysis.']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'Characterization of the competitor\'s general pricing approach in the market. Used to inform the company\'s own pricing and discount strategies in competitive deals.']
  primary_differentiator varchar [note: 'The single most significant competitive differentiator the competitor uses in the market (e.g., lowest total cost of ownership, open architecture, fastest delivery, strongest brand). Used to prepare counter-positioning in sales engagements.']
  primary_loss_reason varchar [note: 'Most frequently cited reason for losing opportunities to this competitor, derived from win/loss analysis. Used to prioritize product, pricing, or service improvements.']
  product_lines varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of the competitor\'s known product lines or solution portfolios (e.g., PLCs, drives, switchgear, SCADA, MES), used to map competitive overlap against the company\'s own product families.']
  publicly_traded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the competitor is a publicly traded company listed on a stock exchange, enabling access to public financial disclosures for competitive analysis.']
  recent_acquisitions varchar [note: 'Narrative or comma-separated list of recent mergers, acquisitions, or divestitures by the competitor that may affect their product portfolio, market reach, or competitive threat level.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which this competitor record should be reviewed and updated by the competitive intelligence team.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or commonly used market name for the competitor, used in reports, dashboards, and opportunity records for brevity.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system from which this competitor record was sourced or synchronized, supporting data lineage and master data governance.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current tracking status of the competitor record. Active indicates actively monitored; watch indicates emerging threat; inactive indicates no longer relevant; archived indicates historical record.']
  stock_ticker varchar [note: 'Stock exchange ticker symbol for publicly traded competitors, used to link to financial market data for revenue and valuation tracking.']
  strategic_alliances varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of known strategic partnerships, alliances, or OEM relationships the competitor maintains, which may affect competitive dynamics or channel conflict.']
  technology_platform varchar [note: 'Description of the competitor\'s core technology platform or architecture (e.g., proprietary IIoT platform, open standards-based, cloud-native MES, legacy DCS). Used in technical competitive positioning.']
  tier varchar [note: 'Strategic tier ranking of the competitor based on market share, revenue, and competitive threat level. Tier 1 represents the most significant global competitors; Tier 3 represents niche or regional players.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the competitor based on the nature of competitive overlap. Direct competitors offer equivalent products; indirect offer substitutes; potential are emerging threats; partner_competitor are entities that are both partners and competitors.']
  win_rate_against_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of competitive opportunities where the company won against this competitor, calculated from closed opportunity win/loss data. Supports competitive performance tracking and sales coaching.']

  Note: 'Master reference record for competitors tracked in the industrial automation and electrification market. Captures competitor name, product lines, market segments, geographic presence, known strengths and weaknesses, and win/loss context. Used in opportunity competitive analysis and sales strategy planning.'
}

Table sales.opportunity_competitor {
  opportunity_competitor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the opportunity-competitor association record in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  competitor_id varchar [note: 'FK to sales.competitor']
  competitor_installation_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.installation_record. Business justification: In competitive displacement deals, the opportunity-competitor record references the competitor\'s installed equipment at the customer site. Sales engineers use this to understand what is being replaced']
  crm_opportunity_competitor_id varchar [note: 'Native record identifier for the opportunity competitor association as stored in Salesforce CRM (OpportunityCompetitor object), used for source system traceability.']
  product_variant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.product_variant. Business justification: Competitive analysis in manufacturing tracks which of our product variants is being compared against a competitor\'s offering in a given opportunity. Sales and product management use this to identify w']
  battlecard_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the competitive battlecard document used by the sales team when engaging against this competitor in the opportunity. Enables tracking of battlecard effectiveness.']
  competitive_strength varchar [note: 'Sales representative\'s assessment of the competitor\'s relative strength in this specific opportunity, used to prioritize competitive response resources.']
  competitor_price_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the competitor price estimate (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  competitor_price_estimate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated or known price/bid value submitted by the competitor for this opportunity. Used for competitive pricing analysis and deal strategy. Classified as confidential business intelligence.']
  competitor_product_category varchar [note: 'Category of the competitor\'s proposed product or solution, aligned to the industrial manufacturing solution taxonomy for competitive benchmarking.']
  competitor_product_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific product, solution, or system proposed by the competitor in this opportunity (e.g., a competing PLC platform, automation suite, or electrification solution).']
  competitor_relationship_strength varchar [note: 'Assessment of the competitor\'s relationship strength with the customer\'s key decision-makers and stakeholders in this opportunity.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the market where the competitive engagement occurred. Supports regional win/loss analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the opportunity competitor association record was first created in the source system.']
  displacement_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategy employed by the sales team to displace or counter this competitor in the opportunity. Supports post-deal analysis of which strategies are most effective.']
  displacement_strategy_detail varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the specific tactics and actions taken as part of the competitive displacement strategy for this opportunity.']
  identified_date date [note: 'Date on which this competitor was first identified as active in the opportunity. Supports competitive timeline analysis and early warning metrics.']
  incumbent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this competitor is the incumbent supplier at the customer account, representing an existing installed base or contract relationship that must be displaced.']
  industry_segment varchar [note: 'Industry vertical or market segment of the customer account in this opportunity (e.g., Automotive, Food and Beverage, Oil and Gas, Building Technologies), used for segment-level competitive analysis.']
  intelligence_confidence varchar [note: 'Confidence level of the competitive intelligence captured for this record, reflecting the reliability of the source and data quality.']
  intelligence_source varchar [note: 'Source from which competitive intelligence about this competitor\'s involvement in the opportunity was obtained. Supports data quality assessment of competitive records.']
  loss_reason varchar [note: 'Primary reason the opportunity was lost to this competitor. Captured when outcome is \'Lost\' to enable structured win/loss analysis and corrective action planning.']
  loss_reason_detail varchar [note: 'Free-text elaboration on the primary loss reason, providing qualitative context for win/loss review sessions and competitive intelligence programs.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the opportunity competitor association record was last updated in the source system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional competitive intelligence, sales team observations, or contextual information about this competitor\'s role in the opportunity.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Win/loss outcome of the opportunity relative to this specific competitor. Indicates whether the company won the deal against this competitor, lost to them, or the deal had no decision.']
  price_differential_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage difference between the company\'s proposed price and the competitor\'s estimated price for this opportunity. Positive value indicates company is priced higher; negative indicates lower.']
  primary_competitor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this competitor is the primary or most significant competitive threat in the opportunity. An opportunity may have multiple competitors but only one primary.']
  region varchar [note: 'Sales region where the competitive engagement occurred (e.g., North America, EMEA, APAC), aligned to the company\'s internal sales territory hierarchy.']
  solution_type varchar [note: 'Type of solution or commercial model proposed by the competitor in this opportunity (e.g., product sale, turnkey project, service contract), enabling like-for-like competitive comparison.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this opportunity competitor record was sourced, supporting data lineage and Silver Layer traceability.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current status of this competitor\'s involvement in the opportunity lifecycle. Tracks whether the competitor is still active, has been eliminated, or the outcome has been determined.']
  win_loss_review_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal win/loss review interview or debrief has been completed for this competitive engagement, supporting pipeline quality and sales process compliance.']
  win_loss_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal win/loss review or debrief was conducted for this competitive engagement.']
  win_theme_used varchar [note: 'Primary win theme or value proposition message used to differentiate against this competitor (e.g., \'Integrated Automation + Electrification\', \'Total Cost of Ownership\', \'Local Service Network\').']

  Note: 'Association entity linking a specific competitor to a sales opportunity, capturing the competitive scenario details including competitor product proposed, win/loss outcome, loss reason, and competitive displacement strategy. Enables win/loss analysis across the industrial sales pipeline.'
}

Table sales.team {
  team_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a sales team record assigned to a major industrial project opportunity or key account in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_territory. Business justification: Sales teams assigned to major industrial project opportunities or key accounts operate within defined sales territories. Linking team to sales_territory via sales_territory_id enables territory-level ']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Name of the key account or customer organization this sales team is assigned to support, used for account-level reporting and CRM alignment.']
  code varchar [note: 'Business-facing alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the sales team, used for cross-system reference and reporting (e.g., ST-EMEA0042).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the primary country of operation for this sales team, supporting multi-country reporting and compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sales team record was first created in the source system or lakehouse, used for audit trail, data lineage, and lifecycle tracking.']
  crm_team_code varchar [note: 'Native identifier of the sales team record in the Salesforce CRM system, enabling traceability and synchronization between the lakehouse and the system of record.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the transactional currency for the opportunity or account managed by this team (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this sales team\'s engagement concluded or is scheduled to conclude, used for lifecycle management, commission cutoff, and historical reporting.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which this sales team became active and began its engagement on the assigned opportunity or key account, used for tenure tracking and commission eligibility.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which this sales team\'s primary engagement and quota attainment are tracked (e.g., FY2025), aligning with financial reporting cycles.']
  industry_vertical varchar [note: 'Target customer industry vertical the sales team is focused on, used for segmentation, reporting, and alignment with industry-specific go-to-market strategies.']
  is_lead_team boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this team is the primary (lead) team on the opportunity or account when multiple teams are involved. The lead team holds primary accountability for the commercial outcome.']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Total number of active members currently assigned to this sales team, used for capacity planning, workload analysis, and team sizing governance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this sales team record in the source system or lakehouse, supporting change detection, incremental loads, and audit compliance.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the sales team, typically reflecting the key account or project opportunity it supports (e.g., \'ACME Automotive Electrification Team\').']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context about the team\'s composition, engagement strategy, special circumstances, or internal coordination notes not captured in structured fields.']
  opportunity_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total estimated commercial value of the opportunity or key account engagement managed by this team, expressed in the team\'s transactional currency. Used for pipeline reporting and commission basis.']
  opportunity_amount_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total estimated commercial value of the opportunity converted to USD at the prevailing exchange rate, enabling global pipeline consolidation and cross-currency reporting.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic sales region in which the team operates (e.g., North America, EMEA, APAC), used for regional performance reporting and territory alignment.']
  requires_engineering boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the opportunity or account managed by this team requires dedicated application engineering or solution architecture support, triggering resource allocation workflows.']
  sales_motion varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the team operates under a direct sales motion, channel/partner-led motion, hybrid, or distributor model for the assigned opportunity or account.']
  sales_org_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA Sales Organization code to which this team belongs, enabling alignment with ERP-based revenue reporting, pricing, and order management structures.']
  solution_type varchar [note: 'Primary solution category the team is selling, aligned to the company\'s industrial portfolio: automation systems, electrification solutions, smart infrastructure, system integration contracts, digital services, or MRO (Maintenance, Repair and Operations).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this sales team record originated, supporting data lineage, reconciliation, and audit traceability in the lakehouse.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the sales team. \'active\' indicates the team is actively engaged; \'pending\' indicates formation in progress; \'dissolved\' indicates the team has been disbanded post-opportunity closure.']
  target_close_date date [note: 'Anticipated date by which the team expects to close the associated opportunity or finalize the key account engagement, used for pipeline forecasting and resource planning.']
  total_commission_split_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregate commission split percentage allocated to this team as a whole from the opportunity or account, which is then distributed among individual team members. Must sum to 100% across all teams on the same opportunity.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sales team based on its commercial purpose: opportunity-specific pursuit team, key account coverage team, strategic account team, channel partner-facing team, or overlay specialist team.']

  Note: 'Defines the composition of a sales team assigned to a major industrial project opportunity or key account, capturing team member roles (account executive, application engineer, solution architect, commercial manager), contribution percentages for commission splits, and team lead designation.'
}

Table sales.campaign {
  campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the sales campaign record within the industrial manufacturing CRM and sales operations platform.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.category. Business justification: Marketing campaigns in manufacturing are organized around product categories (e.g., automation systems, electrification). Campaign managers target specific product categories to drive demand generatio']
  cost_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_allocation. Business justification: Marketing campaigns in manufacturing carry budgets that must be allocated to finance cost centers. Finance tracks campaign spend against cost allocations to measure ROI and ensure budget compliance.']
  sales_price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_price_list. Business justification: Sales campaigns for industrial product launches, end-of-life promotions, and solution bundles are executed against a specific price list that defines the promotional or standard pricing in effect duri']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Marketing campaigns in manufacturing are designed for specific customer segments (e.g., energy utilities, automotive OEMs). Segment FK ensures campaign targeting, reporting, and ROI measurement are co']
  spend_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.spend_category. Business justification: Marketing campaigns in industrial manufacturing are budgeted and tracked against procurement spend categories. Finance and procurement teams classify campaign expenditures (trade shows, materials, pro']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the sales or marketing executive who approved the campaign budget and objectives.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which the campaign was formally approved by the sales or marketing leadership, marking the transition from planning to execution readiness.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved budget allocated to the campaign, covering marketing activities, events, partner enablement, and promotional materials. Expressed in the campaign currency.']
  budget_amount_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Campaign budget converted to US Dollars using the applicable exchange rate, enabling global financial consolidation and cross-regional budget comparison.']
  code varchar [note: 'Business-assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the campaign, used for cross-system referencing in SAP S/4HANA SD and Salesforce CRM.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the primary country of campaign execution for multinational operations and regulatory compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign record was first created in the source CRM system, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  crm_campaign_code varchar [note: 'External campaign identifier from the Salesforce CRM system, enabling traceability and synchronization between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational CRM.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in the campaign record, supporting multi-currency operations across global manufacturing entities.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the campaign\'s objectives, scope, target audience, and key messaging for industrial product lines or vertical market initiatives.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date on which the sales campaign is scheduled to conclude, used for duration tracking, resource planning, and post-campaign analysis.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter in which the campaign is primarily active, supporting quarterly revenue forecasting and sales quota alignment.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the campaign is budgeted and executed, aligning campaign performance with financial reporting cycles in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO.']
  industry_vertical varchar [note: 'Target industry vertical for the campaign, enabling segmentation of industrial automation, electrification, and smart infrastructure campaigns by end-market.']
  is_global boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign is executed globally across all regions or is limited to a specific region or country. Supports global vs. local campaign reporting.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'IETF BCP 47 language code for the primary language in which campaign materials and communications are delivered, supporting multilingual global operations.']
  lead_target_count varchar [note: 'Target number of qualified leads expected to be generated through the campaign, used for campaign effectiveness measurement and sales funnel planning.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the campaign record, supporting change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and incremental lakehouse ingestion.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive business name of the sales campaign, such as \'Q3 Industrial Automation Launch EMEA\' or \'Electrification Solutions End-of-Life Promo\'.']
  opportunity_target_count varchar [note: 'Target number of sales opportunities expected to be created from campaign-generated leads, supporting conversion rate tracking and pipeline forecasting.']
  pipeline_target_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target value of sales pipeline (opportunities) expected to be generated by the campaign, expressed in the campaign currency. Supports pipeline coverage ratio analysis.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the campaign, used for resource allocation decisions and executive attention across the global sales organization.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'Primary product family associated with the campaign, such as industrial drives, PLCs, switchgear, or building automation systems. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA product hierarchy.']
  product_line_code varchar [note: 'SAP product line code identifying the specific product line featured in the campaign, used for revenue attribution and quota alignment.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region in which the campaign is executed, supporting multinational campaign management and regional revenue attribution.']
  revenue_target_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Expected revenue to be generated from opportunities and orders attributed to this campaign, expressed in the campaign currency. Used for ROI measurement and sales forecasting.']
