  dispatcher_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the dispatcher providing additional context, special instructions, or site access information for the assigned technician.']
  estimated_travel_time_min varchar [note: 'Estimated travel time in minutes from the technician\'s current location or previous job site to the customer site, calculated by the scheduling optimization engine.']
  is_sla_breached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dispatch schedule resulted in an SLA breach, where the actual arrival or completion exceeded the contractual SLA due datetime.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Datetime when the dispatch schedule record was most recently updated in the source system, supporting change tracking and data freshness monitoring.']
  parts_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether spare parts or materials are required to complete the field service activity, used for pre-dispatch parts staging and inventory coordination.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level of the dispatch assignment, influencing scheduling order and resource allocation. Critical priority typically indicates safety or production-impacting issues.']
  required_skill varchar [note: 'Primary skill or certification required to perform the field service activity, used to validate technician qualification and support skill-based scheduling.']
  reschedule_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this dispatch schedule has been rescheduled, used to identify problematic service orders and measure scheduling stability.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business reference number for the dispatch schedule record, used for communication between dispatchers, technicians, and customers.']
  scheduled_duration_min varchar [note: 'Planned duration in minutes allocated for the service activity, derived from the work type standard duration and used for capacity planning.']
  scheduled_end timestamp [note: 'Planned datetime when the field service technician is expected to complete the service activity, used for scheduling window management and technician utilization.']
  scheduled_start timestamp [note: 'Planned datetime when the field service technician is scheduled to begin the service activity at the customer site, used for SLA compliance and workforce planning.']
  scheduling_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the dispatch was scheduled — manual assignment by a dispatcher, auto-optimized by the scheduling engine, or semi-automated with dispatcher override.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the field service activity being dispatched, used for workforce planning, SLA tracking, and service analytics.']
  site_address varchar [note: 'Full street address of the customer site where the field service technician is dispatched, used for navigation and geographic reporting.']
  site_city varchar [note: 'City of the customer site where the field service activity is scheduled, used for geographic dispatch analysis and territory management.']
  site_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the customer site, supporting multinational dispatch operations and regional compliance reporting.']
  sla_due timestamp [note: 'Contractual deadline by which the field service activity must be completed per the applicable SLA agreement, used for breach detection and escalation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this dispatch schedule record originated, supporting data lineage and integration traceability.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the dispatch schedule, tracking the technician\'s progression from assignment through completion or cancellation.']
  technician_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the field service technician during or after the service visit, capturing observations, actions taken, or follow-up requirements.']

  Note: 'Scheduling and dispatch records assigning field service technicians to field service orders within defined time windows. Captures the assigned technician, field service order reference, scheduled start datetime, scheduled end datetime, estimated travel time, actual arrival datetime, actual departure datetime, dispatch status (scheduled, en-route, on-site, completed, cancelled), scheduling method (manual, auto-optimized), and dispatcher notes. Sourced from Salesforce Field Service scheduling engine. Enables real-time visibility into technician deployment and field workforce utilization.'
}

Table service.spare_parts_request {
  spare_parts_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each spare parts request record within the aftermarket service domain.']
  aftermarket_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.aftermarket_part. Business justification: Field service spare parts requests directly reference aftermarket parts defined in the product domain. Technicians and dispatchers use this link to identify the correct replacement part during repair ']
  approved_manufacturer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.approved_manufacturer. Business justification: Spare parts must be sourced from engineering-approved manufacturers to maintain product integrity and warranty validity. Service procurement teams are required to reference the Approved Manufacturer L']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.batch. Business justification: A spare parts request may be triggered by a defective production batch requiring rework or repair. Service teams link the request to the originating batch to trace root cause and manage warranty or co']
  bom_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.bom_line. Business justification: Spare parts requests reference specific BOM line items to ensure the exact engineering-specified component is ordered — not a generic substitute. Procurement and service teams use BOM line references ']
  contract_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.contract_line. Business justification: Spare parts requests are often fulfilled under a service contract line (spare_parts_included flag on contract_line). The service_contract_number STRING on spare_parts_request is a denormalized referen']
  dispatch_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.dispatch_schedule. Linking spare_parts_request.dispatch_schedule_id → dispatch_']
  fulfillment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.fulfillment_plan. Business justification: Spare parts requests triggered by service events are fulfilled against an existing fulfillment plan (e.g., a scheduled replenishment or field stocking plan). Logistics and service teams use this link ']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Spare parts received for service may require incoming quality inspection. The spare parts request references the inspection lot created at goods receipt to confirm parts passed quality checks before d']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.location. Business justification: A spare parts request must specify the delivery location (customer site, warehouse, or service depot). Logistics uses the canonical location record to route and schedule the shipment correctly without']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Industrial service operations require traceability of which lot or batch a spare part came from, especially for warranty claims and quality investigations. Regulatory compliance and recall management ']
  maintenance_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.maintenance_item. Business justification: Spare parts requests are raised to fulfill specific maintenance items on a work plan. Procurement and warehouse teams use this link daily to source the exact part specified in the maintenance item, pr']
  purchase_requisition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_requisition.   returns_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.returns_order. Business justification: When a defective component is returned, a spare parts request is raised to replace it. Service operations link the spare parts request to the originating returns order to track replacement fulfillment']
  serialized_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit. Business justification: High-value or safety-critical spare parts (motors, drives, sensors) are serialized. Service teams must record exactly which serialized unit was installed during a repair for warranty tracking and asse']
  spare_parts_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.spare_parts_catalog. Business justification: Each spare parts request references a specific part from the spare parts catalog. Adding spare_parts_catalog_id FK normalizes the relationship to the authoritative catalog record, enabling retrieval o']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: When a technician requests spare parts for a service job, the system checks live stock position to confirm availability before fulfilling the request. Warehouse and service teams use this link daily t']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Spare parts must be picked from a specific warehouse bin or storage location. Dispatch and warehouse staff use this to physically locate and stage parts before a technician departs for a service call.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Spare parts requests are often directed to a preferred or approved supplier already registered in procurement. Service teams reference the supplier master to source critical parts quickly for field re']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: Spare parts requests are frequently raised against a specific work center that broke down or needs maintenance. Service teams reference the work center daily to dispatch the right parts and technician']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date on which the spare part was physically delivered to the field service technician or service site, used for SLA performance measurement and on-time delivery KPI reporting.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for the spare parts request, particularly relevant for high-value or restricted parts requiring managerial authorization before warehouse release.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the spare parts request was approved by the authorized approver, used for SLA measurement of approval cycle time.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the service manager or authorized approver who approved the spare parts request, supporting audit trail and authorization compliance.']
  bom_position varchar [note: 'Position or item number of the spare part within the equipment\'s Bill of Materials (BOM), enabling structured parts identification and supporting BOM-driven service parts planning.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'SAP cost center code to which the spare parts cost is allocated, enabling financial reporting of aftermarket service costs by organizational unit.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the unit cost and total cost are expressed, supporting multi-currency operations across global service regions.']
  delivery_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the delivery destination, used for export compliance, customs documentation, and cross-border logistics management.']
  equipment_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the specific customer equipment unit requiring the spare part, enabling precise asset-level parts consumption tracking and failure pattern analysis.']
  fulfillment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by warehouse staff or service coordinators regarding fulfillment exceptions, substitutions, partial shipments, or special handling instructions for the spare parts request.']
  installed_base_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the customer-installed automation or electrification equipment for which the spare part is being requested, linking the parts demand to the specific asset in the installed base.']
  promised_delivery_date date [note: 'Date confirmed by the warehouse or logistics team as the committed delivery date for the spare part, which may differ from the requested delivery date based on stock availability and shipping lead times.']
  quantity_backordered decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the spare part that could not be fulfilled from current stock and has been placed on backorder, requiring procurement or inter-warehouse transfer to satisfy the demand.']
  quantity_issued decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual number of units of the spare part physically issued from the warehouse against this request, which may differ from the quantity requested due to partial availability or substitution.']
  quantity_requested decimal(18,4) [note: 'Number of units of the spare part requested to fulfill the service order or warranty repair, expressed in the part\'s base unit of measure.']
  request_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the spare parts request was formally submitted by the field service technician or service coordinator.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business reference number assigned to the spare parts request, used for cross-system tracking and communication with field technicians and warehouse teams.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (with timezone) when the spare parts request was created in the system, supporting SLA tracking and audit trail requirements.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the spare parts request by the nature of the service demand, distinguishing between field service, warranty repair, preventive maintenance, emergency breakdown, commissioning, upgrade, and MRO replenishment scenarios.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the requesting field technician or service coordinator requires the spare part to be delivered to the service site or staging location, used for SLA compliance and logistics planning.']
  shipment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the outbound shipment or delivery document associated with the physical dispatch of the spare part to the field service location, enabling end-to-end logistics traceability.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation selected for delivering the spare part to the field service location, influencing freight cost, delivery lead time, and SLA compliance.']
  source_storage_location varchar [note: 'Specific storage location or bin within the source warehouse from which the spare part is picked, supporting precise inventory management and warehouse execution.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which the spare parts request originated, supporting data lineage, reconciliation, and multi-system integration in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  source_warehouse_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the warehouse or storage location from which the spare part is to be issued, enabling multi-warehouse fulfillment routing and inventory depletion tracking.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the spare parts request, tracking progression from initial submission through warehouse fulfillment and delivery to the field service location.']
  substitute_part_number varchar [note: 'Part number of an approved substitute or alternative spare part used when the originally requested part is unavailable, supporting continuity of service and backorder resolution.']
  total_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total cost of the spare parts request calculated as unit cost multiplied by quantity issued, representing the actual parts cost charged to the service order or warranty claim.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard or moving average cost per unit of the spare part at the time of the request, used for service cost tracking, warranty cost recovery, and aftermarket profitability analysis.']
  urgency_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification indicating the operational urgency of the spare parts request, used to drive warehouse picking priority, expedited shipping decisions, and SLA compliance management.']
  warranty_covered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the spare part request is covered under an active warranty agreement, determining whether the cost is charged to the customer or absorbed as a warranty liability.']

  Note: 'Transactional records for spare parts and MRO materials requested to fulfill field service orders and warranty repairs for customer-installed automation and electrification equipment. Captures the originating field service order or warranty claim, requested part number, part description, quantity requested, urgency level, requested delivery date, source warehouse, fulfillment status, actual quantity issued, and shipment reference. Distinct from inventory domain stock management — this entity captures the service-driven demand signal and fulfillment tracking specific to aftermarket operations.'
}

Table service.spare_parts_catalog {
  spare_parts_catalog_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each spare part record in the aftermarket catalog. Serves as the primary key for the spare_parts_catalog data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  engineering_bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_bom. Business justification: Spare parts catalogs are derived directly from engineering BOMs — service teams reference the BOM to identify which components are serviceable, orderable, and stocked as spares. Parts catalog creation']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plant. Business justification: Spare parts catalog entries are sourced from specific manufacturing plants. Service operations reference plant-level product data to confirm availability, lead times, and sourcing location for field t']
  preferred_supplier_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.preferred_supplier_list. Business justification: Spare parts catalogs in service are aligned to procurement\'s preferred supplier list to ensure parts are sourced from approved vendors. Procurement governance mandates this linkage for cost control an']
  product_variant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.product_variant. Business justification: Spare parts catalogs are scoped to specific product variants — a motor drive variant has different serviceable parts than another variant. Service teams must know which variant a catalog applies to wh']
  spend_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.spend_category. Business justification: Spare parts are classified under procurement spend categories for budget tracking and sourcing strategy. Finance and procurement use this link to report MRO spend originating from service operations.']
  uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: Spare parts catalog must reference the canonical unit of measure from the product domain to ensure consistent quantity handling across ordering, stocking, and invoicing in service operations.']
  availability_status varchar [note: 'Current commercial availability status of the spare part in the aftermarket catalog. Drives ordering eligibility, customer portal display, and field service planning. \'Phase_out\' indicates the part is being discontinued; \'on_request\' means it requires special ordering.']
  catalog_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this spare part record is commercially active and available for ordering in the aftermarket catalog. Used for catalog versioning and price list validity management.']
  catalog_expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which this spare part record is no longer commercially active in the aftermarket catalog. Null if the part has no planned expiry. Used for catalog lifecycle management and phase-out planning.']
  compatible_product_lines varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or free-text description of specific product lines, model numbers, or series within the product family that are compatible with this spare part. Supports cross-reference lookups in field service and customer portals.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the spare part was manufactured or substantially transformed. Required for customs declarations, import/export compliance, and trade preference programs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the spare part record was first created in the aftermarket catalog system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and catalog change history reporting.']
  customs_tariff_number varchar [note: 'Harmonized System (HS) tariff classification code assigned to the spare part for customs and import/export compliance. Used in trade documentation, duty calculation, and cross-border shipment declarations.']
  ecn_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) that introduced or last modified this spare part in the catalog. Provides traceability to the product engineering change management process.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export Control Classification Number (ECCN) or equivalent classification code indicating whether the spare part is subject to export control regulations (e.g., EAR, ITAR). Required for multinational shipments of automation and electrification components.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the spare part contains or constitutes a hazardous material requiring special handling, packaging, labeling, and shipping documentation under applicable regulations (e.g., REACH, RoHS, OSHA HazCom, IATA DGR).']
  last_price_update_date date [note: 'Date on which the list price for this spare part was last updated in the aftermarket catalog. Supports price audit trails and annual price list revision tracking.']
  list_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard commercial list price for the spare part as published in the aftermarket catalog. Represents the base price before any customer-specific discounts or contract pricing. Used as the reference price for quotations and service orders.']
  list_price_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the list price (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global aftermarket regions.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) or approved manufacturer of the spare part. Used for supplier traceability, approved manufacturer list (AML) compliance, and procurement sourcing.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'The original manufacturer\'s part number for cross-reference purposes. Enables procurement teams and field engineers to source parts from approved manufacturers and verify interchangeability.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(10,3) [note: 'Minimum quantity of the spare part that must be ordered per transaction, as defined by the supplier or manufacturing constraints. Enforced during order entry in the service portal and ERP.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the spare part record was last updated in the aftermarket catalog system. Used for incremental data loads, change detection, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  part_category varchar [note: 'Business category grouping for the spare part within the aftermarket catalog hierarchy (e.g., Electrical, Mechanical, Hydraulic, Electronic, Pneumatic, Safety). Supports catalog navigation and reporting.']
  part_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the spare part by its functional category within the aftermarket catalog. Drives stocking strategy, pricing rules, and service contract coverage.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'The automation system, electrification solution, or smart infrastructure product family for which this spare part is applicable (e.g., SIMATIC S7, SINUMERIK, SINAMICS, SIVACON). Enables field service engineers to filter parts by installed base product family.']
  reach_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the spare part complies with the EU REACH regulation for chemical substance registration, evaluation, and authorization. Required for sale and distribution within the European Union.']
  rohs_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the spare part complies with the EU RoHS Directive restricting the use of specific hazardous substances in electrical and electronic equipment. Required for CE Marking and sale in the European Union.']
  service_contract_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this spare part is eligible for coverage under service contracts and maintenance agreements. Drives entitlement checks during field service dispatch and warranty claim processing.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days the spare part can be stored before it is considered expired or unfit for use, measured from the date of manufacture or receipt. Null if the part has no shelf life restriction. Used by warehouse management for FEFO (First Expired First Out) picking.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this spare part catalog record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA MM, Siemens Teamcenter PLM). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the lakehouse Silver Layer.']
  standard_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Internal standard cost of the spare part used for inventory valuation, margin analysis, and cost of goods sold (COGS) reporting. Maintained in the ERP cost center and updated during standard cost runs.']
  standard_cost_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the standard cost value (e.g., USD, EUR). Supports multi-currency cost reporting across global manufacturing entities.']
  standard_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of calendar days from order placement to delivery for this spare part under normal supply conditions. Used for Available-to-Promise (ATP) calculations and customer delivery commitments.']
  storage_conditions varchar [note: 'Prescribed storage requirements for the spare part, including temperature range, humidity limits, light exposure restrictions, and special handling instructions (e.g., \'Store at 15-25°C, <60% RH, away from direct sunlight\'). Ensures part integrity during warehousing.']
  superseded_by_part_number varchar [note: 'Part number of the newer replacement part that supersedes this part in the aftermarket catalog. Represents the forward link in the supersession chain (old part to new part). Null if this part has not been superseded.']
  supersedes_part_number varchar [note: 'Part number of the older part that this part replaces in the aftermarket catalog. Represents the backward link in the supersession chain. Null if this part does not supersede a prior part.']
  warranty_duration_months varchar [note: 'Standard warranty period in months provided for the spare part from the date of installation or delivery. Used to set warranty expiry dates on installed base records and manage warranty claims.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,4) [note: 'Gross weight of the spare part in kilograms, including standard packaging. Used for freight cost calculation, shipping documentation, and dangerous goods declarations.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of spare parts and aftermarket components available for service and repair of Manufacturing\'s automation systems, electrification solutions, and smart infrastructure products. Captures part number, part description, compatible product families, supersession chain (old part to new part), list price, lead time, minimum order quantity, hazardous material flag, shelf life, storage conditions, and availability status. Serves as the aftermarket parts reference for field service engineers and customer self-service portals. Complements inventory.sku which manages stock positions — this entity manages the commercial aftermarket catalog definition.'
}

Table service.installation_record {

  bop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.bop. Business justification: Installation records reference the Bill of Process that defines the approved installation sequence and process steps. Field service teams follow the BOP during installation to ensure compliance with e']
  calibration_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.calibration_record. Business justification: New equipment installations require initial calibration before handover. Linking the installation record to the calibration record provides a single audit trail confirming the asset was calibrated at ']
  certificate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.certificate. Business justification: Installed equipment must carry a valid quality or compliance certificate (e.g., CE, UL, ISO). Service teams record the certificate against the installation to satisfy regulatory and customer handover ']
  delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.delivery. Business justification: Equipment installation by field technicians depends on successful delivery of automation systems and components. Installation scheduling directly tied to confirmed delivery completion to avoid technic']
  dispatch_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.dispatch_schedule. Business justification: Installation and commissioning activities are scheduled and dispatched via dispatch schedules. Linking installation_record.dispatch_schedule_id → dispatch_schedule.dispatch_schedule_id connects the in']
  fai_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.fai_record. Business justification: First Article Inspection records verify initial production units meet specifications. Installation teams reference FAI records when commissioning first-of-kind installations to ensure quality complian']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.line_item. Business justification: Installation records in industrial manufacturing are tied to the specific ordered line item (e.g., an automation panel or drive unit). Field service teams reference the original order line to confirm ']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.location. Business justification: An installation record must reference the physical site where equipment is installed. Using the logistics location master avoids address duplication and allows logistics to plan future deliveries or s']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: For non-serialized components installed during commissioning (e.g., firmware chips, gaskets), the lot/batch must be recorded for traceability. Quality teams use this to scope field recalls to affected']
  product_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_configuration. Business justification: Installation records must capture the exact product configuration installed at a customer site. Service engineers reference this to understand the specific variant, options, and settings for accurate ']
  product_variant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.product_variant. Business justification: Installation records must capture exactly which product variant was installed at a customer site. This drives warranty eligibility, applicable service procedures, and correct spare parts selection. Fi']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Installed equipment is traceable to the purchase order that sourced it. Service teams reference the originating PO during installation to confirm specifications, warranty start, and supplier accountab']
  serialized_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit. Business justification: Installation records document which serialized unit was installed at customer site. Essential for tracking equipment lifecycle from inventory through deployment and ongoing service operations.']
  technician_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.technician. Business justification: Installation and commissioning activities are performed by certified service technicians. The commissioning_engineer STRING and commissioning_engineer_certification STRING on installation_record are d']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: Installation records for new equipment/machinery document which work center received the installation. Essential for asset tracking, capacity planning, and warranty service tied to specific work cente']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the installation and commissioning activity was completed on site, used for duration calculation and service performance reporting.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the installation activity physically commenced on site, used for duration tracking and SLA compliance measurement.']
  ce_marking_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether CE marking compliance was verified for the installed product at the time of commissioning, required for products sold in the European Economic Area.']
  checklist_completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of commissioning checklist items that have been completed and verified, providing a quantitative measure of commissioning progress.']
  checklist_completion_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether the commissioning checklist has been fully completed, partially completed, or completed with noted exceptions, ensuring all installation steps are verified.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the installation record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  customer_sign_off_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the customer formally signed off on the installation, marking the official acceptance and triggering warranty activation.']
  customer_signatory_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the customer representative who signed off on the installation acceptance, used for legal and contractual documentation.']
  fai_date date [note: 'Date on which the First Article Inspection (FAI) was conducted at the customer site during commissioning.']
  fai_report_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference number or URL for the FAI report generated during commissioning, stored in the document management system for traceability.']
  fai_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the First Article Inspection (FAI) performed during commissioning to verify that the installed product meets all design and specification requirements before handover.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version of the firmware or software loaded onto the product at the time of commissioning, critical for support, patch management, and cybersecurity compliance.']
  functional_location_code varchar [note: 'Structured code identifying the functional location within the customer plant or facility where the asset is installed, aligned with SAP PM and Maximo EAM hierarchies.']
  handover_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or document management system link for the installation handover package, including as-built drawings, test reports, manuals, and certificates.']
  installation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any notable observations, deviations from standard installation procedures, site-specific conditions, or exceptions recorded by the commissioning engineer.']
  installation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the installation activity indicating whether it is a new installation, an upgrade to existing equipment, a replacement of a failed unit, a relocation, or a decommission.']
  is_customer_signed_off boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has formally signed off on the completed installation and commissioning, confirming acceptance of the installed product.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'IETF BCP 47 language code representing the language in which the installation documentation and handover materials were prepared (e.g., en-US, de-DE, zh-CN).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the installation record, supporting audit trail requirements and change tracking.']
  ncr_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of any Non-Conformance Report (NCR) raised during the installation or commissioning process, linking to the quality management system for CAPA tracking.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'Category of the automation or electrification product being installed, used for service analytics and installed base segmentation.']
  product_sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) code identifying the specific product model being installed, aligned with the product catalog and ERP master data.']
  project_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated project or sales order under which the installation was contracted, linking the installation record to the commercial transaction.']
  record_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business reference number for the installation record, used in customer communications, handover documents, and service reports.']
  safety_check_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all mandatory safety checks and electrical safety tests were completed prior to energizing or commissioning the installed equipment.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date on which the installation and commissioning activity is scheduled to begin, used for resource planning and customer coordination.']
  site_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line of the customer installation site, used for field engineer dispatch and logistics.']
  site_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line (suite, floor, building number) of the customer installation site.']
  site_city varchar [note: 'City where the customer installation site is located, used for regional service analytics and engineer routing.']
  site_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the customer installation site, used for multi-country regulatory compliance and reporting.']
  site_name varchar [note: 'Name or designation of the customer site or facility where the product is installed (e.g., Plant A, Building 3, Substation North).']
  site_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the customer installation site, used for regional compliance and service territory management.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the installation and commissioning activity, from scheduling through customer sign-off and completion.']
  warranty_activation_date date [note: 'Date on which the product warranty is activated following successful installation and customer sign-off, marking the start of the warranty coverage period.']
  warranty_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the product warranty expires, calculated from the warranty activation date based on the contracted warranty period.']

  Note: 'Records of product installation and commissioning activities performed at customer sites for automation systems, electrification solutions, PLC panels, drives, switchgear, and smart infrastructure components. Captures installation date, site address, installed product serial number, installation type (new installation, upgrade, replacement), commissioning engineer, commissioning checklist completion status, FAI (First Article Inspection) result, customer sign-off status, handover documentation reference, and warranty activation date. Feeds the installed base registry and triggers warranty start.'
}

Table service.report {
  report_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the field service completion report. Serves as the primary key for this record across all downstream systems.']
  defect_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.defect_code. Business justification: Field service reports document observed defects using quality defect codes. This enables quality teams to analyze field failure patterns and prioritize improvement initiatives.']
  dfmea_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.dfmea. Business justification: Field service reports on product failures are linked to Design FMEAs to feed real-world failure data back to engineering. Quality engineers use this link to update risk priority numbers and trigger de']
  dispatch_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.dispatch_schedule. Business justification: Field service reports are generated upon completing a specific dispatch visit. Linking report.dispatch_schedule_id → dispatch_schedule.dispatch_schedule_id connects the completion report to the specif']
  document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_document. Business justification: Service reports reference product documents such as technical manuals, schematics, or service bulletins used during the service event. Field engineers attach the relevant product documentation to subs']
  downtime_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.downtime_event. Business justification: Service reports generated after a field intervention are directly linked to the production downtime event that triggered the service call. Operations and service managers use this link to measure resp']
  quality_notification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_notification. Business justification: Field service reports that identify a product defect or non-conformance trigger or reference a quality notification. Service engineers raise or link QNs directly from service reports to initiate forma']
  request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.request. Business justification: Field service completion reports are generated to close service requests. Linking report.request_id → request.request_id establishes the service case context for each report, enabling end-to-end case ']
  stock_adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_adjustment. Business justification: After a service visit where parts are consumed or returned, a service report triggers a stock adjustment in inventory. Linking the report to the adjustment provides a full audit trail from field activ']
  technician_id varchar [note: 'Employee or contractor identifier of the primary field service technician who performed the work and authored this report. Used for labor cost allocation, certification tracking, and performance analytics.']
  warranty_claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.warranty_claim. Business justification: When a field service report identifies warranty-applicable work (is_warranty_applicable flag), it should reference the associated warranty claim. Linking report.warranty_claim_id → warranty_claim.warr']
  work_order_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.work_order_operation. Business justification: Service reports document the outcome of completed work order operations. Field technicians close out a work order operation by submitting a service report — this link is the operational handshake betw']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service report was internally approved by a service manager or quality reviewer. Required before invoicing and warranty claim submission.']
  billing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how the service visit will be billed to the customer or charged internally. Determines the invoicing process, revenue recognition treatment, and cost allocation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which service costs and billing amounts are denominated. Supports multi-currency operations across global service territories.']
  customer_acceptance_notes varchar [note: 'Any comments, reservations, or conditions noted by the customer representative at the time of signing the service report. Captures partial acceptance or outstanding concerns that may affect billing or follow-up.']
  customer_signature_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the customer representative who signed and accepted the service report on-site. Constitutes legal acknowledgment of service completion and is required for billing and warranty claim substantiation.']
  customer_signed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer representative signed and accepted the service report on-site. Constitutes formal proof of service delivery and triggers billing eligibility.']
  date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the field service report was completed and submitted by the technician. Used for billing period determination, warranty claim dating, and SLA compliance measurement.']
  equipment_condition_found varchar [note: 'Condition of the equipment as assessed by the technician upon arrival at the site, prior to performing any work. Used for failure analysis, warranty assessment, and FMEA data collection.']
  equipment_condition_left varchar [note: 'Condition of the equipment as assessed by the technician upon completion of the service visit. Indicates whether the equipment was fully restored to operational status or requires further action.']
  equipment_model varchar [note: 'Model or part number of the equipment serviced. Used for spare parts lookup, technical documentation retrieval, and warranty eligibility determination.']
  equipment_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer serial number of the primary equipment or asset on which service was performed. Links the report to the installed base record and warranty entitlement.']
  failure_description varchar [note: 'Technician\'s description of the fault or failure mode observed on the equipment. Feeds into root cause analysis, FMEA updates, and quality improvement processes.']
  is_follow_up_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the technician has identified the need for a follow-up service visit or additional work order. When true, a new field service order should be created based on the recommendations.']
  is_safety_incident_reported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a safety incident or near-miss was observed and formally reported during the service visit. Triggers mandatory safety incident management workflow per ISO 45001 and OSHA requirements.']
  is_warranty_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service performed is covered under an active product or service warranty. Determines billing treatment and triggers warranty claim processing workflow.']
  labor_hours_total decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total number of labor hours expended by the technician(s) during the service visit, including all activity types. Used for billing, SLA compliance measurement, and workforce productivity analytics.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'IETF BCP 47 language code indicating the language in which the service report was authored and presented to the customer (e.g., en-US, de-DE, fr-FR). Supports multinational operations and customer communication requirements.']
  number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, business-facing unique identifier for the service report, used in customer communications, billing, and warranty claim substantiation. Typically formatted as SR-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  parts_consumed_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total cost of all spare parts and materials consumed during the service visit, in the transaction currency. Used for service billing, warranty cost tracking, and COGS reporting.']
  parts_replaced_description varchar [note: 'Narrative listing of all spare parts replaced during the service visit, including part numbers, descriptions, and quantities. Used for inventory reconciliation, warranty claim substantiation, and billing.']
  recommendations varchar [note: 'Technician\'s recommendations for follow-up work, preventive maintenance actions, spare parts to be stocked, or equipment upgrades identified during the service visit. Drives future service order creation and proactive maintenance planning.']
  resolution_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the type of resolution applied during the service visit. Used for service analytics, spare parts consumption reporting, and billing classification.']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code categorizing the root cause of the equipment failure or service need. Used for CAPA initiation, warranty chargeback decisions, and quality trend analysis.']
  safety_observations varchar [note: 'Technician\'s documented observations regarding safety conditions at the customer site or on the equipment, including hazards identified, PPE used, and any safety incidents or near-misses during the service visit.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service report, from initial draft by the technician through customer signature, internal approval, and final archival. Drives billing eligibility and warranty claim processing.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the technician formally submitted the completed service report, including timezone offset. Used for SLA resolution time calculation and audit trail.']
  technician_certification_number varchar [note: 'Professional certification or qualification number of the technician performing the service, demonstrating competency for the specific equipment type. Required for regulatory compliance and warranty validity.']
  test_outcome varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of post-service functional testing. A \'pass\' result is required for equipment release to operation and warranty validation. \'Fail\' or \'conditional_pass\' triggers follow-up action.']
  test_results_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of all functional tests, measurements, and performance verifications conducted on the equipment after service completion. Includes pass/fail outcomes and measured values. Required for commissioning sign-off and warranty validation.']
  travel_time_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of hours spent by the technician traveling to and from the customer site. Used for billing (if billable travel is included in the service contract), cost allocation, and territory optimization.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the service activity documented in this report. Determines applicable billing rates, warranty eligibility, and regulatory documentation requirements.']
  work_performed_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of all work performed by the technician during the service visit, including diagnostic steps, repairs, adjustments, and tests conducted. Serves as the official record of service delivery for warranty substantiation and customer proof of service.']

  Note: 'Field service completion reports generated by technicians upon completing a field service order at a customer site. Captures the associated field service order, work performed narrative, equipment condition found, parts replaced (part numbers and quantities), labor hours by activity type, test results and measurements, safety observations, customer signature and acceptance, and recommendations for follow-up work. Serves as the official record of service delivery and is shared with the customer as proof of service. Required for warranty claim substantiation and service billing.'
}

Table service.entitlement {
  entitlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the service entitlement record. Serves as the primary key for the service_entitlement data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  blanket_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.blanket_order. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing, long-term service entitlements (e.g., multi-year maintenance coverage) are commercially backed by blanket orders. Finance and service operations link entitlements to blank']
  contract_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.contract_line. Business justification: Service entitlements are granted to customers based on their active service contract lines. The contract_line defines the covered equipment, service type, and commercial terms, while the entitlement d']
  product_variant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.product_variant. Business justification: Entitlements (warranty coverage, service level rights) are granted for specific product variants. When a service request arrives, the system checks entitlement against the installed product variant to']
  sales_price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_price_list. Business justification: Service entitlements in industrial manufacturing are priced against a sales price list (e.g., premium support tiers, extended warranty packages). Finance and service ops use this link to ensure entitl']
  sla_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.sla_commitment. Business justification: Service entitlements define the SLA tier and response/resolution time commitments for a customer. The sla_tier, response_time_hours, and resolution_time_hours on entitlement are denormalized copies of']
  warranty_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.warranty_policy. Business justification: Service entitlements are derived from or linked to warranty policies to define what a customer is entitled to receive. Entitlement validation at case creation depends on the governing warranty policy ']
  activation_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the entitlement was activated and made available for service consumption. May differ from start_date if activation was delayed pending installation or commissioning.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this entitlement is configured to automatically renew upon expiry. Drives renewal workflow triggers in Salesforce Service Cloud and SAP S/4HANA SD.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the customer site or jurisdiction where this entitlement is valid. Supports regional SLA enforcement, regulatory compliance, and territory-based service routing.']
  coverage_scope varchar [note: 'Defines what services are covered under this entitlement: parts and labor, labor only, parts only, remote support only, on-site and remote, or preventive maintenance only. Drives billing and dispatch decisions.']
  covered_product_family varchar [note: 'The product family or product line covered under this entitlement when coverage applies to a group of products rather than a single SKU (e.g., \'SIMATIC S7 PLC Series\', \'SINAMIC Drive Family\').']
  covered_product_sku varchar [note: 'The specific product SKU covered under this entitlement. When populated, entitlement validation in Salesforce Service Cloud checks that the case product matches this SKU.']
  covered_serial_number varchar [note: 'The specific equipment serial number covered under this entitlement. When populated, restricts entitlement consumption to service cases for that specific installed asset.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the service entitlement record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and entitlement lifecycle reporting.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to monetary values within this entitlement record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global regions.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date on which the service entitlement expires and the customer is no longer eligible for covered services. Cases submitted after this date are flagged as out-of-entitlement.']
  incidents_consumed varchar [note: 'The number of service incidents consumed against this entitlement to date. Updated each time a case is validated and accepted under this entitlement. Used to calculate remaining incident balance.']
  incidents_remaining varchar [note: 'The number of service incidents remaining available for consumption under this entitlement (total_incidents_allowed minus incidents_consumed). A null value indicates unlimited incidents remain.']
  is_transferable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this entitlement can be transferred to a new owner upon sale or transfer of the covered equipment. Relevant for installed base management and secondary market support.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent modification to the service entitlement record in the source system. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the service entitlement as defined in the service contract or warranty agreement (e.g., \'Gold Support - Automation Line A\', \'Standard Warranty - Drive Series X\').']
  number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the service entitlement, used in Salesforce Service Cloud and customer-facing communications to reference a specific entitlement record.']
  on_site_support_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether on-site field service visits are included in this entitlement. When true, field service orders can be dispatched under this entitlement without additional billing authorization.']
  owning_business_unit varchar [note: 'The internal business unit or division responsible for fulfilling service obligations under this entitlement (e.g., Automation Services, Electrification Services). Used for cost allocation and P&L reporting.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic service region associated with this entitlement (e.g., EMEA, APAC, AMER). Used for territory management, service capacity planning, and regional KPI reporting.']
  remote_support_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether remote technical support (phone, email, or remote access) is included in this entitlement. Drives routing logic in Salesforce Service Cloud for case assignment.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'The date on which this entitlement is scheduled for renewal review or automatic renewal. Used to trigger renewal notifications and sales follow-up activities.']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA sales organization code responsible for the service contract or warranty from which this entitlement originates. Supports revenue attribution and organizational reporting.']
  service_hours_coverage varchar [note: 'The hours of service coverage included in this entitlement (e.g., 24x7 for round-the-clock support, 8x5 for standard business hours). Determines when SLA clocks run and when dispatches can be scheduled.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this entitlement record was sourced (e.g., Salesforce Service Cloud, SAP S/4HANA SD). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  spare_parts_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether spare parts consumed during service are covered under this entitlement at no additional charge to the customer. Drives parts billing decisions in SAP S/4HANA SD.']
  spare_parts_coverage_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary value of spare parts covered under this entitlement per contract period. When the limit is reached, additional parts are billed to the customer. Null indicates no monetary cap.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which the service entitlement becomes effective and the customer is eligible to consume entitled services. Used for date-range validation during case entitlement checks.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service entitlement. Active entitlements allow case creation and service dispatch. Expired or cancelled entitlements block unauthorized service consumption in Salesforce Service Cloud.']
  total_incidents_allowed varchar [note: 'The total number of service incidents (cases or field service visits) included in this entitlement. A null value indicates unlimited incidents. Used to enforce consumption limits in Salesforce Service Cloud.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the entitlement source: warranty (manufacturer or supplier warranty), service_contract (paid service agreement), support_tier (tiered support subscription), subscription (recurring support plan), extended_warranty (purchased warranty extension), or preventive_maintenance (scheduled PM coverage).']
  value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of services covered under this entitlement for its full duration. Used for revenue recognition, contract profitability analysis, and COGS reporting.']

  Note: 'Defines the specific service entitlements granted to customer accounts based on their active service contracts, product warranties, or support tier subscriptions. Captures entitlement name, entitlement type (warranty, contract, support tier), covered product or product family, number of service incidents included, remaining incident balance, entitlement start and end dates, SLA tier reference, and entitlement status. Used in Salesforce Service Cloud to automatically validate whether a customer is entitled to service before a case is accepted, preventing unauthorized service consumption.'
}

  product_variant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.product_variant. Business justification: Knowledge articles (troubleshooting guides, repair procedures) are authored for specific product variants. Technicians search knowledge articles by variant to find relevant repair instructions. Withou']
  regulatory_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_regulatory_certification. Business justification: Service knowledge articles for regulated industrial equipment must reference the applicable regulatory certification to ensure technicians follow compliant procedures. Safety and compliance teams mand']
  technician_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.technician. Business justification: Knowledge articles are authored by service technicians and engineers. The author_name STRING on knowledge_article is a denormalized reference to the technician master record. Adding technician_id FK n']
  applicable_firmware_version varchar [note: 'Firmware or software version range to which this article applies, ensuring technicians apply the correct resolution for the installed firmware on the equipment.']
  applicable_product_skus varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of specific Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) identifiers for products to which this article applies, enabling precise product-level filtering in service workflows.']
  article_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the knowledge article, used for cross-referencing in service cases, field service orders, and customer portals.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 country code indicating the primary country context for which this article was authored, relevant for country-specific regulatory or product variants.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the knowledge article record was first created in the source system, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  error_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of system error codes, fault codes, or alarm identifiers (from PLC, SCADA, MES, or HMI systems) that this article addresses, enabling automated article suggestion.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the knowledge article is considered outdated and must be reviewed, updated, or retired. Supports document lifecycle management per ISO 9001.']
  helpful_votes varchar [note: 'Number of times users have rated the article as helpful, used to assess article quality and prioritize content improvement efforts.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'IETF BCP 47 language tag indicating the language in which the article is authored (e.g., en-US, de-DE, zh-CN), supporting multinational service operations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the knowledge article record, supporting change tracking and content freshness monitoring.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date on which the article was most recently reviewed for technical accuracy and relevance by a subject matter expert.']
  not_helpful_votes varchar [note: 'Number of times users have rated the article as not helpful, used to identify articles requiring revision or retirement.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'Broader product category classification (e.g., Automation Systems, Electrification Solutions, Smart Infrastructure) to support cross-product knowledge discovery.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'The product family or product line to which this knowledge article applies (e.g., SIMATIC S7 PLCs, SINAMIC Drives, SIVACON Switchgear), enabling filtering by product scope.']
  publication_date date [note: 'Date on which the knowledge article was officially published and made available to its intended audience.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the article addresses regulatory compliance requirements such as CE Marking, RoHS, REACH, or UL certification procedures.']
  resolution_steps varchar [note: 'Step-by-step instructions for resolving the issue, performing the repair procedure, or executing the described process, authored by service engineers or product specialists.']
  review_comments varchar [note: 'Reviewer feedback, revision notes, or approval comments recorded during the technical review process.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the editorial and technical review process for the knowledge article prior to publication.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the subject matter expert or quality reviewer who approved the article for publication.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Documented root cause of the issue or failure addressed by this article, derived from field investigations and Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) analysis.']
  safety_advisory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this article contains safety-critical information requiring mandatory compliance, such as electrical safety warnings, lockout/tagout procedures, or OSHA-regulated hazard notifications.']
  search_keywords varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of keywords and tags that enhance discoverability of the article in knowledge base search, including error codes, product names, and symptom terms.']
  service_territory_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or regional scope for which this article is applicable (e.g., specific country codes or regions), supporting localized service delivery and regulatory variation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this knowledge article was sourced or ingested into the lakehouse (e.g., Salesforce Knowledge, Siemens Teamcenter PLM).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the knowledge article from authoring through publication and eventual retirement.']
  symptom_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the observable symptoms, fault conditions, or error codes that trigger the need for this knowledge article, used for search and first-call resolution matching.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the knowledge article summarizing the issue, procedure, or topic covered.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the article by its content purpose: troubleshooting guide, repair procedure, product bulletin, FAQ, installation guide, best practice, safety advisory, or software release note.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times the knowledge article has been accessed or referenced by service engineers and customers, used to measure article effectiveness and first-call resolution impact.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the knowledge article following major.minor versioning convention, tracking revisions and updates over the article lifecycle.']
  visibility varchar [note: 'Defines the audience scope for the article: internal service engineers only, partner network, customer self-service portal, or publicly accessible.']

  Note: 'Technical knowledge base articles authored by service engineers and product specialists to document solutions to common field issues, troubleshooting procedures, product-specific repair guides, and best practices for automation and electrification products. Captures article title, article type (troubleshooting guide, repair procedure, product bulletin, FAQ), applicable product family, symptom description, root cause, resolution steps, related part numbers, author, review status, publication date, and usage count. Enables first-call resolution improvement and supports self-service customer portals.'
}

Table shared.contact {
  contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual contact record in the silver layer, serving as the primary key for the contact data product.']
  mailing_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: contact stores mailing_city and mailing_state_province as denormalized address fields. The address product is the authoritative repository for all physical and mailing addresses. Adding mailing_addres']
  ccpa_opt_out boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact has exercised their CCPA right to opt out of the sale or sharing of their personal information. Applicable to contacts who are California residents.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country where the contact is primarily located or operates. Used for regional segmentation, compliance jurisdiction determination, and territory assignment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contact record was first created in the source system (Salesforce CRM). Used for data lineage, audit trails, and measuring time-to-contact from lead acquisition.']
  crm_contact_code varchar [note: 'Native record identifier from Salesforce CRM (Contact object ID, e.g., 003XXXXXXXXXXXXXXX), used for cross-system traceability and lineage back to the source system of record.']
  data_subject_request_status varchar [note: 'Tracks the status of any active data subject rights request (access, deletion, portability, rectification) submitted by this contact under GDPR or CCPA. Supports privacy compliance workflows.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department or functional unit the contact belongs to within their company (e.g., Engineering, Procurement, Operations, Finance, Quality). Supports targeted communication and account mapping.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary business email address of the contact, used for commercial communications, order notifications, service updates, and marketing campaigns. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Contact object.']
  email_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact has opted in to receive marketing and commercial email communications. Must be true before any marketing emails are sent. Supports GDPR and CCPA compliance.']
  email_opt_out boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact has explicitly opted out of all marketing email communications. When true, the contact must be excluded from all commercial email campaigns regardless of opt-in status.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the individual contact as recorded in Salesforce CRM. Used for personalized communication and salutation in customer-facing correspondence.']
  gdpr_consent_date date [note: 'Date on which the contact granted or last updated their GDPR consent. Required for audit and compliance purposes to demonstrate lawful basis for data processing under GDPR.']
  gdpr_consent_status varchar [note: 'Current GDPR consent status for processing this contact\'s personal data. Tracks whether consent has been granted, withdrawn, is pending collection, or is not applicable (e.g., for contacts outside GDPR jurisdiction).']
  is_decision_maker boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact holds decision-making authority for purchasing, contracting, or technical approvals within their organization. Critical for sales qualification and opportunity management in B2B industrial sales cycles.']
  is_influencer boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is identified as a key influencer in the purchasing or technical evaluation process, even if they do not hold final decision-making authority. Supports multi-stakeholder account management strategies.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is designated as the primary point of contact for the associated customer account. Only one contact per account should be flagged as primary for a given role.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title or position of the contact within their organization (e.g., Plant Manager, Procurement Manager, Engineering Lead, VP of Operations). Used for segmentation, routing, and persona-based engagement.']
  last_activity_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent logged activity (call, email, meeting, task) associated with this contact in Salesforce CRM. Used to measure contact engagement recency and identify dormant contacts for re-engagement.']
  last_contacted_date date [note: 'Date on which a sales or service representative last made direct contact with this individual (distinct from last_activity_date which includes all logged activities). Supports contact cadence management and SLA tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contact record in the source system (Salesforce CRM). Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental data loads, and audit compliance.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name or surname of the individual contact. Combined with first_name to form the full display name for reporting, CRM, and correspondence.']
  lead_source varchar [note: 'Channel or origin through which this contact was first acquired or entered the CRM system. Used for marketing attribution, pipeline analysis, and ROI measurement of demand generation activities.']
  linkedin_profile_url varchar [note: 'URL of the contact\'s LinkedIn professional profile. Used by sales and account management teams for social selling, relationship intelligence, and contact verification in B2B industrial markets.']
  mobile_phone varchar [note: 'Mobile or cell phone number for the contact, used for urgent communications, field service coordination, and SMS notifications. Distinct from the primary office phone.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Most recent Net Promoter Score (NPS) survey response from this contact, on a scale of 0-10. Used to measure individual contact loyalty and satisfaction, and to identify promoters, passives, and detractors within key accounts.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Primary business phone number for the contact, typically a direct office line or switchboard extension. Used for sales outreach, service escalations, and account management.']
  phone_opt_out boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact has opted out of phone-based outreach including sales calls and telemarketing. Supports compliance with do-not-call regulations and GDPR consent requirements.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) representing the contact\'s preferred language for communications, documentation, and support (e.g., en-US, de-DE, fr-FR, zh-CN).']
  record_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the current version of the contact record is considered valid in the silver layer. Supports slowly changing dimension (SCD) type 2 history tracking and point-in-time reporting for analytics and compliance.']
  reports_to_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the contact\'s direct manager or superior within the customer organization. Used to understand organizational hierarchy and identify escalation paths within the account.']
  role varchar [note: 'Business role classification of the contact within the customer engagement model. Identifies whether the contact is a technical evaluator, commercial decision-maker, executive sponsor, or operational user. Drives sales strategy and communication routing.']
  salutation varchar [note: 'Formal honorific or title prefix used when addressing the contact in written or verbal communication (e.g., Mr., Dr., Eng.). Supports professional etiquette in B2B industrial contexts.']
  sap_business_partner_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA Business Partner identifier corresponding to this contact, enabling cross-system linkage between Salesforce CRM contact records and SAP ERP business partner master data for order management and billing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this contact record was originally sourced or created. Supports data lineage, master data management, and deduplication workflows.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contact record. Indicates whether the contact is actively engaged, inactive, flagged as do-not-contact, or has been merged into another record.']

  Note: 'Individual person records associated with customer accounts, including procurement managers, engineering leads, plant managers, and executive sponsors at B2B industrial buyer organizations. Captures full name, job title, department, phone, email, preferred language, LinkedIn profile, contact role (technical, commercial, executive), opt-in/opt-out flags, and GDPR/CCPA consent status. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Contact object.'
}

Table shared.account_hierarchy {
  account_hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each parent-child account hierarchy relationship record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for the account_hierarchy data product.']
  account_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account_classification. Business justification: account_hierarchy carries customer_classification and industry_sector_code as denormalized STRING attributes. account_classification is the authoritative multi-dimensional classification record for cu']
  sales_area_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.sales_area_assignment. Business justification: account_hierarchy stores sales_organization_code as a STRING, which is a denormalized reference to the SAP sales area configuration. Adding sales_area_assignment_id links the corporate hierarchy node ']
  sla_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.sla_agreement. Business justification: account_hierarchy carries a denormalized sla_tier STRING that references the governing SLA tier for the corporate hierarchy. Adding sla_agreement_id as a FK to sla_agreement normalizes this relationsh']
  account_group_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA account group classification code assigned to the child account within this hierarchy, controlling field selection, number ranges, and partner functions. Examples include KUNA (one-time customer), DEBI (domestic customer), EXPO (export customer).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this hierarchy relationship was formally approved by the designated authority. Used for audit trail, compliance reporting, and governance of corporate account structure changes.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the business authority (e.g., Global Account Director, Credit Manager) who approved the establishment or modification of this hierarchy relationship. Supports governance, audit trail, and segregation of duties requirements.']
