  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code or facility identifier where this asset BOM is applicable. Supports multi-plant operations in a multinational manufacturing enterprise, enabling plant-specific BOM variants and localized spare parts stocking strategies.']
  position_number varchar [note: 'Sequential numeric position identifier for the component within the BOM structure, following SAP BOM item numbering conventions (e.g., 0010, 0020). Used for ordering and referencing BOM line items in maintenance documentation and work orders.']
  quantity decimal(18,6) [note: 'Quantity of the child component required per one unit of the parent assembly. Used in MRO spare parts planning, maintenance work order material requirements, and inventory replenishment calculations.']
  reach_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this component complies with the EU REACH Regulation (EC 1907/2006) for chemical substance registration and restriction. Critical for environmental compliance reporting and supply chain chemical substance management.']
  recommended_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Recommended minimum on-hand stock quantity for this spare part based on criticality, lead time, and maintenance frequency. Drives MRO inventory stocking policies and reorder point calculations in the Warehouse Management System (WMS).']
  replacement_interval_days varchar [note: 'Recommended or OEM-specified replacement interval for this component expressed in calendar days. Complements the operating-hours-based interval for time-based preventive maintenance scheduling, particularly for components subject to age-related degradation regardless of usage.']
  replacement_interval_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Recommended or OEM-specified replacement interval for this component expressed in equipment operating hours. Used to generate preventive maintenance (PM) work orders and schedule planned replacements as part of the TPM program in Maximo EAM.']
  revision varchar [note: 'Revision or version identifier of the asset BOM, incremented when engineering changes (ECN/ECO) are applied to the component structure. Enables tracking of configuration changes over the asset lifecycle.']
  rohs_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this component complies with the EU RoHS Directive (2011/65/EU) restricting the use of hazardous substances such as lead, mercury, cadmium, and hexavalent chromium. Required for CE Marking compliance and environmental regulatory reporting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this asset BOM line was sourced (e.g., Maximo EAM, SAP S/4HANA PM, Siemens Teamcenter PLM). Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution when multiple systems contribute BOM data to the Silver layer.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this BOM line item. Active lines are valid for use in maintenance planning; obsolete lines have been superseded by newer components; draft lines are pending engineering approval. Drives filtering in maintenance planning and MRO procurement workflows.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the component quantity at this BOM line (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for litre). Aligns with the base unit of measure defined in the material master for accurate MRO procurement and inventory management.']
  usage varchar [note: 'Defines the intended usage context of this BOM, distinguishing maintenance BOMs (for MRO spare parts and work order planning) from production BOMs (for manufacturing) and engineering BOMs (for design). Ensures the correct BOM is consumed by the appropriate business process.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,4) [note: 'Physical weight of the component in kilograms. Used for logistics planning, shipping cost estimation, equipment load calculations, and ergonomic risk assessments for maintenance activities per ISO 45001 occupational health requirements.']

  Note: 'Equipment Bill of Materials defining the hierarchical spare parts and sub-component structure of manufacturing assets. Captures parent-child component relationships, part numbers, quantities, installation positions, and criticality ratings. Enables MRO spare parts planning, maintenance task material requirements, and equipment configuration management. Distinct from the engineering product BOM managed in Teamcenter PLM.'
}

Table shared.work_order_operation {
  work_order_operation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual operation step within a maintenance work order. Serves as the primary key for the work_order_operation entity in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  aftermarket_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.aftermarket_part. Business justification: Work order operations consume specific spare/aftermarket parts from the product catalog. Maintenance planners and technicians reference this link to confirm part availability and cost before executing']
  cost_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_allocation. Business justification: Every maintenance work order operation incurs labor and material costs that must be allocated to a cost center. Plant controllers settle work order costs to cost allocations at period-end — standard p']
  execution_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.location. Business justification: Each work order operation is performed at a defined physical location (plant, building, field site). Referencing the logistics location ensures field technicians, parts dispatchers, and planners share']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Work order operations consuming procured spare parts are confirmed against goods receipts. Warehouse and maintenance teams use this link to verify parts availability and confirm material consumption a']
  maintenance_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.maintenance_item. Business justification: In SAP PM, work order operations are created from maintenance items defined within a maintenance plan. Linking work_order_operation.maintenance_item_id → maintenance_item.maintenance_item_id provides ']
  maintenance_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.maintenance_plan. Business justification: In EAM/CMMS systems (SAP PM), work order operations are generated from maintenance plans during scheduling. Linking work_order_operation.maintenance_plan_id → maintenance_plan.maintenance_plan_id enab']
  production_order_cost_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.production_order_cost. Business justification: Maintenance operations performed on production equipment are often settled against production order costs. Manufacturing finance teams link work order operations to production order cost objects to ca']
  task_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.task_list. Business justification: In SAP PM/EAM, work order operations are derived from standardized task list templates. The existing standard_text_key STRING attribute is a loose reference to task text; replacing it with a proper ta']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'Cost accounting activity type code used to classify the labor cost of this operation for internal order settlement and cost center allocation (e.g., MAINT_LABOR, INSP_LABOR, OVHL_LABOR).']
  actual_duration_hours decimal(10,4) [note: 'Actual elapsed time recorded for completing this operation. Used for Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) analysis, scheduling accuracy assessment, and maintenance performance benchmarking.']
  actual_finish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this operation was confirmed as completed by the executing technician or supervisor. Triggers downstream processes such as cost settlement and equipment return to service.']
  actual_labor_hours decimal(10,4) [note: 'Actual number of labor hours recorded upon confirmation of this operation step. Compared against estimated hours for variance analysis, MTTR tracking, and future planning calibration.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when execution of this operation actually commenced, as recorded by the technician or MES system. Used for schedule adherence analysis and MTTR calculation.']
  completion_confirmation_text varchar [note: 'Free-text remarks entered by the technician or supervisor at the time of operation confirmation, describing findings, deviations from plan, or conditions observed during execution.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the time confirmation document recorded against this operation in the EAM/CMMS system. Links the operation to labor time postings and cost accounting entries.']
  control_key varchar [note: 'SAP PM control key that governs how the operation is processed — whether it is internally performed, externally procured, or a milestone operation. Determines scheduling, costing, and confirmation behavior.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the estimated and actual operation costs are expressed. Supports multi-currency financial reporting in multinational manufacturing environments.']
  cost_element varchar [note: 'General ledger cost element code to which the labor and resource costs of this operation are posted. Enables OPEX tracking and maintenance cost analysis by operation type.']
  craft_type varchar [note: 'Trade or skill discipline required to perform this operation (e.g., Mechanical, Electrical, Instrumentation). Enables multi-craft coordination and ensures qualified technicians are assigned.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this work order operation record was created in the source EAM/CMMS system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA compliance tracking.']
  estimated_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Planned cost for executing this operation step, including labor and any directly assigned resources. Used for work order budget control and CAPEX/OPEX planning.']
  estimated_duration_hours decimal(10,4) [note: 'Planned elapsed time (wall-clock duration) to complete this operation, distinct from labor hours when multiple technicians work in parallel. Used for scheduling and equipment downtime planning.']
  estimated_labor_hours decimal(10,4) [note: 'Planned number of labor hours required to complete this operation step, as defined during work order planning. Used for capacity planning, scheduling, and cost estimation.']
  external_service_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order or service order number raised for externally performed operations where a third-party contractor or service provider executes the maintenance task. Links to procurement records.']
  is_milestone boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this operation is a milestone step whose completion triggers automatic progression of the work order status or downstream workflow actions (e.g., safety permit release, quality inspection gate).']
  is_safety_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this operation involves safety-critical activities requiring special permits, lockout/tagout (LOTO) procedures, confined space entry, or other regulatory safety controls.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to this work order operation record in the source system. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance.']
  number_of_technicians varchar [note: 'Number of technicians or workers required to simultaneously execute this operation. Used for crew sizing, shift planning, and labor cost estimation.']
  operation_long_text varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative instructions for the maintenance technician performing this operation, including safety precautions, Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) references, and step-by-step guidance.']
  operation_number varchar [note: 'Sequential step number identifying the position of this operation within the work order (e.g., 0010, 0020, 0030). Determines execution sequence and is used for multi-craft coordination and scheduling.']
  operation_short_text varchar [note: 'Brief description of the maintenance task to be performed in this operation step (e.g., \'Inspect bearing assembly\', \'Replace hydraulic seal\'). Used in work order printouts and shop floor instructions.']
  operation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance operation type indicating the nature of work to be performed. Drives resource planning, cost allocation, and compliance reporting.']
  permit_to_work_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Permit to Work (PTW) document issued for this operation. Provides audit trail for safety compliance and regulatory inspections.']
  permit_to_work_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal Permit to Work (PTW) must be issued and approved before this operation can commence. Enforces safety compliance for high-risk maintenance activities.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or facility code where this operation is to be executed. Supports multi-plant maintenance operations and regional reporting in multinational environments.']
  qualification_code varchar [note: 'Specific skill or certification code required to perform this operation (e.g., high-voltage certification, confined space entry, forklift license). Ensures regulatory compliance and worker safety.']
  remaining_labor_hours decimal(10,4) [note: 'Forecasted remaining labor hours needed to complete this operation, updated during partial confirmations. Supports real-time work order progress tracking and resource reallocation decisions.']
  scheduled_finish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when this operation is scheduled to be completed. Used for maintenance window management, production impact assessment, and SLA compliance tracking.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when this operation is scheduled to begin. Used for maintenance scheduling, shift planning, and equipment downtime window coordination.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the work order operation step. Tracks progression from creation through execution to completion, enabling operation-level progress monitoring and KPI reporting.']
  sub_operation_number varchar [note: 'Optional sub-step identifier used when an operation is further decomposed into parallel or sequential sub-tasks. Supports detailed multi-craft breakdown within a single operation step.']
  system_status varchar [note: 'System-generated status code from the source EAM/CMMS system (e.g., CRTD, REL, CNF, TECO) reflecting the technical processing state of the operation. Complements the business-facing status field.']
  user_status varchar [note: 'User-defined status code applied by maintenance planners or supervisors to reflect business-specific workflow states beyond the system status (e.g., AWMAT - Awaiting Materials, AWPMT - Awaiting Permit).']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center (maintenance crew, craft group, or production cell) responsible for executing this operation. Used for capacity planning, labor scheduling, and cost center assignment.']

  Note: 'Individual operation steps within a maintenance work order defining the sequence of maintenance tasks, craft/skill requirements, estimated and actual labor hours, work center assignments, and operation-level completion status. Enables detailed labor planning, multi-craft coordination, and operation-level progress tracking within complex maintenance work orders.'
}

Table shared.stock_position {
  stock_position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each stock position record in the inventory data product. Serves as the primary key for the stock_position entity in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  asset_bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_bom. Business justification: Stock positions for spare parts and components are directly tied to an asset\'s BOM. Inventory planners use the asset BOM to determine which parts must be stocked to support that asset — a daily operat']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.location. Business justification: Stock positions must reference a physical logistics location (warehouse, plant, hub). Inventory planners and logistics coordinators use this link to determine where stock is held and plan inbound/outb']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: stock_position.batch_number is a denormalized batch reference. Normalizing to lot_batch_id FK against the lot_batch master enables full batch traceability from the stock position.']
  reorder_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.reorder_policy. Business justification: stock_position tracks reorder_point and safety_stock_quantity which are governed by reorder_policy. Adding reorder_policy_id FK links each stock position to its governing replenishment policy, enablin']
  sales_price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.price_list. Business justification: Stock positions reference price lists to determine inventory valuation and available-to-promise pricing. Warehouse systems check price lists when reserving stock for sales orders to calculate order va']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_territory. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing with regional distribution, stock positions are allocated per sales territory to ensure regional availability commitments are met — territory sales managers check regional ']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing, stock positions are often ring-fenced or allocated by customer segment (e.g., automotive vs. energy). Demand planning and inventory teams use this to manage segment-specif']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: stock_position.storage_location_code is a denormalized location code. Normalizing to storage_location_id FK against the storage_location master eliminates the redundant code column.']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'ABC classification of the material based on consumption value or usage frequency. A-items are high-value/high-usage requiring tight control; B-items are medium; C-items are low-value/low-usage. D may indicate dead stock. Drives cycle counting frequency and replenishment strategy.']
  atp_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the material available to commit to new customer orders or production demands, calculated as unrestricted quantity minus reserved quantity. Supports ATP (Available-to-Promise) and CTP (Capable-to-Promise) checks in order management.']
  blocked_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the material that has been blocked and is not available for any use, typically due to quality rejection, damage, or regulatory hold. Corresponds to SAP MARD-SPEME (blocked stock).']
  consignment_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the material held at the company\'s premises but owned by the supplier (vendor consignment stock). Liability is incurred only upon consumption. Corresponds to SAP special stock type K (vendor consignment).']
  count_variance_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The difference between the physically counted quantity and the system book quantity at the time of the last physical inventory or cycle count. Positive values indicate surplus; negative values indicate shortage. Used for inventory accuracy KPI reporting.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the material was manufactured or produced. Required for customs declarations, import/export compliance, REACH/RoHS regulatory reporting, and trade preference determination.']
  goods_receipt_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent goods receipt was posted for this stock position. Used for FIFO valuation, aging analysis, and supplier delivery performance tracking. Corresponds to SAP MSEG-BUDAT (posting date of goods movement).']
  hazardous_material_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the material is classified as hazardous and subject to special storage, handling, and transportation regulations. Drives compliance with OSHA, EPA, REACH, RoHS, and dangerous goods regulations.']
  in_transit_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the material that has been shipped from a source location but has not yet been received at the destination location. Includes stock in transfer orders between plants or from suppliers. Corresponds to SAP stock in transit (MARD-UMLME).']
  last_count_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent physical inventory count or cycle count was performed for this stock position. Used to assess count freshness and schedule next cycle count. Corresponds to SAP MARD-LSOBS (date of last count).']
  last_movement_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent inventory movement (goods receipt, goods issue, transfer, or adjustment) for this stock position. Used to identify slow-moving or obsolete inventory for write-down analysis.']
  lot_size_procedure varchar [note: 'The lot-sizing rule applied during MRP planning to determine the replenishment order quantity. Options include exact lot size (order exactly what is needed), fixed lot size, economic order quantity (EOQ), and period-based lot sizing.']
  maximum_stock_level decimal(18,4) [note: 'The upper inventory quantity threshold beyond which stock should not be replenished, used to prevent overstock and excess carrying costs. Corresponds to SAP MARC-MABST (maximum stock level).']
  mrp_type varchar [note: 'Defines the planning procedure used for this material\'s replenishment. Determines whether the material is planned via MRP (demand-driven), consumption-based reorder point, forecast-based planning, or managed manually. Corresponds to SAP MARC-DISMM.']
  on_hand_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total physical quantity of the material currently present at the storage location, regardless of stock type. Represents the gross inventory count before any reservations or holds are applied. Sourced from SAP MARD-LABST + EINME + SPEME.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the manufacturing plant or distribution center where the stock position is held. Corresponds to the SAP Plant organizational unit. Enables multi-plant inventory visibility and reporting.']
  position_snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time at which this stock position record was captured or last refreshed from the source systems (SAP S/4HANA MM and Infor WMS). Enables point-in-time inventory reporting and audit trail for the Silver Layer data product.']
  quality_inspection_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the material currently held in quality inspection and not yet released for use. Pending QM (Quality Management) inspection lot disposition. Corresponds to SAP MARD-EINME.']
  reorder_point decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum stock level at which a replenishment order should be triggered to avoid stockout. Used in MRP (Material Requirements Planning) reorder point planning. Corresponds to SAP MARC-MINBE.']
  reserved_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the material that has been reserved for specific production orders, sales orders, or project requirements and is therefore not available for other demands. Sourced from SAP reservation management (RESB).']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The buffer stock quantity maintained to protect against demand variability and supply uncertainty, preventing stockouts. Corresponds to SAP MARC-EISBE (safety stock). A key input to MRP planning runs.']
  shelf_life_expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the current batch or stock position expires and can no longer be used. Applicable to batch-managed materials with shelf-life management enabled. Drives FEFO (First Expired First Out) picking strategies.']
  slow_moving_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this stock position has been classified as slow-moving based on the absence of inventory movements over a defined threshold period. Triggers review for potential write-down or disposal actions.']
  stock_category varchar [note: 'Indicates the ownership or special stock category of the inventory position. Distinguishes company-owned stock from consignment stock, returnable packaging, pipeline stock, and customer/project-specific stock.']
  stock_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the stock by its usability and availability status. Unrestricted stock is available for use; quality inspection stock is pending QM release; blocked stock is not available; in-transit stock is between locations. Drives ATP (Available-to-Promise) calculations.']
  total_stock_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the on-hand stock position, calculated as on-hand quantity multiplied by the valuation price. Expressed in the valuation currency. Used for balance sheet inventory reporting and financial close processes.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The base unit of measure in which all stock quantities for this material are expressed (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for liter, M for meter). Corresponds to SAP MARA-MEINS (base unit of measure).']
  unrestricted_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the material that is freely available for use, sales order fulfillment, or production consumption without any quality or usage restrictions. Corresponds to SAP MARD-LABST (unrestricted-use stock).']
  valuation_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the inventory valuation price and stock value are expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency reporting for multinational operations.']
  valuation_method varchar [note: 'The costing method applied to value the inventory position. Standard price uses a fixed planned cost; moving average price recalculates with each goods receipt. FIFO and LIFO are used in specific regulatory contexts.']
  valuation_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The per-unit price used to value the inventory position, based on the material\'s valuation method (standard price or moving average price). Corresponds to SAP MBEW-STPRS (standard price) or MBEW-VERPR (moving average price).']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'Sub-classification of a material for split valuation purposes, allowing different batches or procurement types (e.g., domestic vs. imported) to be valued separately within the same material and plant.']
  wip_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the material currently in the production process as Work-in-Progress (WIP), issued to production orders but not yet completed as finished goods. Tracked via Siemens Opcenter MES and SAP PP production orders.']

  Note: 'SSOT for current inventory positions across all SKUs, storage locations, plants, and distribution centers. Captures on-hand quantity, reserved quantity, available-to-promise (ATP) quantity, WIP stock, and in-transit stock for raw materials, finished goods, semi-finished goods, and MRO items. Aligned with SAP S/4HANA MM stock overview and Infor WMS inventory balance records.'
}

Table shared.storage_location {
  storage_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each storage location record within the warehouse or plant facility. Serves as the primary key for the storage_location data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  abc_class varchar [note: 'ABC inventory classification assigned to the storage location based on the velocity and value of materials typically stored there. Drives slotting optimization — A-class locations are positioned for fastest access, supporting lean and JIT replenishment strategies.']
  aisle varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric identifier for the aisle within the warehouse where this storage location is physically situated. Used for navigation, putaway routing, and picking path optimization.']
  bin varchar [note: 'Specific bin or slot identifier within a rack level, representing the most granular physical storage unit. Corresponds to the SAP WM storage bin and Infor WMS bin location.']
  block_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why a storage location has been blocked or taken out of service. Required for quality management traceability and corrective action processes (CAPA).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where this storage location is physically situated. Supports multi-country inventory reporting, regulatory compliance, and cross-border logistics management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp recording when this storage location master record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements, and master data governance.']
  cycle_count_indicator varchar [note: 'Cycle counting frequency classification assigned to this storage location, determining how often physical inventory counts are performed. A-class locations are counted most frequently. Supports inventory accuracy programs and audit compliance.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date on which this storage location was or is scheduled to be permanently decommissioned and removed from active warehouse operations. Supports warehouse layout lifecycle management and historical reporting.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this storage location record became or becomes operationally active. Supports temporal validity management for location master data and warehouse layout change tracking.']
  external_location_code varchar [note: 'Location identifier used in external or third-party systems such as 3PL warehouse management systems, customer portals, or EDI integrations. Enables cross-system location reconciliation for multi-system environments.']
  fire_protection_class varchar [note: 'Fire protection classification assigned to the storage location based on the type of materials stored and applicable fire suppression systems. Supports HSE compliance and emergency response planning.']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'UN/DOT hazardous materials classification for the storage location, indicating the category of dangerous goods that may be stored. Drives segregation rules, safety signage requirements, and regulatory compliance for hazmat storage zones.']
  height_mm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Internal height dimension of the storage location in millimeters. Critical for tall or oversized SKU putaway validation and rack clearance compliance.']
  last_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent physical cycle count was performed for this storage location. Used to schedule upcoming counts, assess inventory accuracy, and support audit trail requirements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this storage location master record. Used for change tracking, data synchronization between SAP WM and Infor WMS, and audit trail compliance.']
  length_mm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Internal length dimension of the storage location in millimeters. Used for dimensional compatibility checks during putaway to ensure SKU packaging fits within the bin.']
  level varchar [note: 'Vertical level or tier designation within a rack or shelving unit (e.g., floor level, level 1, level 2). Used for ergonomic slotting, weight distribution planning, and picking efficiency.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Business-facing alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the storage location within a warehouse or plant. Used in SAP WM as the storage bin number and in Infor WMS as the location ID for putaway and picking operations.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive human-readable name or label for the storage location, used for display in warehouse management interfaces, picking lists, and operational reports.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the storage location is fixed (assigned to a specific SKU), random (dynamically assigned), dedicated, shared, or virtual. Determines slotting strategy and WMS putaway logic.']
  max_pallet_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of pallets or handling units that can be stored simultaneously in this location. Used for pallet-level capacity management and putaway strategy in Infor WMS.']
  max_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable temperature in degrees Celsius for this storage location. Enforced for hazardous material storage compliance and product quality preservation.']
  max_volume_m3 decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum volumetric capacity of the storage location in cubic meters. Used for volumetric putaway checks and space utilization analytics.']
  max_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum allowable weight in kilograms that the storage location can safely hold. Enforced during putaway to prevent structural overloading and ensure workplace safety compliance.']
  min_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable temperature in degrees Celsius for this storage location. Used for environmental compliance monitoring and material storage condition validation.']
  mixed_lot_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether multiple lot or batch numbers of the same SKU may be stored together in this location. Critical for pharmaceutical, food, and regulated component traceability and FIFO/FEFO compliance.']
  mixed_sku_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether multiple distinct SKUs may be co-located within this storage location simultaneously. When false, the location is restricted to a single SKU to prevent picking errors and inventory commingling.']
  next_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next physical cycle count at this storage location. Drives cycle count scheduling workflows in SAP MM and Infor WMS to maintain inventory accuracy targets.']
  picking_strategy varchar [note: 'Picking strategy assigned to this storage location, determining the sequence and method by which stock is retrieved for outbound orders. Supports FIFO/FEFO compliance for perishable and regulated materials.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing or distribution facility where this storage location physically resides. Supports multi-plant inventory segregation and reporting.']
  putaway_strategy varchar [note: 'Putaway strategy assigned to this storage location, governing how incoming stock is directed to this bin. Supports FIFO, FEFO, and zone-directed strategies aligned with Infor WMS and SAP WM putaway rules.']
  rack varchar [note: 'Identifier for the rack or shelving unit within the aisle where this storage location is positioned. Supports physical navigation and structural capacity management.']
  replenishment_strategy varchar [note: 'Replenishment strategy governing how this storage location is restocked from reserve or bulk storage. Supports Kanban, min-max, and demand-driven replenishment models aligned with JIT and MRP principles.']
  site_region varchar [note: 'Geographic or organizational region designation for the site where this storage location resides (e.g., EMEA, APAC, Americas). Supports regional inventory reporting, supply chain analytics, and organizational hierarchy alignment.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the storage location. Blocked or inactive locations are excluded from putaway and picking strategies. Supports lifecycle management and WMS operational control.']
  storage_section_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the storage section within a storage type, used to group bins with similar characteristics for putaway strategy optimization in SAP WM and Infor WMS.']
  storage_type_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the physical storage type classification within the warehouse structure, such as high-rack, open storage, fixed bin, or automated storage. Corresponds to SAP WM storage type and Infor WMS location type.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature classification of the storage location defining the environmental control requirements. Ensures temperature-sensitive materials (chemicals, adhesives, electronic components) are stored in compliant conditions.']
  width_mm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Internal width dimension of the storage location in millimeters. Used alongside length and height for volumetric capacity calculations and putaway compatibility validation.']
  zone_type varchar [note: 'Functional zone classification of the storage location defining its operational purpose within the warehouse. Drives putaway and picking strategies, compliance controls, and inventory segregation rules in Infor WMS and SAP WM.']

  Note: 'Granular storage location master defining aisles, racks, bins, shelves, and zones within a warehouse or plant. Captures location code, warehouse association, zone type (bulk, pick, reserve, quarantine, hazmat), bin dimensions, weight capacity, temperature zone, and active status. Supports putaway and picking strategies in Infor WMS and SAP WM storage bin management.'
}

Table shared.lot_batch {
  lot_batch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a material lot or production batch record in the inventory domain. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and traceability references.']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Lot/batch records are created at goods receipt. Every lot in inventory is traceable to the specific goods receipt that introduced it — critical for quality traceability and supplier accountability in ']
  hazardous_substance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.hazardous_substance. Business justification: Batches of hazardous materials must reference the product domain\'s hazardous substance classification for regulatory compliance, safe handling instructions, and transport documentation. Eliminates dup']
  material_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.material_specification. Business justification: Incoming lots are received and inspected against a specific material specification. Quality and receiving teams use this link to pull acceptance criteria, test methods, and certificate of conformance ']
  product_cost_estimate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.product_cost_estimate. Business justification: Lot/batch inventory is valued using the product cost estimate (standard cost) at time of production. Manufacturing cost accountants link batches to cost estimates to compute production variances and i']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line_item. Business justification: Each lot/batch received into inventory originates from a specific PO line item. This link enables full procurement-to-inventory traceability — essential for audits, warranty claims, and supplier quali']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: lot_batch.storage_location_code is a denormalized location code. Normalizing to storage_location_id FK links each batch to its current storage location for warehouse management.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Lot/batch traceability requires knowing which supplier produced the material. Quality teams use this link to issue supplier corrective actions and perform batch recalls traced back to the originating ']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'The ABC inventory classification of the material in this batch based on consumption value or criticality. A-class items represent high-value/high-consumption materials requiring tight control; B-class items are medium; C-class items are low-value/low-consumption; D-class items are obsolete or non-moving. Drives cycle counting frequency and safety stock policies.']
  batch_classification_class varchar [note: 'The SAP classification class assigned to this batch, enabling batch-specific characteristic values (e.g., purity percentage, viscosity, tensile strength) to be stored as classification attributes. Supports batch search, selection, and quality characteristic reporting.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing alphanumeric identifier assigned to a material lot or production batch. Corresponds to the SAP MM batch number (CHARG field) and is used for traceability, quality inspection, and FIFO/FEFO picking across the supply chain.']
  batch_size_uom varchar [note: 'The unit of measure used to express the original batch size at the time of production or receipt, which may differ from the current inventory unit of measure. Supports unit conversion tracking and production yield analysis.']
  batch_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the batch origin indicating whether it was produced internally, purchased from a supplier, received on consignment, returned from a customer, created through rework, or designated as a quality sample.']
  best_before_date date [note: 'The date up to which the batch retains its optimal quality characteristics, distinct from the hard expiry date. Used for quality-sensitive materials where degraded-but-not-expired stock may be sold at discount or redirected. Supports FEFO picking and customer SLA compliance.']
  certificate_of_analysis_number varchar [note: 'The document number of the Certificate of Analysis (CoA) issued for this batch, confirming that the material meets specified quality standards and test results. Required for regulated industries and customer quality documentation packages.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the material in this batch was manufactured or substantially transformed. Required for customs declarations, import/export compliance, REACH/RoHS regulatory reporting, and customer certificate of origin documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this batch master record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and batch age analysis. Corresponds to SAP batch creation date and time (ERSDA/ERSZT).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which this batch is no longer suitable for use, sale, or consumption. Drives FEFO (First Expired First Out) picking strategies, automatic batch blocking, and regulatory compliance for shelf-life-managed materials. Corresponds to SAP batch expiry date (VFDAT).']
  goods_receipt_date date [note: 'The date on which this batch was physically received into inventory via a goods receipt posting. For purchased batches, this is the date the material arrived at the plant. Used as the FIFO baseline date for purchased materials and for supplier lead time analysis.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material in this batch is classified as hazardous under applicable regulations (e.g., OSHA HazCom, REACH, RoHS, UN dangerous goods). When true, triggers special handling, storage, and transportation requirements including hazmat documentation and labeling.']
  inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'The SAP QM inspection lot number (PRUEFLOS) associated with the quality inspection triggered for this batch. Links the batch to its quality inspection results, usage decisions, and defect records in the Quality Management system.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current quality inspection status of the batch controlling its availability for use, picking, and shipment. Unrestricted batches are available for all movements; quality_hold and blocked batches are restricted pending inspection or disposition; restricted batches have limited usage; in_inspection batches are undergoing active QM evaluation. Aligned with SAP QM batch status.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this batch master record was most recently updated. Tracks changes to batch attributes such as inspection status, quantity, or storage location for audit compliance and data freshness monitoring.']
  manufacturing_date date [note: 'The date on which this batch was manufactured or produced. Used as the baseline for shelf life calculations, FIFO/FEFO picking strategy, and regulatory traceability. Corresponds to SAP batch manufacturing date (HSDAT).']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the material in this batch according to its stage in the production lifecycle. Distinguishes raw materials, Work in Progress (WIP), finished goods, Maintenance Repair and Operations (MRO) stock, semi-finished goods, packaging materials, and spare parts.']
  max_storage_temp_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'The maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this batch. Used in conjunction with minimum storage temperature to define the acceptable temperature range for warehouse compliance monitoring and cold chain management.']
  min_storage_temp_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'The minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this batch. Used to validate warehouse temperature zone compliance, trigger alerts for out-of-condition storage, and support regulatory documentation for temperature-sensitive materials.']
  original_batch_size decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total quantity of material in this batch at the time of initial creation (production completion or goods receipt). Used as the denominator for yield calculations, scrap rate analysis, and batch consumption tracking.']
  picking_strategy varchar [note: 'The inventory picking strategy applied to this batch during warehouse order fulfillment. FIFO (First In First Out) prioritizes oldest batches; FEFO (First Expired First Out) prioritizes soonest-to-expire batches; LIFO (Last In First Out) is used in specific regulatory contexts; FMFO (First Manufactured First Out) is based on manufacturing date; manual allows operator discretion.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or distribution center where this batch was produced or received. Determines the organizational unit for inventory valuation, quality management, and storage location assignment.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The current on-hand quantity of material in this batch expressed in the base unit of measure. Reflects the net available quantity after all goods movements, consumption, and adjustments. Core field for inventory position reporting and MRP (Material Requirements Planning) calculations.']
  remaining_shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'The number of days remaining before this batch reaches its expiry date, calculated at the time of the last inventory snapshot or transaction. Used for FEFO picking prioritization, customer minimum shelf life checks, and proactive disposition planning.']
  restricted_substance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material in this batch contains substances restricted under REACH, RoHS, or other environmental regulations. When true, requires compliance documentation, customer disclosure, and may restrict sale in certain markets or regions.']
  retest_date date [note: 'The date on which this batch is scheduled for re-inspection or retesting to confirm continued conformance to quality specifications. Used for materials with defined retest intervals (e.g., chemicals, raw materials) to prevent use of out-of-specification stock.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'The total shelf life of the material in this batch expressed in days, as defined in the material master. Used to calculate expiry dates when manufacturing date is known and to enforce minimum remaining shelf life rules for customer shipments.']
  storage_condition_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the required storage conditions for this batch (e.g., ambient, refrigerated, frozen, controlled atmosphere, hazardous). Drives warehouse slotting, temperature zone assignment, and compliance with material safety data sheet (MSDS) requirements.']
  supplier_batch_number varchar [note: 'The batch or lot number assigned by the external supplier or vendor for purchased materials. Maintained alongside the internal batch number to enable end-to-end supply chain traceability, supplier quality investigations, and recall management. Corresponds to SAP batch field LICHA.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The base unit of measure in which the batch quantity is expressed (e.g., KG, EA, L, M, PC). Aligns with the SAP material master base unit of measure (MEINS) and is required for all inventory transactions, MRP calculations, and reporting.']
  usage_decision varchar [note: 'The quality management usage decision recorded for this batch following inspection. Determines whether the batch is accepted for unrestricted use, rejected for disposal, conditionally released with restrictions, sent for rework, scrapped, or pending a decision. Corresponds to SAP QM usage decision (VCODE).']
  valuation_currency varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the batch valuation price is expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency inventory valuation for multinational operations.']
  valuation_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The unit price used to value the inventory in this batch for financial reporting and cost accounting purposes. May reflect standard cost, moving average price, or actual cost depending on the material\'s valuation method in SAP CO. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity.']
  warehouse_bin varchar [note: 'The specific warehouse bin or slot identifier within the storage location where this batch is physically stored. Enables precise put-away and picking instructions in the Warehouse Management System (WMS) and supports bin-level inventory accuracy.']

  Note: 'Master record for material lots and production batches tracked through inventory. Captures batch number, SKU, manufacturing date, expiry date, shelf life remaining, supplier batch reference, country of origin, quality inspection status (unrestricted, quality hold, blocked, restricted), batch classification characteristics, and storage conditions. Supports FIFO/FEFO picking strategies and traceability requirements. Aligned with SAP MM batch master.'
}

Table shared.serialized_unit {
  serialized_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each serialized unit record in the inventory system. Serves as the primary key for the serialized_unit data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  cad_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.cad_model. Business justification: High-value serialized units (e.g., automation modules, drives) are linked to the CAD model revision they were built to. Service engineers use this during field maintenance to retrieve the exact as-des']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Serialized units (individual tracked components) are registered in inventory at goods receipt. The link to goods_receipt provides the inbound procurement event that created the serialized unit record ']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: serialized_unit.batch_number is a denormalized batch reference. Normalizing to lot_batch_id FK links each serialized unit to its production batch for full genealogy traceability.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line_item. Business justification: Serialized units in industrial manufacturing must be traceable to the exact PO line item they were procured under. Used in warranty, field service, and compliance audits to confirm procurement origin ']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: serialized_unit.storage_location_code is a denormalized location code. Normalizing to storage_location_id FK links each serialized unit to its current granular storage location.']
  warranty_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.warranty_policy. Business justification: Each serialized unit carries the warranty policy applicable at time of manufacture or sale. Field service and after-sales teams look up the warranty policy on the serialized unit daily to determine re']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'The ABC inventory classification of the SKU associated with this serialized unit, indicating its relative value and management priority. A-class items are high-value/high-priority; C-class are low-value/low-priority.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'The SAP company code representing the legal entity that owns this serialized unit for financial accounting and inventory valuation purposes. Supports intercompany and multi-entity reporting.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where this serialized unit is currently located. Supports multi-country inventory reporting, regulatory compliance, and cross-border logistics.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where this serialized unit was manufactured. Required for customs declarations, import/export compliance, and RoHS/REACH regulatory reporting.']
  customer_delivery_number varchar [note: 'The SAP SD outbound delivery document number associated with the shipment of this serialized unit to a customer. Enables end-to-end traceability from inventory to customer delivery.']
  goods_receipt_date date [note: 'The date on which this serialized unit was received into inventory via a goods receipt posting. Marks the start of inventory ownership and is used for aging analysis and FIFO/FEFO valuation.']
  hazardous_material boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this serialized unit is classified as a hazardous material requiring special handling, storage, or transportation procedures. Drives compliance with OSHA, EPA, and transport regulations.']
  infor_wms_serial_code varchar [note: 'The unique serial unit identifier assigned by the Infor WMS system for warehouse operations tracking. Used for reconciliation between SAP MM serial number records and Infor WMS operational data.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'The current quality inspection status of this serialized unit. Indicates whether the unit has passed, failed, or is pending quality inspection before being released to unrestricted stock.']
  inventory_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the serialized unit by inventory category: raw material, work in progress (WIP), finished good, maintenance repair and operations (MRO), spare part, or semi-finished. Supports inventory segmentation and valuation reporting.']
  last_movement_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent inventory movement (goods issue, goods receipt, transfer, or stock change) recorded for this serialized unit. Used for slow-moving inventory analysis and dormancy detection.']
  last_movement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time of the most recent inventory movement for this serialized unit. Provides sub-day precision for operational tracking, audit trails, and MES integration.']
  manufacturing_date date [note: 'The date on which this serialized unit was manufactured or assembled. Used for age-based quality analysis, warranty calculations, shelf-life tracking, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  movement_type_code varchar [note: 'The SAP movement type code (e.g., 101 for goods receipt, 261 for goods issue to production order, 311 for stock transfer) associated with the last inventory movement of this serialized unit.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The SAP plant code representing the manufacturing facility or distribution center where this serialized unit currently resides or was last recorded. Aligns with SAP organizational structure.']
  quality_inspection_lot varchar [note: 'The SAP QM inspection lot number associated with this serialized unit if it is currently under quality inspection. Links the unit to its quality management workflow and usage decision.']
  reach_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this serialized unit complies with the EU REACH regulation governing the use of chemical substances. Required for product safety, regulatory reporting, and EU market access.']
  rohs_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this serialized unit complies with the EU RoHS Directive restricting the use of hazardous substances in electrical and electronic equipment. Required for CE marking and EU market access.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'The unique alphanumeric serial number assigned to an individual physical unit, enabling unit-level traceability throughout its lifecycle. Corresponds to the SAP MM serial number (SERNR) field.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the serialized unit indicating its operational disposition. Drives inventory availability, traceability workflows, and reporting. Aligned with SAP MM serial number status management.']
  stock_type varchar [note: 'The SAP stock type classification indicating the usability of the serialized unit for production or sales. Unrestricted stock is available for use; quality inspection and blocked stock are restricted pending resolution.']
  storage_bin varchar [note: 'The specific bin or slot within the warehouse storage location where this serialized unit is physically placed. Provides the most granular location detail for picking and putaway operations.']
  supplier_number varchar [note: 'The vendor or supplier number identifying the external party that supplied this serialized unit. Used for supplier quality management, traceability, and PPAP compliance.']
  valuation_currency varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the valuation price of this serialized unit is expressed. Supports multi-currency inventory valuation in multinational operations.']
  valuation_method varchar [note: 'The inventory valuation method applied to this serialized unit for cost accounting purposes. Standard price uses a fixed planned cost; moving average price reflects actual procurement costs.']
  valuation_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The unit valuation price of this serialized unit used for inventory accounting. May reflect standard price or moving average price depending on the valuation method configured in SAP MM.']
  warranty_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the warranty for this serialized unit expires. Drives after-sales service eligibility checks, warranty claim processing, and customer service SLA management.']
  warranty_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the manufacturer or supplier warranty for this serialized unit becomes effective. Used to determine warranty coverage windows for after-sales service and claims management.']

  Note: 'Master record for individually serialized inventory items tracked at the unit level rather than quantity level. Captures serial number, SKU, batch, current storage location, plant, serial number status (in stock, issued to production, shipped, returned, scrapped), manufacturing date, warranty start date, and last movement date. Supports traceability for high-value components, electrification equipment, and automation systems. Aligned with SAP MM serial number management.'
}

Table shared.engineering_bom {
  engineering_bom_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the Bill of Materials header record. Serves as the primary key for the BOM entity across all integrated systems including SAP PP and Siemens Teamcenter.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.category. Business justification: Engineering BOMs are classified by product category (e.g., automation module, electrification unit) to route them to the correct engineering team and apply category-specific design rules and complianc']
  ecn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: bom.ecn_number is a STRING reference to the ECN that authorized the BOM revision. Replacing with FK ecn_id → ecn.ecn_id enforces referential integrity for the change management workflow. ecn_number is']
  lifecycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.lifecycle. Business justification: Every engineering BOM is tied to a product lifecycle phase (e.g., prototype, production, obsolete). PLM teams reference this daily to gate BOM releases and control which revisions are active for manuf']
  plm_lifecycle_state_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.plm_lifecycle_state. Business justification: Engineering BOMs are PLM-managed objects that progress through formal lifecycle states (In Work, Under Review, Released, Obsolete, Superseded). Linking engineering_bom to plm_lifecycle_state provides ']
  alternative_bom varchar [note: 'SAP alternative BOM counter (01–99) that distinguishes multiple BOMs for the same material/plant combination, enabling variant or alternative production structures for the same finished good or sub-assembly.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or user ID of the authorized approver who released this BOM revision for production use. Part of the formal BOM approval and release workflow in Siemens Teamcenter PLM and SAP PP, ensuring compliance with ISO 9001 document control requirements.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this BOM revision was formally approved and released by the authorized approver. Provides an auditable timestamp for regulatory compliance, quality management system (QMS) traceability, and engineering change management records.']
  base_quantity decimal(18,6) [note: 'Reference quantity of the parent assembly for which all component quantities in the BOM are specified. Typically 1 unit but may be set to a batch size (e.g., 100 units) for process manufacturing or high-volume production to improve precision in component quantity calculations.']
  base_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure corresponding to the base quantity of the parent assembly (e.g., EA for each, PC for piece, KG for kilogram). Aligns with SAP base unit of measure (MEINS) in the material master.']
  category varchar [note: 'SAP BOM category (STLTY) classifying the object type to which the BOM is assigned. Material BOMs define product structures; Equipment BOMs support plant maintenance (PM) and asset management; Functional Location BOMs define site-level structures; WBS BOMs support project-based manufacturing (ETO/MTO).']
  change_number varchar [note: 'SAP Engineering Change Management (ECM) change number used to control time-phased BOM changes in SAP PP. Enables change-controlled BOM modifications where component additions, deletions, or quantity changes are governed by an approved change record.']
  component_count varchar [note: 'Total number of direct child components (line items) defined in this BOM header. Provides a quick indicator of BOM complexity and is used for completeness validation during BOM release and change management reviews.']
  costing_relevance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this BOM is included in product cost calculations (standard cost estimate) in SAP CO-PC (Product Costing). When true, the BOM components and quantities are used to calculate the Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) and standard cost for the parent material.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the primary country where the product defined by this BOM is manufactured. Required for customs declarations, CE Marking compliance, RoHS/REACH regulatory reporting, and export control classification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this BOM record was initially created in the source system (SAP PP or Siemens Teamcenter). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and lifecycle tracking.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the BOM, providing additional context about the product structure, intended application, or special configuration. Sourced from the BOM header text in SAP PP or the item description in Siemens Teamcenter.']
  engineering_responsibility varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the engineering team, department, or responsible engineer who owns this BOM. Used for change management routing, approval workflows, and accountability tracking in Siemens Teamcenter PLM.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export Control Classification Number (ECCN) or Harmonized System (HS) code assigned to the product defined by this BOM. Required for export license determination, trade compliance, and customs documentation for multinational shipments of automation systems and electrification solutions.']
  is_configurable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this BOM is a configurable/variant BOM (SAP variant configuration) that supports multiple product configurations from a single super-BOM. Relevant for Assemble-to-Order (ATO) and Engineer-to-Order (ETO) automation systems and electrification solutions.']
  is_phantom_assembly boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this BOM represents a phantom assembly — a logical grouping of components that does not physically exist as a stocked sub-assembly. Phantom assemblies are exploded through in MRP and production orders, with their components planned directly at the parent level.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code indicating the language in which the BOM description and component texts are maintained. Supports multilingual BOM management for global manufacturing operations across multiple countries and regions.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to this BOM record in the source system. Used for change tracking, delta processing in the Databricks Silver layer, and audit compliance.']
  level varchar [note: 'Depth level of this BOM within the overall product structure hierarchy. Level 0 represents the top-level finished good; Level 1 represents direct sub-assemblies; deeper levels represent lower-tier components. Used for multi-level BOM explosion in MRP and costing.']
  manufacturing_strategy varchar [note: 'Manufacturing execution strategy associated with this BOM, indicating how the parent assembly is produced relative to customer demand. Drives MRP planning parameters, lead time calculations, and order management behavior in SAP PP and Siemens Opcenter MES.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for engineering notes, special instructions, or contextual remarks associated with this BOM. May include DFM/DFA considerations, APQP notes, PPAP requirements, or special manufacturing constraints relevant to the product structure.']
  number varchar [note: 'Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the BOM, used in engineering and production documents. Corresponds to the BOM number in SAP PP (CS01/CS02) and Siemens Teamcenter BOM Management.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Four-character SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or production site for which this BOM is valid. A BOM may have plant-specific variants to reflect local production capabilities, tooling, or supplier substitutions.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the parent product, finished good, or sub-assembly for which this BOM is defined. Sourced from the material master description in SAP MM or the item name in Siemens Teamcenter PLM.']
  production_version varchar [note: 'SAP production version identifier linking this BOM to a specific routing/work center combination for a given plant. Enables multiple production methods (e.g., different lines or processes) for the same material, each with its own BOM and routing pairing.']
  regulatory_compliance_flags varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited set of regulatory compliance certifications applicable to the product defined by this BOM. Supports compliance reporting for RoHS (Restriction of Hazardous Substances), REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals), CE Marking, and UL (Underwriters Laboratories) certification requirements.']
  released_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this BOM was formally released to production, making it available for use in MRP runs, production orders, and manufacturing execution in Siemens Opcenter MES. Distinct from approval timestamp — release may follow approval after additional validation steps.']
  revision_level varchar [note: 'Engineering revision identifier (e.g., A, B, C, 01, 02) indicating the version of the BOM. Incremented upon each approved Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) that modifies the product structure.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this BOM record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution in the Databricks Silver layer when BOMs are synchronized between SAP S/4HANA PP and Siemens Teamcenter PLM.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the BOM record. Controls whether the BOM is available for use in production planning (MRP), manufacturing execution (MES), or procurement. Released BOMs are active for production; Frozen BOMs are locked for costing; Obsolete/Superseded BOMs are retired.']
  teamcenter_item_code varchar [note: 'Cross-reference identifier of the corresponding BOM or product structure item in Siemens Teamcenter PLM. Enables bidirectional traceability between the SAP PP BOM and the engineering BOM (eBOM) managed in Teamcenter for PLM/PDM alignment.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the BOM by its intended use in the product lifecycle. Engineering BOM (eBOM) reflects design intent from PLM/PDM; Manufacturing BOM (mBOM) reflects production structure from MES/ERP; Service BOM supports after-sales and MRO; Sales BOM supports order configuration; Costing BOM supports COGS calculation; Planning BOM supports MRP/MRP II.']
  usage varchar [note: 'SAP BOM usage indicator that determines in which business context the BOM is applied (e.g., production orders, plant maintenance work orders, sales order processing, product costing). Corresponds to SAP BOM Usage field (STLAN) in CS01.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this BOM revision becomes effective and valid for use in production planning, MRP runs, and manufacturing execution. Supports time-phased BOM management aligned with Engineering Change Notice (ECN) effectivity dates.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'Date after which this BOM revision is no longer valid for production planning or manufacturing execution. Used to manage BOM supersession when a new revision is released via ECN/ECO. A null value indicates the BOM is open-ended and currently active.']

  Note: 'Bill of Materials header record defining the structured product assembly hierarchy for each finished good, sub-assembly, or configurable automation system. Captures BOM type (engineering BOM, manufacturing BOM, service BOM), effectivity dates, revision level, BOM status, and the owning product or assembly. Links to Siemens Teamcenter BOM Management and SAP PP BOM. SSOT for product structure definitions.'
}

Table shared.bop {
  bop_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a Bill of Process header record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.']
  ecn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: bop.ecn_reference is a STRING reference to the ECN that authorized the routing change. Replacing with FK ecn_id → ecn.ecn_id enforces referential integrity for change management. ecn_reference is redu']
  eco_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.eco. Business justification: bop.eco_reference is a STRING reference to the ECO that authorized the routing implementation. Replacing with FK eco_id → eco.eco_id enforces referential integrity. eco_reference is redundant once the']
  engineering_bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.bom. Business justification: bop.linked_bom_number is a STRING reference to the BOM this routing is linked to. A BOP defines the manufacturing process for a specific BOM structure. Replacing with FK bom_id → bom.bom_id enforces t']
  plm_lifecycle_state_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.plm_lifecycle_state. Business justification: Bills of Process (manufacturing routings) are PLM-managed objects that progress through formal lifecycle states before being released to manufacturing execution systems. Linking bop to plm_lifecycle_s']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the process engineer or engineering authority who formally approved this Bill of Process for production release. Required for ISO 9001 document control and audit trail compliance.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this Bill of Process revision was formally approved for production release by the authorized process engineer or engineering authority. Supports ISO 9001 document control and change management audit requirements.']
  base_quantity decimal(13,3) [note: 'Reference quantity for which the Bill of Process operations and time values are defined (e.g., 1 unit, 100 units). All operation times and resource requirements in the BoP are scaled relative to this base quantity during production order creation.']
  base_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure corresponding to the base quantity for this Bill of Process (e.g., EA for each, PC for piece, KG for kilogram). Aligns with SAP unit of measure configuration and ISO 80000 measurement standards.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the manufacturing plant or facility where this Bill of Process is executed. Used for trade compliance, customs declarations, CE marking, and country-of-origin labeling requirements in multinational operations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this Bill of Process record was first created in the source PLM or ERP system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and ISO 9001 document control requirements.']
  cycle_time_seconds decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total elapsed time to complete one full execution of the Bill of Process from first operation start to last operation completion, expressed in seconds. Used for capacity planning, OEE calculation, and production scheduling in Siemens Opcenter MES and SAP PP.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the manufacturing process defined by this BoP, including scope, key process steps, and intended assembly or sub-assembly. Supports operator understanding and process documentation requirements under ISO 9001.']
  effectivity_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this Bill of Process revision is no longer valid for new production orders. Supports date-effective routing expiry in SAP PP and ensures superseded processes are not applied to new manufacturing orders.']
  effectivity_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this Bill of Process revision becomes valid for production use. Used in date-effective routing selection in SAP PP and MES scheduling to ensure the correct process version is applied to production orders.']
  environmental_classification varchar [note: 'Environmental impact classification of the manufacturing process, indicating whether it generates waste, emissions, requires chemical handling, or is energy-intensive. Supports ISO 14001 environmental management compliance and EPA reporting obligations.']
  finished_product_number varchar [note: 'SAP material number or PLM part number of the finished assembly or sub-assembly produced by this Bill of Process. Provides direct linkage to the product master in SAP MM/PP and Siemens Teamcenter for production planning and scheduling.']
  is_concurrent_operations_allowed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether operations within this Bill of Process may be executed in parallel (concurrently) rather than strictly sequentially. Enables parallel scheduling in Siemens Opcenter MES and SAP PP finite scheduling.']
  is_preferred_routing boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this Bill of Process is the preferred (primary) routing for the associated finished or semi-finished product. When true, this BoP is selected by default during production order creation in SAP PP unless overridden.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code for the primary language in which this BoP documentation and work instructions are authored (e.g., EN, DE, ZH, FR). Supports multilingual shop floor documentation in global manufacturing operations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to this Bill of Process record in the source system. Used for incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer and change detection in downstream MES and ERP integrations.']
  lead_time_days decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total planned manufacturing lead time for the process in calendar days, from order release to completion. Used in SAP MRP/MRP II scheduling, ATP/CTP calculations, and delivery promise management.']
  lot_size_max decimal(13,3) [note: 'Maximum production quantity for which this Bill of Process is valid. Production orders exceeding this quantity may require splitting or use of an alternate routing. Used in SAP PP lot-size-dependent scheduling.']
  lot_size_min decimal(13,3) [note: 'Minimum production quantity for which this Bill of Process is valid. Production orders below this quantity may require a different routing or process variant. Used in SAP PP lot-size-dependent scheduling and MRP planning.']
  manufacturing_strategy varchar [note: 'Production strategy governing how this process is triggered: Make to Stock (MTS), Make to Order (MTO), Assemble to Order (ATO), or Engineer to Order (ETO). Determines how the BoP is linked to demand signals in SAP PP/MRP and Siemens Opcenter MES.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive human-readable name for the Bill of Process, summarizing the assembly or sub-assembly manufacturing process it defines (e.g., \'Motor Drive Assembly – Standard Process\'). Used in MES scheduling and shop floor documentation.']
  number varchar [note: 'Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the Bill of Process, used in PLM, MES, and ERP systems to reference the process structure. Corresponds to the routing number in SAP PP and the BoP document number in Siemens Teamcenter.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Four-character SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where this Bill of Process is valid and executed. Supports multi-plant, multinational operations and plant-specific routing assignments.']
  process_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the manufacturing process type: discrete (individual units), process (batch/continuous), repetitive (high-volume standard), project (one-off), or continuous. Determines scheduling and execution methodology in SAP PP and Siemens Opcenter MES.']
  process_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the BoP indicating whether it represents the standard production process, an alternate routing, a rework process, a prototype build, or a pilot run. Drives routing selection logic in SAP PP and Siemens Opcenter MES.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'Product family or product line classification to which the finished assembly produced by this BoP belongs (e.g., \'Drive Systems\', \'Switchgear\', \'Automation Controllers\'). Supports aggregate production planning and portfolio-level reporting.']
  production_version varchar [note: 'SAP production version identifier linking this BoP to a specific combination of BOM and routing for a finished or semi-finished product. Enables alternative production scenarios (e.g., different work centers or batch sizes) within SAP PP/MRP.']
