  quality_inspection_plan_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP QM inspection plan or Siemens Opcenter quality plan associated with this Bill of Process. Links the process structure to mandatory quality checkpoints, AQL sampling plans, and SPC control points required by the QMS.']
  revision varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric revision identifier indicating the version of the Bill of Process (e.g., A, B, 01, 02). Incremented upon each approved Engineering Change Order (ECO) affecting the process structure. Aligns with PLM revision management in Siemens Teamcenter.']
  safety_classification varchar [note: 'Safety classification of the manufacturing process defined by this BoP, indicating special handling, access control, or environmental requirements (e.g., hazardous materials, ESD-sensitive components, cleanroom conditions). Supports OSHA and ISO 45001 compliance.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this Bill of Process record originates (e.g., Siemens Teamcenter PLM, SAP S/4HANA PP, Siemens Opcenter MES). Supports data lineage and master data governance in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the Bill of Process record, controlling its availability for production use. Only BoPs in \'released\' status are eligible for MES execution and SAP PP order creation. Managed through the ECO/ECN change workflow in Siemens Teamcenter.']
  takt_time_seconds decimal(10,3) [note: 'The available production time divided by customer demand rate, expressed in seconds. Defines the required pace of production for this process to meet customer demand. A fundamental Lean Manufacturing metric used in line balancing and capacity planning.']
  total_operation_count varchar [note: 'Total number of manufacturing operations (process steps) defined within this Bill of Process. Provides a quick structural indicator of process complexity and is used in MES work order generation and shop floor documentation.']
  work_center_category varchar [note: 'Category of the primary work center type associated with this Bill of Process, indicating the dominant manufacturing technology or process family. Used for capacity planning aggregation and shop floor routing in SAP PP and Siemens Opcenter MES.']

  Note: 'Bill of Process header record defining the sequence of manufacturing operations, work centers, and process steps required to produce a finished assembly or sub-assembly. Captures process type (standard, alternate), revision level, effectivity dates, cycle time, takt time, and linkage to the corresponding engineering BOM. SSOT for process structure feeding Siemens Opcenter MES and SAP PP routing.'
}

  cad_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.cad_model. Business justification: engineering_drawing.cad_model_reference is a STRING reference to the associated CAD model from which the drawing was derived. Replacing with FK cad_model_id → cad_model.cad_model_id enforces the PLM r']
  ecn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: engineering_drawing.ecn_number is a STRING reference to the ECN that authorized the drawing revision. Replacing with FK ecn_id → ecn.ecn_id enforces change management traceability for controlled engin']
  plm_lifecycle_state_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.plm_lifecycle_state. Business justification: Engineering drawings are PLM-managed controlled documents that progress through formal lifecycle states (In Work, Under Review, Released, Obsolete). Linking drawing to plm_lifecycle_state provides for']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the authorized approver who released the drawing for production or procurement use. Establishes formal engineering authority and sign-off accountability.']
  assembly_level varchar [note: 'Position of the depicted item within the product structure hierarchy. Indicates whether the drawing represents a top-level assembly, sub-assembly, individual component, or purchased part.']
  cad_format varchar [note: 'Native or exchange file format of the CAD model or drawing file (e.g., Siemens NX, CATIA, STEP, PDF). Determines software compatibility for viewing, editing, and downstream manufacturing use.']
  checked_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the engineer who performed the technical check/peer review of the drawing. Part of the mandatory drawing approval workflow per quality management standards.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Information security classification of the drawing content. Controls access permissions, distribution rights, and handling requirements for the drawing across internal teams, suppliers, and customers.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the engineering site where the drawing was originally created. Relevant for export control compliance, intellectual property jurisdiction, and regulatory certification.']
  created_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the drawing was originally created in the PLM system. Used for drawing age analysis, audit trails, and compliance with document retention policies.']
  drawn_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the engineer or drafter who created the drawing. Captured in the drawing title block for accountability and traceability per engineering documentation standards.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export control classification of the drawing and its associated technology under US EAR (Export Administration Regulations) or ITAR (International Traffic in Arms Regulations). Governs distribution of drawings to foreign nationals and international sites.']
  is_controlled_document boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the drawing is a controlled document subject to formal change management, revision control, and distribution tracking per the quality management system. Controlled drawings require ECN authorization for any modification.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code of the drawing annotations and title block text (e.g., en, de, zh-CN, fr). Supports multilingual drawing management for global manufacturing sites and international customer deliveries.']
  last_modified_date date [note: 'Date on which the drawing record was most recently updated in the PLM system. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and identification of recently revised drawings.']
  material_specification varchar [note: 'Primary material specification or standard called out on the drawing (e.g., ASTM A36, DIN EN 10025, AL6061-T6). Drives material procurement, supplier qualification, and incoming inspection requirements.']
  number varchar [note: 'Formal alphanumeric identifier assigned to the engineering drawing per company numbering standards. Used as the primary business key for cross-system reference in Teamcenter PLM and SAP DMS.']
  obsolescence_date date [note: 'Date on which the drawing was formally declared obsolete or superseded. Prevents use of outdated drawings in manufacturing and supports document lifecycle management.']
  originating_department varchar [note: 'Engineering department or design team responsible for creating and owning the drawing (e.g., Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Systems Engineering). Supports ownership accountability and routing.']
  paper_size varchar [note: 'Standard paper or sheet size designation for the drawing (ISO A-series or ANSI letter sizes). Governs print and reproduction specifications for physical drawing distribution.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility for which the drawing is primarily applicable. Supports plant-specific drawing distribution and manufacturing execution.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'Business product line or product family to which the drawn component or assembly belongs (e.g., automation systems, electrification solutions, smart infrastructure). Supports portfolio-level drawing management and reporting.']
  projection_method varchar [note: 'Orthographic projection convention used in the drawing (first-angle per ISO/European practice or third-angle per ANSI/US practice). Critical for correct interpretation of multi-view drawings across global manufacturing sites.']
  regulatory_compliance_flags varchar [note: 'Applicable regulatory compliance certifications or directives that the drawn component or assembly must satisfy (e.g., CE Marking, RoHS, REACH, UL). Drives compliance documentation and design constraint requirements.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date on which the drawing was formally approved and released for use in production, procurement, or field operations. Marks the transition from engineering control to manufacturing control.']
  revision_level varchar [note: 'Current revision identifier of the drawing (e.g., A, B, C or 01, 02). Tracks the engineering change history and ensures the correct version is used in production and procurement.']
  scale varchar [note: 'Nominal drawing scale expressed as a ratio (e.g., 1:1, 1:2, 2:1, NTS for Not to Scale). Defines the proportional relationship between the drawn representation and the actual object dimensions.']
  sheet_count varchar [note: 'Total number of sheets comprising the complete engineering drawing package. Used for document completeness verification during review, approval, and distribution.']
  sheet_number varchar [note: 'Identifies the specific sheet within a multi-sheet drawing set (e.g., sheet 1 of 5). Enables precise reference to individual drawing sheets during manufacturing and inspection.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the engineering drawing within the PLM workflow. Controls whether the drawing may be used for production, procurement, or field installation activities.']
  superseded_by_drawing_number varchar [note: 'Drawing number of the newer drawing that replaces this drawing when it is obsolete or superseded. Enables forward navigation through the drawing revision history for configuration management.']
  surface_finish varchar [note: 'Surface finish or surface texture requirement specified on the drawing (e.g., Ra 1.6 µm, 63 µin RMS). Governs machining, grinding, and coating process requirements for manufactured components.']
  title varchar [note: 'Official descriptive title of the engineering drawing as it appears in the title block. Identifies the subject component, assembly, or system depicted.']
  tolerance_standard varchar [note: 'Governing dimensional and geometric tolerance standard applied to the drawing. Ensures consistent interpretation of tolerances by manufacturing, quality, and supplier teams.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the engineering drawing by its technical purpose. Determines the applicable drafting standards, review workflow, and downstream usage in manufacturing, installation, or maintenance.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Primary dimensional unit used throughout the drawing (millimeters, inches, etc.). Prevents measurement interpretation errors across global manufacturing and supplier facilities.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,4) [note: 'Nominal weight of the component or assembly depicted in the drawing, expressed in kilograms. Used for logistics planning, structural analysis, packaging design, and shipping documentation.']

  Note: 'Master record for formal engineering drawings and technical documents including assembly drawings, detail drawings, wiring diagrams, and schematics. Captures drawing number, title, revision level, drawing type, sheet count, scale, tolerance standard, approval status, release date, and associated component or assembly. Managed in Siemens Teamcenter Document Management and linked to SAP DMS.'
}

Table shared.ecn {
  ecn_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the Engineering Change Notice record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the ecn data product.']
  actual_implementation_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual total cost incurred to implement the engineering change upon completion. Compared against estimated_implementation_cost for variance analysis, budget control, and continuous improvement of change cost estimation accuracy.']
  affected_item_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the primary affected component, assembly, drawing, or process document. Provides context for reviewers and approvers without requiring a PLM system lookup.']
  affected_item_number varchar [note: 'Primary part number, assembly number, drawing number, or BOM node identifier that is the subject of the engineering change. Represents the main item being modified. Links to the PLM/PDM product structure in Teamcenter.']
  affected_item_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary item being changed: component (individual part), assembly/sub_assembly (multi-part structure), drawing (2D/3D technical drawing), bom (Bill of Materials structure), process (manufacturing process/BoP), document (SOP, specification), software (firmware/embedded code), or tooling (jigs, fixtures, dies).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the Engineering Change Notice received final approval from the Engineering Change Board (ECB) or designated approval authority. Marks the transition from review to authorized implementation status.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the final approving authority (e.g., Chief Engineer, Engineering Change Board chairperson) who authorized the ECN. Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance documentation.']
  bom_revision_from varchar [note: 'The current BOM or drawing revision level that is being superseded by this engineering change. Enables before/after comparison and revision history tracking in Teamcenter PLM.']
  bom_revision_to varchar [note: 'The new BOM or drawing revision level that will be established upon implementation of this engineering change. Defines the target state of the product structure post-change.']
  capa_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the CAPA record in the Quality Management System that triggered this ECN, when the change is driven by a corrective or preventive action. Provides traceability between quality events and engineering changes per ISO 9001 requirements.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Business driver or root cause triggering the Engineering Change Notice. CAPA-driven changes link to corrective/preventive actions; obsolescence indicates end-of-life component replacement; regulatory_compliance reflects REACH, RoHS, CE Marking, or other mandates. Critical for change governance and traceability.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the engineering change by its primary nature: design (geometry, tolerances, CAD), process (manufacturing method, BoP), material (raw material substitution), regulatory (compliance-driven), software (firmware/embedded), documentation (drawing/SOP update), or safety (hazard mitigation). Drives routing and approval authority.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the estimated implementation cost and actual implementation cost fields. Supports multi-currency reporting for multinational operations (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY, GBP).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when the ECN record was first created in Teamcenter, including draft records not yet submitted. Supports full lifecycle audit trail from initial creation through implementation.']
  customer_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the engineering change requires formal notification to customers per contractual obligations or regulatory requirements. When true, triggers customer communication workflow in Salesforce CRM and may require customer approval before implementation.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the full scope, rationale, and technical details of the proposed engineering change. Supports engineering review, impact assessment, and regulatory documentation.']
  disposition_of_existing_stock varchar [note: 'Decision on how to handle existing inventory of the affected component or assembly after the engineering change is implemented. Drives warehouse and inventory management actions in Infor WMS and SAP S/4HANA WM to prevent use of obsolete parts.']
  eco_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Engineering Change Order (ECO) issued to authorize and execute the physical implementation of this ECN. The ECO is the downstream execution document that follows ECN approval in the change management workflow.']
  effectivity_type varchar [note: 'Method by which the engineering change becomes effective in production: date_effectivity (change applies from a specific date), serial_number_effectivity (change applies from a specific serial number), lot_effectivity (change applies from a specific production lot), or unit_effectivity (change applies to specific unit numbers). Critical for production control and spare parts management.']
  effectivity_value varchar [note: 'The specific value defining when the change becomes effective, interpreted according to effectivity_type. For date_effectivity, this is a date string; for serial_number_effectivity, this is the first serial number; for lot_effectivity, this is the lot number. Enables precise production cutover control.']
  estimated_implementation_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost to implement the engineering change, including engineering labor, tooling modifications, material write-offs, qualification testing, and production line changeover. Used for change prioritization, CAPEX/OPEX planning, and ROI analysis.']
  implementation_date_actual date [note: 'Date on which the engineering change was fully implemented and the new revision became effective in production. Used for on-time delivery measurement, change cycle time analytics, and regulatory compliance records.']
  implementation_date_planned date [note: 'Target date by which the engineering change is planned to be fully implemented in production. Used for production planning, material procurement scheduling in SAP MRP, and milestone tracking.']
  initiator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the engineer or technical staff member who initiated the Engineering Change Notice. Retained for display in reports and approval documents where the employee ID alone is insufficient for human-readable audit trails.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the ECN record in Teamcenter. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and for detecting concurrent modification conflicts.']
  number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, business-facing unique identifier for the Engineering Change Notice as assigned by Teamcenter Change Management. Used for cross-system referencing, audit trails, and communication with engineering, procurement, and manufacturing teams.']
  owning_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department responsible for the engineering change, such as Product Engineering, R&D, Manufacturing Engineering, or Quality. Used for workload distribution, cost allocation, and departmental KPI reporting.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or site where the engineering change will be implemented. Supports multi-site change management for multinational operations.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the ECN indicating urgency of implementation. Critical priority typically indicates safety, regulatory, or production-stopping issues requiring immediate action. Drives scheduling of review boards and implementation timelines.']
  regulatory_compliance_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the engineering change affects the product\'s compliance status with applicable regulations such as RoHS, REACH, CE Marking, UL certification, or IEC standards. When true, triggers compliance re-evaluation and potential re-certification activities.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory standard, directive, or compliance requirement driving the engineering change, such as RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU, REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, CE Marking Directive, UL certification requirement, or OSHA standard. Populated when change_reason is regulatory_compliance.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation provided by the reviewer or approver when an ECN is rejected. Documents the specific technical, commercial, or compliance reasons for rejection to guide the initiator in revising and resubmitting the change request.']
  requires_dfmea_update boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the engineering change necessitates an update to the Design Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (DFMEA) document. Ensures risk assessment documentation remains current and aligned with the changed design.']
  requires_pfmea_update boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the engineering change requires an update to the Process Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (PFMEA) document. Ensures manufacturing process risk documentation is updated to reflect changed process steps or parameters.']
  requires_ppap boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the engineering change requires a Production Part Approval Process submission to the customer or internal quality authority before the changed part can be used in production. Mandatory for automotive and aerospace supply chain compliance.']
  safety_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the engineering change has a potential impact on product safety, operator safety, or end-user safety. When true, triggers mandatory safety review and may require additional regulatory approvals (CE Marking, UL certification, OSHA compliance review).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which the ECN record was ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports data lineage tracking and multi-source reconciliation in the lakehouse architecture.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the ECN within the Teamcenter Change Management workflow. Tracks progression from initial draft through engineering review board approval to physical implementation on the shop floor. Drives downstream actions in SAP S/4HANA MM/PP modules.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the Engineering Change Notice was formally submitted for review in Teamcenter. Used for change cycle time measurement, SLA compliance tracking, and audit trail.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short, descriptive title summarizing the nature of the proposed engineering change. Used in dashboards, approval workflows, and search interfaces within Teamcenter and SAP S/4HANA.']

  Note: 'Engineering Change Notice header record capturing the formal initiation and approval of a proposed change to a component, BOM, drawing, or process. Records ECN number, change title, change type (design, process, material, regulatory), initiator, priority, affected items, reason for change (e.g., CAPA-driven, cost reduction, obsolescence), estimated implementation cost, and current workflow state (draft, review, approved, rejected, implemented). SSOT for engineering change governance in Teamcenter Change Management.'
}

Table shared.product_specification {
  product_specification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the product specification record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.']
  drawing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_drawing. Business justification: product_specification.drawing_number is a STRING reference to the engineering drawing that this specification is associated with. Replacing with FK engineering_drawing_id → engineering_drawing.enginee']
  ecn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: product_specification.ecn_number is a STRING reference to the ECN that authorized this specification revision. Replacing with FK ecn_id → ecn.ecn_id enforces change management traceability for product']
  material_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.material_specification. Business justification: Product specifications often reference specific material specifications (e.g., a housing spec requiring a specific aluminum alloy grade, or an insulation spec referencing a polymer material standard).']
  plm_lifecycle_state_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.plm_lifecycle_state. Business justification: Product specifications are PLM-managed controlled documents that progress through formal lifecycle states (In Work, Under Review, Released, Obsolete). Linking product_specification to plm_lifecycle_st']
  product_variant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.product_variant. Business justification: Product specifications define the functional, performance, and environmental requirements for a specific product variant (e.g., a 400V/22kW drive variant has different specs than a 690V/45kW variant).']
  regulatory_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_regulatory_certification. Business justification: Product specifications must reference the regulatory certifications (CE, UL, IEC) the product is designed to meet. Engineering uses this to validate design requirements against certification scope bef']
  warranty_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.warranty_policy. Business justification: Product specifications encode design-for-reliability targets that must align with the commercial warranty policy. Engineering references warranty policy to set MTBF targets, test durations, and design']
  applicable_standard varchar [note: 'Primary industry or regulatory standard that governs this specification parameter (e.g., \'IEC 61131-2\', \'EN 60204-1\', \'ISO 9283\', \'CE Marking Directive 2006/42/EC\', \'RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU\'). Supports regulatory traceability and compliance reporting.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Formal approval workflow status of the specification record. Approved specifications are authorized for use in design, procurement, and manufacturing. Distinct from lifecycle status; captures the approval gate outcome.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the engineering authority who formally approved the specification. Required for regulatory traceability and design review records under ISO 9001 and PPAP.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the specification was formally approved by the designated engineering authority. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset for global operations.']
  ce_marking_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this specification parameter contributes to CE Marking conformity assessment for the European market. CE-relevant parameters must be verified and documented in the Technical File.']
  country_of_applicability varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the primary country or market for which this specification applies. Relevant for region-specific regulatory requirements (e.g., CE for EU, UL for USA, CCC for China).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the specification record was first created in the source system. Provides audit trail for specification lifecycle management and regulatory documentation.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the specification scope, purpose, and applicability. Provides context for design engineers, quality teams, and regulatory reviewers.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this specification revision becomes the authoritative design baseline for production, procurement, and quality inspection. Supports time-bounded specification validity management.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this specification revision expires or is superseded by a newer revision. Null indicates the specification is currently active with no planned expiry.']
  is_critical_characteristic boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this specification parameter is designated as a Critical Characteristic (CC) or Key Characteristic (KC) requiring enhanced control, SPC monitoring, and mandatory documentation in the Control Plan. Critical characteristics directly impact safety, regulatory compliance, or product function.']
  is_safety_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this specification parameter has direct implications for product safety, operator safety, or end-user safety. Safety-relevant parameters require additional review, documentation, and regulatory approval under CE Marking, UL, and ISO 45001.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code of the specification document (e.g., \'en\', \'de\', \'zh-CN\', \'fr\'). Supports multilingual documentation management for global engineering teams.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the specification record in the source system. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail maintenance in the Silver Layer.']
  maximum_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Absolute maximum acceptable value for the parameter, representing the hard upper bound. Used in design verification, test planning, and quality inspection to define pass/fail criteria.']
  minimum_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Absolute minimum acceptable value for the parameter, representing the hard lower bound beyond which the product fails to meet specification. Distinct from tolerance_lower which is a deviation from nominal.']
  nominal_value varchar [note: 'The target or ideal value for the specification parameter (e.g., \'24\', \'85\', \'500\'). Stored as STRING to accommodate numeric, alphanumeric, and enumerated nominal values across diverse parameter types.']
  parameter_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific technical parameter being specified (e.g., \'Input Voltage\', \'Operating Temperature\', \'Tensile Strength\', \'IP Protection Rating\'). Each specification record captures one parameter for traceability granularity.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or site for which this specification is applicable. Supports multi-plant, multinational operations where specifications may vary by production site.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'The product family or product line to which this specification applies (e.g., \'SIMATIC S7 Controllers\', \'SINUMERIK CNC Systems\', \'SIRIUS Motor Starters\'). Enables specification reuse and family-level compliance reporting.']
  reach_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this specification parameter is subject to REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals) regulation, applicable to chemical composition and substance-of-very-high-concern (SVHC) thresholds.']
  revision varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric revision identifier (e.g., A, B, C, 01, 02) indicating the version of the specification. Incremented upon each approved engineering change. Aligns with PLM revision management in Siemens Teamcenter.']
  rohs_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this specification parameter is relevant to RoHS (Restriction of Hazardous Substances) compliance, particularly for material composition specifications governing restricted substances such as lead, mercury, cadmium, and hexavalent chromium.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this specification record was ingested (e.g., Siemens Teamcenter PLM, SAP S/4HANA). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  specification_category varchar [note: 'Engineering discipline category of the specification parameter. Enables domain-specific filtering and routing to the appropriate engineering team for review and verification.']
  specification_number varchar [note: 'Formal business identifier for the product specification document, typically assigned by the PLM/PDM system (Siemens Teamcenter). Used for cross-referencing in engineering change notices (ECN), design reviews, and regulatory submissions.']
  specification_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the specification by its engineering purpose. Functional specs define what the product must do; interface specs define integration points; material specs define composition; environmental specs define operating conditions; safety and regulatory specs define compliance requirements.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the specification record. Controls whether the specification is active for design, manufacturing, and procurement use. Released specifications are the authoritative design baseline.']
  teamcenter_item_code varchar [note: 'Native item identifier from Siemens Teamcenter PLM system, serving as the source system reference key for the specification document. Enables bidirectional traceability between the Silver Layer and the PLM system of record.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the product specification document, summarizing the product or component and the nature of the specification (e.g., \'Servo Drive Unit – Functional Performance Specification\').']
  tolerance_lower decimal(18,6) [note: 'Minimum allowable deviation below the nominal value for the specification parameter. Used in SPC and inspection to define the lower specification limit (LSL). Null if not applicable.']
  tolerance_upper decimal(18,6) [note: 'Maximum allowable deviation above the nominal value for the specification parameter. Used in Statistical Process Control (SPC) and inspection to define the upper specification limit (USL). Null if not applicable (e.g., enumerated parameters).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Engineering unit associated with the nominal value and tolerances (e.g., \'V\' for volts, \'°C\' for degrees Celsius, \'MPa\' for megapascals, \'mm\' for millimeters, \'kg\' for kilograms). Follows SI unit conventions.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method by which compliance with this specification parameter is verified during design validation and production quality inspection (e.g., test, analysis, inspection, demonstration). Aligns with APQP and PPAP verification planning.']

  Note: 'Master record for formal product technical specifications defining the functional, performance, environmental, and regulatory requirements for a product or component. Captures specification number, revision, specification type (functional, interface, material, environmental), applicable standards (IEC 61131, CE, RoHS, REACH), parameter name, nominal value, tolerance, unit of measure, and approval status. SSOT for design requirements traceability.'
}

Table shared.material_specification {
  material_specification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a material specification record in the silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and references.']
  ecn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: material_specification.ecn_reference_number is a STRING reference to the ECN that authorized this material specification revision. Replacing with FK ecn_id → ecn.ecn_id enforces change management trac']
  hazardous_substance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.hazardous_substance. Business justification: Material specifications must reference controlled hazardous substances (RoHS, REACH) to enforce compliance during design. Engineering teams use this link to flag restricted materials before BOM approv']
  applicable_standard varchar [note: 'The primary industry or international standard governing the material specification, such as ASTM A36, DIN EN 10025, EN 573-3, or ISO 6892. Defines the normative requirements that the material must meet.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the engineering or quality authority who formally approved this material specification revision. Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance under ISO 9001 document control requirements.']
  approved_supplier_list_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this material specification to the corresponding Approved Supplier List (ASL) record in SAP Ariba or SAP S/4HANA MM. Ensures procurement is restricted to qualified and audited suppliers for this material.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the material specification was formally approved by the authorized engineering or quality representative. Provides a precise audit trail for document control and regulatory compliance.']
  base_material varchar [note: 'The primary elemental or compound material, such as \'Steel\', \'Aluminum\', \'Copper\', \'Polypropylene\', or \'Epoxy Resin\'. Provides the foundational material identity before alloy or grade qualification.']
  chemical_composition_spec varchar [note: 'Structured text or JSON-serialized representation of the required chemical composition limits (min/max percentages by weight) for key elements such as C, Mn, Si, Cr, Ni, Cu, Fe. Stored as a structured string for Silver layer compatibility.']
  conflict_minerals_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material contains conflict minerals (tin, tantalum, tungsten, gold — 3TG) sourced from conflict-affected and high-risk areas (CAHRAs). Triggers supply chain due diligence and reporting obligations under EU Conflict Minerals Regulation and SEC Rule 13p-1.']
  country_of_origin_restriction varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes from which sourcing of this material is restricted or prohibited, based on export control regulations, trade compliance, or supply chain risk policies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the material specification record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and change history tracking in the Silver layer.']
  density_kg_per_m3 decimal(12,4) [note: 'Nominal density of the material expressed in kilograms per cubic meter (kg/m³). Used in weight calculations for BOM costing, shipping logistics, and structural analysis in CAD/CAM systems.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the material specification including intended application, key characteristics, and any special notes relevant to procurement or manufacturing use.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this revision of the material specification is valid and approved for use in procurement and manufacturing. Supports time-bounded specification management and change effectivity control.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The date on which this revision of the material specification expires or is superseded. A null value indicates the specification is currently active with no defined expiry. Used to manage specification lifecycle and prevent use of obsolete standards.']
  elongation_min_percent decimal(6,2) [note: 'Minimum required elongation at break expressed as a percentage, indicating the material\'s ductility. Used in mechanical property verification during incoming inspection and First Article Inspection (FAI).']
  en10204_certificate_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the EN 10204 material test certificate type required for metallic materials: 2.1 (Declaration of Compliance), 2.2 (Test Report), 3.1 (Inspection Certificate by manufacturer), or 3.2 (Inspection Certificate by independent body). Critical for traceability in regulated industries.']
  grade_designation varchar [note: 'Specific grade or alloy designation of the material as defined by the applicable standard, such as \'6061-T6\', \'Grade 304\', \'S235JR\', or \'A36\'. Uniquely identifies the material within its standard family.']
  hardness_max decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable hardness value for the material. Paired with hardness_min to define the acceptable hardness range for incoming inspection and supplier qualification.']
  hardness_min decimal(8,2) [note: 'Minimum required hardness value for the material. The hardness scale (HRC, HRB, HV, HB) is specified in the hardness_scale attribute. Used in incoming inspection and quality assurance.']
  hardness_scale varchar [note: 'The hardness measurement scale used for the hardness_min and hardness_max values (e.g., Rockwell C, Vickers, Brinell). Ensures correct interpretation of hardness requirements during inspection.']
  hazardous_material_class varchar [note: 'UN/GHS hazardous material classification code for the material if applicable (e.g., Class 3 Flammable Liquid, Class 8 Corrosive). Drives storage, handling, and transportation requirements under OSHA HazCom and EPA regulations.']
  incoming_inspection_plan_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the quality inspection plan in SAP S/4HANA QM module that governs incoming material inspection for this specification. Defines sampling procedures, test methods, and acceptance criteria (AQL) applied at goods receipt.']
  is_reach_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material complies with the EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 regarding Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals. Flags whether the material contains Substances of Very High Concern (SVHC) above threshold.']
  is_rohs_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material meets the EU Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) Directive 2011/65/EU requirements, restricting the use of lead, mercury, cadmium, hexavalent chromium, PBB, PBDE, and other substances. Mandatory for products sold in the EU market.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the material specification record in the source system. Supports incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail requirements in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  material_group varchar [note: 'SAP material group code used to classify materials for procurement, reporting, and spend analytics. Aligns with the enterprise commodity taxonomy managed in SAP Ariba for sourcing and supplier management.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the material by physical or chemical category. Drives storage conditions, handling requirements, and applicable regulatory compliance checks (e.g., RoHS for electronic components, REACH for chemicals).']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive short name of the material specification, such as \'Low-Carbon Steel Sheet Grade A\' or \'Aluminum Alloy 6061-T6 Bar Stock\'. Used for display in procurement and engineering systems.']
  operating_temp_max_c decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum operating temperature in degrees Celsius at which the material maintains its specified mechanical and physical properties. Used in thermal analysis and material selection for electrification and industrial automation components.']
  operating_temp_min_c decimal(8,2) [note: 'Minimum operating temperature in degrees Celsius at which the material maintains its specified mechanical and physical properties. Critical for material selection in automation systems deployed in cold environments.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or site for which this material specification is applicable. Supports multi-plant operations where material standards may vary by production location or regional regulatory requirements.']
  revision varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric revision identifier indicating the current version of the material specification. Incremented upon each approved Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) affecting the specification.']
  sds_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number or document identifier for the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) associated with this material, as required by OSHA HazCom Standard (29 CFR 1910.1200) and EU REACH Regulation. Links to the document management system for retrieval.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this material specification record was sourced or originated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver layer lakehouse.']
  specification_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the material specification, typically assigned by the engineering or quality organization. Used for cross-referencing in procurement, incoming inspection, and supplier qualification documents.']
  standard_body varchar [note: 'The standards development organization (SDO) that published the applicable standard for this material specification. Used to filter and group specifications by regional or international standards alignment.']
  status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the material specification indicating whether it is actively approved for use in procurement and manufacturing, under engineering review, superseded by a newer revision, or withdrawn from use.']
  surface_roughness_max_um decimal(8,3) [note: 'Maximum allowable surface roughness expressed in micrometers (µm Ra) as required by the material specification. Relevant for sealing surfaces, bearing interfaces, and precision-machined components.']
  surface_treatment varchar [note: 'Required surface treatment or coating specification for the material, such as zinc plating, anodizing, passivation, powder coating, or hot-dip galvanizing. Defines the surface condition required for corrosion protection or functional performance.']
  svhc_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material contains one or more Substances of Very High Concern (SVHC) as listed on the ECHA Candidate List under REACH. Triggers mandatory disclosure obligations when concentration exceeds 0.1% w/w.']
  teamcenter_document_code varchar [note: 'Unique document identifier in Siemens Teamcenter PLM system for the master specification document (drawing, datasheet, or specification PDF). Enables direct retrieval of the authoritative specification document from the PLM/PDM system.']
  temper_condition varchar [note: 'Heat treatment, temper, or condition designation of the material (e.g., T6, T651, H32, annealed, normalized, quenched and tempered). Defines the mechanical property state resulting from processing.']
  tensile_strength_min_mpa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum required ultimate tensile strength of the material expressed in megapascals (MPa), as specified by the applicable standard. Used in incoming inspection and supplier qualification to verify mechanical property compliance.']
  test_certificate_requirement varchar [note: 'Specifies the type of material test certificate or documentation required from the supplier at delivery. Options include mill certificates (EN 10204 3.1/3.2), certificates of conformance, third-party test reports, or First Article Inspection (FAI) reports.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The base unit of measure in which the material is procured and inventoried (e.g., KG for kilogram, M for meter, PC for piece, SHT for sheet). Aligns with SAP S/4HANA MM unit of measure configuration.']
  yield_strength_min_mpa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum required yield strength (0.2% proof stress) of the material expressed in megapascals (MPa). Critical for structural and load-bearing component qualification in automation and electrification systems.']

  Note: 'Master record defining approved material standards and chemical composition requirements for raw materials and purchased components used in manufacturing. Captures material specification number, material type, applicable standard (ASTM, DIN, EN), chemical composition limits, mechanical property requirements, surface treatment, RoHS/REACH compliance status, and approved supplier list reference. Feeds procurement qualification and incoming inspection.'
}

Table shared.approved_manufacturer {
  approved_manufacturer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each Approved Manufacturer List (AML) / Approved Vendor List (AVL) entry in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  bom_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.bom_line. Business justification: AML/AVL entries are qualified for specific BOM component positions. Linking approved_manufacturer to bom_line identifies which component (by BOM position) the manufacturer is approved to supply. This ']
  classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.classification. Business justification: Approved manufacturers are classified by product classification (e.g., electronic components, mechanical fasteners, raw materials) to enforce sourcing rules and quality requirements specific to each c']
  ecn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: Changes to the Approved Manufacturer List (adding, removing, or modifying approved manufacturers) are governed by Engineering Change Notices in industrial manufacturing. Linking approved_manufacturer ']
  material_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.material_specification. Business justification: For raw material and commodity components, AML entries are qualified against a material specification (e.g., a steel grade or alloy spec). Linking approved_manufacturer to material_specification provi']
  aml_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the manufacturer\'s role in the supply strategy for this component. Preferred indicates primary source; alternate is secondary; restricted requires engineering approval for use.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the manufacturer was formally approved for the component following successful qualification testing and review.']
  approval_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the manufacturer approval expires and re-qualification is required. Supports periodic supplier re-evaluation per quality management requirements.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current qualification status of the manufacturer for the specified component. Drives procurement eligibility and supply chain decisions.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the engineering or quality authority who formally approved this manufacturer-component combination on the AML/AVL.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the formal approval was recorded in the system, providing an audit trail for quality management compliance.']
  ce_marking_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the manufacturer has confirmed CE marking compliance for the part, required for sale in the European Economic Area.']
  country_of_manufacture varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the component is manufactured. Used for trade compliance, tariff classification, and supply chain risk assessment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the approved manufacturer record was first created in the system. Provides full audit trail for quality management and data governance.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export Control Classification Number (ECCN) or equivalent classification for the manufacturer\'s part under applicable export control regulations (EAR/ITAR).']
  is_reach_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the manufacturer\'s part complies with EU REACH regulation regarding chemical substance registration and restriction.']
  is_rohs_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the manufacturer\'s part meets EU RoHS Directive requirements restricting hazardous substances in electrical and electronic equipment.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the approved manufacturer record. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and data lineage in the Silver layer.']
  last_requalification_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent periodic requalification assessment performed for this manufacturer-component combination. Supports ongoing supplier monitoring per ISO 9001.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard procurement lead time in calendar days from purchase order placement to delivery for this manufacturer\'s part. Used in MRP/MRP II planning calculations.']
  manufacturer_code varchar [note: 'Unique short code or identifier assigned to the manufacturer within the enterprise system (e.g., SAP vendor/manufacturer code). Enables cross-system linkage.']
  manufacturer_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the manufacturer\'s registered business location. Used for geopolitical risk assessment and trade compliance screening.']
  manufacturer_datasheet_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference number or URL pointing to the manufacturer\'s official datasheet or technical specification for the approved part.']
  manufacturer_duns_number varchar [note: 'Dun & Bradstreet Data Universal Numbering System (D-U-N-S) nine-digit identifier for the manufacturer. Used for global supplier identity verification and risk assessment.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Legal or trade name of the manufacturer approved to supply the component. Used for procurement qualification and supply chain risk management.']
  manufacturer_part_description varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s description of the part as stated in their datasheet or catalog. Supports incoming inspection and procurement verification.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'The manufacturer\'s own part number for the component. Critical for procurement, incoming inspection, and cross-referencing with supplier documentation.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered from this manufacturer per purchase order. Impacts procurement planning and inventory management.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional engineering or procurement notes regarding this manufacturer approval, such as special handling instructions, known limitations, or pending actions.']
  ppap_level varchar [note: 'PPAP submission level required or achieved for this manufacturer-component combination, indicating the depth of documentation provided during qualification.']
  ppap_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the PPAP submission for this manufacturer-component combination. Determines whether full production release is authorized.']
  ppm_defect_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured defect rate in Parts Per Million (PPM) for incoming parts from this manufacturer. Key quality KPI for supplier performance monitoring and AQL compliance.']
  price_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the unit price of the manufacturer\'s part (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency global procurement.']
  qualification_test_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the qualification test report, First Article Inspection (FAI) record, or PPAP package that substantiates the manufacturer approval.']
  quality_rating decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric quality performance score (0–100) assigned to this manufacturer for the component based on incoming inspection results, PPM defect rates, and audit outcomes.']
  requalification_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Interval in months at which this manufacturer-component approval must be periodically requalified and reviewed. Drives quality management scheduling.']
  restricted_to_plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code indicating that use of this approved manufacturer is restricted to a specific manufacturing plant. Null if approved globally across all plants.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this AML/AVL entry was sourced (e.g., SAP S/4HANA MM, SAP Ariba, Siemens Teamcenter PLM).']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Engineering\'s Approved Manufacturer List (AML) must reference the procurement supplier master. Sourcing and engineering teams use this daily to validate that a component\'s approved manufacturer exists']
  ul_certification_number varchar [note: 'UL certification file number issued by Underwriters Laboratories for the manufacturer\'s part, confirming product safety compliance for North American markets.']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Negotiated or catalog unit price for the manufacturer\'s part. Used for cost estimation, BOM costing, and procurement benchmarking.']
  use_restriction varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any restrictions on the use of this manufacturer\'s part, such as limited to specific plants, product lines, or applications. Null if no restrictions apply.']

  Note: 'Association record defining the Approved Manufacturer List (AML) and Approved Vendor List (AVL) entries for each component, specifying which manufacturers and their specific part numbers are approved for use in production. Captures component reference, manufacturer name, manufacturer part number, approval status, approval date, qualification test reference, and any use restrictions. Critical for procurement qualification and supply chain risk management.'
}

Table shared.product_variant {
  product_variant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each product variant record in the silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and references.']
  cad_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.cad_model. Business justification: Each product variant (e.g., a specific voltage/power rating configuration of an automation drive) has an associated master CAD model defining its physical geometry and design. Linking product_variant ']
  engineering_bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.bom. Business justification: product_variant.variant_bom_number is a STRING reference to the BOM that defines this product variant\'s structure. Replacing with FK bom_id → bom.bom_id establishes the authoritative variant-to-BOM tr']
  hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.hierarchy. Business justification: Engineering product variants must be positioned within the commercial product hierarchy (platform > family > variant) to ensure correct BOM structuring, cost rollups, and alignment with sales catalog ']
  plm_lifecycle_state_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.plm_lifecycle_state. Business justification: Product variants are PLM-managed objects that progress through formal lifecycle states (Concept, Development, Released, Obsolete). Linking product_variant to plm_lifecycle_state provides formal lifecy']
  product_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_configuration. Business justification: Product variants in engineering are directly derived from commercial product configurations. Engineering uses this link to ensure each variant\'s BOM and drawings align with the configuration options s']
  uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: Engineering variants require a canonical unit of measure for quantity specifications in BOMs and drawings. Referencing the shared UOM master prevents inconsistencies between engineering and commercial']
  ce_marking_status varchar [note: 'Current status of CE marking certification for this product variant, confirming conformity with applicable EU directives (e.g., Low Voltage Directive, Machinery Directive, EMC Directive). Required for sale in the European Economic Area.']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'Industrial communication protocol supported by this product variant for integration with PLCs, SCADA systems, and IIoT platforms. Determines compatibility with factory automation architectures.']
  configuration_code varchar [note: 'Encoded string representing the full combination of selected configuration options (e.g., voltage, power, protocol, enclosure) that define this variant. Used in SAP variant configuration (VC) and order management for ATO/ETO fulfillment.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the primary country of manufacture for this product variant. Used for customs declarations, trade compliance, and country-of-origin labeling requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this product variant record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA or Siemens Teamcenter PLM). Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed technical and commercial description of the product variant, including key differentiating characteristics such as configuration options, intended application, and performance highlights.']
  enclosure_class varchar [note: 'Ingress protection (IP) or NEMA enclosure classification of the product variant, indicating resistance to dust, water, and environmental hazards. Critical for application suitability and regulatory compliance.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export Control Classification Number (ECCN) or equivalent classification assigned to this product variant under US Export Administration Regulations (EAR) or EU dual-use regulations. Controls export licensing requirements.']
  fulfillment_model varchar [note: 'Manufacturing and fulfillment strategy applicable to this variant: Make to Stock (MTS), Make to Order (MTO), Assemble to Order (ATO), or Engineer to Order (ETO). Drives production planning, lead time commitments, and ATP/CTP logic in SAP S/4HANA.']
  harmonized_tariff_code varchar [note: 'Harmonized System (HS) tariff code assigned to this product variant for customs classification in international trade. Used for import/export declarations, duty calculation, and trade compliance reporting.']
  is_reach_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product variant complies with EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 regarding chemical substance registration and restriction. Required for EU market access and supply chain transparency.']
  is_rohs_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product variant complies with the EU RoHS Directive (2011/65/EU) restricting the use of hazardous substances in electrical and electronic equipment. Mandatory for EU market access.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code for the language in which the variant name and description are maintained (e.g., en, de, zh-CN). Supports multi-language product catalog management for global operations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this product variant record in the source system. Used for change detection, incremental data loading, and audit compliance.']
  list_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Published list price per unit for this product variant as maintained in SAP S/4HANA SD pricing conditions. Serves as the baseline for customer quotations, pricing agreements, and revenue reporting.']
  list_price_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the list price is expressed. Supports global pricing management across multiple sales regions.']
  market_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of target markets or regions where this product variant is approved for sale and use (e.g., EU, North America, Asia-Pacific). Drives regional catalog availability and regulatory compliance checks.']
  name varchar [note: 'Short commercial name of the product variant used in catalogs, sales orders, and customer-facing materials. Describes the variant in human-readable form.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA plant code identifying the primary manufacturing plant responsible for producing this product variant. Drives production planning, MRP, and inventory management at the plant level.']
  power_rating_kw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Nominal power rating of the product variant expressed in kilowatts (kW). Core technical specification used for product selection, energy compliance reporting, and ISO 50001 energy management.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'Engineering and commercial classification of the product variant within the product hierarchy (e.g., frequency inverter, circuit breaker, PLC module, electrification panel). Aligns with SAP material group.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'Commercial product line or product family to which this variant belongs (e.g., automation drives, low-voltage switchgear, smart building controllers). Used for portfolio management and sales reporting.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date on which this product variant was formally released from engineering into production and commercial availability, following completion of APQP/PPAP processes and ECO approval.']
  responsible_engineer varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the lead engineer responsible for the design and lifecycle management of this product variant in Siemens Teamcenter PLM. Used for accountability and change management workflows.']
  revision_level varchar [note: 'Current engineering revision level of the product variant as maintained in Siemens Teamcenter PLM. Incremented upon approved Engineering Change Orders (ECOs). Critical for BOM and drawing traceability.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this product variant record originates (e.g., SAP_S4HANA, TEAMCENTER_PLM). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and master data governance.']
  standard_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard manufacturing cost per unit for this product variant as calculated in SAP S/4HANA CO (Controlling) module. Used for inventory valuation, COGS reporting, and profitability analysis. Expressed in the standard cost currency.']
  standard_cost_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the standard cost is expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Supports multi-currency operations across global manufacturing sites.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the product variant as managed in PLM and ERP. Controls whether the variant can be ordered, produced, or procured. Aligns with SAP material status and Teamcenter lifecycle state.']
  target_industry_segment varchar [note: 'Primary industry segment for which this product variant is designed and marketed (e.g., factory automation, building automation, transportation). Supports portfolio segmentation and go-to-market strategy.']
  ul_certification_number varchar [note: 'UL certification file number assigned to this product variant by Underwriters Laboratories, confirming compliance with North American safety standards. Required for sale in the US and Canadian markets.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this product variant is commercially valid and available for ordering, production, and sale. Used for effectivity management in SAP S/4HANA and Teamcenter PLM.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this product variant remains commercially valid. After this date, the variant is no longer available for new orders. Supports phase-out planning and end-of-life management.']
  variant_code varchar [note: 'Business-facing alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the product variant within the product catalog. Used in order management, BOM references, and customer-facing documentation. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM module material number for configurable variants.']
  variant_number varchar [note: 'Internal engineering variant number as maintained in Siemens Teamcenter PLM, used to cross-reference the variant across PLM and ERP systems. May differ from the commercial variant code.']
  variant_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the variant by its engineering origin: standard (catalog item), custom (customer-specific modification), engineered (Engineer to Order), configurable (Assemble to Order with option selection), or phantom (planning-only variant).']
  voltage_rating varchar [note: 'Nominal voltage rating of the product variant (e.g., 24V DC, 400V AC, 690V AC). Key configuration option differentiating variants within the same base product for automation and electrification solutions.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Gross weight of the product variant in kilograms, including packaging. Used for logistics planning, freight cost calculation, and shipping documentation.']

  Note: 'Master record defining configurable product variants and option combinations for automation systems and electrification solutions offered in multiple configurations. Captures variant code, variant description, base product reference, configuration options (voltage, power rating, communication protocol, enclosure class), variant-specific BOM reference, and market applicability. Supports ATO (Assemble to Order) and ETO (Engineer to Order) fulfillment models.'
}

Replacing with FK engineering_drawing_id → engineering_drawing.engineering_drawing_id establishe']

  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_location. Business justification: Tooling equipment must track its physical storage location for inventory management and deployment planning. Manufacturing engineers use this daily to locate dies, molds, and fixtures when setting up ']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plant. Business justification: Tooling and equipment are assigned to specific manufacturing plants where they are used to produce products. Engineering references the product-plant assignment to ensure tooling designs are scoped to']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Engineering-defined tooling and equipment is sourced from specific suppliers. Linking to the procurement supplier master enables maintenance teams to raise POs for tooling repairs, spare parts, and ca']
  acquisition_cost_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the acquisition cost amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Supports multi-currency reporting for multinational operations.']
  calibration_interval_days varchar [note: 'Frequency of required calibration expressed in calendar days. Applicable only when calibration_required is true. Drives the calibration schedule in the QMS.']
  calibration_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tooling or equipment item requires periodic calibration per quality management system requirements. Drives calibration scheduling and compliance tracking.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date on which the tooling was formally accepted, qualified, and placed into active production service. Marks the start of tool life cycle tracking.']
  country_of_use varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where this tooling is deployed. Supports multinational compliance, export control, and regional regulatory reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tooling equipment master record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  cycles_used varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of production cycles, shots, or strokes consumed against the rated tool life. Used to calculate remaining tool life and trigger preventive maintenance.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed technical description of the tooling or equipment, including its function, design intent, and applicable manufacturing process.']
  design_revision varchar [note: 'Current engineering design revision level of the tooling item, aligned with the PLM/PDM revision control system. Changes are tracked via ECN/ECO workflows.']

  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent preventive or corrective maintenance was completed on this tooling item.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the tooling equipment master record. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and audit compliance.']
  maintenance_interval_days varchar [note: 'Scheduled preventive maintenance interval in calendar days for this tooling item. Drives work order generation in Maximo EAM and SAP PM.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date on which the tooling item was manufactured or first commissioned. Used to calculate tool age, depreciation, and remaining useful life.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the tooling or equipment item (e.g., \'Progressive Die Stamping Tool\', \'CMM Fixture Assembly\', \'Injection Mold - Housing Cover\').']
  next_calibration_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date by which the next calibration must be completed to maintain compliance with quality management system requirements.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the tooling is owned by the manufacturing company, a customer (customer-supplied tooling), a supplier, or leased. Drives asset accounting treatment and liability.']
  ppap_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the tooling under the Production Part Approval Process (PPAP). Required for automotive and industrial customers before production release.']
  responsible_engineer varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the engineering owner responsible for the design, qualification, and lifecycle management of this tooling item.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date on which the tooling was formally retired or scrapped. Populated when status transitions to \'retired\' or \'scrapped\'. Used for asset lifecycle reporting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this tooling master record originates (e.g., SAP PM, Siemens Teamcenter, Maximo EAM).']
  source_system_key varchar [note: 'The native primary key or equipment number from the originating source system (e.g., SAP PM Equipment Number, Teamcenter Item ID). Enables traceability and reconciliation.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the tooling or equipment. Drives availability for production scheduling and triggers maintenance or procurement workflows.']
  supplier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external vendor or toolmaker who manufactured or supplied this tooling item. Used for supplier performance tracking and warranty claims.']
  supplier_tool_number varchar [note: 'The toolmaker\'s or supplier\'s own reference number for this tooling item. Required for warranty claims, spare part ordering, and supplier communications.']
  tool_category varchar [note: 'Broader classification grouping tools by their lifecycle stage and investment level. Hard tooling is production-grade; soft tooling is used for prototyping or low-volume runs.']
  tool_life_cycles varchar [note: 'Maximum rated tool life expressed in number of production cycles, shots, or strokes before the tool requires refurbishment or replacement. Used for predictive maintenance scheduling.']
  tool_material varchar [note: 'Primary material used in the construction of the tooling (e.g., \'H13 Tool Steel\', \'P20 Steel\', \'Aluminum 7075\', \'Carbide\', \'Hardened Steel\'). Influences tool life and maintenance requirements.']
  tool_number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned alphanumeric identifier for the tooling or equipment item, used across engineering, production, and maintenance systems. Corresponds to the tool number in SAP PM and Siemens Teamcenter.']
  tool_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tooling or equipment by its functional category. Drives maintenance schedules, calibration requirements, and tool life tracking policies.']
  tool_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Physical weight of the tooling assembly in kilograms. Used for logistics planning, crane/lift requirements, and storage rack load calculations.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the production work center or machine group where this tooling is primarily deployed. Aligns with SAP PP work center and Siemens Opcenter MES resource definitions.']

  Note: 'Master record for specialized manufacturing tooling, jigs, fixtures, molds, and test equipment designed or managed by the engineering function. Captures tool number, tool type, associated process operation, design revision, tool material, tool life (shots or cycles), current status (active, under repair, retired), storage location, and calibration requirement flag. Distinct from production asset records owned by the asset domain.'
}

Table shared.line_item {
  line_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each order line item record in the silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for the order_line_item data product.']
  asset_bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_bom. Business justification: When ordering spare parts or components for industrial assets, each order line item references the asset BOM to ensure the correct part configuration is ordered. Procurement and service parts teams us']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.channel. Business justification: Each sales order line item is received through a specific sales channel (direct, distributor, EDI, portal, etc.). Linking line_item to channel enables channel-level reporting, SLA enforcement, commiss']
  priority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.priority. Business justification: Individual sales order line items carry fulfillment priority levels that drive production scheduling, warehouse pick priority, and expedited freight decisions. Linking line_item to the priority refere']
  product_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_configuration. Business justification: Each order line item references a specific product configuration (variant/options selected). Order management uses this daily to confirm what exact configured product was ordered and to drive producti']
  product_variant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.product_variant. Business justification: Each sales order line item is for a specific product variant. Order management, production planning, and shipping all need the engineering variant record to pull BOM, drawing, and specification data f']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Each order line item designates a specific storage location for goods issue or picking. Warehouse operations use this daily to route pick tasks and confirm stock availability at the correct physical l']
  tax_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.tax_code. Business justification: Every order line item carries a tax code determining applicable VAT/sales tax. Finance and order management teams use this link to ensure correct tax calculation on each billable line.']
  uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: Order line items must reference the unit of measure for quantity and pricing. Sales and warehouse teams use this to ensure correct quantity interpretation (pieces, kg, meters) per ordered product.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the goods were delivered and goods issue was posted for this line item. Used for on-time delivery (OTD) performance measurement and customer SLA compliance reporting.']
  atp_ctp_confirmed boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the line item quantity and delivery date have been confirmed through an Available to Promise (ATP) or Capable to Promise (CTP) check in SAP S/4HANA. True indicates a firm commitment has been made.']
  billing_block varchar [note: 'Code indicating a billing block applied to this line item, preventing invoice creation until the block is released (e.g., pending approval, dispute resolution). Corresponds to FAKSP in SAP VBAP.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'The delivery date confirmed by the system following ATP (Available to Promise) or CTP (Capable to Promise) check, representing the committed delivery date to the customer. May differ from requested delivery date.']
