  storage_conditions varchar [note: 'Two-digit code defining the required storage conditions for this material at the plant (e.g., temperature-controlled, humidity-controlled, hazardous material storage). Corresponds to the LGBKZ field in SAP MM Plant Data/Storage view.']
  temperature_conditions_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicator specifying the temperature range or climate class required for storing this material at the plant. Critical for electrification components and chemical materials. Corresponds to the MHDRZ field in SAP MM Plant Data/Storage view.']
  valuation_class varchar [note: 'Four-digit code that determines the general ledger accounts updated when inventory movements occur for this material at the plant. Links material master to financial accounting. Corresponds to the BKLAS field in SAP MM Accounting view.']

  Note: 'Defines the manufacturing and storage plant-level attributes for each catalog item — capturing the producing plant, MRP type, MRP controller, lot sizing procedure, safety stock level, reorder point, planned delivery time, in-house production time, and storage conditions. This is the SAP MM plant-level material master view, governing how each product is planned, produced, and stored at each manufacturing facility. Essential for MRP/MRP II planning and production scheduling.'
}

Table shared.uom {
  uom_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each unit of measure assignment record for a catalog item. Serves as the primary key for the product_uom entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  code varchar [note: 'The standardized unit of measure code as defined by ISO 80000 and aligned with SAP S/4HANA internal UoM codes (e.g., EA, KG, L, M, PC, ST, PAL, CTN). Identifies the specific unit used for this UoM assignment.']
  conversion_denominator decimal(18,6) [note: 'The denominator of the conversion factor from this UoM to the base UoM. Together with the numerator, defines the ratio: (numerator / denominator) units of this UoM = 1 base UoM unit. Aligned with SAP S/4HANA MARM-UMREN field.']
  conversion_factor decimal(18,6) [note: 'The pre-computed decimal conversion factor (numerator / denominator) representing how many base UoM units are equivalent to one unit of this UoM. Stored for query performance and reporting convenience. Example: 1 PAL = 48 EA → conversion_factor = 48.000000.']
  conversion_numerator decimal(18,6) [note: 'The numerator of the conversion factor from this UoM to the base UoM. Together with the denominator, defines the ratio: (numerator / denominator) units of this UoM = 1 base UoM unit. Aligned with SAP S/4HANA MARM-UMREZ field.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this UoM assignment record was first created in the source system or lakehouse. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and change history reporting.']
  description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the unit of measure (e.g., \'Each\', \'Kilogram\', \'Liter\', \'Pallet\', \'Carton\'). Supports display in user interfaces, reports, and order documents.']
  ean_upc_code varchar [note: 'The EAN or UPC barcode associated with this UoM packaging level. Used for point-of-sale scanning, warehouse receiving, and inventory tracking. Aligned with SAP S/4HANA MARM-EAN11 and GS1 standards.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this UoM assignment and its conversion factors are valid and applicable for transactions. Supports time-bound UoM configurations and change management.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which this UoM assignment is no longer valid for use in transactions. Supports planned UoM transitions and packaging changes without disrupting historical records.']
  global_trade_item_number varchar [note: 'The GS1-standard Global Trade Item Number (GTIN) associated with this specific UoM packaging level (e.g., GTIN-8 for inner pack, GTIN-13 for case, GTIN-14 for pallet). Enables barcode scanning, EDI transactions, and retail/logistics interoperability.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(18,4) [note: 'The gross weight of one unit in this UoM, expressed in kilograms. Used for freight calculation, shipping documentation, and hazardous material compliance. Aligned with SAP S/4HANA MARM-BRGEW.']
  height_mm decimal(18,4) [note: 'The height dimension of one unit in this UoM, expressed in millimeters. Used for packaging design, storage slot assignment, and transportation planning.']
  is_base_uom boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this UoM is the base unit of measure for the catalog item. The base UoM is the reference unit to which all other UoM conversions are anchored. Only one UoM per SKU may be the base UoM.']
  is_delivery_uom boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this UoM is the delivery unit of measure used in outbound deliveries and shipping documents. Supports logistics and warehouse management processes.']
  is_order_uom boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this UoM is the order unit of measure used in purchase orders and procurement transactions. Corresponds to the SAP S/4HANA purchase order unit (BESTELLEINHEIT).']
  is_production_uom boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this UoM is used in production orders and manufacturing execution. Supports Bill of Materials (BOM) and production planning processes in SAP PP and Siemens Opcenter MES.']
  is_sales_uom boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this UoM is the sales unit of measure used in customer orders, quotations, and invoices. Corresponds to the SAP S/4HANA sales unit (VRKME).']
  is_storage_uom boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this UoM is the storage unit of measure used in warehouse and inventory management. Determines how stock is tracked and managed in the warehouse management system.']
  iso_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 80000-compliant international unit of measure code (e.g., \'KGM\' for kilogram, \'LTR\' for litre, \'MTR\' for metre, \'PCE\' for piece). Used for EDI, cross-system interoperability, and regulatory reporting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this UoM assignment record was most recently updated in the source system or lakehouse. Supports incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance.']
  length_mm decimal(18,4) [note: 'The length dimension of one unit in this UoM, expressed in millimeters. Used for packaging design, storage slot assignment, and transportation planning.']
  maximum_stack_height varchar [note: 'The maximum number of units of this UoM that can be stacked on top of each other during storage or transportation. Used for warehouse slot planning and load safety compliance.']
  min_order_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum quantity in this UoM that can be ordered in a single purchase or sales transaction. Enforced during order entry to ensure supplier and production efficiency requirements are met.']
  net_weight_kg decimal(18,4) [note: 'The net weight of one unit in this UoM, expressed in kilograms, excluding packaging. Used for product content weight declarations, customs documentation, and regulatory compliance.']
  packaging_type varchar [note: 'Describes the physical packaging form associated with this UoM (e.g., each, inner pack, case, carton, pallet). Supports warehouse operations, shipping documentation, and customer order fulfillment.']
  quantity_per_package decimal(18,4) [note: 'The number of base units contained within one unit of this UoM packaging level (e.g., 12 EA per case, 48 cases per pallet). Supports packaging hierarchy navigation and order quantity validation.']
  rounding_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The rounding quantity in this UoM to which order quantities are rounded up during Material Requirements Planning (MRP) and procurement. Ensures order quantities align with packaging multiples.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this UoM assignment record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit traceability in the lakehouse Silver Layer.']
  source_system_key varchar [note: 'The natural key or composite key from the originating source system that uniquely identifies this UoM assignment record (e.g., SAP MARM key: MATNR + MEINH). Enables reconciliation between the lakehouse and source systems.']
  status varchar [note: 'The current operational status of this UoM assignment for the catalog item. Controls whether the UoM is available for use in transactions. \'active\' = in use; \'inactive\' = temporarily disabled; \'obsolete\' = retired; \'pending_review\' = under change review.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classifies the business role of this UoM assignment for the catalog item. Determines how the UoM is used across procurement, production, sales, delivery, and storage processes. Aligned with SAP S/4HANA UoM usage categories.']
  variable_order_unit_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order unit of measure can be changed at the time of order entry, allowing flexibility in procurement and sales transactions. Aligned with SAP S/4HANA variable order unit configuration.']
  volume_m3 decimal(18,6) [note: 'The volume of one unit in this UoM, expressed in cubic meters. Used for warehouse space planning, container loading optimization, and freight volume calculations. Aligned with SAP S/4HANA MARM-VOLUM.']
  width_mm decimal(18,4) [note: 'The width dimension of one unit in this UoM, expressed in millimeters. Used for packaging design, storage slot assignment, and transportation planning.']

  Note: 'Defines all valid units of measure (UoM) for a catalog item and the conversion factors between them — base unit, order unit, sales unit, delivery unit, and storage unit. Captures the UoM code (ISO 80000), conversion numerator and denominator, and whether the UoM is the base, order, or sales unit. Supports multi-UoM transactions across procurement, production, sales, and logistics. Aligned with SAP S/4HANA UoM conversion configuration.'
}

Table shared.warranty_policy {
  warranty_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a warranty policy record in the product catalog.']
  applicable_market_regions varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or named regional market groupings (e.g., \'USA|CAN|MEX\' or \'GLOBAL\') identifying the geographic markets where this warranty policy is valid and enforceable.']
  applicable_product_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the scope of catalog items to which this warranty policy applies: all products, a specific product family, product category, individual SKU, or a named product line such as automation systems or electrification solutions.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the authorized person who approved this warranty policy version for publication and use. Required for ISO 9001 documented information control.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this warranty policy version was formally approved by the authorized approver. Provides audit trail for ISO 9001 documented information control compliance.']
  claim_limit_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the max_claim_amount is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global markets.']
  coverage_scope varchar [note: 'Defines what is covered under the warranty: parts only (materials cost), parts and labor (materials plus technician time), full replacement (complete unit swap), on-site service (field technician dispatch), depot repair (return-to-depot), software only, or consumables excluded.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this warranty policy record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with ISO 9001 documented information control requirements.']
  description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the warranty policy terms, conditions, and coverage scope as communicated to customers and service teams.']
  dispute_resolution_mechanism varchar [note: 'Specifies the contractual mechanism for resolving warranty disputes between the manufacturer and customer: arbitration, mediation, litigation, expert determination, or ombudsman. Relevant for legal and compliance teams.']
  duration_months varchar [note: 'Total duration of the warranty coverage expressed in months from the warranty start date. Standard industrial automation warranties typically range from 12 to 60 months.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this warranty policy version becomes effective and applicable to new product sales or registrations. Used to determine which policy version applies to a given transaction date.']
  exclusion_conditions varchar [note: 'Narrative description of conditions, events, or damage types that void or exclude warranty coverage (e.g., misuse, unauthorized modifications, improper installation, use outside rated environmental conditions, consumable wear parts, damage from external causes).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which this warranty policy version is no longer applicable to new sales. Policies with no expiry date remain active until explicitly superseded or retired.']
  governing_law_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of this warranty policy. Critical for multinational legal compliance and dispute resolution.']
  labor_coverage_months varchar [note: 'Number of months during which labor costs are covered under the warranty. May differ from total duration_months when labor coverage is shorter than parts coverage (e.g., 12 months labor within a 24-month parts warranty).']
  language_code varchar [note: 'IETF BCP 47 language tag identifying the language in which this warranty policy record is authored (e.g., \'en\', \'de\', \'fr\', \'zh-CN\'). Supports multi-language policy management for global markets.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this warranty policy record was most recently updated in the data platform. Supports change tracking, incremental data loads, and audit compliance.']
  max_claim_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary value of claims that can be processed under this warranty policy over its lifetime. Caps the manufacturer\'s financial liability exposure per policy instance.']
  name varchar [note: 'Short descriptive name of the warranty policy (e.g., \'Standard 2-Year Parts and Labor\', \'Extended 5-Year Performance Guarantee\') used in customer-facing documentation and service portals.']
  on_site_service_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warranty policy includes on-site field service dispatch at no additional charge to the customer. Drives field service scheduling and cost allocation in Salesforce Field Service and Maximo EAM.']
  parts_coverage_months varchar [note: 'Number of months during which replacement parts are covered under the warranty. May differ from labor_coverage_months in tiered warranty structures.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Business-facing alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the warranty policy, used for cross-system reference in SAP SD, Salesforce Service Cloud, and customer-facing documentation.']
  preventive_maintenance_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether scheduled preventive maintenance visits are included within the warranty coverage at no additional cost. Relevant for Total Productive Maintenance (TPM) programs and Maximo EAM maintenance planning.']
  pro_rata_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether warranty claims are settled on a pro-rata basis (i.e., the reimbursement value decreases proportionally over the warranty period). Common in battery and consumable component warranties.']
  registration_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer must register the product to activate warranty coverage. When true, warranty claims may be rejected if registration was not completed within the required registration window.']
  registration_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from the warranty start basis event within which the customer must register the product to activate warranty coverage. Applicable only when registration_required is true.']
  regulatory_compliance_flags varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of regulatory frameworks or certifications that this warranty policy is designed to satisfy (e.g., \'CE|RoHS|REACH|UL|ISO9001\'). Supports compliance reporting and customer documentation requirements.']
  renewable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this warranty policy can be renewed or extended at the end of the coverage period, typically through a service contract or extended warranty purchase.']
  resolution_time_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum number of hours within which the manufacturer commits to resolve or provide a remedy for a warranty claim. Distinct from response_time_hours which covers acknowledgment only.']
  response_time_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum number of hours within which the manufacturer commits to respond to a warranty claim or service request. Defines the Service Level Agreement (SLA) response commitment embedded in the warranty policy.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this warranty policy record originates (e.g., SAP SD warranty condition, Salesforce Service Cloud warranty object, Siemens Teamcenter PLM). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system_key varchar [note: 'Natural key or identifier of this warranty policy record in the originating source system (e.g., SAP warranty condition number, Salesforce warranty record ID). Enables cross-system traceability and reconciliation.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the warranty policy: draft (being authored), active (in use for new sales), superseded (replaced by a newer policy version), retired (no longer applicable), or under_review (pending approval for changes).']
  transferable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warranty coverage can be transferred to a subsequent owner of the product (e.g., upon resale of industrial equipment). Relevant for asset-intensive industries where equipment changes ownership.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the warranty policy document following semantic versioning (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.1\', \'3.0.1\'). Enables tracking of policy revisions and ensures correct version is applied to warranty claims.']
  warranty_start_basis varchar [note: 'Defines the event that triggers the start of the warranty period: ship_date (date product leaves factory), delivery_date (date received by customer), installation_date (date physically installed), commissioning_date (date system is operational), invoice_date, first_use_date, or registration_date.']
  warranty_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the warranty policy type: standard (default manufacturer warranty), extended (purchased additional coverage), performance_guarantee (output/uptime commitment), limited (restricted scope), statutory (legally mandated), or supplier_passthrough (warranty passed from component supplier).']

  Note: 'Defines the standard warranty terms and conditions applicable to catalog items and product families — capturing warranty type (standard, extended, performance guarantee), warranty duration (months), coverage scope (parts only, parts and labor, full replacement), exclusion conditions, warranty registration requirement flag, and applicable geographic markets. Serves as the reference policy for warranty claims processed in the service domain and for customer-facing warranty documentation.'
}

Table shared.sales_territory {
  sales_territory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the sales territory record within the enterprise data platform.']
  sales_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sales_org. Business justification: Sales territories are defined within and owned by a sales organization. Territory management and alignment processes in manufacturing always operate within the sales org hierarchy — a territory cannot']
  account_count_target varchar [note: 'Target number of active customer accounts to be managed within this territory during the quota period, used for territory sizing, workload balancing, and coverage planning.']
  annual_revenue_quota decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total annual revenue target assigned to this territory in the territory\'s local currency, representing the committed sales performance expectation for the fiscal year.']
  code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric business code uniquely identifying the territory, used for cross-system referencing and reporting (e.g., SAP SD sales area code, Salesforce territory code).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the primary country of the territory (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN). For multi-country territories, represents the primary country of operation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the territory record was first created in the source CRM system or the enterprise data platform, used for audit trail and data lineage purposes.']
  crm_territory_code varchar [note: 'Native territory identifier from the source CRM system (Salesforce CRM), used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing between the lakehouse and the operational system of record.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for all quota, revenue, and financial reporting within this territory (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the scope, coverage, and strategic intent of the territory, including key industries, geographies, or named accounts covered.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this territory definition, assignment, or quota ceases to be effective. A null value indicates the territory is currently active with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this territory definition, assignment, and quota become operationally effective. Supports bi-temporal modeling and territory realignment tracking.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Position of this territory within the sales organization hierarchy: region (top-level, e.g., EMEA), district (mid-level), area (sub-district), or zone (lowest operational unit).']
  industry_vertical varchar [note: 'Primary industry vertical served by this territory, aligning with the company\'s industrial automation and electrification solution portfolio segments.']
  is_overlay boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this territory is an overlay (specialist) territory that operates across other territories (e.g., a vertical industry specialist or solution overlay team) rather than a primary geographic or named-account territory.']
  is_strategic boolean [note: 'Flags this territory as a strategic priority territory, typically associated with key accounts, high-growth markets, or flagship industrial automation projects requiring executive attention and dedicated resources.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) representing the primary business language used in this territory for customer communications, contracts, and documentation (e.g., en-US, de-DE, zh-CN).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the territory record in the source system, used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Salesforce Territory Management model or equivalent CRM territory planning model to which this territory belongs, enabling multi-model territory management for different fiscal years or sales strategies.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive business name of the sales territory (e.g., \'Northeast Industrial Automation\', \'DACH Electrification Solutions\').']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, special instructions, or strategic commentary about the territory, such as competitive landscape notes, key account relationships, or realignment rationale.']
  pipeline_target decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target value of the sales pipeline (open opportunities) that should be maintained in this territory at any given time, expressed in local currency. Typically a multiple of the annual quota.']
  postal_code_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending postal/ZIP code defining the upper boundary of the geographic coverage range for this territory, used in conjunction with postal_code_range_start.']
  postal_code_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting postal/ZIP code defining the lower boundary of the geographic coverage range for this territory, used for precise geographic territory delineation.']
  primary_product_line varchar [note: 'The primary product line or solution portfolio that this territory is responsible for selling, aligned with the company\'s industrial automation and electrification product categories.']
  quota_attainment_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage of annual revenue quota that must be achieved to meet the territory\'s performance threshold, used in commission and incentive plan calculations.']
  quota_fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four-digit fiscal year to which the annual revenue quota applies, enabling year-over-year quota tracking and performance comparison.']
  region_name varchar [note: 'Named geographic macro-region to which this territory belongs (e.g., North America, EMEA, Asia Pacific), used for global sales reporting and executive dashboards.']
  sales_model varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the territory is served through direct field sales, channel/partner sales, or a hybrid of both motions for industrial automation and electrification solutions.']
  sap_distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Two-character SAP S/4HANA distribution channel code (e.g., direct sales, dealer, OEM) associated with this territory\'s primary go-to-market route.']
  sap_division varchar [note: 'Two-character SAP S/4HANA division code representing the product line or business unit (e.g., automation systems, electrification) primarily sold within this territory.']
  sap_sales_org varchar [note: 'Four-character SAP S/4HANA Sales Organization code associated with this territory, enabling alignment between CRM territory structures and ERP sales area configurations for order management and revenue recognition.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative subdivision covered by this territory, applicable for geographic-type territories. Uses ISO 3166-2 subdivision codes where applicable.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the territory indicating whether it is actively assigned, pending activation, inactive, or archived for historical reference.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the primary operating time zone of this territory (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/Berlin), used for scheduling field sales activities and SLA calculations.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the territory by its organizational basis: geographic (defined by physical boundaries), vertical (defined by industry sector such as automotive or energy), named_account (defined by specific strategic accounts), or hybrid (combination of criteria).']

  Note: 'Defines the geographic, industry-vertical, or named-account sales territory assignments used to organize the field sales force for industrial automation and electrification solutions. Captures territory hierarchy (region, district, area), assigned revenue quota, territory type (geographic/vertical/named), and effective date ranges.'
}

Table shared.sales_price_list {
  sales_price_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the price list record. Serves as the primary key for the price_list entity in the sales domain lakehouse silver layer.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_contract. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing, sales price lists for configured products are anchored to procurement contract costs. Pricing teams reference active procurement contracts to set floor prices and ensure m']
  currency_exchange_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.currency_exchange_rate. Business justification: Industrial manufacturers sell globally; price lists must reference a validated exchange rate from finance to ensure consistent multi-currency pricing. Finance controls rate governance to prevent reven']
  pricing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.pricing_agreement. Business justification: Sales price lists for key accounts in manufacturing are derived from or constrained by negotiated pricing agreements. Linking them ensures the price list respects contractual commitments and avoids co']
  product_price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_price_list. Business justification: Sales price lists are derived from or aligned to product price lists. Sales teams reference the master product price list when creating customer-facing pricing. Pricing operations use this link daily ']
  product_variant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.product_variant. Business justification: Sales price lists in industrial manufacturing are built per product variant (e.g., motor size, voltage rating). Pricing teams reference the exact engineering-defined variant to ensure quotes reflect t']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the individual who formally approved this price list. Supports governance, audit, and compliance requirements for pricing authorization.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which the price list was formally approved by the authorized pricing authority (e.g., VP of Sales, Regional Pricing Manager). Required for SOX compliance and internal audit trails.']
  base_price_type varchar [note: 'Defines the pricing methodology used to establish base prices on this list. List price is the standard published price; transfer price applies to intercompany transactions; cost-plus adds a margin to COGS; market-based aligns to competitive benchmarks; negotiated reflects customer-specific agreements.']
  code varchar [note: 'Business-facing alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the price list, used for cross-system reference and integration with SAP S/4HANA SD condition records and Salesforce CRM pricing agreements.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the primary country for which this price list is applicable. Supports country-specific pricing strategies for multinational industrial manufacturing operations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this price list record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all prices on this list are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, CNY). Supports multinational pricing operations across all regions.']
  customer_classification varchar [note: 'Defines the customer segment or classification for which this price list is intended. Enables differentiated pricing strategies for key accounts, strategic partners, distributors, OEM customers, government entities, and intercompany transactions.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the price list scope, intended audience, applicable product lines, and any special conditions or notes for sales and commercial teams.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'The sales distribution channel for which this price list is applicable. Differentiates pricing for direct sales, distributor/channel partner sales, OEM partnerships, EPC contractor projects, online commerce, intercompany transfers, and aftermarket service.']
  division varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA division code representing the product line or business unit (e.g., Industrial Automation, Electrification Solutions, Smart Infrastructure, Aftermarket Parts) to which this price list applies.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) applicable to this price list, defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between seller and buyer for industrial equipment shipments.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price list is the default list applied when no other specific price list is determined during sales order processing. Only one price list per sales organization and distribution channel combination should be flagged as default.']
  is_net_price boolean [note: 'Indicates whether prices on this list represent net prices (after all standard discounts have been applied) or gross list prices (before discounts). Critical for correct pricing calculation in SAP S/4HANA condition records.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code for the primary language in which this price list is published (e.g., EN, DE, FR, ZH, JA). Supports multilingual price list management for global operations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this price list record was most recently updated. Supports change tracking, incremental data loading in the lakehouse, and audit compliance.']
  max_discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum allowable discount percentage that sales representatives are authorized to apply on top of this price list without requiring additional approval. Enforces discount governance and margin protection policies.']
  min_order_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum order value (in the price list currency) required for this price list to be applicable. Enforces commercial thresholds for industrial automation and electrification solution orders.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the price list, such as \'North America Industrial Automation Standard 2025\' or \'EMEA Electrification Solutions Channel Partner List\'. Used for display and selection in CRM and ERP systems.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal review of this price list by the pricing governance team. Triggers workflow notifications to pricing managers and commercial directors.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional commercial notes, special conditions, or contextual information about this price list. Used by pricing managers and sales operations to document exceptions, market context, or approval rationale.']
  price_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure basis on which prices are expressed in this list (e.g., each unit, per 100 units, per kilogram, per meter). Aligns with SAP S/4HANA condition pricing unit (KPEIN) for accurate order pricing.']
  pricing_procedure varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA pricing procedure code that governs how condition types (base price, discounts, surcharges, taxes) are calculated and sequenced when this price list is applied during order processing.']
  product_hierarchy varchar [note: 'SAP product hierarchy code defining the scope of products covered by this price list (e.g., automation systems, electrification components, aftermarket spare parts). Enables hierarchical pricing analysis and reporting.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic sales region code to which this price list applies (e.g., AMER, EMEA, APAC, DACH, NORDICS). Enables regional pricing differentiation for global industrial automation and electrification markets.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Defines the scheduled frequency at which this price list is reviewed and potentially updated by the pricing team. Supports pricing governance and ensures alignment with market conditions, COGS changes, and competitive dynamics.']
  rounding_rule varchar [note: 'Defines the price rounding rule applied to calculated prices on this list. Commercial rounding follows standard mathematical rounding; round_up and round_down enforce directional rounding for margin protection or competitive positioning.']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA sales organization code to which this price list is assigned. Defines the legal entity and organizational unit responsible for the pricing, enabling multi-entity and multi-country price list management.']
  scale_basis varchar [note: 'Defines whether volume-based pricing scales on this list are determined by order quantity, order value, weight, or volume. \'None\' indicates flat pricing with no scale breaks. Supports tiered pricing for large industrial automation orders.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this price list record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA SD, Salesforce CRM). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this price list record in the originating source system (e.g., SAP condition table key, Salesforce Price Book ID). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse and operational systems.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price list. Controls whether the list is available for use in sales transactions, under review, or retired. Drives workflow in SAP S/4HANA and Salesforce CRM.']
  superseded_by_code varchar [note: 'The code of the price list that replaces this one when it is superseded or retired. Enables version chain tracking and ensures sales teams can identify the current active replacement list.']
  tax_classification varchar [note: 'Tax treatment classification for prices on this list, determining how VAT, GST, or sales tax is applied during order processing. Supports multi-jurisdiction tax compliance for global industrial manufacturing sales.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price list by commercial purpose. Standard lists apply to general sales; promotional lists support time-limited campaigns; contract lists are tied to specific customer agreements; channel partner and distributor lists support indirect sales motions; intercompany lists govern internal transfer pricing; aftermarket lists cover MRO and spare parts; project-specific lists apply to ETO/MTO industrial automation projects.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the price list, enabling tracking of revisions and supersession history. Follows semantic versioning (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0.1). Critical for audit trails and regulatory compliance.']
  valid_from date [note: 'The date from which this price list becomes effective and can be applied to sales transactions. Used in SAP S/4HANA condition record date-based pricing determination.']
  valid_to date [note: 'The date on which this price list expires and is no longer applicable to new sales transactions. After this date, the status transitions to \'expired\'. Supports time-bound promotional and contract pricing.']

  Note: 'Master price list defining standard list prices for industrial automation products, electrification solutions, and aftermarket parts — consolidated with product domain as SSOT owner'
}

  regulatory_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_regulatory_certification. Business justification: Supplier qualification in industrial manufacturing requires verifying that suppliers meet the same regulatory certifications required by the products they supply (e.g., CE, RoHS, ATEX). Supplier quali']

  Note: 'Master record for all suppliers, vendors, and sub-contractors engaged in sourcing raw materials, components, subassemblies, and MRO supplies. Captures supplier identity, classification, qualification status, risk tier, payment terms, diversity classification, and global registration details as managed in SAP Ariba Supplier Management. Serves as the SSOT for supplier identity within the procurement domain.'
}

Table shared.contract {
  contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each procurement contract record in the lakehouse silver layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.']
  controlling_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.controlling_area. Business justification: Procurement contracts are scoped to a controlling area to enforce budget and cost object validity. Controlling area determines which cost centers and internal orders are valid for contract releases.']
  pricing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.pricing_agreement. Business justification: In back-to-back contract manufacturing, procurement contracts are structured to mirror customer pricing agreements. Finance and procurement align supplier cost ceilings with customer-committed prices ']
  spend_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.spend_category. Business justification: Procurement contracts are categorized by spend category (direct materials, MRO, indirect, etc.). The current category_code (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the spend category taxonomy. Adding s']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to procurement.supplier']
  supply_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_supply_agreement. Business justification: Procurement contracts often formalize and supersede supply agreements. A contract may reference the underlying scheduling agreement or blanket PO it governs. This link enables tracking the evolution f']
  uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: Procurement contracts specify quantities and pricing per unit of measure. Contract managers must reference the product master UOM to ensure contractual quantities align with ordering and invoicing pro']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status of the contract in SAP Ariba. Determines whether the contract is authorized for purchase order release.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the authorized approver who granted final approval for this contract in the procurement approval workflow.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract received final approval from the authorized approver, enabling it to become active for purchase order release.']
  ariba_contract_reference varchar [note: 'Native contract identifier from SAP Ariba Contract Management system, used for system-of-record traceability and integration reconciliation.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract automatically renews upon expiry unless a termination notice is issued within the notice period. Drives contract expiry alert workflows.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary country from which goods or services are supplied under this contract. Used for trade compliance and risk assessment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the procurement contract record was first created in SAP Ariba Contract Management. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the contract value and pricing conditions are denominated. Supports multi-currency global procurement operations.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract expires and is no longer valid for purchase order release. Triggers renewal workflow or re-sourcing event if not renewed.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract becomes legally effective and purchase orders may be released against it. Used for MRP source list validity and compliance checks.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the transfer of risk, cost, and responsibility between buyer and supplier for deliveries under this contract.']
  incoterms_location varchar [note: 'Named place or port associated with the Incoterms code, specifying the exact point of risk and cost transfer (e.g., \'Hamburg Port\' for FOB Hamburg).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the contract record in SAP Ariba. Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance.']
  minimum_order_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum monetary value that must be ordered per release or call-off under this contract. Enforced during purchase order creation to comply with supplier commercial terms.']
  number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique contract identifier as assigned in SAP Ariba Contract Management. Used for cross-system reference and supplier communication.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the procurement professional responsible for managing this contract, including renewals, compliance monitoring, and supplier performance.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'SAP payment terms key defining the payment schedule, discount periods, and due date calculation for invoices issued under this contract (e.g., NET30, 2/10NET30).']
  penalty_clause_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the penalty terms, including trigger conditions, calculation method, and maximum liability cap as negotiated with the supplier.']
  penalty_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract contains penalty clauses for supplier non-performance, late delivery, or quality failures. Triggers penalty tracking and enforcement workflows.']
  procurement_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the contract covers direct materials (production-related), indirect materials, services, capital expenditure (CAPEX), or maintenance repair and operations (MRO).']
  purchasing_group_code varchar [note: 'SAP purchasing group (buyer group) responsible for day-to-day management and release of purchase orders against this contract.']
  purchasing_org_code varchar [note: 'SAP purchasing organization responsible for negotiating and managing this contract. Defines the organizational scope and authority for contract execution.']
  reach_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract mandates supplier compliance with the EU REACH regulation (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals).']
  released_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative monetary value of purchase orders released against this contract to date. Compared against total contract value to monitor consumption and remaining commitment.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days prior to contract expiry by which a renewal or termination notice must be issued. Used to trigger automated renewal reminders.']
  rohs_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract mandates supplier compliance with the EU Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) directive for all delivered materials.']
  sap_outline_agreement_number varchar [note: 'Corresponding SAP S/4HANA MM outline agreement number (scheduling agreement or contract) linked to this procurement contract for purchase order release.']
  sourcing_event_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP Ariba sourcing event (RFQ/RFP) that resulted in the award of this contract. Provides traceability from sourcing to contract.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the procurement contract. Drives procurement eligibility, MRP source list activation, and compliance reporting.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the contract was terminated prior to its scheduled expiry, if applicable. Populated only for contracts with status \'terminated\'.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the contract was terminated before its scheduled end date. Used for supplier performance analysis and sourcing strategy review.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the procurement contract summarizing the scope of supply or service covered by the agreement.']
  total_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum total monetary value committed under this contract over its full validity period, in the contract currency. Used for spend commitment tracking and financial planning.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the procurement contract by its commercial structure. Determines release mechanism, pricing model, and SAP outline agreement category.']

  Note: 'Master record for all procurement contracts and supplier agreements including blanket purchase agreements, long-term supply agreements, frame contracts, and spot buy contracts. Captures contract type, spend category, contracted value, validity period, payment terms, Incoterms, penalty clauses, renewal options, and compliance obligations. Managed via SAP Ariba Contract Management.'
}

Business justification: Raw material POs must reference the engineering material specification to enforce alloy grade, temper, and certification requirements. Quality and procurement teams use this link to validate mill cert']

  product_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.product_specification. Business justification: POs for engineered/custom parts must reference the controlling product specification. Suppliers are contractually bound to the spec version on the PO — engineering and procurement use this link during']
  purchase_requisition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_requisition. Business justification: Purchase orders are created to fulfill purchase requisitions in the standard procure-to-pay process. Linking purchase_order to purchase_requisition enables requisition-to-PO conversion tracking, MRP-d']

  sla_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.sla_agreement. Business justification: Purchase orders for make-to-order manufacturing are directly tied to customer SLA agreements.   supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: A purchase order is a formal authorization issued to a specific supplier.   tax_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.tax_code. Business justification: Purchase orders reference tax codes to calculate input VAT and withholding tax on procurement transactions. Tax codes drive correct tax postings when goods receipts and invoices are processed.']
  uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: Every PO line item specifies a quantity in a unit of measure that must align with the product master UOM. Purchasing teams reference this daily to ensure correct goods receipt and invoice matching.']

  Note: 'Formal PO issued to a supplier authorizing the supply of materials, components, subassemblies, or MRO items at agreed terms. Captures PO type (standard, blanket release, subcontracting), supplier, plant, delivery address, payment terms, Incoterms, total PO value, currency, and approval status. Core transactional entity in SAP S/4HANA MM (ME21N).'
}

Business justification: Incoming inspection at goods receipt must verify received material against the engineering material specification. Quality inspectors use this link daily to pull acceptance criteria, test requirements']

Business justification: Goods receipts are posted against a specific purchase order in the GRN process (SAP MIGO/MIRO).   storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Goods receipts must specify which storage location the material is being received into. Warehouse and receiving teams use this to direct putaway. Standard in all ERP systems — goods receipt drives sto']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: goods_receipt should directly reference supplier master. Adding supplier_id FK eliminates redundant supplier_number string and enables direct supplier lookup.']
  uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: Goods receipts record received quantity in the product\'s unit of measure. Any UOM mismatch between PO and goods receipt triggers a conversion or exception — warehouse teams validate this on every rece']

  Note: 'Records the physical receipt of goods against a purchase order at a plant or warehouse location. Captures GRN (Goods Receipt Note) number, received quantity, receiving plant, storage location, batch number, quality inspection flag, and posting date. Triggers inventory update and initiates three-way match for invoice verification in SAP S/4HANA MM (MIGO).'
}

Table shared.location {
  location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each logistics network location record in the silver layer master data.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the logistics location, including street number and street name. Used for carrier pickup/delivery instructions and customs documentation.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address detail such as suite, building, floor, or unit number for the logistics location.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality in which the logistics location is situated. Used in route planning, carrier zone determination, and regulatory reporting.']
  code varchar [note: 'Business-assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the logistics location across all operational systems (SAP S/4HANA, Infor WMS, TMS). Used as the cross-system reference key for origin_location_code and destination_location_code in shipment and route records.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the logistics location is physically situated. Drives customs, trade compliance, and incoterms applicability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the logistics location master record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements, and master data governance.']
  customs_bond_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the customs bond or bonded warehouse license. Triggers renewal workflows to ensure uninterrupted bonded storage operations and trade compliance.']
  customs_bond_number varchar [note: 'Official customs bond or warehouse license number issued by the relevant customs authority for bonded warehouse locations. Required for customs compliance documentation and audit trails.']
  dock_count varchar [note: 'Total number of loading/unloading dock doors available at the logistics location. Used in capacity planning, appointment slot management, and freight order scheduling within TMS and WMS.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this logistics location record is valid and active in the logistics network. Supports temporal validity management for network planning and historical analysis.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which this logistics location record is no longer valid or active in the logistics network. Used to manage temporary locations, lease expirations, and network restructuring events.']
  ftz_zone_number varchar [note: 'Official FTZ or SEZ designation number assigned by the relevant trade authority for locations within a free trade zone. Used in customs and export control documentation.']
  iata_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for logistics locations that are air freight terminals or co-located with airports. Used in air freight routing, carrier service selection, and customs documentation.']
  inbound_dock_count varchar [note: 'Number of dock doors designated exclusively for inbound freight receiving operations. Supports inbound appointment scheduling and GRN (Goods Receipt Note) throughput planning.']
  is_customs_bonded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the logistics location is a licensed customs bonded warehouse authorized to store imported goods before customs duties are paid. Critical for trade compliance routing and customs declaration management.']
  is_free_trade_zone boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the logistics location is situated within a designated Free Trade Zone (FTZ) or Special Economic Zone (SEZ). Affects duty deferral, tariff treatment, and export control compliance.']
  is_hazmat_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the logistics location is certified and equipped to handle, store, or process hazardous materials (HAZMAT) in compliance with applicable regulations. Drives routing logic for hazmat shipments.']
  is_temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the logistics location has temperature-controlled storage or handling capabilities (cold chain, frozen, or climate-controlled). Required for routing temperature-sensitive products.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the logistics location master record. Used for change tracking, data synchronization across TMS/WMS systems, and audit compliance.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate (WGS 84 decimal degrees) of the logistics location. Used for geofencing in shipment tracking, distance calculations, and IIoT asset monitoring via Siemens MindSphere.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate (WGS 84 decimal degrees) of the logistics location. Used in conjunction with latitude for geofencing, route optimization, and real-time tracking event correlation.']
  max_weight_capacity_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum total weight in kilograms that the logistics location can handle or store at any given time. Used in freight order feasibility checks and load planning.']
  name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the logistics location (e.g., \'Chicago Distribution Center\', \'Hamburg Port of Entry\'). Used in reporting, carrier communications, and shipment documentation.']
  operating_days varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of days of the week the location is operational (e.g., \'MON,TUE,WED,THU,FRI\'). Used by TMS to validate delivery windows and avoid scheduling shipments on non-operating days.']
  operating_hours_end varchar [note: 'Standard daily closing time of the logistics location in HH:MM (24-hour) format, expressed in the location\'s local timezone. Defines the latest permissible arrival or departure time for freight operations.']
  operating_hours_start varchar [note: 'Standard daily opening time of the logistics location in HH:MM (24-hour) format, expressed in the location\'s local timezone. Used for appointment scheduling, carrier pickup windows, and TMS cutoff enforcement.']
  outbound_dock_count varchar [note: 'Number of dock doors designated exclusively for outbound freight dispatch operations. Supports outbound shipment scheduling and carrier pickup coordination.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA plant code associated with this logistics location when the location corresponds to a manufacturing plant or distribution center managed in SAP. Enables cross-reference between logistics network master data and SAP MM/PP/WM modules.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or postal code of the logistics location. Used by TMS for freight zone calculation, carrier rate lookup, and last-mile routing.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Internal logistics region or zone code (e.g., EMEA, APAC, AMER, LATAM) assigned to the location for freight planning, carrier contract scoping, and transport plan organization.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region of the logistics location. Required for domestic freight zone mapping and tax/regulatory jurisdiction determination.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the logistics location. Inactive or decommissioned locations are excluded from TMS route optimization and carrier assignment.']
  storage_capacity_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total volumetric storage capacity of the logistics location in cubic meters. Used for inventory planning, warehouse utilization reporting, and network capacity optimization.']
  temp_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature in degrees Celsius that the location\'s temperature-controlled storage can maintain. Used alongside temp_min_c to validate cold chain compatibility for inbound and outbound shipments.']
  temp_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature in degrees Celsius that the location\'s temperature-controlled storage can maintain. Used to validate suitability for cold chain shipments with specific temperature requirements.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the logistics location (e.g., \'America/Chicago\', \'Europe/Berlin\'). Used to correctly interpret operating hours, appointment windows, and shipment cutoff times in TMS scheduling.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the logistics node by its functional role in the supply chain network. Drives routing logic, dock assignment, customs handling, and TMS optimization rules.']
  un_locode varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE (United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations) for the logistics location. Internationally recognized code used in customs declarations, bills of lading, and cross-border trade documentation.']
  warehouse_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA or Infor WMS warehouse number assigned to this logistics location for warehouse management operations. Links the logistics network node to its WMS configuration for inventory and dock management.']

  Note: 'Master record for all physical locations participating in the logistics network, including manufacturing plants, distribution centers, cross-dock facilities, customer delivery points, supplier pickup points, ports of entry, and customs bonded warehouses. Stores location code, location type, address, GPS coordinates, operating hours, dock capacity, and customs bonded status. SSOT for logistics network node identity.'
}

Table shared.controlling_area {
  controlling_area_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the controlling area record in the lakehouse silver layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: controlling_area.chart_of_accounts (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the chart_of_accounts master. A controlling area is configured with a specific chart of accounts for cost accounting. Adding ']
  activity_type_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether activity type accounting is activated for this controlling area. When true, cost centers can record and allocate costs based on activity quantities (e.g., machine hours, labor hours), enabling activity-based costing and product cost calculation.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Default internal cost allocation method configured for this controlling area. Governs how overhead costs are distributed across cost centers, orders, and profit centers. Options include assessment (with secondary cost elements), distribution (with primary cost elements), indirect activity allocation, and template allocation.']
  capex_budget_profile varchar [note: 'Identifier of the CAPEX budget profile assigned to this controlling area, defining the rules for capital expenditure budgeting, availability control, and tolerance limits for investment orders and WBS elements. Supports CAPEX governance and IFRS IAS 16 compliance.']
  co_version varchar [note: 'Default controlling version used for plan/actual comparisons within this controlling area. Version \'0\' is the standard actual version; other versions (e.g., \'1\', \'2\') represent planning versions for budget scenarios, forecasts, or what-if analyses.']
  code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the controlling area within SAP CO. Typically a 1-4 character uppercase code (e.g., \'CO01\', \'AMER\'). This is the business key used in all CO transactions and cost accounting documents.']
  company_code_assignment_type varchar [note: 'SAP assignment type governing how company codes are linked to the controlling area. Value \'1\' = controlling area and company codes use the same fiscal year variant and chart of accounts; Value \'2\' = company codes may have different fiscal year variants (cross-company code controlling).']
  copa_type varchar [note: 'Type of Profitability Analysis configured for this controlling area. \'costing_based\' uses value fields for flexible margin analysis; \'account_based\' aligns with G/L accounts for IFRS-compliant segment reporting; \'both\' enables parallel CO-PA for comprehensive profitability management.']
  cost_center_standard_hierarchy varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the standard hierarchy for cost centers within this controlling area. The standard hierarchy defines the top-level grouping structure for all cost centers, enabling hierarchical cost reporting, allocation cycles, and management reporting roll-ups.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code representing the primary country of the controlling area. Used for statutory reporting, regulatory compliance, and geographic segmentation in management reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the controlling area master record was first created in the source system (SAP CO). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the controlling area currency used for all internal cost accounting, profitability analysis, and management reporting within this controlling area (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\').']
  currency_type varchar [note: 'SAP currency type code defining the role of the controlling area currency in multi-currency accounting. Standard values: 10=Company Code Currency, 20=Controlling Area Currency, 30=Group Currency, 40=Hard Currency, 50=Index-Based Currency, 60=Global Company Currency. Governs how currency translation is applied in CO documents.']
  description varchar [note: 'Extended free-text description of the controlling area, providing additional context about its organizational scope, geographic coverage, or business purpose beyond the short name field.']
  fiscal_year_variant varchar [note: 'SAP fiscal year variant code (e.g., \'K4\' for calendar year, \'V3\' for April-March) that defines the fiscal year structure — number of posting periods, special periods, and period-to-calendar-month mapping — applied across all company codes assigned to this controlling area.']
  group_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the corporate group reporting currency used in consolidated financial statements and group-level management reporting (e.g., \'USD\' for a US-headquartered multinational). Supports multi-currency consolidation under IFRS and GAAP.']
  language_key varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code representing the primary language used for text descriptions, reports, and correspondence within this controlling area (e.g., \'EN\' for English, \'DE\' for German, \'ZH\' for Chinese). Supports multi-language operations in multinational environments.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the controlling area master record in the source system. Supports change detection, incremental data loading, and audit compliance.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the local (functional) currency of the primary legal entity or region associated with this controlling area. Used for statutory reporting and local tax compliance.']
  name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the controlling area as configured in SAP CO. Used in reports, dashboards, and management accounting outputs to identify the organizational scope of cost accounting.']
  number_of_posting_periods varchar [note: 'Total number of regular posting periods defined for the fiscal year variant of this controlling area (typically 12 for calendar-year companies, 13 for 4-4-5 fiscal calendars). Determines the granularity of period-based cost reporting.']
  number_of_special_periods varchar [note: 'Number of special (adjustment) posting periods beyond the regular periods, used for year-end closing adjustments, audit corrections, and statutory adjustments. Typically 1-4 special periods are configured.']
  order_management_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether internal order management (CO-OPA) is activated for this controlling area. When true, internal orders can be used to collect and settle costs for specific projects, campaigns, or capital expenditure requests.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Primary planning version identifier used for budget and forecast postings within this controlling area. Enables separation of plan data from actuals and supports multi-scenario financial planning (e.g., base plan, revised forecast, stretch target).']
  product_cost_controlling_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Product Cost Controlling (CO-PC) is activated for this controlling area. When true, standard cost estimates, work-in-progress (WIP) calculations, and variance analysis for manufactured products are enabled.']
  profit_center_accounting_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Profit Center Accounting (CO-PCA) is activated for this controlling area. When true, all CO postings are simultaneously updated in profit center ledgers, enabling segment-level P&L and balance sheet reporting.']
  profit_center_standard_hierarchy varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the standard hierarchy for profit centers within this controlling area. Defines the organizational structure for profit center accounting, enabling segment-level profitability reporting and internal P&L analysis.']
  profitability_analysis_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Profitability Analysis (CO-PA) is activated for this controlling area. When true, revenues and costs are analyzed by market segments (e.g., customer, product, region) to support EBITDA reporting and contribution margin analysis.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Internal organizational region code (e.g., \'AMER\', \'EMEA\', \'APAC\') to which this controlling area belongs. Supports regional management reporting, cost allocation hierarchies, and geographic profitability analysis.']
  settlement_profile varchar [note: 'Default settlement profile for this controlling area, defining the rules for settling costs from internal orders, WBS elements, and production orders to receivers (cost centers, G/L accounts, assets, profitability segments). Critical for period-end closing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this controlling area master data was sourced (e.g., \'SAP_S4HANA_PROD\', \'SAP_S4HANA_EU\'). Supports multi-system data lineage and reconciliation in the lakehouse.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the controlling area. \'active\' indicates the area is live and accepting CO postings; \'inactive\' indicates it is suspended; \'decommissioned\' indicates it has been retired from use.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'Europe/Berlin\') for the controlling area, used to correctly interpret period-end closing timestamps, posting cutoff times, and scheduling of CO batch jobs across global operations.']
  transfer_price_variant varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transfer price variant configured for this controlling area, governing how intercompany and intra-company goods and service transfers are priced for internal cost accounting and profit center reporting. Supports arm\'s-length pricing compliance.']
  type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the controlling area spans multiple company codes (cross-company) or is assigned to a single company code (single-company). Cross-company controlling areas enable unified cost accounting across legal entities within a multinational group.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this controlling area configuration is effective and active for CO postings. Supports temporal validity tracking and historical configuration management.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'The date until which this controlling area configuration remains effective. A null or maximum date (9999-12-31) indicates the configuration is open-ended and currently active.']
  variance_key varchar [note: 'Default variance key assigned to this controlling area, defining which variance categories (e.g., price variance, quantity variance, efficiency variance) are calculated during production order settlement and product cost variance analysis.']

  Note: 'Master configuration entity defining the controlling area scope within SAP CO, representing the organizational unit within which cost accounting is performed. Captures controlling area code, name, assigned company codes, fiscal year variant, currency type, cost center standard hierarchy, and CO version settings. Governs the boundaries for internal cost allocation, profitability analysis, and management reporting.'
}

Table shared.fiscal_period {
  fiscal_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key identifying a fiscal period record in the financial calendar master. Used as the primary reference for all financial postings, period-end close workflows, and reporting period assignments across legal entities.']
  controlling_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.controlling_area. Business justification: fiscal_period.controlling_area_code (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the controlling_area master. Fiscal periods are scoped to controlling areas in SAP. Adding controlling_area_id FK normalizes']
  prior_period_fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the immediately preceding fiscal period record. Enables period-over-period variance analysis, prior period comparisons in financial statements, and sequential period navigation in reporting tools without requiring complex date calculations.']
  accounting_standard varchar [note: 'The accounting standard framework under which this fiscal period is reported. \'IFRS\' for International Financial Reporting Standards, \'GAAP\' for US Generally Accepted Accounting Principles, \'IFRS_GAAP\' for dual-reporting entities, \'LOCAL_GAAP\' for local statutory reporting, \'STAT\' for statutory-only periods.']
  ap_posting_allowed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether accounts payable postings (vendor invoices, payments, credit memos) are permitted for this fiscal period. Independently controlled from GL postings via the SAP posting period variant by account type.']
  ar_posting_allowed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether accounts receivable postings (customer invoices, payments, credit memos) are permitted for this fiscal period. Independently controlled from GL postings via the SAP posting period variant by account type.']
  asset_posting_allowed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether fixed asset accounting postings (acquisitions, retirements, depreciation runs) are permitted for this fiscal period. Asset accounting periods may be closed independently from other sub-ledgers.']
  close_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the period-end close process was fully completed and the period was officially closed for all postings. Used to measure close cycle time and track compliance with internal close calendar SLAs.']
  close_initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the period-end close process was formally initiated. Marks the start of the close workflow including sub-ledger reconciliation, accrual posting, and management review activities.']
  close_target_date date [note: 'The planned target date by which the fiscal period close should be completed, as defined in the financial close calendar. Used to monitor close cycle performance and identify delays against the internal close SLA.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the legal entity\'s country of incorporation. Used to apply country-specific fiscal calendar rules, statutory reporting requirements, and tax period definitions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this fiscal period record was created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage tracking, and SOX compliance documentation of fiscal calendar master data changes.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the functional currency of the legal entity for this fiscal period. Used for multi-currency consolidation and foreign currency translation during period-end close.']
  end_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the fiscal period ends. Defines the upper boundary for financial transactions and journal entries that can be posted to this period. Used in period-end close cutoff enforcement.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The four-digit fiscal year to which this period belongs. May differ from the calendar year depending on the company\'s fiscal year-end configuration (e.g., fiscal year 2024 may span April 2023 to March 2024).']
  fiscal_year_variant varchar [note: 'Code identifying the fiscal year variant configuration that defines the fiscal calendar structure (e.g., K4 for calendar year with 4 special periods, V3 for April–March fiscal year). Determines how periods are mapped to calendar months and how special periods are handled.']
  gl_posting_allowed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether general ledger journal entry postings are currently permitted for this fiscal period. Controlled by the posting period variant and period status. When false, all GL postings to this period are blocked.']
  half_year_number varchar [note: 'The fiscal half-year (1 or 2) to which this period belongs. Used for semi-annual financial reporting and interim statutory filings required in certain jurisdictions.']
  is_adjustment_period boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this period is designated for audit adjustments and prior-period corrections. Adjustment periods allow authorized postings after the standard period close to accommodate external audit findings and statutory corrections.']
  is_special_period boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is a special adjustment period (periods 13–16 in SAP) used exclusively for year-end audit adjustments, closing entries, and statutory corrections. Special periods do not correspond to calendar months and are restricted to authorized finance users.']
  is_year_end_period boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this period is the final closing period of the fiscal year. Triggers year-end close workflows including balance carryforward, retained earnings posting, and statutory financial statement preparation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this fiscal period record was last updated. Tracks changes to period status, posting permissions, or calendar definitions. Essential for audit trail and SOX internal controls over financial reporting.']
  ledger_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the accounting ledger (leading or non-leading) in SAP S/4HANA to which this fiscal period applies. Supports parallel accounting where multiple ledgers are maintained for different accounting standards (e.g., IFRS ledger \'0L\', local GAAP ledger \'L1\').']
  legal_entity_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the legal entity (company code in SAP) for which this fiscal period definition applies. Supports multi-entity and multinational operations where different legal entities may have different fiscal calendars or period statuses.']
  mm_posting_allowed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether materials management postings (goods receipts, goods issues, inventory movements) are permitted for this fiscal period. MM period close is managed separately from FI period close in SAP and must be aligned for COGS and inventory valuation accuracy.']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the fiscal period (e.g., \'January 2024\', \'Q1 FY2024\', \'Period 01 FY2024\'). Used in financial reports, dashboards, and period-end close communications.']
